nodes.label: A'P+-form of a holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A vertically equivalent normal form for a holonomy sequence, written p_k = d_k tau_k with d_k in A' and tau_k in P+. It is the coordinate system for the bounded, balanced, mixed, linear, and contracting cases.

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs discrepancy function
nodes.summary: The scalar quantity xi_epsilon = epsilon |F_nabla| - (1-|u|^2)/(2 epsilon), measuring imbalance between curvature and potential terms. Estimates on its positive part drive the improved codimension-two monotonicity.

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs energy-concentration varifold
nodes.summary: The varifold V whose weight measure is the weak limit of bounded abelian Higgs energy measures as epsilon -> 0. The paper proves that this limit is stationary and integral of dimension n-2.

evidence.quote: the energy measures converge subsequentially to (the weight measure mu of) a stationary integral (n-2)-varifold

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs energy measure
nodes.summary: The Radon measure with density e_epsilon(u,nabla) against Riemannian volume, where e_epsilon is the integrand of the rescaled energy. Weak limits of these measures define the weight measure of the limiting varifold.

evidence.quote: the energy measures converge subsequentially, in duality with C^0(M), to the weight measure

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs Euler-Lagrange equations
nodes.summary: The critical point equations for E_epsilon: nabla^*nabla u = (1/(2 epsilon^2))(1-|u|^2)u and epsilon^2 d^* omega = <nabla u,iu>. They are the PDE system analyzed throughout the compactness proof.

evidence.quote: critical point for the energy E_epsilon ... if and only if it satisfies the system nabla^*nabla u = 1/(2epsilon^2)(1-|u|^2)u; epsilon^2 d^*omega = <nabla u,iu>
evidence.section: 2. The Yang-Mills-Higgs equations on U(1) bundles

nodes.label: (n-2)-energy monotonicity for abelian Higgs pairs
nodes.summary: A monotonicity estimate for the normalized energy r^{2-n} int_{B_r} e_epsilon, improved from the usual Yang-Mills codimension-four scaling. It supplies the density bounds needed for rectifiability and compactness.

evidence.quote: improved to (near-) monotonicity of the (n-2)-density r^{2-n}f(p,r) on small balls

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs pair
nodes.summary: A pair (u,nabla) consisting of a section u of a Hermitian line bundle and a Hermitian connection nabla on that bundle. These are the fields varied by the Yang-Mills-Higgs energy.

evidence.quote: couples (u,nabla) consisting of a section u of a given Hermitian line bundle L -> M, and a metric connection nabla on L

nodes.label: Abelian Higgs stress-energy tensor
nodes.summary: The tensor T_epsilon=e_epsilon g - 2 nabla u^* nabla u - 2 epsilon^2 omega^* omega associated to inner variations of E_epsilon. For critical pairs it is divergence-free and yields monotonicity and stationarity identities.

evidence.quote: T_epsilon(u,nabla):=e_epsilon(u,nabla)g-2 nabla u^*nabla u-2 epsilon^2 omega^*omega

nodes.label: abnormal extremal
nodes.summary: A lifted extremal in the annihilator/symplectic-kernel characterization whose projection is a singular horizontal path or singular geodesic.

nodes.label: ABP Hessian-second-fundamental-form correction
nodes.summary: The symmetric form D_Sigma^2 u(x) - <II(x), y> obtained by correcting the intrinsic Hessian of the auxiliary potential by the second fundamental form in a normal direction y. It is central to the ABP determinant comparison and to equality rigidity.

nodes.label: AC1 condition for anisotropic integrands
nodes.summary: The kernel of A(mu) has dimension at most the codimension n for every probability measure mu on G(N,m).

nodes.label: ACL holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A holonomy sequence associated to convergence toward a closed H-orbit, written in A'P+-form, with linear isotropy in G0 at the limiting orbit. It organizes the final section's analysis of points with nonclosed H-orbits.

evidence.section: 6.1. Analysis of holonomy sequences in the absence of a fixed point

nodes.label: Admissible base vertex condition
nodes.summary: A condition on a base vertex A' requiring that lines through nonincident sides of the base form triangles with the two adjacent rays from A' whose two angles away from A' are not larger than pi/2.

evidence.quote: there exists a vertex A' such that the following holds
inner angles of \Delta , except the one at A' , are not larger than \pi/2

nodes.label: Admissible Hermitian-Yang-Mills connection
nodes.summary: A Chern connection of a smooth HYM metric on the locally free locus of a reflexive sheaf, with locally finite L2 curvature. It is the analytic object whose tangent cones are characterized.

nodes.label: Admissible Kähler eigenvalue cone
nodes.summary: An open cone of admissible eigenvalue vectors, lying in the positive trace half-space and containing the first octant. Solutions are required to have $\lambda[h_\varphi]\in\Gamma$.

nodes.label: Admissible measure for Laplace eigenvalues
nodes.summary: A positive Radon measure is admissible when the identity on smooth functions extends compactly from W^{1,2}(M,g) to L^2(mu). This permits a relaxed variational definition of weighted Laplace eigenvalues.

nodes.label: Affine plane in Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A translate of a two-dimensional linear subspace of Euclidean space. The corollary uses an affine plane whose intersection with a discrete Euclidean set is a net.

evidence.quote: there exists an affine plane H \subset \mathbb{R}^3 such that X \cap H is a net in H

nodes.label: ALE gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: One of the four standard end families of gravitational instantons, characterized in the excerpt by volume growth of order 4. It is the maximally growing family in the paper's classification.

evidence.quote: four categories: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH
the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1, respectively

nodes.label: Alekseevskii conjecture
nodes.summary: The conjectural and here proved statement that every homogeneous Einstein space with negative scalar curvature is diffeomorphic to Euclidean space.

evidence.quote: Any homogeneous Einstein space with negative scalar curvature is diffeomorphic to a Euclidean space

nodes.label: Normalizer transitivity step in the Alekseevskii proof
nodes.summary: The argument proving that the normalizer of the chosen solvable group G in the transitive group F acts transitively, enabling passage to a transitive solvable subgroup.

nodes.label: Alexandrov-Bakelman-Pucci maximum principle
nodes.summary: A maximum-principle method for elliptic PDE that bounds quantities using contact sets and Jacobian estimates. The paper's proof is explicitly modeled on this method.

nodes.label: Alexandrov embedded surface
nodes.summary: An immersed surface that bounds, via an isometric immersion, a Riemannian topological three-ball whose boundary is mean convex. Nonexceptional CMC spheres in the paper have this property.

evidence.quote: the immersion of S_H into M extends to an isometric immersion F : B to M of a Riemannian topological 3-ball B

nodes.label: ALF-D_k gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: An ALF gravitational instanton of type D_k. The paper studies its twistor space in the final part of Section 4.

nodes.label: ALF gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: One of the four standard end families of gravitational instantons, characterized in the excerpt by volume growth of order 3. The paper removes Minerbe's volume-growth hypothesis in this case.

evidence.quote: four categories: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH
the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1, respectively

nodes.label: ALF model end
nodes.summary: A gravitational-instanton model end of cubic volume growth, either of cyclic A-type built by the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz from a positive harmonic function on R^3 outside a compact set, or of D-type as a Z2 quotient of an A-type end.

nodes.label: Complex analytic compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons
aliases: compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons
nodes.summary: The compactification construction verifying Yau's complex analytic compactification conjecture for ALG gravitational instantons and ALH non-splitting gravitational instantons under faster than quadratic curvature decay.

evidence.quote: in both the ALG and ALH non-splitting cases, we can prove Yau's conjecture
Compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons

nodes.label: ALG gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: One of the four standard end families of gravitational instantons, characterized in the excerpt by volume growth of order 2. The paper's new compactification results include the ALG case.

evidence.quote: four categories: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH
the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1, respectively

nodes.label: ALG model end
nodes.summary: A model end built from the canonical flat hyperkähler metric on the cotangent bundle of a flat special Kähler cone C_beta, after quotienting by a monodromy-invariant lattice subbundle. It corresponds to two-dimensional asymptotic cones with finite monodromy.

nodes.label: ALG* model end
nodes.summary: A logarithmic two-dimensional-growth model end obtained by the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz on S^1 times the complement of a compact set in R^2, with I_k variants and Z2-quotient I_k* variants.

nodes.label: Algebraic blow-up cycle
nodes.summary: For a torsion-free sheaf F on CP^{n-1}, the codimension-two support cycle of (GrHNS(F))**/GrHNS(F). For an optimal algebraic tangent cone it is a local algebraic invariant of the sheaf germ.

nodes.label: Algebraic curvature tensor
aliases: curvature tensor with first Bianchi identity
nodes.summary: A symmetric bilinear form on Λ²V satisfying the first Bianchi identity, serving as the pointwise algebraic model for a Riemannian curvature tensor.

evidence.quote: Any R∈Sym_B^2(Λ²V) is called an algebraic curvature tensor.

nodes.label: Algebraic entropy of a finitely generated group
nodes.summary: The exponential growth rate of word balls in a finitely generated group; vanishing algebraic entropy is equivalent to subexponential growth in the terminology used by the paper.

evidence.quote: subexponential growth. That is, it has vanishing algebraic entropy

nodes.label: Algebraic group over a local field
aliases: algebraic group H over a local field k
nodes.summary: An algebraic group defined over a local field k. Such groups appear as target objects in the compatibility framework for superrigidity.

evidence.quote: an algebraic group H over a local field k

nodes.label: Algebraic tangent cone of a reflexive sheaf
nodes.summary: A torsion-free sheaf on the exceptional divisor obtained by restricting a reflexive extension of the pullback of E from the punctured ball across the blowup divisor. It is generally not unique.

nodes.label: Algebro-geometric two-step degeneration at infinity
nodes.summary: The degeneration mechanism relating metric blow-downs to algebraic scaling: X degenerates first to the weighted asymptotic cone W and then to the asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone C.

evidence.quote: one may realize the asymptotic cone C as a 2-step degeneration from X' through the intermediate cone W

nodes.label: ALH gravitational instanton end
nodes.summary: One of the four standard end families of gravitational instantons, characterized in the excerpt by volume growth of order 1. The paper studies a non-splitting subcase with exponential convergence to a flat model.

evidence.quote: four categories: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH
the volume growth is of order 4, 3, 2, and 1, respectively

nodes.label: ALH model end
nodes.summary: A gravitational-instanton model end given by the product hyperkähler metric on a flat three-torus times a half-line. It appears when the asymptotic cone has dimension one and the nilpotent symmetry is abelian.

nodes.label: ALH non-splitting instanton
nodes.summary: A non-splitting subcase of an ALH gravitational instanton. The paper treats this class in its compactification result and proves exponential convergence to the flat model.

nodes.label: ALH* Calabi model end
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional-growth model end obtained by applying the Gibbons-Hawking ansatz on a flat two-torus times a half-line to a linear harmonic function. The paper notes that this is precisely the Calabi model end.

nodes.label: Almgren-Pitts min-max theory
nodes.summary: A geometric-measure-theoretic min-max method using sweepouts and varifolds to produce minimal hypersurfaces realizing widths.

nodes.label: Almost-everywhere uniqueness of tangent cones
nodes.summary: The property that tangent cones at singular points are unique outside a set of the relevant Hausdorff measure zero.

nodes.label: Almost near points in a CAT(1) space
nodes.summary: A relation in a CAT(1) space: eta is almost near to xi when eta lies on the geodesic continuation from xi toward every antipode of xi, equivalently the relevant distances add to pi.

evidence.quote: eta is almost near to xi

nodes.label: Almost nilpotent end groups under visibility
aliases: folklore end-group conjecture for visibility manifolds
nodes.summary: For a complete finite-volume noncompact Riemannian manifold with $-1\leq K\leq 0$ whose universal cover is a visibility manifold, every end fundamental group is almost nilpotent, with dimension-uniform index bound.

evidence.quote: if the universal cover of  $M$  is a visibility manifold, then the fundamental group of each end of  $M$  is almost nilpotent

nodes.label: Almost nilpotent group
nodes.summary: A group is almost nilpotent if it contains a nilpotent subgroup of finite index.

evidence.quote: almost nilpotent, i.e., it contains a nilpotent subgroup of finite index

nodes.label: almost properly embedded free boundary minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: The regularity class of free boundary minimal hypersurfaces produced by min-max theory in compact manifolds with boundary, as cited from Li-Zhou's framework.

nodes.label: Alpha-invariant integrability estimate
nodes.summary: An exponential integrability bound for normalized plurisubharmonic potentials, with constants $\alpha$ and $C_\alpha$. The Trudinger-type estimate in the paper depends only on these constants.

evidence.quote: $\alpha$  and  $C_{\alpha}$  are the constants coming from the  $\alpha$ -invariant estimate
all constants there depend only on the  $\alpha$ -invariant of the underlying manifold

nodes.label: Ambient curvature estimate for stable minimal hypersurfaces in four-manifolds
aliases: closed four-manifold stable minimal curvature estimate
nodes.summary: For a closed Riemannian four-manifold (N,g), the second fundamental form of any two-sided stable minimal immersion M^3 -> N^4 is bounded by C(N,g) after multiplying by min{1, distance to boundary}.

nodes.label: Ample Q-line bundle
nodes.summary: A rational line bundle L whose positive multiple is an ample line bundle. It supplies the polarization and cohomology class c1(L).

nodes.label: Analytic-algebraic tangent cone correspondence for reflexive sheaf HYM connections
aliases: algebro-geometric characterization of analytic tangent cones
nodes.summary: The paper's central correspondence: the analytic tangent cone is unique and determined by the sheaf stalk; its limiting HYM cone is the one on G_alg and its analytic blow-up cycle equals the algebraic blow-up cycle.

evidence.quote: analytic blow-up cycle ... equals the algebraic

nodes.label: Analytic blow-up cycle
nodes.summary: A codimension-two algebraic cycle on CP^{n-1} whose components are the projectivized codimension-two bubbling components and whose coefficients are analytic multiplicities.

nodes.label: analytic manifold
nodes.summary: A manifold equipped with analytic coordinate changes. The paper's main setting is a real-analytic manifold of dimension 3.

nodes.label: Analytic multiplicity of HYM bubbling
nodes.summary: A positive integer attached to a codimension-two component of the bubbling set, measuring transverse Yang-Mills energy concentration. It is computed by a limiting curvature integral on a transverse complex surface slice.

evidence.quote: positive integers called the analytic multiplicities

nodes.label: analytic resolution map of the Martinet surface
nodes.summary: A proper analytic morphism from a smooth analytic surface S to the Martinet surface Sigma, with exceptional divisor E, used to study the pulled-back characteristic foliation and metric.

evidence.quote: proper analytic morphism π : S → Σ

nodes.label: Analytic subvariety
nodes.summary: A subvariety V of M, possibly of complex dimension p, over which intersection inequalities and subvariety phases are tested.

evidence.quote: for all p-dimensional subvarieties V with p = 1, 2, ..., n

nodes.label: analytic surface resolution by blowing up
nodes.summary: A proper analytic morphism obtained by admissible blowings-up that replaces a singular analytic surface by a smooth strict transform with normal crossing behavior.

nodes.label: Analytic tangent cone of an admissible HYM connection
nodes.summary: The pair consisting of a subsequential rescaled limit HYM cone connection A_infty and its analytic blow-up cycle. It records both limiting sheaf/connection data and codimension-two energy bubbling.

nodes.label: Ancient asymptotically cylindrical Brakke flow
aliases: ancient Brakke flow with cylindrical blowdown
nodes.summary: An ancient unit-regular integral Brakke flow whose blowdown is cylindrical; this is the main class classified in the paper.

evidence.quote: any ancient unit-regular integral Brakke flow with a cylindrical blowdown

nodes.label: Ancient kappa-solution to Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A non-flat ancient Ricci flow solution that is kappa-noncollapsed and has bounded nonnegative curvature.

evidence.quote: is non-flat and  \kappa -noncollapsed, and has bounded nonnegative curvature

nodes.label: Ancient low-entropy flow
nodes.summary: An ancient, unit-regular, cyclic, integral Brakke flow in R3 with entropy at most the entropy of the round shrinking cylinder, sqrt(2pi/e). This is the central class classified by the paper.

evidence.quote: ancient, unit-regular, cyclic, integral Brakke flows
evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Ancient oval mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A compact nonselfsimilar ancient mean curvature flow model, classified in this paper as the compact neutral-mode case. The excerpt names it as one of the five ancient low-entropy models.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Ancient solution to Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A Ricci flow solution defined on a time interval extending infinitely far into the past, typically (-infty,T].

evidence.quote: exists for all  t \in (-\infty, T] . Such a solution is called an ancient solution

nodes.label: Ancient shrinker mean-convex Brakke/weak-set flow
nodes.summary: A paired Brakke flow and weak set flow constructed on one side of a compact or asymptotically conical shrinker, with entropy controlled by the shrinker and strict shrinker mean convexity for sufficiently negative times.

evidence.section: 7 Existence of a smooth ancient shrinker mean convex flow

nodes.label: Ancient solution
nodes.summary: A solution of a parabolic geometric flow defined on a time interval extending back to negative infinity. Blowup limits at singularities are ancient solutions.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

evidence.quote: is called ancient, if it defined for all  $t \in (-\infty, T)$

nodes.label: Anisotropic area minimizer
nodes.summary: A hypersurface or graph that minimizes the anisotropic parametric surface energy among the relevant competitors. This is stronger than being merely stationary or critical.

evidence.quote: graph of u in R5 minimizes A_Phi

nodes.label: Anisotropic Bernstein problem
aliases: Bernstein problem for anisotropic minimal graphs
nodes.summary: The problem asks whether entire graphical critical points of A_Phi for uniformly elliptic integrands must be hyperplanes. The paper gives negative examples in the remaining dimensions 4 and 5.

nodes.label: Anisotropic Caccioppoli inequality for varifolds
nodes.summary: A reverse-Poincare type estimate controlling the squared tilt excess of a rectifiable varifold by its height excess relative to a plane and the L^2 norm of Psi-mean curvature; its proof uses USAC.

evidence.quote: Caccioppoli inequality similar to the one obtained by Allard

nodes.label: Anisotropic first variation
aliases: first variation with respect to Psi
nodes.summary: The derivative of the anisotropic varifold energy under the flow id + epsilon g generated by a compactly supported vector field g.

evidence.quote: first variation of V ... with respect to Psi

nodes.label: Anisotropic first-variation matrix B_Psi
nodes.summary: The matrix-valued expression determined by Psi and its Grassmannian differential that rewrites the anisotropic first variation as an integral pairing with Dg.

evidence.quote: where B_Psi(T) is defined through the equality

nodes.label: Anisotropic foliation profile ODE
nodes.summary: The nonlinear second-order ODE for the profile sigma of a radial leaf Sigma={|y|=sigma(|x|)}. Solving this ODE is equivalent to the leaf being a critical point of A_Psi.

evidence.quote: critical point is equivalent to sigma solving the ODE

nodes.label: Anisotropic integrand on the Grassmannian
nodes.summary: A positive C^k function Psi on G(N,m) assigning an orientation-free surface density to each tangent m-plane.

nodes.label: Anisotropic mean curvature
nodes.summary: The first-variation curvature associated to an anisotropic surface energy. In this paper, prescribing its sign for perturbed leaves supplies the sign needed for super- and sub-solution inequalities.

nodes.label: Anisotropic minimal graph equation
nodes.summary: For the constructed integrand Phi, the graph of u is critical for A_Phi exactly when u solves the quasilinear equation Psi_ij(grad u) u_ij = 0. This is the PDE solved by the bounded Dirichlet problems and the limiting entire graph.

nodes.label: Anisotropic parametric surface energy
nodes.summary: For an oriented hypersurface, the energy is the integral of Phi evaluated on the unit normal over surface area. Its critical points and minimizers are the anisotropic analogues of minimal hypersurfaces.

nodes.label: Anisotropic stationary varifold
nodes.summary: A varifold whose Psi-mean curvature representative can be chosen to be zero, equivalently critical for the anisotropic energy under compactly supported variations.

evidence.quote: If H can be chosen to be 0

nodes.label: Anisotropic energy of a rectifiable varifold
nodes.summary: For a rectifiable varifold V=(Gamma,theta), the anisotropic energy integrates Psi(T_x Gamma) against theta dH^m over Gamma.

nodes.label: Anosov flow
nodes.summary: A flow with a uniformly hyperbolic invariant splitting into flow, stable, and unstable directions.

nodes.label: Anosov geodesic flow
aliases: geodesic flow with the Anosov property
nodes.summary: A geodesic flow that satisfies the Anosov uniform hyperbolicity property.

nodes.label: Anosov representation
nodes.summary: A discrete representation of a word-hyperbolic group into a semisimple Lie group satisfying an Anosov dynamical boundary condition. In the excerpt, equivariant quasisymmetric maps are linked to Anosov representations and the paper applies its results to their rigidity.

evidence.quote: equivariant ones are linked to Anosov representations
applications of these results to asymptotically hyperbolic surfaces, rigidity of Anosov representations

nodes.label: Anosov surface
nodes.summary: A compact connected Riemannian surface without boundary whose geodesic flow has the Anosov property.

nodes.label: Antipodal lift of a projective-plane harmonic map
aliases: lift to S^2 via antipodal projection
nodes.summary: Given a harmonic map Phi:RP^2 -> S^{2m}, precomposition with the antipodal projection pi:S^2 -> RP^2 gives a harmonic map tilde Phi:S^2 -> S^{2m}. This lift allows sphere-index estimates to be applied to projective-plane maps.

nodes.label: Antipodal points at infinity
nodes.summary: Two ideal boundary points of a CAT(0) space are antipodal when their Tits distance is at least pi.

nodes.label: Approximate hyperkähler triple
nodes.summary: A triple of differential forms satisfying the hyperkähler equations up to controlled error. The paper constructs such a triple before perturbing to exact hyperkähler metrics.

evidence.quote: Construction of the approximate hyperkähler triple

nodes.label: Approximate Lipschitz graph
nodes.summary: An approximate Lipschitz graph is a Lipschitz-graph surrogate constructed in the proof to pass from local flatness and square-function estimates to rectifiability of the finite-square-function set.

evidence.quote: 10. Construction of an approximate Lipschitz graph

nodes.label: Approximate metric
nodes.summary: The Riemannian metric induced by the approximate hyperkähler data before solving the final nonlinear correction problem. The paper studies its geometry and regularity.

evidence.quote: Geometry and regularity of the approximate metric

nodes.label: Approximate tangent plane
nodes.summary: An approximate tangent plane to a set at a point is an affine n-plane that approximates the set at small scales after discarding a part whose Hausdorff n-measure is negligible relative to scale. It is the Euclidean model that the paper replaces by metric tangent models.

evidence.quote: has a unique  $n$ -dimensional approximate tangent plane at  $\mathcal{H}^n$ -a.e.  $x \in E$
an  $n$ -dimensional affine subspace  $W \ni x$  such that

nodes.label: Approximate tangent space
nodes.summary: For an m-rectifiable set, the approximate m-plane tangent exists at H^m-almost every point and is used as the plane argument of the anisotropic integrand.

nodes.label: Approximately stable set
nodes.summary: For a P-module V and sequence p_k in P, the set of limits v = lim v_k such that p_k v_k remains bounded. Applied to g/p, it gives approximately stable tangent directions for conformal dynamics.

nodes.label: Area functional integrand
nodes.summary: The isotropic surface energy used as the reference integrand; in the paper it satisfies USAC and nearby integrands inherit atomic-type conditions.

nodes.label: Area-index and energy-index equivalence for harmonic spheres
nodes.summary: For weakly conformal harmonic maps from S^2 to spheres, viewed as branched minimal immersions, the area index coincides with the energy index. This transfers energy-index lower bounds to area-index lower bounds for minimal spheres.

nodes.label: Area-minimising hypersurface mod $p$
aliases: codimension 1 rectifiable area minimising current mod $p$
nodes.summary: A codimension-one rectifiable current minimizing area among competitors with the same boundary modulo $p$. The paper applies the $S_Q$ theory with $Q=p/2$ to these hypersurfaces.

evidence.quote: codimension 1 rectifiable area minimising currents mod  $p$  for any integer  $p \geq 2$
an area minimising hypersurface (i.e. a codimension 1 rectifiable (representative) current) mod  $p$
evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Area-minimizing hypercone with isolated singularity
nodes.summary: A dilation-invariant area-minimizing hypersurface cone whose singular set is only the vertex. Hardt-Simon proved such cones admit an associated foliation by minimizing hypersurfaces.

evidence.quote: every area-minimizing hypercone  $\mathbf{C}$  having only an isolated singularity

nodes.label: Area-minimizing hypersurface
nodes.summary: A hypersurface minimizing area among admissible competitors. The Hardt-Simon leaves used in the paper are smooth area-minimizing hypersurfaces.

nodes.label: Area-neighborhood examples of atomic integrands
nodes.summary: Integrands sufficiently close to the area functional in C^2 satisfy USAC and hence AC, while C^1-nearby integrands satisfy AC1, giving nontrivial high-codimension examples.

evidence.quote: C2 neighborhood of the area functional satisfy (USAC); (USAC) implies (AC)
C1 neighborhood of the area functional satisfy (AC1)

nodes.label: Area-volume isoperimetric deficit for a sphere map
nodes.summary: The conformal-case isoperimetric deficit comparing $P_{n-1}(u)^{n/(n-1)}$ with $|V_n(u)|$. Its linearization gives the isoperimetric quadratic form $Q_{n,isop}$.

nodes.label: Arithmetic complex hyperbolic manifold
nodes.summary: The property that a complex hyperbolic manifold has an arithmetic complex hyperbolic lattice as its fundamental group, up to commensurability.

nodes.label: arithmetic gap for integer-multiple area spectra
nodes.summary: An elementary number-theoretic argument showing that an increasing sequence with unit gaps drawn from integer multiples of finitely many numbers all greater than one eventually exceeds (1+epsilon)p.

nodes.label: Arithmetic lattice
nodes.summary: A lattice in a semisimple Lie group arising, up to commensurability, from integral points of an algebraic group over a number field. Nonarithmetic lattices are lattices not of this form.

nodes.label: Aspherical manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth manifold whose universal cover is contractible.

evidence.quote: a smooth manifold is aspherical if it has contractible universal cover

nodes.label: Associated metric flow of a super Ricci flow
aliases: metric flow associated to a super Ricci flow
nodes.summary: The construction sending a super Ricci flow on M to X=M x I with time-slices M x {t}, slice metric d_g_t, and conjugate heat kernel measures K(x,t;.,s) dg_s. The resulting metric flow is H_n-concentrated in dimension n.

nodes.label: Associated Ricci-DeTurck perturbation
nodes.summary: The tensor h obtained by pulling back the target spacetime metric by a comparison map and subtracting the source metric. When the inverse comparison evolves by harmonic map heat flow, this tensor satisfies a Ricci-DeTurck perturbation equation.

nodes.label: Asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The unique Calabi-Yau cone obtained as a pointed Gromov-Hausdorff blow-down limit of the complete Calabi-Yau manifold.

evidence.quote: any X as above is asymptotic to a unique Calabi-Yau cone C at infinity
it does not depend on the choice of subsequences involved in the definition

nodes.label: Asymptotic cone of a metric space
nodes.summary: A pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit of a complete metric space under distance rescalings tending to zero at infinity. The asymptotic cones of gravitational instantons determine the ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH classes.

nodes.label: Asymptotic end structure
nodes.summary: The differential and metric geometry of the noncompact end of a gravitational instanton. The paper emphasizes that this is finer than just the tangent cone at infinity.

evidence.quote: What are the differential and metric structures of the infinity of these gravitational instantons?

nodes.label: Asymptotic fibration
nodes.summary: The fibration structure built at infinity of a gravitational instanton. In the paper it is developed from short geodesic loops, their holonomy, loop bases, and local covering spaces.

evidence.quote: From geodesic loops to Riemannian fibration

nodes.label: Asymptotic Riemannian dimension
nodes.summary: The minimal dimension of a complete Riemannian manifold into which a metric space embeds isometrically, when such a manifold exists. The paper introduces it as a large-scale invariant for discrete metric spaces.

evidence.quote: we define the asymptotic Riemannian dimension of a metric space as the minimal dimension of a complete Riemannian manifold in which it embeds isometrically

nodes.label: Asymptotic to a cone at infinity
nodes.summary: A hypersurface approaches a cone at large scales, with smooth asymptotics. This is a defining property of the Hardt-Simon leaves used here.

nodes.label: Asymptotic to the standard metric of order epsilon
aliases: standard metric asymptotics of order epsilon
nodes.summary: A decay-rate statement saying that the metric on the end approaches the appropriate standard model metric with order epsilon. It is the asymptotic conclusion of the paper's first main classification property.

evidence.quote: must be asymptotic to the standard metric of order  $\epsilon$

nodes.label: Asymptotically almost sure event
nodes.summary: A family of events indexed by n holds asymptotically almost surely when its probability tends to one as n tends to infinity. The paper states its random-cover spectral gap in this sense.

evidence.quote: holds asymptotically almost surely (a.a.s.) if they hold with probability tending to one as  $n \to \infty$

nodes.label: Asymptotically conical background Kähler form
aliases: background Kähler metric asymptotic to the cone
background Kähler form with decaying Monge-Ampère error
nodes.summary: A family of Kähler forms on crepant resolutions of approximating varieties, constructed to be uniformly asymptotic to the Calabi-Yau cone and to have controlled Monge-Ampère volume error.

evidence.quote: we construct a family of background Kähler metrics on which are asymptotic to the cone and are approximate solutions
they are uniformly asymptotic to the cone C

nodes.label: Asymptotically conical self-shrinker
nodes.summary: A self-shrinker whose dilations converge smoothly locally away from the origin to a regular cone as the scale tends to zero.

nodes.label: Asymptotically optimal closed hyperbolic surface spectral-gap sequence
aliases: large-genus closed surfaces with lambda one tending to one quarter
nodes.summary: There exists a sequence of closed hyperbolic surfaces with genus tending to infinity and first nonzero Laplacian eigenvalue tending to 1/4. This is the closed-surface consequence obtained by compactifying the non-compact sequence.

evidence.quote: There exist closed hyperbolic surfaces  $X_i$  with genera  $g(X_i)\to \infty$

nodes.label: Asymptotically optimal cusped hyperbolic surface spectral-gap sequence
aliases: finite-area non-compact surfaces with spectral gap tending to one quarter
nodes.summary: There exists a connected finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surface sequence whose Euler characteristics tend to negative infinity and whose bottom positive Laplacian spectrum tends to 1/4. This is the non-compact sequence extracted from the random-cover theorem.

evidence.quote: There exist connected finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surfaces  $X_i$  with  $\chi(X_i)\to -\infty$

nodes.label: Atomic condition for anisotropic integrands
nodes.summary: The condition consisting of AC1 plus the rigidity clause that equality in the kernel dimension forces the probability measure to be a Dirac mass at one m-plane.

evidence.quote: if dim Ker A(mu) = n, then mu = delta_T0

nodes.label: Atomic-condition rigidity of Young measures for stationary graphs
nodes.summary: Under the atomic condition, Young measures associated with stationary graph div-curl inclusions supported in K_FPsi are forced to be trivial.

evidence.quote: only Young measures generated by div-curl inclusions ... are trivial

nodes.label: Automorphic kernel for a hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A kernel adapted to the automorphic or quotient structure of a hyperbolic surface, listed as part of the interior parametrix machinery. The excerpt does not provide its formal definition.

nodes.label: Auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère comparison equation
nodes.summary: A comparison device in which an auxiliary potential solves a complex Monge-Ampère equation with a chosen right-hand side depending on the original solution. This auxiliary equation is then compared to the original equation by a maximum argument.

evidence.quote: compare the given equation with an auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation

nodes.label: Auxiliary divergence equation on a submanifold
nodes.summary: The proof introduces an auxiliary potential u through a divergence equation involving f, its intrinsic gradient, and the mean curvature vector. This potential supplies the Hessian data used in the ABP-type argument.

nodes.label: Average conformal deficit for a sphere map
nodes.summary: The scale-invariant deficit comparing the Dirichlet energy to the generalized area, written in the paper as a nonlinear conformal deficit. Its linearization gives the conformal quadratic form $Q_{n,conf}$.

nodes.label: Averaged B_Psi matrix A(mu)
nodes.summary: For a Borel probability measure mu on G(N,m), A(mu) is the average of B_Psi(T) over the Grassmannian and is the matrix used in the atomic conditions.

nodes.label: Weighted L2 cohomology of b-metrics
nodes.summary: For a b-metric on the interior of a compact manifold with boundary, the sign of a nonzero boundary weight determines whether weighted L2 cohomology behaves like ordinary or compactly supported cohomology. The paper uses this as the depth-two QAC input.

evidence.section: 5. L2-cohomology of quasi-asymptotically conical metrics of depth 2

nodes.label: Backwardly selfsimilar mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A flow invariant under parabolic dilations for negative times. Tangent flows in the paper have this property by Huisken monotonicity.

nodes.label: Bad-angle region
nodes.summary: The bad-angle region is one of the exceptional sets in the rectifiability part of the proof. Its measure is controlled by estimates for the smoothed square function and related Carleson quantities.

evidence.quote: Small measure of LD and BA

nodes.label: Baiocchi time-integral transform
nodes.summary: The transformation u(x,t)=int_0^t theta(x,tau) d tau, which preserves the positivity set and converts the Stefan problem locally into a parabolic obstacle problem.

nodes.label: Bakry-Émery curvature-dimension condition
nodes.summary: An Eulerian curvature-dimension condition expressed through Bakry-Émery/Bochner-type analytic inequalities. The correction uses its tensorization and its equivalence with CD(K,infinity) to justify tensorization in infinitesimally Hilbertian spaces.

evidence.quote: the tensorization property for the Bakry-Émery condition BE(K, infinity)
the equivalence between Eulerian and Lagrangian curvature-dimension conditions BE(K, infinity) and CD(K, infinity)

nodes.label: Balanced cubic energy estimate
nodes.summary: An energy inequality in which the energy growth is controlled by the square of the balanced half-derivative control parameter B, such as dE/dt lesssim B^2 E. It is the key improvement over classical estimates involving ||partial^2 u||_{L^infty}.

evidence.quote: What is important here is that our energy estimates are cubic and balanced.

nodes.label: Balanced linear holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A holonomy sequence that is balanced in the alpha-beta weight sense and has tau_k identically one. The paper shows that such a sequence either forces conformal flatness or produces a codimension-one analytic foliation.

evidence.section: 4. Proof given a linear, balanced holonomy sequence

nodes.label: Ball quotient
aliases: Gamma quotient of the complex ball
nodes.summary: A quotient Gamma \ B^n of the complex unit ball by a torsion-free discrete subgroup of its automorphism group. Ball quotients provide the locally symmetric examples analyzed in the paper.

nodes.label: Ball quotient asymptotics of conical Kähler-Einstein metrics
aliases: asymptotics of conical KE metrics on ball quotients
nodes.summary: The refined asymptotic description of the conical Kähler-Einstein metrics in the small-angle limit when the divisor complement is a ball quotient.

evidence.quote: when X \setminus D is a ball quotient

nodes.label: Barbot surface
nodes.summary: A homogeneous complete maximal surface in H^{2,n} that is flat and is an orbit of a diagonal subgroup of the isometry group. The paper treats Barbot surfaces as rigid extremal examples among maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: Among homogeneous complete maximal surfaces, we have the totally geodesic hyperbolic planes and Barbot surfaces which are flat and orbits of diagonal subgroup of $\mathsf{G}$.

nodes.label: BAUP criterion for uniform rectifiability
nodes.summary: The BAUP criterion is a David-Semmes criterion characterizing uniform rectifiability through bilateral approximation by unions of planes or related approximating sets. It is cited as a background geometric criterion connected to singular-integral boundedness.

evidence.quote: uses the so-called BAUP criterion for uniform rectifiability found by David and Semmes

nodes.label: Bernstein-type classification for quadratic-cone asymptotics
nodes.summary: Area-minimizing hypersurfaces asymptotic to a quadratic cone are classified as either the cone itself or a leaf of the associated Hardt-Simon foliation.

evidence.quote: characterizes area-minimizing hypersurfaces asymptotic to a quadratic cone as either the cone itself, or some leaf

nodes.label: Bessel-zero spectral decomposition of the Euclidean ball
aliases: Bessel zero formulas for ball eigenvalues
nodes.summary: The separation-of-variables description of Dirichlet and Neumann Laplacian eigenvalues on a Euclidean ball in terms of zeros of Bessel functions and their boundary-condition variants. The excerpt supports this at the section-heading level; exact zero formulas are in the omitted middle of the paper.

evidence.quote: Dirichlet and Neumann eigenvalues of the ball and lattice counting problems
On the asymptotics of Bessel functions in the Fresnel regime

nodes.label: Beta endomorphism of a Lie algebra
nodes.summary: A self-adjoint endomorphism associated to a Lie algebra bracket through the real GIT stratum label; beta plus is the normalized shifted endomorphism used in curvature estimates.

evidence.quote: endomorphism beta associated to a Lie algebra

nodes.label: Beta invariant of a divisorial valuation
nodes.summary: For a divisorial valuation over a log Fano pair, the difference between its log discrepancy and its S-invariant.

nodes.label: Beta-volume of nilradical orbits
nodes.summary: A G-invariant function on M obtained by applying the log beta-volume of inner products to the vertical metrics on the nilradical orbits.

nodes.label: Beta-weighted scalar curvature estimate
nodes.summary: The non-negativity estimate for the beta-weighted trace of the vertical Ricci curvature of nilradical orbits, with equality tied to derivations.

evidence.quote: non-negativity of the beta-weighted scalar curvature

nodes.label: Bi-Lipschitz equivalence
nodes.summary: Two metrics or metric spaces are K-bi-Lipschitz equivalent when distances are distorted by at most a factor K in both directions.

evidence.quote: $\rho$  is  $K$ -bi-Lipschitz equivalent to the Euclidean norm

nodes.label: biLip(K) Euclidean metric model
nodes.summary: The class $\mathrm{biLip}(K)$ consists of metric spaces of the form $(\mathbb{R}^n,\rho)$ whose metric is K-bi-Lipschitz equivalent to the Euclidean norm. These models are allowed to vary with point and scale in the paper's rectifiability criterion.

evidence.quote: let  $\mathrm{biLip}(K)$  be the set of metric spaces  $Y = (\mathbb{R}^n, \rho)$  such that  $\rho$  is  $K$ -bi-Lipschitz equivalent to the Euclidean norm

nodes.label: Billiard length spectrum
aliases: length spectrum of a billiard domain
nodes.summary: The closure of the set of lengths of closed billiard trajectories in the domain.

nodes.label: Billiard reflection count
nodes.summary: The integer q counting the number of reflections of a periodic billiard trajectory at the boundary.

nodes.label: Bishop-Gromov volume monotonicity
nodes.summary: The monotonicity of normalized volume ratios under nonnegative Ricci curvature. The paper uses it both to obtain cone limits and to force equality of volume densities on the constructed metric space.

evidence.quote: It follows from the Bishop-Gromov volume monotonicity that ... converges ... to a Calabi-Yau cone.
the Bishop-Gromov inequality applying on W yields that the volume ratio is a non-increasing function

nodes.label: Blowup exceptional divisor at a point
nodes.summary: For the blowup p: Bhat -> B at the origin, the exceptional divisor is D = CP^{n-1}. Algebraic tangent cones are sheaves on this divisor.

nodes.label: Blowup limit singularity model
nodes.summary: A parabolically rescaled limit obtained near a singularity of mean curvature flow; such limits are ancient solutions and model local singularity formation.

evidence.quote: all singularity models (blowup limits) are ancient

nodes.label: Bochner Betti-number vanishing and estimate under partial curvature-operator positivity
aliases: Betti-number estimate under lowest curvature-operator eigenvalue bounds
nodes.summary: The reusable conclusion that positivity of λ_1+...+λ_{n-p} forces b_p and b_{n-p} to vanish, while a lower average bound plus diameter control gives an exponential upper bound and forces harmonic p-forms to be parallel when κ=0.

evidence.quote: If λ_1 + ... + λ_{n-p} > 0, then the Betti numbers b_p(M) and b_{n-p}(M) vanish.
When κ ≤ 0 and diam M ≤ D, then there is a constant C(n,κD²)>0 such that b_p(M) ≤ ...

nodes.label: Bochner technique
nodes.summary: A method that applies a Weitzenböck or Lichnerowicz formula to harmonic tensors or forms and uses curvature-term positivity or lower bounds to prove vanishing, parallelism, or kernel estimates.

evidence.quote: The proof of Theorem A relies on the Bochner technique
The following theorem summarizes the framework of the Bochner technique for general Lichnerowicz Laplacians.

nodes.label: Stability of absence of boundary under noncollapsed pGH convergence
nodes.summary: If a sequence of pointed noncollapsed RCD(K,N) spaces has no boundary in a larger ball and converges in pmGH topology, then the limit has no boundary in a smaller ball. This is a key gap and stability statement.

evidence.quote: spaces without boundary converge to spaces without boundary under non collapsing pGH convergence
evidence.section: 1.4 Stability and gap theorems for boundaries

evidence.quote: Then Y has no boundary on B_1(y).
evidence.section: 1.4 Stability and gap theorems for boundaries

nodes.label: Ahlfors regular boundary measure
nodes.summary: The boundary measure H^{N-1} restricted to ∂X has two-sided r^{N-1} growth on boundary balls, sharply near the Euclidean half-space volume in the epsilon-regularity regime.

evidence.quote: the boundary measure H^{N-1}_∂X is Ahlfors regular on B_1(p) with volume close to the Euclidean volume.
(1-epsilon) omega_{N-1} r^{N-1} <= H^{N-1}(∂X cap B_r(x)) <= (1+epsilon) omega_{N-1} r^{N-1}
evidence.section: 1.2 An epsilon-regularity theorem for top dimensional singularities

nodes.label: boundary area gap for core minimal hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: In the constructed core, every interior minimal hypersurface has n-volume larger than the largest boundary component, with twice the n-volume used for one-sided hypersurfaces.

nodes.label: Boundary ball in a noncollapsed RCD space
nodes.summary: A ball B_r(x) in a noncollapsed RCD space that is δ r-Gromov-Hausdorff close to the radius-r ball centered at the boundary point of R^N_+. It packages the local half-space approximation used throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: B_r(x) is a δ-boundary ball if it is δ r-GH close to B_r^{R^N_+}(0).

nodes.label: Boundary C^2 regularity for Dini-density Monge-Ampère solutions
nodes.summary: The up-to-boundary C^2 regularity of a convex solution to the second boundary value problem when the Monge-Ampère density is Dini continuous. This is a companion regularity statement to the Hölder-density C^{2,α} estimate.

evidence.quote: and $C^2$ regularity when $f$ is Dini continuous

nodes.label: Boundary distance function
nodes.summary: The restriction of the Riemannian distance d_g to pairs of boundary points, d_g|_{partial M x partial M}. In inverse problems it is the travel time data measured at the boundary.

evidence.quote: boundary distance function, i.e.  $d_g|_{\partial M\times \partial M}$
restriction (to  $\partial M \times \partial M$ ) of the distance function  $d_g$
the collection of distances between boundary points
Given a noncollapsed RCD(-(N-1),N) space ... with boundary we denote by the distance function from ∂X.
evidence.section: 7 Distance from the boundary and noncollapsing of boundaries

evidence.quote: d_{∂X_n} -> d_{∂R^N_+} uniformly and in W^{1,2}
evidence.section: 7 Distance from the boundary and noncollapsing of boundaries

nodes.label: Boundary distance rigidity
nodes.summary: The property that boundary-to-boundary distances determine the Riemannian structure up to isometry for a simple Riemannian manifold with boundary. The paper cites it as a route to compactly supported curvature cases.

evidence.quote: This conjecture suggests that in a simple Riemannian manifold with boundary, the collection of distances between boundary points determines the Riemannian structure, up to an isometry.

nodes.label: Boundary-fixing diffeomorphism invariance
nodes.summary: Boundary distance data is unchanged when the metric is pulled back by a diffeomorphism that fixes the boundary pointwise. Boundary rigidity can therefore determine the metric only up to this invariance.

evidence.quote: if  $\psi$  is a diffeomorphism fixing the boundary pointwise
boundary distance functions of  $g$  and  $\psi^{*}g$  are the same

nodes.label: Boundary length-area relation for free boundary minimal surfaces in the ball
aliases: boundary length equals twice area for FBMS in B^3
nodes.summary: For free boundary minimal surfaces in the unit three-ball, the boundary length equals twice the area, so sigma_1=1 converts area estimates into normalized Steklov values.

evidence.quote: boundary length equal to twice its area

nodes.label: Boundary measure convergence under noncollapsed pGH convergence
nodes.summary: Under pointed Gromov-Hausdorff convergence of noncollapsed RCD spaces, the measures H^{N-1} restricted to the boundaries converge weakly to the limiting boundary measure. This is the paper's boundary analogue of volume convergence.

evidence.section: 1.4 Stability and gap theorems for boundaries

evidence.quote: the N-1 Hausdorff measures on the boundaries converge ... to the limit Hausdorff measure on ∂X

nodes.label: Weak-star boundary measure convergence to a weight
aliases: boundary measures converge to beta dmu_g
nodes.summary: For the weighted perforated domains, the boundary Hausdorff measures converge weak-star as measures on M to beta dmu_g, equivalently tested against every continuous function.

evidence.quote: int_{partial Omega^epsilon} f dA_g -> int_M f beta dmu_g

nodes.label: Boundary noncollapsing estimate
nodes.summary: A lower bound comparing H^{N-1}(B_2(p)∩∂X) to H^N(B_1(p)) at boundary points. It follows from the Laplacian analysis of the boundary distance and supports boundary-point stability for Ricci limits with boundary.

evidence.quote: upper bounds on the absolutely continuous part of its Laplacian imply noncollapsing estimates on the boundary measure

evidence.section: 7.1 Laplacian of the distance from the boundary

nodes.label: Boundary normal graph over the unit circle
aliases: normal perturbation of the unit disk boundary
nodes.summary: A boundary representation in which points of the unit circle are displaced in the outward unit normal direction by a scalar function of the angle.

nodes.label: Boundary of a noncollapsed RCD space
nodes.summary: For the boundary notion used in the introduction, ∂X is the topological closure of the top-dimensional singular stratum S^{N-1} \ S^{N-2}. The paper proves rectifiability, topological regularity, and stability properties for this boundary.

evidence.quote: Above we denoted by overline{S^{N-1}\setminus S^{N-2}} the topological closure.

nodes.label: Boundary-point stability for Ricci limits with convex boundary
aliases: stability of boundary points for convex-boundary Ricci limits
nodes.summary: For noncollapsed pGH limits of smooth manifolds with convex boundary and interior lower Ricci bounds, boundary points persist in the limit and convergent sequences of boundary points limit to boundary points. This is stronger than no-boundary stability but is proved under additional geometric hypotheses.

evidence.quote: if B_1(p_n) cap ∂X_n is not empty for every n then ∂X is not empty
evidence.section: 1.4 Stability and gap theorems for boundaries

evidence.quote: if points x_n in ∂X_n converge to x in X, then x in ∂X
evidence.section: 1.4 Stability and gap theorems for boundaries

nodes.label: Boundary rectifiability for noncollapsed RCD spaces
nodes.summary: The property that the boundary ∂X of a noncollapsed RCD(K,N) space is (N-1)-rectifiable, with local finiteness estimates for the boundary measure. It is one of the main structural outputs of the paper.

evidence.section: 1.2 An epsilon-regularity theorem for top dimensional singularities

nodes.label: Boundary rigidity
nodes.summary: The inverse problem or property that the boundary distance function determines the Riemannian metric, modulo the natural boundary-fixing diffeomorphism invariance.

evidence.quote: Boundary rigidity is the question whether the knowledge of the boundary restriction
the map  $g \mapsto d_g|_{\partial M \times \partial M}$  is injective

nodes.label: Boundary topological manifold regularity away from codimension two
nodes.summary: Away from a closed exceptional subset of ambient codimension two, a noncollapsed RCD space is a topological manifold with boundary, and the topological boundary agrees with the metric-measure boundary ∂X.

evidence.quote: the boundary ∂X is homeomorphic to a manifold away from a set of codimension 2
X\setminus C_alpha is a topological manifold with boundary and C^alpha-charts
evidence.section: 1.3 Structure of boundaries and of spaces with boundary

nodes.label: Boundary tubular-neighborhood volume estimate
nodes.summary: A linear-in-radius estimate for the N-dimensional measure of tubular neighborhoods of ∂X inside bounded balls. It supports measure finiteness and structural results for the boundary.

evidence.quote: H^N(B_r(∂X) cap B_1(p)) <= C(N,v) r for any r in (0,1)
evidence.section: 1.3 Structure of boundaries and of spaces with boundary

nodes.label: Bounded Euclidean domain
nodes.summary: A bounded domain Ω in Euclidean space R^d, used as the spatial domain for Dirichlet and Neumann Laplacian eigenvalue problems.

nodes.label: Bounded-index min-max stationary harmonic map
nodes.summary: A stationary harmonic map obtained as the strong W^{1,2} limit of regularized min-max critical points, with Morse index bounded by the number of min-max parameters.

nodes.label: Bounded metric flow pair class
nodes.summary: The subset of metric flow pair classes represented by H-concentrated metric flow pairs satisfying terminal or limiting variance and mass-distribution bounds. These classes contain metric flow pairs arising from compact super Ricci flows with suitable conjugate heat data.

nodes.label: Bounded nonnegative curvature condition
nodes.summary: The requirement that the curvature remains bounded and nonnegative along the ancient flow, as part of Perelman's definition of ancient kappa-solutions.

nodes.label: bounded-length singular endpoint set
nodes.summary: The subset of the singular endpoint set reached by singular horizontal paths whose length is bounded by L with respect to a chosen Riemannian metric g.

nodes.label: Brakke flow
nodes.summary: A weak mean curvature flow represented by a family of Radon measures that are two-rectifiable for almost all times and satisfy Brakke's inequality against nonnegative test functions.

evidence.quote: ancient Brakke flows that arise as potential blowup limits

nodes.label: Gaussian density of a Brakke flow
nodes.summary: The limit of Huisken backward-heat-kernel density ratios at a spacetime point. It detects regularity and is used in unit-regularity and tangent-flow analysis.

nodes.label: Branch point of density $Q$ for a varifold
nodes.summary: A singular point of a stable codimension-one varifold with density $Q$ and planar multiplicity-$Q$ tangent behavior, locally described by branching multi-valued sheets rather than separated embedded sheets. The main new result describes $S_Q$ varifolds near these points.

evidence.quote: a description of  $V \in S_{Q}$  near branch points of density  $Q$
multi-valued (in fact  $\frac{p}{2}$ -valued) Lipschitz graph over the tangent hyperplane
evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Bryant extension principle
nodes.summary: A procedure for extending a pre-existing comparison map over an extension cap by matching approximate Bryant-soliton charts, controlling relative scales, and gluing to the old map. It resolves the main obstruction caused by newly inserted cap regions.

nodes.label: Bryant soliton
nodes.summary: The rotationally symmetric noncompact steady gradient Ricci soliton in dimension three, appearing as a backward limit of Perelman's ancient solution.

evidence.quote: backward limits which are either the Bryant soliton

nodes.label: Bubble limit
nodes.summary: A pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit obtained after rescaling a sequence of metrics at scales tending to infinity near curvature blow-up. In this paper Tian-Yau and Taub-NUT metrics occur as such bubbles.

evidence.quote: we will call any such limit a bubble limit

nodes.label: Bubbling set for rescaled HYM connections
nodes.summary: The subset outside the Zariski tangent cone where the rescaled HYM connections fail to converge smoothly, equivalently where scaled Yang-Mills energy stays above the epsilon-regularity threshold.

evidence.quote: where the convergence is not smooth

nodes.label: Buser-Burger-Dodziuk handle compactification
nodes.summary: A compactification method, referred to through the handle construction, used to pass from finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surfaces to closed hyperbolic surfaces while controlling the first eigenvalue. The excerpt names the method but omits its technical statement.

evidence.quote: using a compactification procedure due to Buser, Burger, and Dodziuk
the 'handle lemma' of Buser, Burger, and Dodziuk

nodes.label: C11 anisotropic foliation integrand Psi
nodes.summary: A uniformly elliptic integrand on R^4 built from an even analytic one-variable function phi by a radial formula in |x| and |y|. It is chosen so that sides of C_11 admit minimizing foliations.

evidence.quote: defined by analytic, even, one-variable functions phi

nodes.label: C^{1,1} convex domain
nodes.summary: A bounded convex Euclidean domain whose boundary is C^1 with Lipschitz first derivatives. The paper proves global regularity under this boundary smoothness assumption for both source and target domains.

evidence.quote: bounded convex domains in $\mathbb{R}^n$ with $C^{1,1}$ boundary
the boundary smoothness can be reduced to $C^{1,1}$

nodes.label: C^{1,1} estimate for Kähler potentials
nodes.summary: A second-derivative estimate controlling the Laplacian of the Kähler potential and related gradient terms. The paper reduces these estimates to W^{2,p} bounds in the generalized scalar-curvature setting.

evidence.quote: Reduction of C1,1 estimates to W2,p estimates

nodes.label: C11 foliation leaf profile
nodes.summary: A hypersurface Sigma={|y|=sigma(|x|)} on one side of C_11, where sigma is even, analytic, locally uniformly convex, above |.|, and asymptotic to tau+a tau^{-mu}. Its dilations and reflection form the basic foliation.

nodes.label: $C^{1,\alpha}$ graphical perturbation
nodes.summary: The property that a surface is representable as a normal graph with $C^{1,\alpha}$ regularity over the reference cone or a leaf of its foliation.

evidence.quote: $C^{1,\alpha}$  perturbation of either the cone itself, or some leaf

nodes.label: C^{1,alpha} partial regularity for Lipschitz graphs with bounded anisotropic mean curvature
aliases: regularity for graphs with bounded anisotropic mean curvature
nodes.summary: If a Lipschitz graph varifold has L^p Psi-mean curvature with p>m and Psi satisfies USAC, then the graphing map is C^{1,alpha} on an open full-measure subset.

evidence.quote: open set Omega0 of full measure

nodes.label: $C^{1,\alpha}$ regularity near minimizing quadratic cones
aliases: regularity of minimal surfaces near quadratic cones
nodes.summary: If a stationary integral varifold in the unit ball is sufficiently close to a minimizing quadratic cone, then in a smaller ball its support is a $C^{1,\alpha}$ graph over either the cone or a leaf of the Hardt-Simon foliation.

nodes.label: C1-eta free-boundary regular domain
nodes.summary: A phase has C^{1,eta} free-boundary regularity near a point when its boundary in a neighborhood can be represented as a C^{1,eta} graph. In this paper this is the regularity asserted near every two-phase point for both phases.

nodes.label: C^{1+} regularity of stable and unstable foliations
nodes.summary: The stable and unstable foliations are differentiable with slightly more than C^1 regularity, sufficient for the microlocal argument despite lack of smoothness.

nodes.label: Calabi ansatz
nodes.summary: A Kähler-geometric ansatz used to build metrics with prescribed radial structure, especially on line bundles or tubular neighborhoods. The paper includes it as a separate construction tool for later curvature and gluing arguments.

nodes.label: Calabi ansatz background metric
nodes.summary: An ansatz used to write a complete background Kähler metric that is approximately Ricci-flat before solving the Monge-Ampère equation.

evidence.quote: use the Calabi ansatz to write down a background complete Kähler metric which is approximately Ricci-flat

nodes.label: Calabi dream manifold
nodes.summary: A Kähler manifold for which every Kähler class admits an extremal Kähler metric. The paper introduces this term while discussing surfaces and examples related to cscK existence results.

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau cone
nodes.summary: A cone with vertex O whose punctured space is diffeomorphic to R_{>0} x L, carries a Calabi-Yau structure, and has Riemannian cone metric dr^2 + r^2 g_L. Such a cone is naturally a normal affine algebraic cone.

evidence.quote: g_C = dr^2 + r^2 g_L is a Riemannian cone. A Calabi-Yau cone is naturally a normal affine algebraic cone.

nodes.label: Calabi-Yau metric on the intermediate weighted cone
nodes.summary: The Ricci-flat Kähler metric constructed on W minus its singular point, with asymptotic cone C at infinity and metric completion homeomorphic to W. It is the key object enabling the volume comparison proof.

evidence.quote: construct a Calabi-Yau metric omega_W on W setminus {0}
the asymptotic cone of omega_W at infinity is given by C
agrees with the metric completion of W setminus {0} and is naturally homeomorphic to W

nodes.label: Canonical limiting partially defined constant-curvature metric
nodes.summary: A construction that evolves an arbitrary metric by singular Ricci flow, takes the asymptotic space-form component, and pushes its metric back along surviving time-vector-field trajectories to obtain a constant-curvature metric on an open subset W_g. The resulting partial metric is canonical, continuous in the PartMet topology, and isometric to the reference space form with finitely many points removed.

evidence.section: 3. The canonical limiting constant curvature metric

nodes.label: Canonical neighborhood assumption for three-dimensional Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A high-curvature local-geometry condition saying that sufficiently small-scale regions are geometrically close, after rescaling, to regions in three-dimensional kappa-solutions. In the paper this gives spherical, neck-like, or cap-like structure near almost singular regions.

nodes.label: Canonical quasisymmetric parametrization of a limit curve
nodes.summary: A canonical parametrization of the Einstein-Universe limit curve of a quasiperiodic surface by a quasisymmetric map, extending the surface uniformization continuously to the boundary.

evidence.quote: the limit curves of these surfaces in the Einstein Universe admits a canonical quasisymmetric parametrisation
every quasisymmetric curve in the Einstein Universe bounds a quasiperiodic surface in such a way that the quasisymmetric parametrisation is a continuous extension of the uniformisation

nodes.label: Cap-size control for low-entropy flow
nodes.summary: A uniform bound saying that outside a fixed ball around the tip, points lie on fine necks, together with parabolic asymptotics for the end and linear motion of the tip height.

evidence.quote: every point ... lies on a fine neck

nodes.label: Carleson epsilon deviation
nodes.summary: For a proper planar open set Ω^+ with boundary Γ and complementary exterior Ω^-, ε(x,r) is r^{-1} times the maximum deviation of the longest Ω^+ and Ω^- arcs on the circle ∂B(x,r) from the half-circle length πr.

evidence.quote: denote by I^{+}(x,r) and I^{-}(x,r) the longest open arcs of the circumference ∂B(x,r) contained in Ω^{+} and Ω^{-}, respectively

nodes.label: Finite Carleson ε² square-function set
nodes.summary: For a boundary Γ equipped with the Carleson ε² square function, the finite square-function set is G = {x∈Γ : E(x)<∞}. The main theorem studies its rectifiability and tangent behavior.

evidence.quote: Then the set G={x∈Γ:E(x)<∞} is rectifiable and at H^1-almost every point of G there exists a tangent to Γ.

nodes.label: Carleson ε² square function
nodes.summary: The Carleson ε² square function at a point x integrates the squared Carleson epsilon deviation over scales: E(x)^2 = ∫_0^1 ε(x,r)^2 dr/r. It measures multiscale flatness of a planar boundary relative to half-circles in the complementary domains.

evidence.quote: The Carleson ε^2-square function is given by E(x)^2 := ∫_0^1 ε(x,r)^2 dr/r.
If Γ is a line then E(x)=0 for all x∈Γ.

nodes.label: Carleson ε² tangent-point characterization
nodes.summary: For Jordan curves, except for a set of zero one-dimensional Hausdorff measure, points with a tangent are exactly the points where the associated Carleson ε² square function is finite. The paper proves this characterization and extends the converse direction to two-sided corkscrew boundaries.

evidence.quote: Except for a set of zero H^1-measure, Γ has a tangent at x∈Γ if and only if E(x)<∞.
the set of tangent points of Γ coincides with the points x∈Γ such that E(x)<∞, up to a set of zero measure H^1.

nodes.label: Carnot group
nodes.summary: A Carnot group is a simply connected nilpotent Lie group with a stratified Lie algebra and compatible dilations. The Heisenberg groups are the main examples considered in this paper.

evidence.quote: in bigger generality to Carnot groups

nodes.label: Cartan connection
nodes.summary: A one-form on the Cartan principal bundle giving pointwise isomorphisms to the model Lie algebra and satisfying P-equivariance and reproduction of vertical generators. In this paper it is denoted omega.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Cartan curvature
nodes.summary: The curvature two-form Omega = d omega + 1/2[omega,omega], viewed as a P-equivariant function on the Cartan bundle. It measures failure of local equivalence with the flat model.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Exponential map of a Cartan geometry
nodes.summary: The map obtained by flowing for time one along omega-constant vector fields on the Cartan bundle. It is used to propagate holonomy sequences along conformal-geodesic directions.

evidence.quote: exponential map of a Cartan geometry

nodes.label: CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A geodesic metric space of global nonpositive curvature in the Alexandrov comparison sense.

nodes.label: CAT(kappa) space
aliases: space with an upper curvature bound
nodes.summary: A complete metric space satisfying the CAT(kappa) comparison condition: short geodesic triangles are no thicker than comparison triangles in the simply connected surface of constant curvature kappa.

evidence.section: 2.3 Spaces with an upper curvature bound

nodes.label: CAT(1) space
nodes.summary: A CAT(kappa) space with kappa equal to 1; in this paper CAT(1) spaces occur as Tits boundaries and spaces of directions of CAT(0) spaces.

evidence.quote: appear as Tits boundaries and links

nodes.label: CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A CAT(kappa) space with kappa equal to 0, used as the paper's ambient nonpositively curved metric space.

evidence.quote: Let X be a CAT(0) space

nodes.label: Reduction from Cauchy data to the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map
nodes.summary: A method showing that the global Cauchy data set determines the finite-time Dirichlet-to-Neumann map after embedding into a cylindrical time slab. The proof uses the double-null exterior unique continuation theorem to enforce uniqueness of the Neumann trace.

evidence.section: 2.2. Reduction from Cauchy data set to the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map

nodes.label: Causal future and past
nodes.summary: The sets of points causally reachable from, or able to causally reach, a point p. They are used to define the exterior of the double null cone.

evidence.quote: J+(p) and J−(p) denote the causal future and past

nodes.label: CD^1 condition
nodes.summary: An L1-optimal-transport or localization variant of the curvature-dimension condition, formulated through one-dimensional transport-ray data and conditional measures. The paper studies it as one of the equivalent CD variants in the essentially non-branching setting.

nodes.label: Cellwise Neumann potential for boundary terms
nodes.summary: On each perforated Voronoi cell Q_p^epsilon, an auxiliary Neumann problem converts boundary integrals on small geodesic spheres into volume integrals plus controlled error terms.

nodes.label: Center of symmetry of a surface
nodes.summary: A point p in the ambient manifold such that all ambient isometries fixing p preserve the surface. It is used to formulate maximal symmetry and centered moduli spaces of CMC spheres.

evidence.quote: a point p in M, which we call the center of symmetry of S_H

nodes.label: Centered CMC sphere moduli space
nodes.summary: The space of nonnegative-CMC spheres in M whose center of symmetry is a fixed base point p. The paper uses this space to parameterize CMC spheres by mean curvature.

evidence.quote: M_M(p) denotes the space of spheres of nonnegative constant mean curvature in M that have a base point p in M as a center of symmetry

nodes.label: Centered Hilbert scheme of points on C^2
aliases: Douady space of n points on C^2
nodes.summary: The centered Hilbert scheme Hilb_0^n(C^2), realized as a crepant resolution of the quotient of centered n-tuples in C^2 by the symmetric group. It is the space on which the Nakajima metric is studied.

nodes.label: QAC compactification of the centered Hilbert scheme
nodes.summary: The QAC compactification of Hilb_0^n(C^2) whose boundary hypersurfaces correspond to nonminimal partitions of n indistinguishable points. Its boundary fibration fibers are compactified products of smaller centered Hilbert schemes.

nodes.label: Non-homogeneous centered maximal operator
nodes.summary: For a measure μ, the maximal operator M~_μ f(x) is the supremum over balls containing x of μ(3B)^{-1}∫_B |f| dμ. The proof uses its L^2(μ) boundedness to estimate square-function terms.

evidence.quote: where M~_μ is the maximal operator defined by M~_μ f(x)=sup_{B∋x} 1/μ(3B) ∫_B |f| dμ.
Using that M~_μ is bounded in L^2(μ)

nodes.label: Cocharacter space of the central torus
nodes.summary: The lattice and real vector space of one-parameter subgroups of the central torus T, used to parameterize twists of test configurations, filtrations, and valuations.

evidence.quote: N_Z = Hom(C*, T), N_R = N_Z tensor_Z R

nodes.label: Central torus of a reductive automorphism group
nodes.summary: The identity component of the center of the reductive group G; it is an algebraic torus and supplies the directions modulo which uniform stability is reduced.

evidence.quote: T := C(G)_0 is the identity component of C(G)

nodes.label: Chamber in a Morse-flat boundary
nodes.summary: The closure of a component of the boundary sphere of a periodic Morse flat after removing the finite arrangement of singular hyperspheres; each chamber is an n-dimensional spherical polytope.

evidence.quote: closure of one of the components
evidence.section: 5.2 Periodic Morse flats and symmetric sets

nodes.label: Change-of-variables rigidity
nodes.summary: A rigidity principle for the density change-of-variables formula that allows the proof to bootstrap initially available regularity. The excerpt identifies the property but does not state its exact formulation.

evidence.quote: a certain rigidity property of the change-of-variables formula

nodes.label: Characteristic curve on an intrinsic graph
nodes.summary: Characteristic curves are curves on an intrinsic graph tangent to the horizontal distribution, giving a one-dimensional foliation that organizes the graph's geometry.

evidence.quote: 12.  $L_{1}$  bounds and characteristic curves on monotone intrinsic graphs 175

nodes.label: characteristic line foliation
nodes.summary: A possibly singular line foliation on the Martinet surface, regular off a lower-dimensional analytic set, to which singular horizontal paths are tangent wherever regular.

nodes.label: characteristic transverse-singular trajectory
nodes.summary: A convergent transverse-singular trajectory with limit in the tangency/zero stratum set that is not monodromic.

nodes.label: characteristic vector field on the Martinet surface
nodes.summary: A local vector field generating the characteristic line foliation on the Martinet surface; in a local model with h defining Sigma, it is Z = X1(h)X2 - X2(h)X1.

nodes.label: QFB compactification of the charge-three monopole moduli space
nodes.summary: The announced QFB compactification of the universal cover of the reduced charge-three SU(2)-monopole moduli space. The paper uses its two-boundary-hypersurface fibered-corners structure to identify middle-degree weighted QFB cohomology with intersection cohomology.

evidence.section: 6. L2-cohomology of the moduli space of monopoles

nodes.label: Cheeger constant
nodes.summary: The isoperimetric constant of a Riemannian manifold, given by an infimum of boundary-area to enclosed-volume ratios. It sets the threshold for existence of CMC spheres when the universal cover is diffeomorphic to R^3.

evidence.quote: Ch(X) denotes the Cheeger constant of X

nodes.label: Cheeger energy
nodes.summary: A lower semicontinuous convex energy on L^2 of a metric-measure space obtained by relaxing squared slopes of Lipschitz approximations. It defines the Sobolev space H^{1,2} and the minimal relaxed slope.

evidence.quote: The Cheeger energy Ch:L^2(X,m)->[0,+infty] associated to a m.m.s.
the Cheeger energy E on M is a quadratic functional on L^2(M,m)

nodes.label: Chern-Simons/Bott-Chern multiplicity comparison
nodes.summary: A comparison method computing algebraic multiplicities of a torsion quotient using Bott-Chern/Chern-Simons formulas and identifying them with analytic energy-defect multiplicities.

nodes.label: Chord arc property for H-disks
aliases: chord arc result for constant mean curvature disks
nodes.summary: A scale-invariant comparison between intrinsic distance in an H-disk and ambient Euclidean distance, triggered by a local lower curvature bound and independent of the mean curvature value.

evidence.quote: we obtain a chord arc result for compact disks embedded in \mathbb{R}^3 with constant mean curvature that does not depend on the value of the mean curvature

nodes.label: Classical cone of density $Q$
aliases: cone with three or more half-hyperplanes
nodes.summary: A stationary cone whose support is the union of at least three half-hyperplanes meeting along a common axis and whose vertex density is $Q$. It is one of the three model geometries in the $S_Q$ structure theory.

evidence.quote: stationary cone with vertex density  $Q$  and support the union of 3 or more half-hyperplanes meeting along a common axis
Structure of varifolds in  $\mathcal{S}_Q$  near classical cones

nodes.label: Classical Gaussian isoperimetric inequality
nodes.summary: Among Borel sets of prescribed Gaussian measure in R^n, halfspaces minimize Gaussian-weighted perimeter, and the minimizer is unique up to null sets.

evidence.quote: among all Borel sets U in R^n having prescribed Gaussian measure gamma(U)=v... halfspaces minimize Gaussian-weighted perimeter
halfspaces are in fact the unique minimizers for the Gaussian isoperimetric inequality

nodes.label: Classical singular stratification
nodes.summary: The filtration S^k of the singular set consisting of points at which no tangent cone is (k+1)-symmetric, equivalently no tangent cone splits an R^{k+1} factor.

evidence.quote: $S^k \coloneqq \{x \in X : \text{no tangent cone at } x \text{ is } (k + 1) \text{-symmetric}\}$

nodes.label: Classical singularity of a codimension-one varifold
nodes.summary: A non-regular codimension-one varifold point modeled by several smooth hypersurface sheets or half-hyperplanes meeting along a common lower-dimensional axis. The class $S_Q$ excludes such singularities below density $Q$.

evidence.quote: admitting no classical singularities of density  $< Q$
support the union of 3 or more half-hyperplanes meeting along a common axis

nodes.label: Classification of ancient asymptotically cylindrical flows
aliases: classification of ancient Brakke flows with cylindrical blowdown
nodes.summary: The classification asserting that an ancient unit-regular integral Brakke flow with cylindrical blowdown must be a round shrinking cylinder, translating bowl soliton, or ancient oval.

evidence.quote: must be either a round shrinking cylinder, a translating bowl soliton, or an ancient oval

nodes.label: Classification of ancient low-entropy flows
nodes.summary: The classification asserting that every ancient low-entropy flow in R3 is exactly one of five models: a flat plane, round shrinking sphere, round shrinking cylinder, translating bowl soliton, or ancient oval.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Classification of compact ancient kappa-solutions on S^3
aliases: compact ancient κ-solution classification in dimension three
nodes.summary: The classification problem asserting that compact ancient kappa-solutions to three-dimensional Ricci flow on S^3 are exactly shrinking round spheres or Perelman's compact ancient solution.

evidence.quote: We prove that such a solution is either isometric to a family of shrinking round spheres, or the Type II ancient solution

nodes.label: Clifford cone C_11
nodes.summary: The four-dimensional cone C_11 = {|x|=|y|} with x,y in R^2. Its two sides are foliated by anisotropic minimizers and provide the symmetry interface for the constructed graph.

nodes.label: Closed aspherical horospherical cross-section
nodes.summary: A codimension-one closed aspherical submanifold serving as a cross-section of an end; in this setting it is a compact quotient manifold of a horosphere.

evidence.quote: codimension-one closed aspherical submanifold, called a cross-section

nodes.label: Closed aspherical positive-scalar-curvature rigidity in dimensions 4 and 5
aliases: positive scalar curvature obstruction for closed aspherical 4- and 5-manifolds
nodes.summary: For dimensions 4 and 5, a smooth closed aspherical manifold admits no positive scalar curvature metric, and every nonnegative scalar curvature metric on it is flat.

evidence.quote: For n in {4,5}, a smooth closed aspherical n-manifold N does not admit
Any metric of non-negative scalar curvature on N is flat
