nodes.label: Closed billiard trajectory
nodes.summary: A billiard trajectory reflecting off the boundary that returns to its initial phase point after finitely many reflections.

nodes.label: Closed equidistant decomposition
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a metric space into closed pairwise equidistant subsets, which induces a natural quotient metric and a submetry projection.

nodes.label: Closed hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A closed Riemannian surface whose metric has constant curvature -1. The paper constructs large-genus examples with first positive Laplace eigenvalue tending to 1/4.

evidence.quote: The corresponding question for closed surfaces  $X$  is as follows.

nodes.label: Closed isotropic orbit with linear unipotent isotropy
aliases: closed lightlike H-orbit with unipotent isotropy
nodes.summary: A closed orbit of the reduced abelian conformal group whose tangent directions are isotropic and whose stabilizer has linear unipotent isotropy. Such orbits are the boundary objects over which the final lightlike foliation is extended.

evidence.quote: closed, isotropic orbits with linear, unipotent isotropy
evidence.section: 6. End of proof using approximately-stable hypersurfaces

nodes.label: Closed multicurve
nodes.summary: A finite positive-integer weighted sum of primitive simple closed curve components on a surface, written in the excerpt as m_1 gamma_1 + ... + m_k gamma_k. The weights record multiplicities of the primitive components.

evidence.quote: random closed multicurve on a surface of large genus  $g$

nodes.label: Closed orientable surface of genus g
nodes.summary: A compact connected orientable surface with genus g, used as the ambient surface for multicurves and square-tiled structures in the large-genus regime.

evidence.quote: on a surface of large genus  $g$

nodes.label: Closed oriented Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A compact Riemannian manifold without boundary equipped with an orientation. It is the base space on which the line bundle, energy integrals, currents, and varifolds are defined.

evidence.quote: over a closed, oriented Riemannian manifold M

nodes.label: Closed positive (1,1)-current
nodes.summary: A weak positive (1,1)-form used as a limit object in the proof. The paper uses such currents locally as sqrt(-1)∂∂bar of a real function.

evidence.quote: any closed positive (1,1) current can be written as √−1∂∂bar acting on a real function locally
evidence.section: 3 Concentration of mass and its application

nodes.label: Closed quasi-positive (1,1)-current
nodes.summary: A closed (1,1)-current that is the sum of a positive current and a smooth form. The twisting current theta in the paper is of this type.

evidence.quote: $θ$  a closed, quasi-positive  $(1,1)$ -current on  $X$ , i.e. the sum of a positive current and a smooth form

nodes.label: Closed Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian manifold that is compact and has no boundary. The paper works with closed connected n-dimensional Riemannian manifolds for its Betti-number vanishing and estimation results.

evidence.quote: Let n ≥ 3 and let (M, g) be a closed connected n-dimensional Riemannian manifold.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

evidence.quote: Let $(M,g)$ be a smooth compact connected Riemannian manifold of dimension $n$
a smooth closed aspherical n-manifold N

nodes.label: Closed Riemannian surface
nodes.summary: A compact two-dimensional manifold without boundary equipped with a Riemannian metric. It is the domain for the Laplacian, normalized eigenvalue functionals, and many harmonic maps in the paper.

evidence.quote: closed immersed surface of genus zero
a smooth, closed Riemann surface  $(\Sigma, g_0)$  (2-dimensional, connected and without boundary)
If  $X$  is closed then the spectrum of  $\Delta_X$  consists of eigenvalues

nodes.label: Closed simply connected real-analytic Lorentzian manifold
aliases: compact simply connected analytic Lorentzian manifold
nodes.summary: A compact boundaryless simply connected manifold equipped with a real-analytic Lorentzian metric. This is the class of manifolds for the paper's compactness statement.

evidence.quote: closed, simply connected, real analytic Lorentzian manifold

nodes.label: Cluster interface
nodes.summary: The hypersurface pieces separating adjacent cells of a cluster; in the cited tripod cluster these are half-hyperplanes, and in stable regular clusters the paper proves flatness.

evidence.quote: whose interfaces consist of three half-hyperplanes
stable regular clusters must have flat interfaces

nodes.label: CMC flux
nodes.summary: A flux invariant associated with CMC surfaces and ambient Killing fields, typically computed by integrating along cycles. The appendix uses a nonvanishing result for this invariant.

evidence.quote: A nonvanishing result for the CMC flux

nodes.label: CMC sphere existence range by Cheeger constant
nodes.summary: The existence values of the constant mean curvature are all real H if the universal cover is not diffeomorphic to R^3, and exactly |H| > Ch(X)/2 if it is diffeomorphic to R^3.

evidence.quote: the values H in R for which there exists a sphere of constant mean curvature H in M are exactly those with |H| > Ch(X) / 2

nodes.label: CMC sphere uniqueness up to ambient isometry
nodes.summary: In a Riemannian homogeneous three-manifold, any two CMC spheres with the same constant mean curvature are equivalent by an ambient isometry. This is the main classification property stated in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: any two spheres in M of the same constant mean curvature differ by an isometry of M

nodes.label: CMC stability operator
nodes.summary: The Jacobi-type linear elliptic operator governing second variation for a CMC surface under volume-preserving variations. The paper records index and nullity for CMC spheres using this operator.

nodes.label: C^n-small boundary perturbation
nodes.summary: A boundary graphing function is C^n-small when its C^n norm is bounded by A_n times epsilon for a constant depending only on n.

nodes.label: Co-area formula
nodes.summary: An integration formula that rewrites integrals over M in terms of integrals over level sets of a function. The proof applies it to functions of the Green's function u.

nodes.label: Coarse blow-up of a varifold
nodes.summary: A rescaled limiting object obtained from $S_Q$ varifolds as their coarse excess tends to zero, used to analyze first-order deviations from the flat multiplicity model. The proof develops continuity, regularity, frequency, and classification properties for these blow-ups.

evidence.quote: Coarse blow-ups of varifolds in  $S_{Q}$  and their initial properties
Classification of homogeneous degree 1 coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Coarse excess for a varifold
nodes.summary: A scale-normalized quantity measuring how far a varifold is from a coarse planar or conical comparison model. It is a smallness hypothesis in the blow-up analysis of $S_Q$ varifolds.

evidence.quote: Varifolds in  $S_{Q}$  with small coarse excess and significantly smaller fine excess
A continuity estimate for coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Cocompact action
nodes.summary: A group action property saying the quotient is compact; it is part of the paper's definition of a geometric action.

nodes.label: Cocompact principal isometric Lie group action
nodes.summary: A connected Lie group action on a connected complete Riemannian manifold that is proper, isometric, cocompact, and has a single orbit type. This is the paper's main symmetry setup.

evidence.quote: properly, isometrically, cocompactly and with a single orbit type

nodes.label: Codimension-four orbifold-cone tangent model
nodes.summary: The tangent-cone model consisting of an Euclidean factor of dimension $n-4$ times the metric cone over the spherical space form $S^3/\Gamma$, where $\Gamma\subseteq O(4)$ acts freely away from the origin.

evidence.quote: unique and isometric to  $\mathbb{R}^{n - 4} \times C(S^3 / \Gamma_x)$  for some  $\Gamma_x \subseteq O(4)$

nodes.label: Codimension-four rectifiability under two-sided Ricci bounds
aliases: (n-4)-rectifiability of S under bounded Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: In the two-sided Ricci curvature setting, the singular set is (n-4)-rectifiable with uniformly bounded measure, and tangent cones are unique almost everywhere in that dimension.

evidence.quote: the singular set  $\mathcal{S}(X)$  is  $n - 4$  rectifiable with the uniform Hausdorff measure estimates

nodes.label: Codimension-two support cycle of a torsion sheaf
nodes.summary: For a torsion sheaf supported in codimension at least two, this cycle sums codimension-two support components with multiplicity h^0 of the restriction to a transverse complex surface slice.

nodes.label: Coercivity modulo Mobius directions for the linearized form
nodes.summary: The property that $Q_n(w)$ controls the $L^2$ norm of $\nabla_Tw$ after subtracting the projection of $w$ onto the infinitesimal Mobius kernel. This is the linear estimate underpinning the nonlinear conformal rigidity result.

nodes.label: cohomological p-sweepout
nodes.summary: A flat-continuous map from a finite-dimensional simplicial complex to the relative cycle space whose pullback of the p-th power of the fundamental cohomology generator is nonzero.

nodes.label: Collapse map to an interval with nilmanifold fibers
nodes.summary: A continuous surjective map from the K3 surface to the limiting interval whose regular fibers are diffeomorphic to three-tori or Heisenberg nilmanifolds.

evidence.quote: continuous surjective map from the K3 surface to the interval, with regular fibers diffeomorphic to either 3-tori or Heisenberg nilmanifolds

nodes.label: Collapsed hyperkähler 4-manifold limit
nodes.summary: A measured Gromov-Hausdorff limit of hyperkähler four-manifolds whose essential dimension is less than four. Its regular region carries extra affine, special Kähler, or one-dimensional structures according to the limit dimension.

evidence.section: 3. Geometric structures over the regular region

nodes.label: Collapsed hyperkähler K3 limit space
aliases: collapsed K3 limit in the full GH compactification
nodes.summary: A volume-collapsed measured Gromov-Hausdorff limit of unit-diameter hyperkähler metrics on the K3 manifold. The paper classifies these spaces as the flat orbifold T^3/Z2, a singular special Kähler metric on S^2 with local integral monodromy, or the unit interval.

evidence.quote: must be isometric to one of the following

nodes.label: Collapsing Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces
nodes.summary: Families of Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces whose metric spaces degenerate to an interval, with Tian-Yau and Taub-NUT bubble limits and nilmanifold-type regular fibers.

evidence.quote: families of Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces which collapse to an interval

nodes.label: Combined conformal-isoperimetric deficit
nodes.summary: The scale-invariant quantity $E_{n-1}(u)=D_{n-1}(u)^{n/(n-1)}/|V_n(u)|-1$, defined for maps with nonzero signed volume. It is nonnegative by Wente's inequality and vanishes exactly on generalized conformal maps onto round spheres, up to translation and scaling.

nodes.label: Common-leaf property for square-tiled singularities
aliases: all singularities lie on the same horizontal and vertical leaves
nodes.summary: A property of a square-tiled surface saying that all conical singularities belong to one horizontal leaf and also to one vertical leaf.

evidence.quote: all conical singularities of a random square-tiled surface belong to the same leaf of the horizontal foliation and to the same leaf of the vertical foliation

nodes.label: Compact ancient kappa-solution on S^3
nodes.summary: An ancient kappa-solution to three-dimensional Ricci flow whose underlying manifold is the compact three-sphere.

evidence.quote: compact ancient  \kappa -solution to the Ricci flow on  S^3

nodes.label: Compact convex Euclidean polytope
nodes.summary: A compact convex subset of Euclidean space with nonempty interior represented as the finite intersection of closed linear halfspaces.

evidence.quote: $\Omega$  be a compact, convex polytope in  $\mathbb{R}^n$  with nonempty interior. We may write  $\Omega = \bigcap_{i \in I} \{u_i \leq 0\}$

nodes.label: Compact hyperbolic 3-manifold
nodes.summary: A compact connected oriented 3-dimensional Riemannian manifold with a hyperbolic metric, used as the base metric before perturbation.

nodes.label: Compact Kähler manifold
nodes.summary: A compact complex manifold equipped with a Kähler form. The paper fixes such a manifold as the geometric background for the equations.

evidence.quote: Let  $X$  be a compact Kähler manifold without boundary of dimension  $n$

nodes.label: Compact Kähler manifold without boundary
nodes.summary: A compact complex manifold equipped with a Kähler structure and no boundary. This is the ambient setting for the paper's global a priori estimates.

nodes.label: Compact Riemannian manifold with boundary
nodes.summary: A compact Riemannian manifold whose boundary supports boundary spectral problems such as weighted Steklov and Robin eigenvalue problems.

nodes.label: Compact self-shrinker
nodes.summary: A self-shrinker with compact underlying hypersurface. The paper treats compact shrinkers together with asymptotically conical shrinkers as one-sided ancient-flow models, and perturbs away compact non-spherical ones.

nodes.label: compact smooth approximation of a cylindrical end
nodes.summary: A family of compact manifolds with boundary and smoothed stretched metrics that converges geometrically to the cylindrical-end manifold while retaining enough control near the folding region.

nodes.label: Compact smooth orbit space
nodes.summary: The quotient B=M/G of a proper single-orbit-type action, endowed with smooth manifold structure and compact topology.

nodes.label: Image of compactly supported cohomology in absolute cohomology
nodes.summary: The natural image of compactly supported cohomology inside ordinary cohomology. Both the Vafa-Witten and Sen predictions compare L2 harmonic forms with this image.

nodes.label: Compactness of bounded metric flow pair classes
nodes.summary: For finite time intervals and finite marked time sets, the bounded classes F_I^J(H,V,b,r) are compact in the F-distance. This provides the abstract compactness theorem behind subsequential limits of pointed super Ricci flows.

nodes.label: Compactness and quantization for subcritical Moser-Trudinger critical points
nodes.summary: A compactness theory controlling limiting behavior of critical points of $I_{p,\beta}$ and quantizing possible concentration or energy-loss phenomena. The excerpt states this as a key new analytic input but omits the detailed statements.

evidence.quote: prove new compactness and quantization results for such critical points

nodes.label: Comparison domain
nodes.summary: A union of closed product domains over successive time intervals inside a Ricci flow spacetime, with spherical neck boundaries and possible three-disk additions at transition times. It is chosen to contain the non-too-singular region where two flows can be compared.

nodes.label: Comparison map between Ricci flow spacetimes
nodes.summary: A time-preserving diffeomorphism, defined on a comparison domain up to cuts, whose inverse evolves by harmonic map heat flow on each time slab. It provides the evolving almost-isometry used in the stability proof.

nodes.label: Comparison scale r_comp
nodes.summary: A small length scale used to choose the comparison domain, set curvature thresholds, and determine time-step lengths of order r_comp squared. It separates the controlled region from the almost singular region.

nodes.label: Compatible Calabi-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A complete ddc-exact Calabi-Yau metric with Euclidean volume growth on a smooth affine variety whose polynomial-growth holomorphic functions coincide with the coordinate ring.

evidence.quote: is said to be a compatible metric if the space of holomorphic functions ... with polynomial growth ... coincides with R

nodes.label: Complete Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: A complete noncompact Calabi-Yau manifold. It is the class appearing in Yau's compactification conjecture discussed in the introduction.

evidence.quote: every complete Calabi-Yau manifold can be compactified

nodes.label: Complete connected Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A connected Riemannian manifold whose Riemannian distance is complete. The paper's rigidity results place the target of an isometric embedding in this class.

evidence.quote: Let M be a complete, connected, n-dimensional Riemannian manifold.

nodes.label: Complete connected Riemannian surface
nodes.summary: A two-dimensional complete connected Riemannian manifold. This is the target class in the main planar rigidity theorem.

evidence.quote: Let M be a complete, connected Riemannian surface

nodes.label: Complete hyperkahler 4-manifold
nodes.summary: A complete four-dimensional Riemannian manifold equipped with a hyperkahler structure. In the paper this is the ambient geometric class before imposing the curvature decay condition.

nodes.label: Complete locally homogeneous three-manifold
nodes.summary: A complete Riemannian three-manifold whose local geometry is homogeneous. The paper includes a section extending CMC sphere considerations to this setting.

evidence.quote: Constant mean curvature spheres in complete locally homogeneous three-manifolds

nodes.label: Complete metric space
nodes.summary: A metric space in which every Cauchy sequence converges. The paper states its rectifiability characterization for subsets of a complete ambient metric space.

evidence.quote: rectifiable subsets of a complete metric space  $X$

nodes.label: Complete Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A completeness condition for Ricci flow spacetimes requiring finite-length spatial paths or time-vector trajectories above a curvature-scale cutoff to have limits. The zero-cutoff case is the completeness notion used for singular Ricci flows.

nodes.label: Complete Riemannian metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric for which the metric space, equivalently every geodesic, is complete.

evidence.quote: complete metric of positive scalar curvature

nodes.label: Completely solvable Lie group
nodes.summary: A solvable Lie group whose adjoint maps have only real eigenvalues on the Lie algebra.

evidence.quote: eigenvalues of ad_g X are real
evidence.section: The mean curvature vector of the N-orbits is G-vertical

nodes.label: Complex analytic compactification
aliases: compactification in the complex analytic sense
nodes.summary: A compactification of a noncompact complex manifold as a complex analytic space or compact complex object. The paper studies this for fast-decay ALG and ALH non-splitting gravitational instantons.

evidence.quote: compactified in the complex analytic sense
Compactification of ALG and ALH non-splitting instantons

nodes.label: Complex-analytic compactification of a gravitational instanton
aliases: Yau compactification conjecture for gravitational instantons
nodes.summary: The property that a gravitational instanton admits a complex structure for which it is biholomorphic to the complement of an anti-canonical divisor in an algebraic surface. In the paper this follows from the model-end classification together with known compactification results.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic geodesic submanifold
nodes.summary: A properly immersed totally geodesic submanifold of a complex hyperbolic manifold whose induced geometry is itself complex hyperbolic.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic lattice
nodes.summary: A discrete subgroup of PU(n,1), or a finite cover such as SU(n,1), with finite covolume; such a lattice is the fundamental group of a finite-volume complex hyperbolic orbifold or manifold in the torsion-free case.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic metric on a ball quotient
nodes.summary: The complete complex hyperbolic metric on a ball quotient, obtained by descending the Bergman metric on the unit ball and normalized in the excerpt by Ric omega_hyp = -omega_hyp.

nodes.label: Complex hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: The rank-one Hermitian symmetric space whose finite-volume quotients are complex hyperbolic manifolds. In complex dimension n its holomorphic isometry group is modeled by PU(n,1), up to finite covers.

nodes.label: Complex isotropic subspace
nodes.summary: A complex linear subspace V of C^{2m+1} on which the complex-bilinear extension of the Euclidean inner product vanishes identically. Such subspaces are the points of the twistor space used for S^{2m}.

nodes.label: Complex Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: A nonlinear equation for a potential of a Kähler current. When theta is smooth, the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation becomes a complex Monge-Ampère equation, and pluripotential theory extends this formulation to singular theta.

evidence.quote: this equation amounts to a complex Monge-Ampère equation for a potential of  $\omega$

nodes.label: Complex Monge-Ampère equation for Calabi-Yau metrics
nodes.summary: The volume-form equation equating the top exterior power of the Kähler form with the holomorphic volume form times its conjugate. Solving this equation on noncompact spaces is the analytic step producing Ricci-flat Kähler metrics.

evidence.quote: the following complex Monge-Ampère equation is satisfied
then solve the complex Monge-Ampère equation on the noncompact manifold

nodes.label: Complex orthogonal group action on harmonic two-spheres
nodes.summary: The group SO(2m+1,C) acts holomorphically on the twistor space Z_m, preserves the horizontal distribution, and therefore induces an action on linearly full harmonic maps S^2 -> S^{2m} through their twistor lifts. Via first-order twistor correspondence, this action preserves energy nullity and supports the energy-index induction.

nodes.label: Complex projective manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold that is also a projective algebraic variety. The paper takes such an X as the ambient compact space containing the divisor complement.

evidence.quote: Let X be a complex projective manifold

nodes.label: Complex spinor dimension in flat Euclidean space
nodes.summary: For flat $\mathbb{R}^n$, the complex spinor representation used in the paper has dimension $m=2^{\lfloor n/2\rfloor}$, determining the size of the harmonic spinor tuple.

evidence.quote: Let  $m = 2^{\left[\frac{n}{2}\right]}$  denote the dimension of the space of spinors on flat  $\mathbb{R}^n$ .

nodes.label: Complex-surface trace inverse identity
nodes.summary: In complex dimension two, after diagonalization, the sum of reciprocal eigenvalues equals the inverse determinant factor times the trace, written in the paper as sum_i 1/φ_{i\bar{i}} = e^{-G} Δφ. The local estimate in the excerpt explicitly relies on this dimension-two identity.

evidence.quote: sum_i 1/φ_{i\bar{i}} = e^{-G} Δφ, which is true only in dimension 2

nodes.label: Component and horizontal-cylinder count statistics
nodes.summary: The paired random statistics consisting of the number of components of a random multicurve and the number of maximal horizontal cylinders of a random square-tiled surface. The paper approximates both by a non-uniform permutation cycle count and obtains an expected value asymptotic equivalent to (log(6g-6)+gamma)/2 + log 2.

evidence.quote: the number of components of a random multicurve and the number of maximal horizontal cylinders of a random square-tiled surface of genus  $g$
both very well-approximated by the number of cycles of a random permutation

nodes.label: Conditional measure
nodes.summary: A measure obtained by disintegrating a reference measure along a partition, such as an L1 or L2 optimal-transport partition. Conditional measures are compared in the proof of the change-of-variables formula.

nodes.label: Conditional-measure comparison between L1 and L2 partitions
nodes.summary: A comparison between conditional measures induced by L1 and L2 optimal-transport partitions. It is part of the bridge from L1 localization to L2/Wasserstein curvature-dimension inequalities.

nodes.label: Cone angle parameter
nodes.summary: A parameter beta in (0,1) determining the cone angle 2 pi beta transverse to the divisor. The small-angle regime is the limit beta to zero.

nodes.label: Level set flow of a cone
nodes.summary: The level set flow generated by a cone; uniqueness and dilation invariance force it to evolve self-similarly as $F_t(\mathcal{C})=\sqrt{t}F_1(\mathcal{C})$. It is the model flow for isolated conical singularities.

evidence.quote: dilation invariance and uniqueness of the level set flow yields $F_{t}(\mathcal{C}) = \sqrt{t} F_{1}(\mathcal{C})$
the level set flow of a smooth hypersurface ... with an isolated conical singularity is modeled on the level set flow of the cone

nodes.label: Link $\Sigma$ of a cone
nodes.summary: The spherical cross-section of the cone, used as the domain for boundary data and spectral projections in the Jacobi analysis.

nodes.label: Cone splitting
nodes.summary: A method that promotes approximate cone symmetries when cone points occur in independent directions; the table of contents identifies it as a component of the codimension-four neck decomposition.

nodes.label: Cone splitting via content
nodes.summary: A quantitative cone-splitting method using volume-pinching sets and content estimates to force additional Euclidean splitting. The paper uses it in the construction and control of neck regions.

evidence.quote: Theorem 2.19 is a quantitative version of the following statement: if a metric cone with vertex z is a metric cone also with respect to z' not equal z, then it contains a line.
a quantitative version of the cone splitting theorem via content

nodes.label: Cone-type polyhedron
nodes.summary: A Euclidean polyhedron with a distinguished vertex, side faces, and a base face, used as the comparison model for the Riemannian polyhedron.

evidence.quote: Let P \subset \mathbf{R}^3 be a cone type polyhedron with a vertex p' , side faces F_{j}' and base face B'

nodes.label: confined min-max realization in the core
nodes.summary: For the cylindrical-end extension of a core, every p-width is realized as an integer-multiplicity sum of smooth closed connected embedded minimal hypersurfaces lying in the interior of the compact core.

nodes.label: Conformal class of a Lorentzian metric
nodes.summary: The equivalence class of Lorentzian metrics obtained from a metric by multiplication by a positive smooth function. It is the rigid geometric structure whose automorphisms form the conformal group.

nodes.label: Conformal gauge reduction to potential recovery
nodes.summary: In a fixed conformal class and dimension n>1, recovering a conformal factor for the metric wave operator reduces to recovering a scalar potential after the change w=c^{(n-1)/4}u. This explains why the potential problem models conformal metric recovery.

evidence.quote: reduces to the former one by using a gauge transformation

nodes.label: Conformal-group compactness for closed simply connected analytic Lorentzian manifolds
aliases: compactness of the conformal group in the analytic Lorentzian simply connected case
nodes.summary: The property that a closed simply connected real-analytic Lorentzian manifold has compact conformal group. It is the central mathematical statement established by the paper.

nodes.label: Conformal group of a Lorentzian manifold
nodes.summary: The group of conformal transformations of a Lorentzian manifold. The paper proves compactness of this group under closed, simply connected, real-analytic Lorentzian hypotheses.

nodes.label: Conformal hyperbolicity of a surface
nodes.summary: A conformal type condition for a surface, typically meaning that its conformal structure is hyperbolic rather than parabolic. The paper uses it as a characterization of quasiperiodic maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: characterised by a curvature condition, Gromov hyperbolicity or conformal hyperbolicity

nodes.label: Cartan geometry of a conformal Lorentzian structure
nodes.summary: The canonical principal P-bundle with regular normal Cartan connection associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure of dimension at least three. It is infinitesimally modeled on the Lorentzian Einstein universe.

evidence.quote: realize our conformal Lorentzian structure as a Cartan geometry
evidence.section: 3. Holonomy sequences and proof in the contracting case

nodes.label: Conformal metric normalizations for the minimal surface flow
nodes.summary: The conformally related metrics tilde g=(g^{00})^{-1}g and hat g=g^{-1/2}g introduced to normalize the time coefficient and put the linearized equation in divergence form.

evidence.quote: we will also introduce two additional, conformally equivalent metrics

nodes.label: Conformal metric perturbation of a hyperbolic metric
nodes.summary: A one-parameter family of metrics obtained by multiplying a hyperbolic metric g_H by a smooth conformal factor e^{-2 tau b}.

nodes.label: Conformal transformation
nodes.summary: A diffeomorphism preserving the conformal class of the metric. In the Cartan formulation such transformations lift uniquely to automorphisms of the canonical Cartan bundle preserving the Cartan connection.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Conformal vector field
nodes.summary: A vector field whose local flow consists of conformal transformations. The paper builds the maximal abelian subgroup from a maximal commuting collection of such vector fields.

evidence.section: 2.1. Gromov's stratification and reduction to a connected, abelian group

nodes.label: Conformally flat Lorentzian manifold
nodes.summary: A Lorentzian manifold locally conformally equivalent to the flat model, equivalently to Minkowski space in local charts. In dimensions at least four, vanishing of the Weyl tensor on an open set characterizes this property.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Conical singularity of a flat surface
nodes.summary: A singular point of the flat metric of a square-tiled or quadratic-differential surface, typically occurring at identified square vertices with non-Euclidean cone angle.

evidence.quote: all conical singularities of a random square-tiled surface

nodes.label: Conical special affine singularity
nodes.summary: A three-dimensional collapsed-limit singularity whose flat background is a punctured neighborhood in R^3 or R^3/Z2 and whose metric is V times that flat background, with V having a harmonic singular expansion. Under a uniform L2 curvature bound it becomes an orbifold singularity.

nodes.label: Conjugate heat flow on a metric flow
nodes.summary: A family of probability measures mu_t on the time-slices of a metric flow satisfying mu_s = integral nu_x;s d mu_t(x) for s <= t. In metric flow pairs it serves as the parabolic analogue of a basepoint.

evidence.section: 3.2 (Conjugate) Heat flows on a metric flow

nodes.label: Conjugate heat kernel family
nodes.summary: For a point x at time t(x), a family of probability measures nu_x;s on earlier time-slices s <= t(x), normalized by nu_x;t(x)=delta_x and satisfying a reproduction formula. These kernels are part of the structure of a metric flow.

nodes.label: Connected reductive automorphism subgroup
nodes.summary: A connected reductive subgroup G of the identity component of the automorphism group of the log Fano pair; in the main equivalence it is chosen to contain a maximal torus of Aut(X,D)_0.

evidence.quote: G is a connected reductive subgroup of Aut(X,D)_0

nodes.label: Connected sum
nodes.summary: A construction that forms a new n-manifold by deleting balls from two n-manifolds and gluing along the resulting sphere boundaries.

evidence.section: 1.2. The Geroch conjecture with arbitrary ends

nodes.label: Orbit fibration of constant curvature metrics
nodes.summary: When the constant curvature metric is unique up to isometry, the diffeomorphism group acts transitively on the constant-curvature metric space with stabilizer the isometry group, yielding a fibration with that fiber.

nodes.label: Contractibility of the constant-curvature metric space
nodes.summary: For the spherical space forms other than S^3 and RP^3 and for compact hyperbolic 3-manifolds, the space of metrics of fixed constant sectional curvature is contractible. This is the paper's main mathematical mechanism for proving the Generalized Smale Conjecture in these cases.

nodes.label: Smale equivalence via constant-curvature metric space
nodes.summary: The constant-curvature metric space is contractible if and only if the inclusion of the isometry group into the diffeomorphism group is a homotopy equivalence. The paper uses this equivalence to convert contractibility into the Generalized Smale Conjecture.

nodes.label: Uniqueness of constant curvature metrics up to isometry
nodes.summary: On the spherical space forms and hyperbolic 3-manifolds considered, a constant curvature metric is unique up to isometry, using spherical space-form classification or Mostow rigidity. This makes the diffeomorphism action on the constant-curvature metric space transitive.

nodes.label: Constant-density normalized Steklov limit
aliases: normalized Steklov scaling under constant beta
nodes.summary: For constant beta, the homogenised normalized Steklov eigenvalues converge to a beta- and volume-scaled normalized Laplace eigenvalue. This scaling is decisive in dimensions at least three.

evidence.section: 1.2.2 Isoperimetric inequalities in dimension at least three

nodes.label: Constant mean curvature sphere
nodes.summary: A genus-zero closed immersed surface in an ambient three-manifold whose mean curvature is constant. This is the central object classified in the paper.

evidence.quote: classifying constant mean curvature spheres in an arbitrary homogeneous three-manifold

nodes.label: Constant mean curvature surface
nodes.summary: An immersed surface whose mean curvature is constant. The paper studies such surfaces in homogeneous three-manifolds and metric Lie groups.

evidence.quote: constant mean curvature surfaces in homogeneous three-manifolds
embedded in \mathbb{R}^3 with constant mean curvature H_n
it has non-negative constant mean curvature H

nodes.label: Constant scalar curvature Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric in a fixed Kähler class whose scalar curvature is constant, equivalently a solution of the cscK equation. Existence of these metrics is the central object of the paper.

evidence.quote: constant scalar curvature Kähler metrics on a compact Kähler manifold
constant scalar curvature Kähler metrics (cscK metrics)

nodes.label: Constant sectional curvature metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric whose sectional curvature is the same constant k at every point and tangent 2-plane; the paper uses k equal to +1 or -1.

nodes.label: Space of constant sectional curvature metrics
aliases: moduli space of constant sectional curvature metrics
nodes.summary: The subspace of the Riemannian metric space consisting of metrics with fixed constant sectional curvature k. Contractibility of this space is the paper's central route to diffeomorphism-group information.

nodes.label: Constrained critical point of the weighted Moser-Trudinger functional
aliases: positive critical point of $F$ on $\mathcal E_\beta$
nodes.summary: A positive function in the $H^1$ energy constraint sphere at which the weighted Moser-Trudinger functional is critical. Equivalently, it solves the constrained Euler-Lagrange equation with the associated Lagrange multiplier.

evidence.quote: A positive function  $u$  is a critical point of  $F$  constrained to  $\mathcal{E}_{\beta}$  if and only if it satisfies the Euler-Lagrange equation

nodes.label: Contact flow
nodes.summary: A flow generated by X for which there is a 1-form alpha satisfying i_X alpha = 1, i_X d alpha = 0, and a nonvanishing contact volume condition.

evidence.quote: The geodesic flow is a contact flow

nodes.label: Contact set
nodes.summary: The zero set of the obstacle-problem solution. Its density at a free-boundary point distinguishes singular points in the Stefan problem.

evidence.quote: singular points at which the contact set \{u = 0\} has zero density

nodes.label: Continuity method for elliptic Kähler PDEs
nodes.summary: A method that connects a solvable equation to the desired nonlinear Kähler PDE through parameter paths, proving openness by elliptic theory and closedness by a priori estimates. The paper uses it for the generalized J-equation and the dHYM perturbation.

evidence.quote: we use the continuity method twice to prove
We will use the continuity method three times

nodes.label: Continuous convergence of singular Ricci flows
aliases: stability of singular Ricci flow under initial metric convergence
nodes.summary: Smooth convergence of initial metrics on a compact 3-manifold yields spacetime diffeomorphisms on large bounded-curvature regions and smooth convergence of pulled-back time vector fields and metrics. The paper uses this to prove continuous dependence of the canonical limiting metric.

nodes.label: Contractible space
nodes.summary: A topological space homotopy equivalent to a point.

nodes.label: contracting, expanding, and mixed neighborhoods
nodes.summary: Local foliated neighborhoods of a minimal hypersurface whose nearby leaves have mean curvature vector pointing toward the hypersurface, away from it, or toward on one side and away on the other.

nodes.label: Contracting holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A holonomy sequence in A'P+-form whose dilation weights are uniformly contracting, represented in the paper by alpha(D_k)+beta(D_k) tending to infinity with bounded tau_k. Such dynamics force local conformal flatness.

evidence.section: 3.4. Proof of main theorem given a contracting holonomy sequence

nodes.label: Control parameters A and B for timelike minimal surface energy
nodes.summary: The L^-infinity-based Besov control quantities A and B built from dyadic pieces of partial u. A is scale-critical at the level of ||partial u||_{L^infty}; B is a half-derivative stronger quantity controlling balanced energy growth.

evidence.quote: We will use two such primary quantities, ${\mathcal{A}}$ and ${\mathcal{B}}$, which are defined as $L^{\infty}$ based Besov norms

nodes.label: control system associated with a distribution
nodes.summary: A representation of horizontal paths as trajectories of an ODE driven by L2 controls and vector fields spanning the distribution.

nodes.label: convergent transverse-singular trajectory
nodes.summary: An absolutely continuous path in the Martinet surface tangent to the characteristic foliation, remaining in the two-dimensional and transverse one-dimensional strata; it is convergent when it has a limit as t approaches 1.

nodes.label: Convex caustic
nodes.summary: A convex invariant curve or envelope for billiard trajectories, used in the definition of rational integrability by specifying caustics of prescribed rotation number.

nodes.label: Convex domain in Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A domain Ω in R^n such that each line segment joining two points of Ω lies in Ω. The source and target domains in the paper are bounded convex domains.

evidence.quote: where $\Omega$ and $\Omega^*$ are bounded convex domains in the Euclidean space $\mathbb{R}^n$

nodes.label: Convex polyhedral cluster cell
nodes.summary: A cluster cell that is a convex polyhedron; in this paper it arises from flat interfaces and has at most q-1 facets in the relevant stable regular cluster setting.

evidence.quote: convex polyhedral cells (with at most q - 1 facets)

nodes.label: Convex solution to the Monge-Ampère second boundary problem
nodes.summary: A convex function u on Ω satisfying the real Monge-Ampère equation and the gradient-image boundary condition Du(Ω)=Ω*. Convexity is the solution class in which ellipticity and optimal transport interpretation are formulated.

evidence.quote: Let $u$ be a convex solution to (1.1) and (1.2).

nodes.label: Corkscrew condition
nodes.summary: The corkscrew condition is a scale-invariant quantitative accessibility condition requiring balls of comparable radius inside a domain near each boundary ball. The two-sided version requires such balls on both sides of the boundary.

evidence.quote: two-sided corkscrew open sets, which have a scale invariant topological assumption

nodes.label: Correspondence between metric flows
nodes.summary: A family of common ambient metric spaces Z_t with isometric embeddings of the time-slices of several metric flows, possibly undefined on exceptional times. It is used to compare metric flows and define F-distance and F-convergence.

nodes.label: Cosphere bundle
nodes.summary: The bundle S^*M of unit covectors inside T^*M, defined using the Riemannian metric.

nodes.label: Coulomb gauge for U(1) connections
nodes.summary: A gauge choice for a U(1) connection form imposing d^*A=0, with boundary normalization locally or globally. It is used for compactness, Palais-Smale arguments, and regularity of weak critical points.

evidence.quote: Selection of a Coulomb gauge gives a continuous retraction R: Xhat -> X
evidence.section: 7.1 Min-max families for the trivial line bundle

nodes.label: Covariant d'Alembertian for the induced metric
nodes.summary: The Lorentzian wave operator associated with the induced metric g. For the timelike minimal surface equation it simplifies to Box_g=g^{alpha beta} partial_alpha partial_beta.

nodes.label: Crepant resolution of an affine Calabi-Yau variety
nodes.summary: A resolution of singularities preserving the canonical class; in the paper the natural map from X to Spec R(X) is a crepant resolution.

evidence.quote: There is a natural map pi:(X,p)->(X',p') which is a crepant resolution of singularities.

nodes.label: Critical boundary density and free boundary harmonic correspondence
nodes.summary: Critical boundary densities for sigma_m(rho) times total boundary density correspond to smooth free boundary harmonic maps into a unit ball, with sigma_m(rho) rho equal to the boundary normal derivative magnitude.

evidence.quote: correspond to free boundary harmonic maps

nodes.label: Critical density and harmonic energy-density correspondence
nodes.summary: Smooth critical densities for lambda_m(beta) times total density, and smooth critical potentials for the corresponding Schrodinger optimization, are exactly energy densities |du|^2 of smooth sphere-valued harmonic maps, with components in the relevant eigenspace.

evidence.quote: energy densities of classical sphere-valued harmonic maps

nodes.label: Critical dimension for one-phase free-boundary singularities
nodes.summary: A dimension d* in [5,7] governing possible singularities of the one-phase-type free boundary: no singularities below d*, locally finite singularities at d*, and rectifiable singular strata above d*.

evidence.quote: there is a critical dimension  $^1 d^* \in [5,7]$
if  $d > d^{*}$ , then  $\mathrm{Sing}(\partial \Omega_u^{\pm})$  is a closed  $(d - d^{*})$ -rectifiable subset

nodes.label: Cross-ratio for the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A cross-ratio adapted to maps from the projective real line into the Einstein Universe and to positivity there. It is the defining tool for the paper's quasisymmetric maps.

evidence.quote: As for the case of maps into the complex projective line, quasisymmetric maps are defined using a cross-ratio.

nodes.label: cscK continuity path
nodes.summary: A one-parameter path of twisted scalar-curvature equations connecting an easier trace equation to the cscK or twisted cscK equation. The paper uses openness, closedness, and a priori estimates along this path.

nodes.label: cscK metric equation
aliases: constant scalar curvature Kähler metric equation
nodes.summary: The scalar-curvature PDE requiring the scalar curvature of omega_phi to equal its topological average. In the paper it is treated via the K-energy and via coupled Monge-Ampere type equations.

evidence.quote: links the cscK equation to certain second order elliptic equation

nodes.label: Cubic curvature decay for ALF instantons
nodes.summary: The improved curvature decay rate O(r^{-3}) for ALF instantons obtained after the end classification removes the need for a volume-growth assumption.

evidence.quote: improve the curvature decay rate of an ALF instanton to  $O(r^{-3})$

nodes.label: Cubic volume growth for minimal hypersurfaces
aliases: Euclidean volume growth in dimension three
nodes.summary: The property that Vol(B_r(0) cap M) grows at most on the order of r^3 as r tends to infinity.

nodes.label: Current of integration along a divisor
nodes.summary: The closed positive current associated to integration over the divisor D. It appears as the singular contribution in the Ricci equation for conical Kähler-Einstein metrics.

nodes.label: Wedge positivity for J-equation currents
nodes.summary: A definition of cω^p − pχ∧ω^(p−1) ≥ 0 for a closed positive current ω, formulated by testing local smoothings against constant-coefficient Kähler forms below χ. It extends the J-equation cone inequality to unbounded potentials.

evidence.quote: we have to figure out the correct definition of cωφ^p − pχ ∧ ωφ^(p−1) ≥ 0 for unbounded φ
We say that cωp − pχ ∧ ωp−1 ≥ 0 if ... χ ≥ χ0
evidence.section: 3 Concentration of mass and its application

nodes.label: Curvature blow-up along a singular line for H-disks
nodes.summary: In one alternative for a sequence of H-disks, points approach the origin while the norm of the second fundamental form diverges, and the surfaces converge in C^alpha away from the line through the origin orthogonal to the limiting planes.

evidence.quote: There are points p_n \in M_n such that \lim_{n \rightarrow \infty} p_n = \mathbf{0} and \lim_{n \rightarrow \infty} |A_{M_n}|(p_n)=\infty
the convergence is C^\alpha, for any \alpha \in (0,1), away from the line containing the origin and orthogonal to the planes in the foliation

nodes.label: Curvature-controlled Hessian comparison for the exterior distance
nodes.summary: Under the curvature-diameter and exponential-map assumptions, the Hessian of r_p on E_p is bounded below by a curvature-dependent factor ψ_{K,p}/r_p times the metric component orthogonal to ∇r_p. This supplies the spacetime convexity input for the Carleman estimate.

nodes.label: Curvature-diameter Lorentzian bound
aliases: Lorentzian curvature bound with diameter restriction
nodes.summary: The geometric hypothesis requiring R≤K, with an additional spatial diameter bound Diam(M)<π/(2√K) when K>0. It is the curvature condition used to obtain spacetime convexity and unique continuation.

evidence.quote: R ≤ K for some K ∈ R

nodes.label: Globalization property for the Curvature-Dimension condition
nodes.summary: The property that local validity of CD(K,N) implies global validity of CD(K,N). The paper establishes this for essentially non-branching finite metric-measure spaces with length support.

evidence.quote: the Curvature-Dimension condition enjoys the globalization (or local-to-global) property
According to the globalization theorem in [4], the latter implies that (M,d,m) also satisfies the CD(K,N) condition

nodes.label: Tensorization property for curvature-dimension conditions
nodes.summary: The stability property asserting that a tensor product of metric-measure spaces satisfying a curvature-dimension condition again satisfies the corresponding curvature-dimension condition, under suitable structural hypotheses. In this correction, the old non-branching hypothesis is replaced by infinitesimal Hilbertianity or smooth Finslerianity for CD(K,infinity), CD*(K,N), and CD(K,N).

evidence.quote: In the formulations of the tensorization property for ... the assumption non-branching has to be replaced by the stronger assumption
If all the (M_i, d_i, m_i) are infinitesimally Hilbertian spaces and satisfy CD(K, infinity), then so does their tensor product

nodes.label: Curvature-dual current
aliases: current dual to the curvature form
nodes.summary: On an oriented manifold, the normalized curvature two-form (1/(2pi)) omega_epsilon is identified by duality with an (n-2)-current. These currents are uniformly mass-bounded by the energy.

evidence.quote: The curvature forms 1/(2pi) omega_epsilon can be identified with (n-2)-currents

nodes.label: Curvature operator
nodes.summary: The self-adjoint operator on Λ²V associated to an algebraic or Riemannian curvature tensor. Its ordered eigenvalues are the curvature quantities used in the paper's hypotheses.

evidence.quote: consider the eigenvalues λ_1 ≤ ... ≤ λ_{binom(n,2)} of the curvature operator
The associated algebraic curvature operator R:Λ²V→Λ²V is defined by

nodes.label: Curvature-operator eigenvalue spectrum
nodes.summary: The ordered list λ_1≤...≤λ_{binom(n,2)} of eigenvalues of the curvature operator on Λ²T_xM.

evidence.quote: λ_1 ≤ ... ≤ λ_{binom(n,2)} of the curvature operator

nodes.label: Curvature scale rho
nodes.summary: A length scale rho defined from scalar and full curvature so that high curvature corresponds to small rho; in canonical-neighborhood regions it is normalized by scalar curvature. It organizes thick-thin decompositions, comparison domains, and perturbation weights.

nodes.label: Curvature two-form of a Hermitian connection
nodes.summary: For a Hermitian line bundle connection, the curvature is identified with a closed real two-form omega by F_nabla(X,Y)u = -i omega(X,Y)u. This form is used in the energy, Euler-Lagrange equations, and limiting current.

evidence.quote: identify the curvature F_nabla with a closed real two-form omega via F_nabla(X,Y)u = -i omega(X,Y)u
evidence.section: 2. The Yang-Mills-Higgs equations on U(1) bundles

nodes.label: Cusp parametrix for the hyperbolic surface Laplacian
nodes.summary: A proof method indicated by the paper for analyzing the non-compact cusp part of the Laplacian problem. The excerpt gives only the section heading, so its construction is not expanded here.

nodes.label: cutting along minimal hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: A construction that removes or splits along a connected minimal hypersurface and then takes the metric completion of an appropriate component, producing a compact manifold with boundary.

nodes.label: Cyclic Brakke flow
nodes.summary: A Brakke flow whose associated Z2 flat chain is boundaryless for almost all times. It retains mod-2 information about inside and outside under weak limits.

nodes.label: Linearized mean-curvature-flow operator on the cylinder
nodes.summary: The linear operator L = Delta - one half x^tan dot grad + 1 on the round cylinder that governs the leading evolution of graph functions in the fine neck analysis.

nodes.label: Positive-neutral-negative mode decomposition on the cylinder
nodes.summary: The spectral decomposition of the Gaussian L2 Hilbert space on the round cylinder into positive, zero, and negative eigenspaces of the cylindrical linearized operator. It separates the plus-mode and neutral-mode cases in the proof.

evidence.section: 4.1 Setting up the fine neck analysis

nodes.label: Cylindrical blowdown
nodes.summary: A backward asymptotic limit of an ancient flow, obtained by parabolic rescaling toward time minus infinity, that is a round shrinking cylinder.

nodes.label: cylindrical-end extension C(U)
nodes.summary: The noncompact manifold obtained by gluing the straight cylinder boundary U x [0,infinity) to a compact core U along its boundary, with the original metric on U and product metric on the end.

nodes.label: Self-shrinker with cylindrical end
aliases: non-cylindrical shrinker with a cylindrical end
nodes.summary: A self-shrinker whose end is asymptotic to a cylinder. The paper's three-dimensional genericity theorem stops at a possible non-cylindrical tangent shrinker with such an end.

nodes.label: Cylindrical neck singularity
nodes.summary: A mean curvature flow singularity whose tangent or blowup model is the round shrinking cylinder S^{n-1} x R.

nodes.label: Cylindrical region of a rotationally symmetric ancient solution
nodes.summary: The neck-like region of a rotationally symmetric compact ancient solution modeled on a shrinking round cylinder and analyzed with Gaussian weighted energies.

evidence.quote: Energy estimates in the cylindrical region

nodes.label: Cylindrical scale
nodes.summary: A scale associated to a space-time point at which the flow first becomes epsilon-close to a cylindrical model, with controlled cylindrical behavior at larger backward scales.

nodes.label: cylindrical Weyl law for cylindrical-end widths
aliases: linear asymptotic law for cylindrical widths
nodes.summary: For a connected manifold with cylindrical ends, the p-widths are finite, strictly increase by at least the n-volume of the largest end cross-section, and grow linearly with leading term p times that volume up to a lower-order error.

nodes.label: d1 geodesic distance
nodes.summary: The path-length distance induced on H by the L1 Finsler norm integral |xi| omega_phi^n/n!. It is the metric used for properness and for the E^1 completion.

nodes.label: Damped wave equation
nodes.summary: A wave equation modified by a damping term, whose energy may decay when the damping coefficient is nontrivial.

nodes.label: Deformed Hermitian-Yang-Mills equation
nodes.summary: The fully nonlinear Kähler PDE Σ_i arctan(λ_i)=θhat for the eigenvalues of ωφ with respect to χ. In the paper it is also written as Im(ωφ+iχ)^n = tan(θ0) Re(ωφ+iχ)^n after setting θ0=nπ/2−θhat.

evidence.quote: the deformed Hermitian-Yang-Mills equation Σ_i arctan λi = constant

nodes.label: Degenerate Carleman weight for the double-null exterior
nodes.summary: A Carleman weight built from r_p, specifically a logarithmic plus large quadratic function of r_p-ρ, used on truncated exterior regions. Its degeneracy at the inner boundary is essential for proving optimal exterior unique continuation.

nodes.label: Foliation by degenerate hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: A codimension-one foliation whose leaves are lightlike or degenerate hypersurfaces. The proof constructs such a foliation on an open dense set and then studies its extension over isotropic limit orbits.

nodes.label: Degenerate-phase oscillatory integral method
nodes.summary: Analysis of oscillatory integrals with degenerate phase functions, used with the low-bounce parametrix to extract wave trace singularity information.

nodes.label: degenerate stable minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A two-sided stable minimal hypersurface whose Jacobi operator has nontrivial kernel; in the stable case the relevant kernel is generated by a positive eigenfunction.

nodes.label: Degenerating background Kähler metric setting
nodes.summary: A setting where the background Kähler metrics vary or degenerate, requiring estimates whose constants remain uniform under controlled degeneration.

evidence.quote: both cases of fixed background as well as degenerating background metrics
uniform over degenerating families with uniform  $\alpha$ -invariants

nodes.label: Degeneration to the normal cone
nodes.summary: A special Kähler test configuration associated to a subvariety. Uniform slope J-stability restricts the J-stability inequality to these degenerations.

evidence.quote: special test configurations called “degeneration to normal cones”
for any subvariety V of M, the degeneration to normal cone (X, Ω)

nodes.label: Degree-filtered module of sections
nodes.summary: The filtration M_k = {s : d(s) >= mu_k} and graded modules N^mu = direct sum M_k/M_{k+1} grouped by degrees modulo integers. The paper proves these are finitely generated torsion-free graded C[z]-modules.

evidence.quote: N_mu is a finitely generated torsion-free module
evidence.section: 3.2 Construction of torsion-free sheaves on D

nodes.label: Degree-function filtration from a Calabi-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A filtration on the polynomial-growth holomorphic function ring determined by a growth-degree function associated to the Calabi-Yau metric. Its associated graded ring is used to describe the intermediate cone.

evidence.quote: R(W) can be intrinsically described as the graded ring associated to a filtration on R(X) determined by a degree function

nodes.label: Degree function for an HYM sheaf germ
nodes.summary: A function on the stalk or local holomorphic sections measuring the asymptotic L2 growth exponent near the singular point. It links the original sheaf to homogeneous sections on analytic tangent cones.

nodes.label: Degree n Riemannian covering space
nodes.summary: An n-sheeted Riemannian covering space of a base hyperbolic surface X. The finite set of such covers is the sample space in the random model.

evidence.quote: the space of degree  $n$  Riemannian covering spaces of  $X$

nodes.label: Degree-spectral index inequality for projective-plane harmonic maps
nodes.summary: For a harmonic map Phi:RP^2 -> S^n of harmonic degree d, the spectral index satisfies ind_S(Phi)>=(d-1)/2, with equality only at d=3. This is the key technical inequality that feeds the sharp RP^2 eigenvalue bound.

evidence.section: 2.4. Relation between spectral index and degree

nodes.label: Deligne-Mumford compactification of curves
nodes.summary: The compactified moduli space of stable n-pointed genus-g curves, which supports the psi-classes whose intersection numbers enter the paper's asymptotics.

nodes.label: $\delta$-approximation by $(n-2)$-planes
nodes.summary: A local property saying that rescaled pieces of a frequency stratum lie in a $\delta$-neighborhood of some $(n-2)$-dimensional linear subspace. The appendix uses this property to conclude a Hausdorff dimension bound.

evidence.section: Appendix A. Hausdorff dimension bound for the branch set of coarse blow-ups

evidence.quote: From this "δ-approximation property", the proof can then be concluded
evidence.section: Appendix A. Hausdorff dimension bound for the branch set of coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Delta-neck
nodes.summary: An open region in a Riemannian three-manifold that is, after scaling, C^k-close to a standard round cylinder S^2 x interval. Its central two-spheres are used as cutting boundaries for comparison domains and surgery-like truncations.

nodes.label: delta_s-stable minimal immersion
nodes.summary: A weakened stability notion in which (1-delta_s) int_M |A_M|^2 f^2 <= int_M |grad f|^2. The paper records the flatness extension for delta_s in the stated range.

evidence.section: Appendix E. An extension to almost stable minimal hypersurfaces

nodes.label: delta-splitting function
nodes.summary: A harmonic map or tuple of harmonic functions whose gradients and Hessians approximate Euclidean coordinate functions, used to quantify local metric splitting in bounded-Ricci spaces.

nodes.label: δ-splitting map on an RCD space
nodes.summary: An R^k-valued harmonic map on a ball whose gradients are uniformly bounded, almost orthonormal on average, and whose Hessians are small at the scale of the ball. It converts analytic control into GH product-splitting information.

evidence.quote: u=(u_1,...,u_k):B_r(p)->R^k is a δ-splitting map provided it is harmonic
the equivalence between the existence of an R^k-valued δ-splitting map and the GH-closeness to a product with factor R^k

nodes.label: Density change-of-variables formula for Wasserstein geodesics
nodes.summary: A formula describing how densities transform along Wasserstein geodesics, expressed using a second-order temporal derivative of associated Kantorovich potentials. It is one of the paper's main proof ingredients.

evidence.quote: an explicit change-of-variables formula for densities of Wasserstein geodesics

nodes.label: Density of a varifold
nodes.summary: The normalized limiting mass ratio of a varifold at a point, used to record multiplicity and classify tangent cones or singularities. The paper uses thresholds such as density less than $Q$, density $Q$, and multiplicity $p/2$.

nodes.label: Depth-two QAC reduced L2 image formula
aliases: image formula for reduced L2 cohomology of depth-two QAC metrics
nodes.summary: For depth-two QAC metrics satisfying the paper's fiber and decay assumptions, reduced L2 cohomology is identified with the image of the positive-weight QAC cohomology group in the corresponding negative-weight group. This generalizes known QALE depth-two computations.

evidence.section: 5. L2-cohomology of quasi-asymptotically conical metrics of depth 2

nodes.label: Destabilizing geodesic ray
nodes.summary: A locally finite energy geodesic ray along which the K-energy, or twisted K-energy in the twisted setting, is non-increasing. Its existence is the analytic destabilizing alternative to a cscK metric.

nodes.label: dHYM admissible cone Γχ
nodes.summary: The elliptic branch for the dHYM proof, consisting of positive eigenvalues λ_i such that Σ_{i≠j} arctan(1/λ_i)<θ0 for every j. A Kähler form lies in Γχ when its relative eigenvalues satisfy this condition.

evidence.quote: Let Γ be the region consisting of ... λi > 0 and Σ_{i≠j} arctan(1/λi) < θ0
ω ∈ Γbarχ is equivalent to Pχ(ω) ≤ θ0

nodes.label: dHYM subvariety phase function
nodes.summary: For a p-dimensional subvariety V, θ_V(t) is the argument of the complex intersection integral ∫_V(χ+i tω0)^p, required to be defined for t≥1 and to tend to pπ/2 as t→∞.

evidence.quote: θV(t) = arg(∫V (χ + √−1 tω0)^p), limt→∞ θV(t) = pπ/2

nodes.label: Diffeomorphism group of a smooth manifold
nodes.summary: The group of smooth self-diffeomorphisms of a smooth manifold, equipped in the paper with the C-infinity topology.

nodes.label: Differential p-form
nodes.summary: A smooth section of Λ^pT*M. The paper applies the Hodge and Lichnerowicz Laplacian framework to p-forms.

nodes.label: Dihedral angle between faces
nodes.summary: The angle at which two faces of a polyhedron or tangent cone meet. The correction compares dihedral angles of the Riemannian polyhedron with those of its Euclidean model.

evidence.quote: Denote by \theta the dihedral angle between F_{1,\infty} , F_{2,\infty}

nodes.label: Dimension-uniform index bound for end-group nilpotence
nodes.summary: The strengthening that the finite index of the nilpotent subgroup in each end group is bounded above by a constant depending only on the dimension.

evidence.quote: the index can be bounded from above by a uniform constant depending only on the dimension

nodes.label: Ding functional on a log Fano pair
nodes.summary: An energy functional on finite-energy potentials of a log Fano pair, defined as minus Monge-Ampere energy plus the logarithmic L-term; its coercivity is used to obtain Kähler-Einstein metrics.

nodes.label: Dini-continuous Monge-Ampère density
nodes.summary: A right-hand side f whose modulus of continuity satisfies the Dini integrability condition. The paper states a C^2 boundary regularity conclusion under this weaker assumption than Hölder continuity.

nodes.label: Dirac boundary value problem
aliases: Dirac operators on manifolds with boundary
nodes.summary: A boundary value problem for the Dirac operator acting on spinor fields over a Riemannian manifold or smooth domain with boundary.

evidence.quote: The proof uses the Fredholm theory for Dirac operators on manifolds with boundary.
we work with boundary value problems for Dirac operators on smooth domains

nodes.label: Dirichlet boundary condition
nodes.summary: The boundary condition requiring the eigenfunction to vanish on the boundary of the domain.

nodes.label: Dirichlet energy for maps
nodes.summary: For a map between Riemannian manifolds, the Dirichlet energy is one half the integral of the squared differential norm over the domain.

nodes.label: Dirichlet exhaustion compactness for entire anisotropic graphs
nodes.summary: Solve the anisotropic minimal graph equation on balls B_k with barrier boundary data, trap the solutions, and use interior estimates to pass to a smooth solution on all of R^4. This is the final construction method for the nonlinear entire graph.

nodes.label: Dirichlet Laplacian counting function
nodes.summary: The function N^D_Ω(λ) counting Dirichlet eigenvalues λ_n(Ω) not exceeding λ², with multiplicities included.

nodes.label: Dirichlet Laplacian eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: The boundary value problem -Δu = λu in Ω with u=0 on ∂Ω. Its spectrum is a discrete sequence of finite-multiplicity eigenvalues accumulating at infinity.

evidence.quote: Consider the Dirichlet eigenvalue problem for the Laplacian

nodes.label: Dirichlet Pólya inequality for Euclidean balls
nodes.summary: The verified Dirichlet special case of Pólya's conjecture for Euclidean balls: the Dirichlet counting function is bounded above by the Weyl leading term for balls in any dimension.

evidence.quote: in the Dirichlet case, for balls of any dimension

nodes.label: Dirichlet-to-Neumann map for the Lorentzian wave equation
nodes.summary: The operator sending boundary Dirichlet data f on the timelike boundary Σ to the normal derivative ∂νu|Σ of the solution of the wave equation with zero initial data. It is recovered from the global Cauchy data set on compact time intervals.

evidence.section: 2.2. Reduction from Cauchy data set to the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map

nodes.label: Dirichlet-to-Neumann operator
nodes.summary: The boundary operator mapping boundary data to the outward normal derivative of its harmonic extension; Steklov eigenvalues are its eigenvalues.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Discrepancy time between weak flows
nodes.summary: The first time when the outer flow, inner flow, and level set flow are not all equal. Proving no discrepancy implies nonfattening in the paper's application.

nodes.label: Discrete automorphism group condition
nodes.summary: The assumption that the identity component of the automorphism group of the complex manifold is zero, so the automorphism group is discrete. It is imposed in the paper's main cscK existence, properness, and geodesic stability statements.

nodes.label: Discrete Euclidean set with planar net slice
aliases: nonplanar discrete set with planar net intersection
nodes.summary: A discrete subset X of R^3 that is not contained in any affine plane but whose intersection with some affine plane is a Euclidean net in that plane. This condition produces an obstruction to two-dimensional Riemannian embedding.

evidence.quote: X \subseteq \mathbb{R}^3 be a discrete set that is not contained in any affine plane, yet there exists an affine plane H \subset \mathbb{R}^3 such that X \cap H is a net in H

nodes.label: Disk-embedding extension property from the Smale conjecture
nodes.summary: A consequence of the Smale conjecture for S^3: restrictions of orientation-preserving 3-disk embeddings to the boundary sphere have contractible fibers and relative lifting properties. The paper converts local constant-curvature metric extension over a ball into this embedding extension problem.

nodes.label: Distributional differential k-form
nodes.summary: A k-form on a manifold whose coefficients are distributions rather than smooth functions.

nodes.label: Div-curl differential inclusion
nodes.summary: The compactness/oscillation formalism used to encode stationary graphical systems through divergence-curl constraints and a constraint set in matrix space; the paper refers to external definitions.

evidence.quote: Young measures generated by div-curl inclusions

nodes.label: divergence bound for the characteristic vector field
nodes.summary: For the vector field associated with the pullback of the distribution-defining one-form, the divergence lies in the ideal generated by its coefficients, yielding a local bound by the pushed-forward vector field.

nodes.label: Divisor complement
nodes.summary: The open complex manifold obtained by removing the divisor from X, denoted X^circ = X \ D. The complete limiting metrics in the paper live on this complement.

nodes.label: Divisorial valuation
nodes.summary: A valuation on X given by order of vanishing along a prime divisor over X. Such valuations appear in the log discrepancy term used by the non-Archimedean Ding functional and stability threshold.

nodes.label: Donaldson geodesic stability conjecture
aliases: Donaldson's conjecture on cscK geodesic rays
nodes.summary: The conjectural equivalence, under discrete automorphism group, between nonexistence of cscK metrics and existence of a geodesic ray along which K-energy is non-increasing. The paper proves this in the finite-energy geodesic setting.

nodes.label: Double-limit construction of a singular Calabi-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A limiting procedure that first collapses the exceptional set in the family of resolutions and then passes to the limiting weighted cone, producing a Calabi-Yau metric on the smooth locus of W.

evidence.quote: take a double limit by first letting epsilon tend to 0 and then letting delta tend to 0
Passing to a subsequence we may take a pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit

nodes.label: Doubling measure
nodes.summary: A measure is doubling when the measure of a ball is controlled by a uniform multiple of the measure of a comparable smaller ball. In the excerpt it is the hypothesis under which metric tangent measures agree with measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent spaces.

nodes.label: Dual anisotropic integrand
nodes.summary: The integrand on G(N,n) obtained by evaluating the original integrand on the orthogonal complement plane, Psi*(P)=Psi(id-P).

nodes.label: Egorov theorem up to local Ehrenfest time
nodes.summary: A semiclassical propagation principle identifying quantum conjugation of observables with classical pullback by the geodesic flow for logarithmic h-dependent times.

nodes.label: Eigenvalue counting function
nodes.summary: The function counting Laplace eigenvalue frequencies not exceeding $\lambda$, namely $N(\lambda)=\#\{j:\lambda_j\leq\lambda\}$.

evidence.quote: Let $N(\lambda) \coloneqq \# \{j : \lambda_j \leq \lambda\}$ be the eigenvalue counting function

nodes.label: Eigenvalue supremum Lambda_k
nodes.summary: The supremum of the k-th normalized Laplace eigenvalue over all Riemannian metrics on a fixed surface M. It is the target quantity in the paper's isoperimetric eigenvalue inequalities.

nodes.label: Einstein leaf of the nilradical foliation
nodes.summary: A maximal leaf of the integrable distribution spanned by nilradical Killing fields and the mean curvature vector N; the paper proves these leaves are minimal and locally Einstein solvmanifolds.

evidence.quote: spanned by the Killing fields in n and N

nodes.label: Einstein metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric whose Ricci tensor is a constant multiple of the metric, Ric_g = lambda g. Ricci-flat metrics are the special case lambda = 0.

nodes.label: Einstein Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian manifold whose Ricci tensor is a constant multiple of the metric, \operatorname{ric}(g)=\lambda g.

evidence.quote: called Einstein if its Ricci tensor satisfies

nodes.label: Einstein solvmanifold
nodes.summary: A homogeneous Einstein manifold admitting a transitive solvable Lie group of isometries, often realized by a left-invariant Einstein metric on a solvable Lie group.

evidence.quote: admits a transitive solvable Lie group of isometries

nodes.label: Einstein Universe boundary
nodes.summary: The boundary at infinity of pseudo-hyperbolic space H^{2,n}, realized as the projective quadric associated to the ambient quadratic form. It is the target space for positive loops, semi-positive loops, limit curves, and quasisymmetric parametrizations in the paper.

evidence.quote: admits a boundary at infinity denoted $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ and called the Einstein Universe which is the quadric in $\mathbf{P}(E)$ associated to $\mathbf{q}$
the limit curves of these surfaces in the Einstein Universe admits a canonical quasisymmetric parametrisation

nodes.label: ell^p simple-two-vector anisotropic integrands satisfying AC1
nodes.summary: For G equal to the ell^p norm on the six 2-vector coordinates in R^4, the induced even one-homogeneous integrand Psi_G on G(4,2) satisfies AC1, giving examples far from the area functional.

nodes.label: Ellipse eccentricity
nodes.summary: The standard numerical parameter measuring how far an ellipse is from being circular; small eccentricity means the ellipse is close to a disk.

nodes.label: Embedded surface in Euclidean three-space
nodes.summary: A surface mapped into Euclidean three-space by an embedding, so that it is identified with a subset of the ambient space. This is part of the definition of an H-surface in the paper.

evidence.quote: an oriented surface M immersed in \mathbb{R}^3 an H-surface if it is embedded, connected

nodes.label: Fundamental group of an end
nodes.summary: The fundamental group associated to an end of the manifold, identified in this setting with the fundamental group of a closed aspherical cross-section of the end.

evidence.quote: the fundamental group of each end of  $M$

nodes.label: End of a noncompact manifold
nodes.summary: An asymptotic component of a noncompact manifold outside compact subsets. The paper studies the finite collection of ends of finite-volume visibility manifolds.

nodes.label: End-Point Mapping
nodes.summary: For a fixed initial point, the map sending an admissible control to the terminal point of the corresponding controlled horizontal trajectory.

nodes.label: Energy-index halving under antipodal lift
nodes.summary: For a linearly full harmonic map Phi:RP^2 -> S^{2m} and its antipodal lift tilde Phi:S^2 -> S^{2m}, the energy index and energy nullity of Phi are one half of those of tilde Phi. The proof uses the harmonic sequence and twistor decomposition of Jacobi fields.

nodes.label: Energy-index lower bound for harmonic spheres
aliases: lower bound for ind_E of harmonic two-spheres
nodes.summary: For a linearly full harmonic map S^2 -> S^{2m} of degree d, the paper proves a lower bound for ind_E(Phi) in terms of m, d, and the largest odd integer not exceeding sqrt(8d+1). It improves known lower bounds in higher codimension and applies to branched minimal spheres through the area-index equivalence.

nodes.label: Energy-index method for isoperimetric eigenvalue inequalities
nodes.summary: The paper's strategy of proving sharp eigenvalue inequalities by translating extremal metrics into harmonic maps and comparing their spectral index, energy index, and harmonic degree. For RP^2 this method proves the sharp Lambda_k formula through the degree-spectral index inequality.

nodes.label: Energy index of a harmonic map
nodes.summary: The Morse index of a harmonic map as a critical point of the energy functional, equivalently the number of negative eigenvalues of its Jacobi operator. Energy nullity is the dimension of the Jacobi-field space ker J.

nodes.label: Energy Morse index of a harmonic map
nodes.summary: The energy Morse index of a weakly harmonic map is the maximal dimension of a subspace on which the second variation of the Dirichlet energy is negative definite.

nodes.label: Energy non-concentration for coarse blow-ups
nodes.summary: An estimate preventing the energy of coarse blow-ups from concentrating too strongly on small sets or singular strata. It supports the later frequency and regularity analysis.

evidence.quote: Squeeze identity and frequency monotonicity for generalised-  $C^1$  coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: energy of a horizontal path
nodes.summary: The integral of the squared norm of the horizontal velocity, with the norm induced by the metric on Delta.

nodes.label: Entire anisotropic minimal graph
nodes.summary: A graph over all of R^n that is a critical point of an anisotropic parametric surface energy. The Bernstein question asks whether such graphs must be affine hyperplanes.

evidence.quote: graphs of functions defined on all of R^n

nodes.label: Entropy of a Brakke flow
nodes.summary: The supremum of the entropies of the time-slice measures of a Brakke flow. For smooth mean curvature flow it is nonincreasing in time by Huisken's monotonicity formula.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Entropy of a hypersurface or Brakke flow
nodes.summary: The supremum of Gaussian weighted areas over centers and scales, extended to Brakke flows by taking the supremum over time slices. Huisken monotonicity makes it non-increasing along mean curvature flow.

nodes.label: Entropy of a surface or Radon measure
nodes.summary: The supremum, over all centers and scales, of the Gaussian weighted area of a surface or two-rectifiable Radon measure. It measures the complexity relevant to singularity models.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Entropy pinching
nodes.summary: A quantitative small-variation condition for local pointed entropy, used as a criterion for cone or splitting structure.

nodes.label: epsilon-bi-Holder equivalence
nodes.summary: A two-sided Holder-type equivalence with an epsilon-controlled exponent or distortion, used to compare the regular part of the limit space with a smooth manifold.

evidence.quote: $X^n \setminus S_\epsilon^{n-2}$  is  $\epsilon$ -bi-Hölder equivalent to a smooth Riemannian manifold

nodes.label: Epsilon-nearly circular domain in C^n
nodes.summary: A simply connected smooth planar domain whose boundary is a normal graph over the unit circle with C^n norm of the graphing function bounded by a constant times epsilon.

nodes.label: Epsilon-regularity for hyperkähler 4-manifolds
nodes.summary: A curvature estimate asserting that sufficiently small L2 curvature energy on a ball in a hyperkähler four-manifold bounds the pointwise curvature on a smaller ball. It implies finitely many singular points under a uniform L2 energy bound.

nodes.label: epsilon-regularity for two-sided Ricci bounds
nodes.summary: A regularity criterion in the setting of two-sided Ricci curvature bounds, used to upgrade structural control to codimension-four singular-set estimates.

nodes.label: Equality rigidity for the codimension-two submanifold Sobolev inequality
nodes.summary: Equality in the sharp codimension-two submanifold Sobolev inequality forces the test function to be constant and the submanifold to be a flat round ball. The proof derives D_Sigma^2 u = lambda g, II = 0, and nabla^Sigma f = 0 on a dense set and then on all of Sigma.

nodes.label: Equivariant divergence theorem
nodes.summary: A divergence theorem adapted to invariant vector fields over a compact orbit space, used to turn nonnegative divergence inequalities into rigidity.

nodes.label: Equivariant modified Helmholtz decomposition
nodes.summary: An equivariant decomposition of an invariant vector field as a gradient term plus a weighted divergence-free invariant vector field, applied to the mean curvature vector of nilradical orbits.

nodes.label: Essential conformal group
nodes.summary: A conformal group is inessential when it is isometric for some metric in the conformal class; otherwise it is essential. The Lorentzian Lichnerowicz context concerns manifolds admitting essential conformal groups.

nodes.label: Essential dimension of a Ricci limit space
nodes.summary: The integer d such that almost every point in the Ricci limit space has unique Euclidean tangent cone R^d. The paper's collapsing cases are organized by d<4.

nodes.label: Essentially non-branching metric-measure space
nodes.summary: A metric-measure space whose relevant optimal-transport geodesics are concentrated on non-branching families. It is the structural hypothesis under which local CD(K,N) globalizes in the paper.

nodes.label: Euclidean ball
