nodes.summary: A ball in Euclidean space, used here as the principal family of domains for which Pólya-type Laplacian counting inequalities are studied.

evidence.quote: Dirichlet and Neumann eigenvalues of the ball and lattice counting problems

nodes.label: Euclidean building
nodes.summary: A CAT(0) building modeled on Euclidean Coxeter complexes, appearing as one of the rigidity alternatives.

nodes.label: Euclidean discrete metric space
aliases: discrete subset with the Euclidean metric
nodes.summary: A discrete subset of a Euclidean space endowed with the Euclidean distance inherited from the ambient space. The paper studies when such spaces embed isometrically into Riemannian manifolds.

evidence.quote: Endow X with the Euclidean metric.

nodes.label: Euclidean disk
nodes.summary: The two-dimensional Euclidean ball. In this paper it is the first non-tiling planar domain for which Pólya's conjecture is verified.

evidence.quote: we prove Pólya's conjecture for the disk
the first non-tiling planar domain for which the conjecture is verified

nodes.label: Euclidean distance function
nodes.summary: The distance between points in Euclidean space induced by the Euclidean norm. For x in R^n the norm is |x| = sqrt(sum_i x_i^2).

evidence.quote: d(x, L) = \inf_{y \in L} |x - y| and |x| = \sqrt{\sum_i x_i^2}

nodes.label: Euclidean factor splitting
nodes.summary: The property that a tangent cone or limit cone decomposes as a product with a Euclidean factor.

evidence.quote: no tangent cone splits off a Euclidean factor  $\mathbb{R}^{k+1}$

nodes.label: Euclidean half-space model
nodes.summary: The model metric space R^N_+ used for boundary tangent cones, boundary balls, and epsilon-regularity. Closeness to balls in this model detects codimension-one boundary behavior.

evidence.quote: a half space B_2(0) subset R^N_+

nodes.label: Euclidean Laplacian operator
nodes.summary: The positive Euclidean Laplacian used in the eigenvalue problems, written as -Δ = -∑_{j=1}^d ∂²/∂x_j².

nodes.label: Euclidean plane
nodes.summary: The two-dimensional Euclidean space with its standard flat Riemannian metric. The paper proves that a complete connected surface containing an isometric Euclidean net must be isometric to it.

nodes.label: Euclidean space
nodes.summary: The standard n-dimensional real coordinate space equipped with its Euclidean norm and distance. Nets and lattice examples in the paper live in Euclidean space.

nodes.label: Euclidean submanifold with boundary
nodes.summary: A smooth compact n-dimensional submanifold of an ambient Euclidean space R^{n+m}, possibly with boundary. Its induced geometry supplies tangent and normal spaces, volume, boundary measure, and curvature quantities.

nodes.label: Euclidean three-space
nodes.summary: The three-dimensional Euclidean space with its standard metric; it is the ambient space for the H-surfaces and H-disks in the paper.

evidence.quote: disks M_n embedded in \mathbb{R}^3 with constant mean curvature H_n
an oriented surface M immersed in \mathbb{R}^3

nodes.label: Euclidean unit ball volume notation
nodes.summary: B^n denotes the open unit ball in R^n, and |B^n| denotes its Euclidean volume. These constants set the sharp constants in the inequalities.

nodes.label: Euclidean unit ball volume recursion
nodes.summary: The standard relation between volumes of Euclidean unit balls, used here in the form (n+2)|B^{n+2}| = 2|B^2||B^n| to simplify the codimension-two constant.

nodes.label: Euclidean volume growth
nodes.summary: A lower volume-growth condition requiring metric balls of radius R to have volume bounded below by a positive multiple of R to the real dimension.

evidence.quote: Euclidean volume growth: there exists a kappa > 0 such that Vol(B_g(p, R)) >= kappa R^{2n} for all R > 0.

nodes.label: Small-spectrum triviality for Euler characteristic minus one hyperbolic surfaces
nodes.summary: For a finite-area hyperbolic surface with Euler characteristic -1, the spectrum below 1/4 consists only of the simple zero eigenvalue. This input lets the random-cover result produce positive spectral gaps tending to 1/4.

evidence.quote: is just the multiplicity one eigenvalue at 0

nodes.label: Eulerian-Lagrangian curvature-dimension equivalence
nodes.summary: The equivalence between the Eulerian Bakry-Émery curvature-dimension condition and the Lagrangian optimal-transport curvature-dimension condition. The correction invokes it to transfer tensorization from BE(K,infinity) to CD(K,infinity).

evidence.quote: the equivalence between Eulerian and Lagrangian curvature-dimension conditions BE(K, infinity) and CD(K, infinity)

nodes.label: Exact controllability in rough Sobolev spaces
nodes.summary: The property that suitable distributional boundary data can drive the Lorentzian wave equation to prescribed compactly supported distributional Cauchy data at a later time. It is used to create waves focused at one spatial point.

evidence.quote: Exact controllability in rough Sobolev spaces
evidence.section: 6. Exact controllability in rough Sobolev spaces

nodes.label: Exact QFB metric
aliases: product-type QFB metric up to first order
nodes.summary: A QFB metric that is product-type near each boundary hypersurface modulo a controlled term vanishing with the relevant boundary defining function. This exactness is used when invoking decay of L2 harmonic forms.

nodes.label: Excess decay for anisotropic Lipschitz graphs
nodes.summary: A small-excess improvement estimate showing that, under a scale-normalized L^p mean-curvature bound, the quadratic excess decays at smaller scales.

evidence.quote: decay of the following classical quadratic excess
E(x, tau r) <= c tau^2 E(x, r)

nodes.label: Expander barrier construction
nodes.summary: A method that builds super- and subsolutions as graphs over an outermost expander using the positive Jacobi field and a positive eigenfunction. These barriers push or pinch rescaled mean curvature flows toward the outermost expander.

evidence.quote: we find barriers that push the outermost flows onto the outermost expanders in the forward blowup limit
The “interior” barrier is then formed by taking the graph over $\Sigma$ of a small multiple of $f:=v+\alpha\phi_{3R}$.
The barriers then pinch $\mathcal{M}^{2}$ towards $\mathcal{M}^{1}$ from above and below as $s\to 0$ proving uniqueness.

nodes.label: Expander functional
nodes.summary: The weighted area functional whose compactly supported critical points are mean curvature self-expanders. One-sided minimization of this functional gives stability for outermost expanders.

evidence.quote: self-expanders are critical points (under compact perturbations) of the expander functional
minimizes the expander functional to the outside ... Similarly, minimizes to the inside
evidence.section: 2.6. The level set flow of a cone and the outermost expanders

nodes.label: Expected vanishing order S-invariant
nodes.summary: For a valuation v, the normalized integral of the volumes of the induced filtration on the anticanonical section ring; it measures expected vanishing order with respect to L.

nodes.label: Exponential decay away from the Higgs zero set
aliases: energy decay away from zero set
nodes.summary: For bounded-energy critical pairs, the Higgs defect and full energy density decay exponentially at distance much larger than epsilon from the region where |u| is bounded away from one. This localizes energy near the zero set.

evidence.quote: the energy density e_epsilon(u,nabla) decays exponentially away from the set u^{-1}(0)

nodes.label: Exponential energy decay for damped waves on Anosov surfaces
nodes.summary: For the damped wave equation on an Anosov surface, every nontrivial damping coefficient yields exponential decay of the wave energy.

nodes.label: Exponential flat-model convergence for ALH non-splitting instantons
aliases: exponential convergence to the flat model
nodes.summary: The property that an ALH non-splitting instanton's metric converges exponentially fast to its flat asymptotic model. The paper presents this as a curvature-decay self-improvement in the ALH non-splitting case.

evidence.quote: the metric must converge to the flat model exponentially
Exponential decay of curvature of ALH instantons

nodes.label: Exponential growth weight
nodes.summary: A weight with exponential growth used in weighted analytic estimates. The paper develops separate elliptic estimates using such weights.

evidence.quote: Elliptic estimates with exponential growth weights

nodes.label: Extended monotone set in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: An extended monotone set satisfies a strengthened local monotonicity condition over an enlarged family of line tests or regions. The paper uses this notion to obtain stable approximation by half-spaces.

evidence.quote: 11. Extended-monotone sets are close to half-spaces 157

nodes.label: Extended non-monotonicity
nodes.summary: Extended non-monotonicity is a quantitative linewise measure of how far a set is from being monotone, evaluated on enlarged intervals or regions. It drives the subdivision and approximation arguments.

nodes.label: Extension cap
nodes.summary: A three-disk component added to a comparison domain at a transition time when a cap region enters the controlled scale range. Its geometry must be well approximated by a Bryant soliton to extend the comparison map across it.

nodes.label: Exterior normal derivative
nodes.summary: The derivative of a function in the direction of the exterior unit normal n on the boundary, ∂_n u = <∇u,n>|_{∂Ω}.

evidence.quote: where  $\partial_n u = \langle \nabla u, n \rangle|_{\partial \Omega}$  denotes the normal derivative

nodes.label: Exterior of the double null cone
nodes.summary: For a point p, the set E_p=M∖(J^-(p)∪J^+(p)), consisting of points outside both its causal future and causal past. It is the domain on which the paper proves unique continuation.

nodes.label: Extremal and maximal metric for normalized eigenvalues
nodes.summary: A metric is extremal for a normalized eigenvalue functional when the one-sided derivatives along analytic metric deformations have opposite signs or one is zero; it is conformally extremal when this holds within a conformal class. A maximal metric realizes the supremum Lambda_k(M).

nodes.label: Extremal Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A canonical Kähler metric generalizing cscK metrics, classically characterized by extremality of the Calabi functional or holomorphicity of the scalar-curvature gradient. It appears in the paper through Calabi's broader existence vision.

nodes.label: Extremal metric and harmonic map correspondence
nodes.summary: Conformally extremal metrics for normalized eigenvalue functionals yield harmonic maps to spheres whose components are eigenfunctions, and conversely a harmonic map produces a conformally extremal induced metric for the functional indexed by its spectral index. If the map is weakly conformal, the induced metric is extremal.

nodes.label: Extrinsic curvature estimate for H-disks
aliases: curvature estimates for constant mean curvature disks
nodes.summary: An a priori estimate controlling the norm of the second fundamental form of embedded H-disks in Euclidean three-space using extrinsic data. The paper states that its main proofs depend essentially on such estimates.

evidence.quote: depend in an essential manner on the existence of extrinsic curvature estimates for disks embedded in \mathbb{R}^3 of non-zero constant mean curvature
by the extrinsic curvature estimates in Theorem 2.4

nodes.label: F-convergence of metric flow pairs
nodes.summary: Convergence of metric flow pairs in the F-distance, equivalently convergence of almost every time-slice in Gromov-W1-Wasserstein sense together with compatible convergence of conjugate heat kernels. It is the paper's compactness topology.

nodes.label: F-distance between metric flow pairs
nodes.summary: A distance on metric flow pairs obtained by taking the infimum over correspondences of the size of an exceptional time set and W1 discrepancies between pushed-forward conjugate heat kernels. It is the parabolic analogue of pointed Gromov-Hausdorff type distances in this paper.

nodes.label: F-precompactness of pointed super Ricci flows
nodes.summary: Sequences of compact n-dimensional super Ricci flows equipped with suitable conjugate heat equation solutions have subsequences whose associated metric flow pairs converge in the F-distance. For pointed flows, the base conjugate heat flow is often the conjugate heat kernel based at the final-time point.

nodes.label: Fano manifold with smooth anticanonical divisor
nodes.summary: A pair (X,D) where X is Fano and D is a smooth divisor representing the anticanonical class. This is the positive-curvature case in which the Tian-Yau metric appears as a rescaled limit.

evidence.quote: X is Fano and D is anticanonical
one starts with a Fano manifold X and a smooth divisor D which is Q-linearly equivalent to K_X^{-1}

nodes.label: Fano manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth projective variety with ample anticanonical class. The untwisted Fano case is the classical setting of the Yau-Tian-Donaldson conjecture discussed in the introduction.

evidence.quote: For metrics in the anticanonical class of a Fano manifold  $X$

nodes.label: Faster than quadratic curvature decay
nodes.summary: A curvature decay condition requiring the Riemann curvature norm to be bounded by a negative power strictly faster than r^{-2}, namely |Rm|(x) <= r(x)^{-2-epsilon} for some small epsilon > 0.

nodes.label: Fattening dichotomy for conical level set flows
nodes.summary: For a smooth hypersurface with an isolated conical singularity, instantaneous fattening of the hypersurface level set flow is equivalent to fattening of the level set flow of the model cone.

evidence.quote: the flow fattens (instantaneously) if and only if the level set flow of the cone fattens
Then the level set flow of $M$ fattens instantly if and only if the level-set flow from the cone $\mathcal{C}$ fattens.

nodes.label: Fattening of level set flow
aliases: non-empty interior of level set flow
nodes.summary: The phenomenon that a level set flow of an initial surface develops nonempty interior, so the weak evolution is no longer a hypersurface-like set.

evidence.quote: If the level set flow $F_t(X)$ develops an interior at $t = T$, we say that the flow fattens at time $T$.

nodes.label: Fefferman-Phong boundary trace inequality
nodes.summary: A Fefferman-Phong-type estimate controlling a weighted boundary $L^2$ term by interior Dirichlet energy and a lower-order boundary $L^2$ term, under a scale-invariant $L^\sigma$ control on the weight.

evidence.quote: A variant of a theorem of Fefferman and Phong plays a central role in our analysis.

nodes.label: Fiber pushforward on a sphere bundle
nodes.summary: A pushforward operation that sends forms or densities on the sphere bundle to objects on the base manifold by integrating or contracting along sphere fibers.

nodes.label: Fine excess for a varifold
nodes.summary: A refined excess quantity measuring closeness to a more accurate multi-valued or fine comparison object. The paper contrasts it with coarse excess in a key reduction step.

evidence.quote: small coarse excess and significantly smaller fine excess

nodes.label: Fine neck analysis
aliases: fine neck analysis for ancient asymptotically cylindrical flows
nodes.summary: The method of writing the rescaled flow as a small graph over long cylindrical regions and extracting sharp asymptotics for the graph function using the cylinder linearized operator and spectral modes.

evidence.quote: Fine neck analysis for ancient asymptotically cylindrical flows

nodes.label: Finite-area hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A hyperbolic surface whose Riemannian area is finite. In this paper it is the base object for the random covering model.

evidence.quote: Let  $X$  be a finite-area hyperbolic surface

nodes.label: Finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A finite-area hyperbolic surface that is not compact. The paper's first main spectral-gap result concerns random covers of such surfaces.

evidence.quote: if  $X$  is a finite area non-compact hyperbolic surface
If  $X$  is non-compact, which is the first focus of our paper

nodes.label: Finite-dimensional Banach tangent model
nodes.summary: A finite-dimensional Banach space used as the tangent model for subsets of arbitrary metric spaces, replacing Euclidean affine tangent planes; its norm need not be Euclidean.

evidence.quote: the affine subspace must be replaced by an  $n$ -dimensional Banach space; the tangent space may not be equipped with the Euclidean norm.

nodes.label: Finite disk complement for a punctured space form
nodes.summary: For a partial metric isometric to a punctured space form, one chooses a compact domain inside the partial domain whose complement in the closed manifold is a finite disjoint union of closed 3-disks. This reduces global extension to ball-by-ball extension.

nodes.label: Finite energy geodesic
nodes.summary: A geodesic segment in E^1 obtained as the decreasing limit of C^{1,1} geodesics between smooth approximants. It is the segment-level notion used to define locally finite energy rays.

nodes.label: Finite energy geodesic metric space
nodes.summary: The metric completion of the smooth Kähler potential space under d1, identified with the finite-energy potential class E^1. The paper works in this complete geodesic metric space to define weak rays and minimizers.

nodes.label: Finite energy Kähler potential on a singular variety
nodes.summary: A plurisubharmonic Hermitian metric or potential in the finite-energy space E^1 on a singular projective variety, used as the analytic domain for Ding, Mabuchi, and Kähler-Einstein equations.

evidence.quote: E1(L) := E1(Z,L) = {psi + u; u in E1(Z,omega)}
evidence.section: 2.1 Space of Kähler metrics over singular projective varieties

nodes.label: Finite energy non-Archimedean potential
nodes.summary: A non-Archimedean potential satisfying a finite energy condition, used as the non-Archimedean counterpart of finite energy psh potentials. The paper has a dedicated section relating these objects to geodesic rays.

evidence.quote: Non-Archimedean potentials of finite energy and geodesic rays

nodes.label: Finite energy potential
nodes.summary: A plurisubharmonic potential or associated positive current with finite Monge-Ampère energy. This is the regularity class used to interpret singular twisted Kähler-Einstein currents.

evidence.quote: of finite energy in the sense of [BBGZ13]
Finite energy potentials and psh geodesics

nodes.label: End structure of finite-index minimal hypersurfaces in R^4
nodes.summary: For a complete two-sided minimal immersion M^3 -> R^4 with finite Morse index, there are finitely many ends, each regular at infinity, and the hypersurface has cubic volume growth.

nodes.label: Partial regularity of finite-index stationary harmonic maps
aliases: optimal partial regularity for finite-index harmonic maps
nodes.summary: Finite-index stationary harmonic maps into targets without stable harmonic two-spheres are locally stable and smooth away from a singular set of dimension at most n-3, with sharper bounds when stable 0-homogeneous tangent maps are absent.

evidence.quote: smooth away from a singular set
evidence.section: 2.3 Partial regularity for the min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Finite-index subgroup
nodes.summary: A subgroup whose set of left cosets in the ambient group is finite.

nodes.label: Finite-index finite-total-curvature equivalence in R^4
aliases: finite Morse index iff finite total curvature in R^4
nodes.summary: The equivalence, for complete two-sided minimal immersions M^3 -> R^4, between finite Morse index and finite total curvature.

nodes.label: Finite invariant volume for a conformal action
aliases: invariant volume on an open subset
nodes.summary: A finite volume measure on an invariant open set preserved by the conformal action. In the paper, existence of such a volume for the reduced abelian group forces compactness of that group.

evidence.section: 2.3. Proof à la D'Ambra given a finite, invariant volume on an open subset

nodes.label: Finite L2 curvature energy
nodes.summary: The condition that the integral of the squared norm of the Riemann curvature tensor over a four-manifold is finite. It is the energy condition used for gravitational instantons and for epsilon-regularity arguments.

nodes.label: Finite Morse index minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A complete two-sided minimal immersion whose index form is negative definite on only finitely many independent compactly supported variations.

nodes.label: Finite order boundary contact for null geodesics
nodes.summary: The technical condition that each null geodesic meets or tangentially contacts the boundary only to finite order. The excerpt states the assumption but does not expand the local analytic definition of contact order.

evidence.quote: finite order of contact with the boundary

nodes.label: Finite Riemannian volume
nodes.summary: The property that the total volume of a Riemannian manifold, computed from its Riemannian measure, is finite.

evidence.quote: complete noncompact manifold  $M$  of finite volume

nodes.label: Finite-time cylindrical Lorentzian manifold
nodes.summary: A time slab M=[-T,T]×M0 with metric g(t,x)=c(t,x)(-dt²+g0(t,x)), where c is positive and g0(t,·) is a smoothly time-dependent Riemannian metric on M0. The paper reduces local inverse problems to this setting.

nodes.label: Finite total curvature minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: For a three-dimensional minimal hypersurface in R^4, the property that int_M |A_M|^3 is finite.

nodes.label: Finite-volume complex hyperbolic manifold
nodes.summary: A quotient of complex hyperbolic n-space by a lattice with finite Riemannian volume. The paper fixes the convention that n denotes complex dimension.

evidence.quote: a finite volume complex hyperbolic n-manifold will always have complex dimension n

nodes.label: Finite-volume irreducible locally symmetric space of noncompact type
aliases: irreducible locally symmetric space of noncompact type
nodes.summary: A finite-volume quotient of a symmetric space of noncompact type associated with an irreducible lattice in a semisimple Lie group with no compact factors.

evidence.quote: a nonarithmetic finite volume irreducible locally symmetric space of noncompact type

nodes.label: Finite-volume noncompact nonpositively curved Riemannian manifold
aliases: complete finite-volume noncompact manifold of nonpositive curvature
complete noncompact manifold of finite volume with -1 <= K <= 0
nodes.summary: A complete noncompact Riemannian manifold of finite total volume satisfying the paper's nonpositive sectional-curvature bounds, usually $-1\leq K\leq 0$. This is the ambient class of manifolds whose ends are studied.

evidence.quote: a complete noncompact manifold  $M$  of finite volume with sectional curvature  $-1\leq K\leq 0$

nodes.label: Finitely presented group
nodes.summary: A group admitting a presentation with finitely many generators and finitely many defining relations.

nodes.label: First Betti number
nodes.summary: The rank of the first cohomology group of the underlying manifold. In the paper it appears in the formula for the order of vanishing at zero.

nodes.label: First Chern class
nodes.summary: The degree-two characteristic class of a complex line bundle represented in de Rham cohomology by the normalized curvature form (1/(2pi))omega. It determines the Poincare-dual homology class of the limiting curvature cycle.

evidence.quote: the cohomology class [1/(2pi) omega_epsilon] represents the first Chern class c1(L)

nodes.label: First-eigenfunction perturbation of a shrinker
nodes.summary: Perturbing a shrinker along the positive first eigenfunction of its stability operator to produce a nearby hypersurface on one side that is strictly shrinker mean convex.

evidence.section: 7 Existence of a smooth ancient shrinker mean convex flow

nodes.label: First non-generic time of a mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: For a unit-regular Brakke flow, the supremum of times before which all tangent flows at support points are multiplicity-one spheres, cylinders, or planes.

evidence.section: 11 The first non-generic time for flows in R3

nodes.label: First nonzero Laplacian eigenvalue on a closed hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: The first positive eigenvalue in the closed-surface Laplace-Beltrami eigenvalue sequence. The paper constructs closed hyperbolic surfaces with this value tending to 1/4.

evidence.quote: first non-zero eigenvalue of the Laplacian tending to  $\frac{1}{4}$

nodes.label: First-order twistor correspondence for Jacobi fields
nodes.summary: A linear isomorphism between Jacobi fields along a linearly full harmonic map Phi:S^2 -> S^{2m} and twistor fields along its twistor lift, with inverse l -> -2 Re(l^*(Phi)). It is a main new technical tool of the paper.

nodes.label: First Steklov eigenvalue one for Platonic-symmetric free boundary minimal surfaces
nodes.summary: For the selected embedded genus-zero free boundary minimal surfaces with tetrahedral, octahedral, or icosahedral symmetry and specified boundary counts, the first nonzero Steklov eigenvalue is 1.

nodes.label: First variation of a varifold
nodes.summary: The distributional derivative of varifold mass under ambient deformations. Vanishing first variation is the stationarity condition for generalized minimal surfaces.

nodes.label: First variation under conformal metric perturbation
nodes.summary: The infinitesimal variation of the relevant resonant or zeta-theoretic quantity under a conformal deformation of the hyperbolic metric.

evidence.quote: using first variation for the perturbation

nodes.label: Fixed-point scheme for minimal graphs over cones
nodes.summary: A contraction mapping method on a weighted $C^{2,\alpha}$ ball: solve the linear Jacobi equation with source $-\mathcal{E}(w)$ and fixed projected boundary data, then obtain the nonlinear minimal graph as a fixed point.

nodes.label: Fixed-point set at infinity of an isometry
nodes.summary: For an isometry $\varphi$, the set of boundary points fixed by $\varphi$ in the geometric boundary of the space.

nodes.label: Flat Euclidean polyhedron
nodes.summary: A polyhedron isometric to a region in Euclidean three-space with the Euclidean metric; it is the rigidity conclusion of the corrected theorem.

evidence.quote: then (M,g) is isometric to a flat polyhedron in \mathbf{R}^3

nodes.label: Flat half-space in a CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A closed convex subset of a CAT(0) space isometric to a Euclidean half-space.

evidence.quote: closed convex subset isometric to a Euclidean half-space

nodes.label: Flat interface property for stable Gaussian clusters
nodes.summary: The property that the interfaces of a stable regular Gaussian cluster are flat. In the paper this implies that such clusters consist of convex polyhedral cells with at most q-1 facets.

evidence.quote: stable regular clusters must have flat interfaces
therefore consisting of convex polyhedral cells (with at most q - 1 facets)

nodes.label: Flat measure in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A flat codimension-one measure in H^n is a constant multiple of Koranyi Hausdorff measure restricted to a (2n+1)-dimensional homogeneous subgroup. The paper's main density result says that measures with (2n+1)-density have only such tangents.

evidence.quote: measures with $(2n + 1)$-density have only flat tangents

nodes.label: Flat metric cone
nodes.summary: A metric cone whose regular part is flat. The paper proves tangent or asymptotic cones in the relevant collapsed settings are flat cones with dimensions one through four.

nodes.label: Flat orbifold T^3/Z2
aliases: quotient of a flat 3-torus by an involution
nodes.summary: The three-dimensional collapsed K3 limit space obtained as a flat three-torus quotient by the standard involution. In the compact K3 case the renormalized limit measure is proportional to Hausdorff measure on this orbifold.

nodes.label: Flat plane mean-curvature-flow model
nodes.summary: The static multiplicity-one plane model appearing as the lowest-entropy ancient low-entropy flow and as the regular tangent-flow case.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Flat Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian manifold with zero Riemannian curvature. In the main two-dimensional result, the target surface is forced to be flat.

evidence.quote: Then the manifold M is flat

nodes.label: Flat Riemannian metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric with vanishing curvature tensor; in the paper it is the rigidity alternative for nonnegative scalar curvature.

evidence.quote: the Riemann curvature tensor of  $g$  vanishes at each point in  $\Omega$

nodes.label: Flat round ball
nodes.summary: A compact submanifold equal to a Euclidean n-ball contained in an affine n-plane. In the equality proof it has the form {x in P : lambda |x-p| <= 1}.

nodes.label: Flat special Kähler cone C_beta
nodes.summary: A flat two-dimensional cone with cone angle parameter beta carrying a natural special Kähler structure and rotation monodromy. These cones model tangent cones of singular special Kähler metrics and enter ALG model ends.

nodes.label: Focusing waves from boundary control
nodes.summary: Boundary-controlled distributional wave solutions whose Cauchy data on a time slice are supported at a single point, typically as a delta distribution in the time derivative. These focused waves localize the inverse identity at a point.

nodes.label: Foliated corona decomposition
nodes.summary: A foliated corona decomposition is a multiscale hierarchical partition of an intrinsic Lipschitz graph into foliation-adapted pseudoquad pieces, with controlled number and size. It encodes the anisotropic texture of the graph and is the paper's main structural method.

evidence.quote: foliated corona decomposition, which is a multi-scale hierarchical partition of the surface
Crucially, we can control the number and size of these pieces

nodes.label: Foliated patchwork
nodes.summary: A foliated patchwork is a compatible collection of pseudoquads equipped with the characteristic-curve foliation structure needed to assemble local graph pieces.

nodes.label: Foliation of cone sides by anisotropic minimizers
aliases: anisotropic minimizing foliation of C11 sides
nodes.summary: The dilations of a radial minimizing leaf and its reflection foliate the two sides of C_11 by A_Psi-minimizing hypersurfaces. This foliation is the starting point for constructing barriers for the entire graph problem.

evidence.quote: foliation of each side of C11 by minimizers of A_Psi

nodes.label: Foliation of Euclidean three-space by planes
nodes.summary: A decomposition of Euclidean three-space into parallel planar leaves. It is the limiting object in the curvature blow-up alternative for sequences of H-disks.

evidence.quote: The surfaces M_n converge to a foliation of \mathbb{R}^3 by planes
away from the line containing the origin and orthogonal to the planes in the foliation

nodes.label: Forward blow-up of an outermost flow
nodes.summary: A subsequential limit obtained by parabolically rescaling a flow forward from a conical singular point. The paper identifies these limits for outermost flows with the outermost flows or expanders of the model cone.

evidence.quote: forward blow-ups (the (subsequential) weak limits of $\lambda^{2}M(\lambda^{-1}t)$ as $\lambda\to\infty$)
the outermost flows have forward blowup at $\mathbf{0}$ equal to the outermost flows of the cone $\mathcal{C}$

nodes.label: Forward rescaled mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A rescaling of a smooth mean curvature flow used to study behavior emerging from a singular point. Under this rescaled evolution, expanders are stationary points.

evidence.quote: Given a (smooth) mean curvature flow ... one obtains a solution to forward rescaled mean curvature flow by considering the rescaling
Note that expanders are the stationary points of this evolution.

nodes.label: Fourth-order cscK PDE
nodes.summary: The analytic feature that the cscK condition is a fourth-order PDE for the Kähler potential. This explains why standard maximum-principle and Ricci-bound methods for Kähler-Einstein metrics do not directly apply.

evidence.quote: cscK metrics which satisfy a 4th order PDE

nodes.label: Fractal uncertainty principle
nodes.summary: An uncertainty estimate preventing simultaneous concentration of a function and its transform near suitably fractal or porous sets; it is a central analytic input in the proof.

nodes.label: Fractal Venetian blinds surface construction
nodes.summary: A multiscale intrinsic-graph construction with anisotropic texture whose features align along a one-dimensional foliation. It supplies examples and sharpness phenomena for the vertical inequality.

evidence.quote: with features of many different scales that line up along a 1-dimensional foliation

nodes.label: Frankel property for minimal hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: A Riemannian manifold has this property when any two closed embedded minimal hypersurfaces intersect each other.

nodes.label: Fraser-Li first Steklov eigenvalue conjecture
aliases: sigma_1=1 conjecture for free boundary minimal hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: The conjectural property that the coordinate-function Steklov value sigma=1 is the first nonzero Steklov eigenvalue for compact properly embedded free boundary minimal hypersurfaces in the unit ball.

evidence.quote: sigma = 1 is actually equal to ... sigma_1(Omega)

nodes.label: Fredholm theory for Dirac boundary value problems
aliases: Fredholm theory for Dirac operators on manifolds with boundary
nodes.summary: Fredholm theory for first-order elliptic Dirac boundary value problems, used here with deformation invariance of the Fredholm index to obtain nontrivial harmonic spinors satisfying an elliptic local boundary condition.

evidence.quote: To prove the existence of an  $m$ -tuple of spinors with these properties, we use the Fredholm theory from [12] together with the deformation invariance of the Fredholm index.

nodes.label: free-boundary Almgren-Pitts min-max theory
nodes.summary: An extension of Almgren-Pitts min-max theory to compact manifolds with boundary, producing almost properly embedded free boundary minimal hypersurfaces from relative-cycle sweepouts.

nodes.label: Free boundary harmonic map
aliases: harmonic map to the unit ball with free boundary
nodes.summary: A smooth map from a compact Riemannian manifold with boundary to the unit ball whose boundary maps to the sphere, is harmonic in the interior, and has normal derivative perpendicular to the boundary sphere. Its components equivalently solve weighted Robin or Steklov eigenvalue problems.

nodes.label: free boundary minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A minimal hypersurface in a manifold with boundary whose own boundary, when present, lies on the ambient boundary and satisfies the free boundary variational condition.

nodes.label: Free boundary minimal submanifold in the unit ball
aliases: free boundary minimal surface in the unit ball
nodes.summary: A submanifold of the Euclidean unit ball whose boundary lies on the sphere and which is equivalently area-critical with boundary constrained to the sphere, minimal and orthogonal at the boundary, or has coordinate functions solving the Steklov problem with eigenvalue 1.

evidence.quote: vanishing mean curvature and meets partial B^m orthogonally

nodes.label: Frequency envelope for Sobolev energy
nodes.summary: A slowly varying l^2 sequence controlling the H^s size of Littlewood-Paley pieces of a function. It tracks energy transfer between dyadic shells and is used for convergence, continuous dependence, and uniqueness.

evidence.quote: a very useful device that tracks the evolution of the energy of solutions between dyadic energy shells
We say that $\{c_{k}\}_{k\geq 0}\in\ell^{2}$ is a frequency envelope for a function $u$ in $H^{s}$

nodes.label: Frequency function for multi-valued blow-ups
nodes.summary: A scale-dependent quotient measuring the homogeneity order of a multi-valued or coarse blow-up. The paper uses it in monotonicity and stratification arguments for branch sets.

evidence.quote: Squeeze identity and frequency monotonicity for generalised-  $C^1$  coarse blow-ups

evidence.section: Appendix A. Hausdorff dimension bound for the branch set of coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Full support for semiclassical measures on Anosov surfaces
nodes.summary: Every semiclassical measure associated with the Laplacian on an Anosov surface has support equal to the whole relevant cosphere bundle.

nodes.label: Fully nonlinear elliptic a priori estimates
nodes.summary: Uniform elliptic estimates used to close the continuity paths for the generalized J-equation and perturbed dHYM equation. In the dHYM proof the paper invokes Székelyhidi's estimates, ellipticity, concavity, and Evans-Krylov regularity.

evidence.quote: It will be achieved by Székelyhidi’s estimates
The Evans-Krylov estimate requires the uniform ellipticity and concavity

nodes.label: Fully nonlinear Kähler eigenvalue equation
nodes.summary: The paper studies normalized equations of the form $f(\lambda[h_\varphi])=e^F$, with $\sup_X\varphi=0$ and admissible eigenvalues in $\Gamma$. This class contains the complex Monge-Ampère equation as a special case.

nodes.label: Fundamental domain for a group action
nodes.summary: For a group action on Omega, a closed connected subset W whose group translates cover Omega and whose distinct translates have disjoint interiors.

evidence.section: 7 First Steklov eigenvalue of free boundary minimal surfaces

nodes.label: Fundamental group
nodes.summary: The group of homotopy classes of based loops in a path-connected topological space or manifold.

nodes.label: Futaki invariant on the central torus
nodes.summary: A linear invariant on N_R, defined analytically using the Hamiltonian of the vector field, whose vanishing on N_R is part of the valuative criterion.

evidence.section: 2.3 Analytic criterion for the existence under group actions

nodes.label: Future completion of a metric flow
nodes.summary: An extension X* of an H-concentrated metric flow over the future-completed time set, adding limiting time-slices so that the extension is future continuous at newly added times and has full support there. It is unique up to flow isometry under the paper's hypotheses.

nodes.label: Future-continuous metric flow
nodes.summary: A metric flow whose time-slices are continuous when approached from later times, equivalently through the continuity of variance-type integrals for conjugate heat flows. Future-continuous representatives make metric flow pair limits uniquely defined at all intended times.

nodes.label: G-coercivity of an energy functional
nodes.summary: For F equal to the Ding or Mabuchi energy, G-coercivity means F is bounded below by a positive multiple of the torus-reduced analytic J functional, up to an additive constant, on K-invariant finite-energy potentials.

evidence.section: 2.3 Analytic criterion for the existence under group actions

nodes.label: G-equivariant test configuration
nodes.summary: A test configuration for a polarized log pair equipped with a G-action commuting with the C* action and restricting to the original G-action on the product over C*.

evidence.quote: G acts ... such that the action of G commutes with the C*-action

nodes.label: G-invariant divisorial valuation
nodes.summary: A divisorial valuation over X invariant under the action of G; the valuative criterion quantifies over these valuations.

evidence.quote: G-invariant divisorial valuation v over X

nodes.label: G-uniform Ding-stability
nodes.summary: The Ding analogue of G-uniform K-stability, replacing the non-Archimedean Mabuchi functional by the non-Archimedean Ding functional in the reduced J-norm inequality.

evidence.quote: If one replace the MNA invariant by DNA

nodes.label: G-uniform K-stability
nodes.summary: A group-reduced uniform K-stability condition requiring the non-Archimedean Mabuchi functional of every G-equivariant test configuration to dominate a positive multiple of the reduced non-Archimedean J-norm.

evidence.quote: called G-uniformly K-stable if there exists gamma > 0

nodes.label: G-vertical mean curvature of nilradical orbits
nodes.summary: Under additional completely solvable Einstein-solvmanifold assumptions, the mean curvature vector of the nilradical orbits lies tangent to the larger G-orbits.

evidence.quote: mean curvature vector N of the N-orbits is G-vertical
evidence.section: The mean curvature vector of the N-orbits is G-vertical

nodes.label: Γ(1,q) billiard orbit class
nodes.summary: The class of closed billiard trajectories with winding number 1 and q reflections off the boundary.

nodes.label: Gamma structural condition for Kähler eigenvalue operators
nodes.summary: A structural hypothesis on the fully nonlinear operator, recorded in the excerpt only through the paper's reference to a structural condition and constants depending on $\gamma$. The exact inequality is not visible in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: fully non-linear equations satisfying a structural condition

nodes.label: Gauge-invariant Jacobian of an abelian Higgs pair
nodes.summary: A two-form J(u,nabla) satisfying J(u_epsilon,nabla_epsilon)-omega_epsilon = d<nabla_epsilon u_epsilon, i u_epsilon> and the pointwise bound |J| <= e_epsilon. It connects curvature currents to energy measures in the limiting cycle argument.

nodes.label: Gaussian area F-functional
nodes.summary: The Gaussian weighted area functional for which self-shrinkers are critical points under compactly supported deformations, translations, and dilations.

nodes.label: Gaussian-area index of a self-shrinker
nodes.summary: The number of negative eigenvalues of the self-shrinker stability operator, equivalently the dimension of the unstable space for Gaussian area. It gives the parameter count for the family of ancient rescaled flows.

nodes.label: Gaussian beam along a null geodesic
nodes.summary: A high-frequency approximate solution to the wave equation of the form exp(iλφ)A_λ concentrating near an inextendible null geodesic. The paper uses such beams to produce controlled solutions with prescribed point value and leading gradient.

evidence.quote: concentrate on an inextendible null geodesic

nodes.label: Gaussian cluster isoperimetric problem
nodes.summary: The variational problem of finding q-clusters with a prescribed Gaussian measure vector that minimize total Gaussian perimeter. The cases q=2, q=3, and general q are referred to as single-bubble, double-bubble, and multi-bubble problems.

evidence.quote: The isoperimetric problem for q-clusters consists of identifying those clusters Omega of prescribed Gaussian measure gamma(Omega)=v... which minimize the total Gaussian perimeter
the case q = 3 is called the double-bubble problem, and the case of general q is referred to as the multi-bubble problem

nodes.label: Gaussian density of a Brakke flow
nodes.summary: The limit at a space-time point of the backward heat-kernel weighted mass of a Brakke flow. It detects regularity and is upper semicontinuous by monotonicity.

nodes.label: Gaussian measure vector of a cluster
nodes.summary: The Gaussian measure of a q-cluster is the vector of Gaussian measures of its cells, lying in the (q-1)-dimensional probability simplex.

evidence.quote: The Gaussian measure of a cluster is defined as

nodes.label: Gaussian multi-bubble minimizer uniqueness
nodes.summary: For 2 <= q <= n+1 and prescribed positive Gaussian cell measures, simplicial Gaussian clusters uniquely minimize total Gaussian perimeter among q-clusters, up to null sets.

evidence.quote: We establish the Gaussian Multi-Bubble Conjecture
simplicial clusters are the unique isoperimetric minimizers (up to null-sets)

nodes.label: Gaussian q-cluster
nodes.summary: A q-cluster is a q-tuple of pairwise disjoint Borel cells in R^n, each of finite Gaussian perimeter, whose union covers R^n up to a Gaussian null set. The cells are not required to be connected.

evidence.quote: A q-cluster Omega = (Omega_1, ..., Omega_q) is a q-tuple of Borel subsets Omega_i subset R^n called cells
pairwise disjoint, P_gamma(Omega_i) < infinity for each i, and gamma(R^n setminus union Omega_i)=0

nodes.label: Gaussian weighted cylindrical energy estimate
nodes.summary: An energy-estimate method using the cylindrical Gaussian weight exp(-xi^2/4) to control functions such as H and its xi-derivative in rescaled cylindrical variables.

nodes.label: Gaussian-weighted Hausdorff measure
nodes.summary: The k-dimensional Gaussian-weighted Hausdorff measure is gamma^k = e^{-W(x)} H^k, where H^k is k-dimensional Hausdorff measure and W is the Gaussian potential.

evidence.quote: if H^k denotes the k-dimensional Hausdorff measure, let gamma^k denote its Gaussian-weighted counterpart

nodes.label: Gaussian-weighted perimeter
nodes.summary: For a Borel set U in R^n, the Gaussian-weighted perimeter is the variational weighted divergence supremum over compactly supported smooth vector fields of norm at most one. For sufficiently regular sets it agrees with the Gaussian-weighted boundary measure gamma^{n-1}(partial U).

evidence.quote: The Gaussian-weighted (Euclidean) perimeter of a Borel set U subset R^n is defined as
For nice sets... P_gamma(U) is known to agree with gamma^{n - 1}(partial U)

nodes.label: Generalised $C^{1,\alpha}$ $Q$-valued graph
nodes.summary: A $Q$-valued graphical representation with $C^{1,\alpha}$ control in a generalized sense compatible with branching. It is the local regularity conclusion near density-$Q$ planar tangent cones.

evidence.quote: the graph of a  $\frac{p}{2}$ -valued function with  $C^{1,\alpha}$  regularity in a certain generalised sense

nodes.label: Generalised-$C^1$ coarse blow-up
nodes.summary: A coarse blow-up with generalized $C^1$ regularity, used as the setting for squeeze identities and frequency monotonicity in the presence of branching.

evidence.quote: Squeeze identity and frequency monotonicity for generalised-  $C^1$  coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Generalized area of a sphere map
nodes.summary: The normalized integral of $\sqrt{\det(\nabla_Tu^t\nabla_Tu)}$, representing the area produced by a Sobolev sphere map. It is the middle quantity in Wente's isoperimetric inequality for mappings.

nodes.label: Generalized conformal sphere map
aliases: weak conformal map from the sphere
nodes.summary:
A Sobolev map $u:\mathbb{S}^{n-1}	o\mathbb{R}^n$ whose tangential metric tensor is a scalar multiple of the identity almost everywhere: $(
abla_Tu)^t
abla_Tu=|
abla_Tu|^2 I_x/(n-1)$. Equivalently, its principal stretches coincide almost everywhere.

nodes.label: Generalized cylinder self-shrinker
nodes.summary: A self-shrinker of the form R^{n-k} x S^k(sqrt(2k)). These include planes, spheres, and cylinders and model the generic spherical and cylindrical singularities.

nodes.label: Generalized J-equation with volume term
nodes.summary: A strengthened PDE used in the proof: tr_{ωφ}χ + f χ^n/ωφ^n = c, together with an integral condition on f. The original J-equation is recovered by taking f=0 in the relevant argument.

evidence.quote: Theorem 1.1 is a corollary ... by choosing f = 0

nodes.label: Generalized Pollicott-Ruelle resonant forms at zero
nodes.summary: Generalized zero-resonant differential forms, corresponding to generalized eigenspaces rather than only kernels of the flow generator acting on forms.

evidence.quote: generalized Pollicott-Ruelle resonant differential forms at 0

nodes.label: Generalized scalar-curvature type equation
nodes.summary: A coupled system consisting of a complex Monge-Ampere equation for omega_phi^n and a Laplacian equation Delta_phi F = -f + tr_phi eta. It contains cscK and twisted cscK equations as special cases.

nodes.label: Generalized Smale Conjecture for 3-manifolds
nodes.summary: For a compact connected smooth 3-manifold carrying a constant sectional curvature metric of curvature plus or minus one, the inclusion of its isometry group into its diffeomorphism group is a homotopy equivalence.

nodes.label: Generalized soap bubble
nodes.summary: A surface or hypersurface that is stationary for a prescribed mean curvature functional.

evidence.quote: generalized soap bubbles—surfaces that are stationary for prescribed-mean-curvature functionals

nodes.label: Generalized uniqueness for compatible Calabi-Yau metrics
aliases: bijective asymptotic-cone map for compatible metrics
nodes.summary: The expected bijectivity of the map sending a compatible Calabi-Yau metric with fixed valuation to the Calabi-Yau cone metric obtained as its appropriate weighted rescaled limit.

evidence.quote: define a natural map ... by taking the (appropriate) rescaled limit of the Kähler form
The map is well-defined and is bijective.

nodes.label: Generalized volume minimization for K-semistable valuations
nodes.summary: A comparison principle from valuation theory saying that the normalized volume associated to the natural valuation bounds the relevant metric tangent-cone volume density. The paper combines it with Bishop-Gromov monotonicity to force equality.

evidence.quote: by ... the generalized volume minimization ... we know the latter is not bigger than kappa(C)
by the minimizing property of K-semistable valuations ... the volume densities satisfy

nodes.label: Generic avoidance of asymptotically conical and non-spherical compact shrinker singularities
nodes.summary: The paper's perturbative genericity mechanism: after small smooth initial perturbations, flows avoid asymptotically conical and non-spherical compact shrinker singularities, except for specified multiplicity or cylindrical-end frontier cases in dimension three.

nodes.label: Generic conformal metric perturbation
nodes.summary: A conformal perturbation whose conformal factor belongs to an open dense subset of smooth real-valued functions on the manifold.

nodes.label: Generic low-entropy flow disappearing in a round point
nodes.summary: For generic closed low-entropy hypersurfaces under the paper's entropy hypotheses, every relevant unit-regular Brakke flow is smooth until its final singularity, which is a round shrinking sphere.

nodes.label: Multiplicity-one spherical or cylindrical tangent-flow singularity
nodes.summary: A singularity whose tangent flows are multiplicity-one shrinking spheres, cylinders, or planes. This is the good singularity class used to define the first non-generic time.

nodes.label: Genus monotonicity before the first non-generic time
nodes.summary: For smooth times before the first non-generic time of a cyclic unit-regular Brakke flow from a closed embedded surface in R3, the genus of the level-set-flow slice is non-increasing.

evidence.section: 11 The first non-generic time for flows in R3

nodes.label: Genus-zero closed immersed surface
nodes.summary: A closed immersed surface whose genus is zero. This is the paper's convention for the word sphere.

evidence.quote: by a sphere, we mean a closed immersed surface of genus zero

nodes.label: Geodesic beam
nodes.summary: A microlocal piece of a quasimode concentrated along a geodesic; the method decomposes quasimodes into such pieces and controls averages using their $L^2$ norms.

evidence.quote: The key technique used in our study of the spectral projector is that of geodesic beams
decomposing the quasimodes into geodesic beams and controlling the averages in terms of the $L^2$ norms of these beams

nodes.label: Geodesic flow
nodes.summary: The classical flow moving unit tangent or cotangent vectors along geodesics of a Riemannian manifold.

evidence.quote: we impose dynamical conditions on the periodicity properties of the geodesic flow $\varphi_t$

nodes.label: Geodesic flow on the sphere bundle
nodes.summary: The flow on the unit tangent sphere bundle obtained by following unit-speed geodesics; its generator is the vector field X on M = S\Sigma.

nodes.label: Geodesic loop basis decomposition
nodes.summary: A decomposition of geodesic loops into a basis, used to organize the loop data that determines the fibration at infinity.

nodes.label: Geodesic stability
nodes.summary: A stability condition for a Kähler class requiring positivity, or nonnegativity in the semistable case, of the asymptotic U-invariant along every locally finite energy geodesic ray. It is formulated using K-energy increments along rays.

nodes.label: Geodesic-submanifold arithmeticity criterion
aliases: arithmeticity from infinitely many maximal geodesic submanifolds
nodes.summary: For n at least two, a finite-volume complex hyperbolic n-manifold with infinitely many maximal totally geodesic submanifolds of real dimension at least two is arithmetic.

evidence.quote: Suppose that n >= 2 and M is a finite volume complex hyperbolic n-manifold containing infinitely many maximal totally geodesic submanifolds

nodes.label: Finiteness of geodesic subspaces in nonarithmetic locally symmetric spaces
nodes.summary: A nonarithmetic finite-volume irreducible locally symmetric space of noncompact type contains only finitely many maximal geodesic subspaces of real dimension at least two, and only finitely many nonarithmetic such geodesic subspaces.

evidence.quote: contains only finitely many maximal geodesic subspaces of real dimension at least 2
contains only finitely many nonarithmetic geodesic subspaces of real dimension at least 2

nodes.label: Geodesic Voronoi tessellation
nodes.summary: The partition of M associated to S^epsilon, where V_p^epsilon consists of points no farther from p than from any other point of S^epsilon.

nodes.label: Geodesic X-ray transform on symmetric 2-tensors
nodes.summary: The integral transform that assigns to a symmetric 2-tensor its integrals or contractions along geodesics of a Riemannian metric. Its analysis is the key analytic tool in the paper.

evidence.quote: The key tool is the analysis of the geodesic X-ray transform on 2-tensors

nodes.label: Geodesically complete metric space
nodes.summary: A metric space in which every geodesic segment is contained in a complete local geodesic.

evidence.quote: every geodesic segment is contained in a complete local geodesic

nodes.label: Geometric boundary of a nonpositively curved space
nodes.summary: The boundary at infinity of a complete nonpositively curved space, used to record asymptotic classes of geodesic rays and fixed points of isometries.

evidence.quote: where  $\widetilde{M}(\infty)$  is the geometric boundary of  $\widetilde{M}$

nodes.label: Geometric closeness at scale
nodes.summary: A pointed Riemannian manifold is close to another at a scale when a pointed diffeomorphism from a large ball makes the scaled pullback metric close in a controlled C^k norm. The canonical neighborhood assumption uses this to compare regions to kappa-solutions.

nodes.label: Geometric group action
nodes.summary: An isometric group action that is properly discontinuous and cocompact.

evidence.quote: called geometric if it is properly discontinuous and cocompact

nodes.label: Geometric square function
nodes.summary: A geometric square function aggregates a multiscale geometric deviation quantity, typically with an L^2-in-scale integral, to measure regularity or rectifiability of sets. The Carleson ε² square function and β-number square functions are examples in the paper's setting.

evidence.quote: geometric square functions which measure the regularity of sets through a multi-scale analysis
Carleson ε^2-square function is given by E(x)^2 := ∫_0^1 ε(x,r)^2 dr/r
left-hand side replaced by an  $L_2(dt / t)$  norm

nodes.label: Geometric transformation method
nodes.summary: A method for transforming and normalizing harmonic splitting data on neck regions in order to control degeneration of harmonic functions.

evidence.quote: a geometric transformation theorem, which will allow us to control the degeneration of harmonic functions on these neck regions

nodes.label: Gibbons-Hawking ansatz
nodes.summary: A construction of four-dimensional hyperkähler metrics with circle symmetry from harmonic-function monopole data on a three-dimensional base. The paper uses it as a model-building tool.

evidence.quote: The Gibbons-Hawking ansatz and the model space

nodes.label: Ginzburg-Landau regularized harmonic map energy
nodes.summary: A family of energies on maps into an ambient Euclidean space obtained by adding an epsilon^{-2} potential penalizing distance from the embedded target manifold. Critical points approximate harmonic maps into the target as epsilon tends to zero.

nodes.label: Global C^{2,alpha} regularity for the Monge-Ampère second boundary problem
nodes.summary: The property that a convex solution of the second boundary value problem has a uniform C^{2,α} norm up to the boundary. In the paper this is proved under C^{1,1} convex domains and positive Hölder density.

evidence.quote: we obtain the sharp boundary $C^{2,\alpha}$ regularity when $f \in C^{\alpha}(\overline{\Omega})$ and $f > 0$

nodes.label: Global W^{2,p} regularity for the Monge-Ampère second boundary problem
nodes.summary: The property that second derivatives of the convex solution belong globally to L^p for every p≥1. The paper derives this estimate for positive continuous density on C^{1,1} convex domains.

evidence.quote: Our argument also leads to the global $W^{2,p}$ estimate for the solution.

nodes.label: Gluing with the Tian-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A gluing method using the Tian-Yau metric as a model piece in the analysis of the positive case. The excerpt identifies it as a dedicated section of the paper.

nodes.label: Graded filtration of the anticanonical section ring
nodes.summary: A decreasing, left-continuous, multiplicative, linearly bounded family of subspaces of the graded section ring of multiples of the anticanonical polarization.

evidence.quote: A filtration ... consists of a family of subspaces

nodes.label: Gradient-image boundary condition
nodes.summary: The boundary condition requiring the gradient map of the convex potential to send the source domain onto the target domain, Du(Ω)=Ω*. This is the natural or second boundary condition for the Monge-Ampère equation in quadratic optimal transport.

evidence.quote: subject to the boundary condition $D u (\Omega) = \Omega^ {*}$

nodes.label: Gradient map
nodes.summary: The map sending a point x to the gradient Du(x) of a differentiable potential u. The paper prescribes the image of the source domain under this map.

nodes.label: Caccioppoli inequality for Lipschitz graph gradients
nodes.summary: The graphical form of the anisotropic Caccioppoli estimate, bounding the L^2 oscillation of Du from a constant matrix by the height deviation of u from an affine map and the pulled-back mean curvature.

nodes.label: Graph chart h and area element A
nodes.summary: For X in R^{n x m}, M(X) stacks the identity over X, A(X) is the graph area element, and h(X) is the projection matrix onto the corresponding tangent m-plane.

evidence.quote: A(X) simply corresponds to the area element

nodes.label: Growth rate r^(1+mu) for entire anisotropic graphs
nodes.summary: For each mu in (0,1/2), the constructed graph has superlinear but subquadratic growth comparable to r^(1+mu). The rate is inherited from the homogeneous model barrier functions.

nodes.label: Graph parametric integrand F_Psi
nodes.summary: The function on gradient matrices associated to an anisotropic Grassmannian integrand by F_Psi(X)=Psi(h(X))A(X), turning graphical anisotropic energy into a parametric integral.

nodes.label: Graphical critical point of anisotropic energy
aliases: anisotropic minimal graph as a critical point
nodes.summary: A function graph whose associated hypersurface is stationary for the anisotropic parametric surface energy. In the graphical setting this is encoded by the anisotropic minimal graph equation.

evidence.quote: graph of a function u...is a critical point of A_Phi

nodes.label: Graphical rescaled flow over a self-shrinker
nodes.summary: A representation of a nearby rescaled flow as a normal graph over a fixed shrinker, reducing the flow to a scalar parabolic equation for the graph function.

nodes.label: Grassmannian of m-planes as projection matrices
nodes.summary: The paper identifies the Grassmannian of unoriented m-dimensional subspaces of R^N with symmetric idempotent N by N matrices of trace m.

evidence.quote: G(N, m) = {P ... P = Pt, P2 = P, tr(P) = m}

nodes.label: Gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: In this paper, a gravitational instanton is a complete hyperkahler four-manifold satisfying faster than quadratic curvature decay. This is the core object classified by its asymptotic end.

evidence.quote: we define a gravitational instanton as a complete, hyperkähler 4-manifold satisfying a decay condition

nodes.label: Model-end asymptotics of gravitational instantons
nodes.summary: The classification property that every nonflat gravitational instanton is asymptotic to one of the standard model ends: ALE, ALF, ALG, ALG*, ALH, or ALH*, according to the dimension and monodromy of its unique flat asymptotic cone.

evidence.quote: asymptotic to a model end at infinity

nodes.label: Greatest twisted Ricci lower bound
nodes.summary: The optimal lower bound for Ricci curvature in the twisted setting considered by the paper. The paper expresses this analytic invariant in terms of the algebro-geometric stability threshold.

evidence.quote: The stability threshold and the greatest Ricci lower bound

nodes.label: Green's-function curvature functional A
nodes.summary: The continuous function A(s)=int_{Sigma_s^*} |A_M|^2, interpreted as measuring bending of M near infinity through Green's-function level sets.

nodes.label: Green function estimate on a neck region
aliases: standard Green's function estimates on neck regions
nodes.summary: Estimates for standard Green's functions adapted to neck regions, used as analytic input for harmonic and splitting-function estimates.

evidence.quote: 5.1. Estimates on Standard Green's Functions on Neck Regions

nodes.label: Green's-function flux functional F
nodes.summary: The continuous function F(s)=int_{Sigma_s^*} |grad u|^2 measuring squared gradient flux through Green's-function level sets.

evidence.section: 2.1. Integration over level sets of the Green's function

nodes.label: Green's-function level set Sigma_s
nodes.summary: The level hypersurface Sigma_s := u^{-1}(s) of the Green's function. At regular values it is a closed connected hypersurface in M.

evidence.section: 2.1. Integration over level sets of the Green's function

nodes.label: Sharp Green's-function flux decay
nodes.summary: The estimate F(t)=O(t^2) as t approaches zero for the Green's-function flux on the stable minimal hypersurface. It is obtained by combining the A(s)-estimate and extended monotonicity formula and then absorbing an integral term.

evidence.section: 4. An extension of Munteanu-Wang's monotonicity formula

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff closeness
nodes.summary: Quantitative small Gromov-Hausdorff distance between metric balls or spaces. The paper uses it to compare RCD balls with Euclidean or half-space model balls.

evidence.quote: We will deal with the Gromov-Hausdorff (GH), measured Gromov-Hausdorff (mGH) and pointed measured Gromov-Hausdorff (pmGH) convergence.

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: A notion of convergence for metric spaces based on approximating their distances in a common metric space. The excerpt uses it for limits of Einstein manifolds and rescaled bubble limits.

evidence.quote: will converge in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff distance
nodes.summary: The Gromov-Hausdorff distance compares metric spaces by isometrically embedding them into a common metric space and taking the Hausdorff distance between the images. The paper uses it to formulate tangent-like approximation in arbitrary metric spaces.

evidence.quote: one must replace the Hausdorff distance  $d_{\mathrm{H}}$  with the Gromov-Hausdorff distance  $d_{\mathrm{GH}}$
there exist a metric space  $Z$  and isometric embeddings  $\iota_i: M_i \to Z$

nodes.label: Gromov hyperbolic metric space
nodes.summary: A coarse negative-curvature property of metric spaces expressed by thin triangles or equivalent Gromov product inequalities. The paper uses Gromov hyperbolicity as one characterization of quasiperiodic maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: characterised by a curvature condition, Gromov hyperbolicity or conformal hyperbolicity

nodes.label: Gromov stratification for rigid geometric structures
nodes.summary: A stratification method for automorphism orbits of rigid analytic geometric structures, giving open dense orbit-regular regions, locally closed orbits, closed orbits in orbit closures, and semianalytic orbit closures.

evidence.section: 2.1. Gromov's stratification and reduction to a connected, abelian group

nodes.label: Gromov-W1-Wasserstein distance
nodes.summary: A distance between metric measure spaces defined by isometrically embedding their underlying metric spaces into a common metric space and taking the W1 distance between pushed-forward measures. It is the time-slice comparison notion underlying F-distance.

nodes.label: H^1 energy constraint sphere
nodes.summary: The constraint set of functions in $H^1(\Sigma)$ whose squared $H^1$ norm equals a prescribed value $\beta$. The Moser-Trudinger functional is studied on this constraint.

nodes.label: H^1 norm on a closed surface
nodes.summary: The norm on $H^1(\Sigma)$ defined by the integral of the squared gradient plus the squared function with respect to the metric volume form.

nodes.label: H^1 Sobolev space on a closed surface
nodes.summary: The first-order Sobolev space of functions on the closed surface whose function value and gradient are square-integrable. It is the ambient space for the Moser-Trudinger and subcritical functionals.

evidence.quote: we endow the usual Sobolev space  $H^1 = H^1(\Sigma)$  with the standard norm

nodes.label: H-concentrated metric flow
nodes.summary: A metric flow satisfying the variance inequality Var(nu_x1;s,nu_x2;s) <= d_t(x1,x2)^2 + H(t-s) for all x1,x2 in the same time-slice and s <= t. This is the dimension-dependent compactness input satisfied by metric flows coming from n-dimensional super Ricci flows.

nodes.label: H-disk
nodes.summary: An H-surface that is homeomorphic to a closed unit disk in the Euclidean plane. The main results concern sequences of compact H-disks whose boundaries diverge in Euclidean three-space.

evidence.quote: We will call an H-surface an H-disk if the H-surface is homeomorphic to a closed unit disk in the Euclidean plane
sequence of H_n-disks in \mathbb{R}^3 containing the origin

nodes.label: H-surface
nodes.summary: An oriented surface immersed in Euclidean three-space that is embedded, connected, and has non-negative constant mean curvature H.

evidence.quote: we will call an oriented surface M immersed in \mathbb{R}^3 an H-surface if it is embedded, connected and it has non-negative constant mean curvature H

nodes.label: Half-harmonic 1-form
nodes.summary: A 1-form satisfying the paper's half-harmonic elliptic condition, used in a Liouville-type analysis. The excerpt names the object but does not give the defining equation.

nodes.label: Half-line exterior derivative method
nodes.summary: The method of decomposing forms in the v-direction and using mapping properties of d on the b-metric half-line to build homotopy operators. It replaces Zucker's warped-product formula for QFC local models.

nodes.label: Half-space approximation for extended monotone sets
aliases: extended-monotone sets are close to half-spaces
nodes.summary: Extended monotonicity forces quantitative closeness to a Heisenberg half-space. This stability principle supplies flat models for the corona decomposition argument.

evidence.quote: 11. Extended-monotone sets are close to half-spaces 157

nodes.label: Half-space epsilon-regularity for boundary balls
nodes.summary: A local regularity principle: if an RCD ball is sufficiently close to the half-space model, then boundary points appear and the boundary in a smaller ball has Ahlfors regular, rectifiable, and topological structure. This is the paper's key local input.

evidence.quote: if a ball B_2(p) subset X is sufficiently close to a half space ... then B_1(p) is biHolder to an open set of R^N_+
it holds that ∂X cap B_1(p) is not empty
evidence.section: 1.2 An epsilon-regularity theorem for top dimensional singularities

nodes.label: Halfspace in Gaussian space
nodes.summary: A Euclidean halfspace regarded as a Borel set in Gaussian space; halfspaces are the minimizers in the classical Gaussian isoperimetric inequality.

evidence.quote: halfspaces are in fact the unique minimizers

nodes.label: Hamilton Harnack estimate for Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A differential Harnack estimate for Ricci flow that, in the excerpt, yields monotonicity R_t >= 0 and contributes to curvature bounds for blow-up limits.

nodes.label: Hamilton-Ivey pinching estimate
nodes.summary: A three-dimensional Ricci flow curvature pinching estimate used to infer nonnegative sectional curvature for suitable blow-up limits.

evidence.quote: Hamilton-Ivey pinching estimate tells us that any such blow-up limit has nonnegative sectional curvature

nodes.label: Harder-Narasimhan filtration
nodes.summary: The canonical filtration of a torsion-free sheaf by subsheaves whose successive quotients are semistable with strictly decreasing slopes. It is used to define the spread Phi and optimal algebraic tangent cones.

nodes.label: Harder-Narasimhan-Seshadri graded sheaf
nodes.summary: The associated graded sheaf obtained by refining HN semistable factors by Seshadri filtrations into stable factors. Its double dual and quotient by the graded sheaf define the algebraic invariants matched with analytic tangent cones.

nodes.label: Hardt-Simon foliation
nodes.summary: For an area-minimizing hypercone with isolated singularity, two smooth area-minimizing hypersurfaces asymptotic to the cone have dilations that foliate the two complementary components of the cone.

evidence.quote: the associated family of dilations  $\cup_{\lambda} \lambda S_{\pm}$  is called the Hardt-Simon foliation

nodes.label: Hardy-field monotonicity for saddle transition maps
nodes.summary: A Hardy field containing transition maps of saddle planar analytic vector fields implies eventual monotonicity for algebraic combinations of those transition maps.

nodes.label: Harmonic 1-form
nodes.summary: A differential 1-form annihilated by the Hodge Laplacian. The paper relates such forms on the base manifold to pushforwards of products of resonant and coresonant forms.

nodes.label: Harmonic degree of a harmonic sphere or projective plane
nodes.summary: For a linearly full harmonic map S^2 -> S^{2m}, the integer d defined by E(Phi)=4*pi*d; for RP^2 -> S^{2m}, E(Phi)=2*pi*d. This degree is central in the index bounds and the RP^2 eigenvalue argument.

nodes.label: Harmonic epsilon-splitting function
nodes.summary: A collection of harmonic functions that approximately realizes Euclidean coordinate functions and detects an approximate product splitting.

nodes.label: Harmonic extension across perforations
nodes.summary: Given a Steklov eigenfunction on Omega^epsilon, extend it to all of M by keeping it on the perforated domain and solving the harmonic equation inside the removed balls.

evidence.quote: equal to u_k on ... and harmonic in T^epsilon

nodes.label: Harmonic extension to the unit ball
nodes.summary: The componentwise harmonic extension of boundary data $u$ from $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$ into the unit ball $B_1$. It is used to rewrite signed volume as a bulk Jacobian integral and to prove estimates via spherical harmonics.

nodes.label: Harmonic function
nodes.summary: A function with vanishing Laplacian, used here as the analytic input for constructing splitting maps on neck regions.

evidence.quote: control the degeneration of harmonic functions on these neck regions

evidence.section: 2. The Green's function on a stable minimal hypersurface

nodes.label: Harmonic map between Riemannian manifolds
nodes.summary: A map between Riemannian manifolds is harmonic when it is a critical point of the Dirichlet energy on the space of maps.

evidence.quote: critical point for the Dirichlet energy

nodes.label: Harmonic map energy functional
nodes.summary: For a map Phi from a Riemannian surface, the energy is E_g(Phi)=1/2 integral_M |dPhi|_g^2 dv_g. Harmonic maps are critical points of this functional.

nodes.label: Harmonic map heat flow between Ricci flow spacetimes
nodes.summary: A heat-flow equation for maps between evolving time-slices, adapted to Ricci flow spacetimes by time-equivariance. The inverse of the comparison map is required to evolve by this flow.

nodes.label: Metric induced by a harmonic map
nodes.summary: For a harmonic map Phi, the associated metric is g_Phi=(1/2)|nabla Phi|_g^2 g. For weakly conformal maps it agrees with the pullback of the round metric and may have conical singularities at branch points.

nodes.label: Singular set of a harmonic map
nodes.summary: The subset of the domain where a weak or stationary harmonic map fails to be smooth. The paper estimates its Hausdorff dimension or codimension under finite-index and target hypotheses.

evidence.quote: smooth away from a singular set
evidence.section: 2.3 Partial regularity for the min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Harmonic map to a Euclidean sphere
nodes.summary: A critical point of the harmonic map energy whose target is a round Euclidean sphere S^n. In coordinates it satisfies Delta_g Phi = |nabla Phi|_g^2 Phi, componentwise.

nodes.label: Harmonic p-form
nodes.summary: A p-form annihilated by the Hodge Laplacian; in this paper's convention it is equivalently closed and divergence-free.

evidence.quote: a p-form ω is harmonic if and only if it is closed and divergence free

nodes.label: Harmonic radius
nodes.summary: A scale on which harmonic coordinates give controlled metric regularity; it appears with epsilon-regularity in the preliminary tools for bounded Ricci curvature.

nodes.label: Harmonic sequence of a projective harmonic map
nodes.summary: For a harmonic map from a Riemann surface to complex projective space, one iteratively takes the (1,0) and (0,1) derivative line-subbundle images to obtain line bundles L_p and maps between them. For sphere-valued harmonic maps, this sequence encodes the twistor lift and the Jacobi-field computations.

nodes.label: Harmonic spinor frame on a smooth domain
nodes.summary: An $m$-tuple of harmonic spinors on a smooth approximating domain, where $m$ is the dimension of the flat Euclidean spinor representation; the tuple is normalized and satisfies the chosen local boundary condition.

evidence.quote: For each  $l$ , we construct a non-trivial  $m$ -tuple of harmonic spinors  $s^{(l)} = (s_1^{(l)}, \ldots, s_m^{(l)})$  on the domain  $\Omega_{\lambda_l}$

nodes.label: Hat tensor construction
nodes.summary: For a tensor T, hat T is the Λ²V-valued tensor that records all infinitesimal so(V)-actions on T by duality against L∈so(V).

evidence.quote: For T∈T^(0,k)(V) define hat T∈Λ²V⊗T^(0,k)(V) implicitly
The information on how all L∈so(V) interact with a fixed T ... can be encoded in a tensor hat T

nodes.label: Hausdorff convergence of Higgs level sets
nodes.summary: For every 0 <= delta < 1, the low-modulus sets {|u_epsilon| <= delta} converge in the Hausdorff topology to the support of the limiting energy measure. This identifies the geometric support of energy concentration with the limiting Higgs zero set.

evidence.quote: For any 0 <= delta < 1 we have spt(mu)=lim_{epsilon -> 0}{|u_epsilon| <= delta}, in the Hausdorff topology
evidence.section: 6.2 Integrality of the limit varifold and convergence of level sets

nodes.label: Hausdorff dimension
nodes.summary: The dimension notion associated with Hausdorff measures, used to bound the size of singular strata.

evidence.quote: the Hausdorff dimension of  $S^k$  satisfies  $\dim S^k \leq k$

nodes.label: Hausdorff dimension bound for a branch set
nodes.summary: A stratification conclusion that the branch set of a coarse blow-up has bounded Hausdorff dimension, derived from frequency information and approximation by $(n-2)$-dimensional planes.

evidence.quote: Hausdorff dimension bound for the branch set of coarse blow-ups
the proof can then be concluded by applying [Sim96, Section 3.4, Lemma 3]
evidence.section: Appendix A. Hausdorff dimension bound for the branch set of coarse blow-ups

nodes.label: Hausdorff dimension bound for singular strata
nodes.summary: The classical property that the k-th singular stratum has Hausdorff dimension at most k, with no codimension-one singular stratum in the noncollapsed lower-Ricci setting.

evidence.quote: the Hausdorff dimension of  $S^k$  satisfies  $\dim S^k \leq k$  and  $S = S^{n-2}$

nodes.label: Hausdorff distance
nodes.summary: The Hausdorff distance measures how close two subsets of a common metric space are by the smallest scale at which each lies in the other's neighborhood.

evidence.quote: Here  $d_{\mathrm{H}}$  denotes the Hausdorff distance between two subsets of  $\mathbb{R}^m$ .

nodes.label: Hausdorff measure
nodes.summary: The k-dimensional Hausdorff measure, used for almost-everywhere statements and measure bounds on strata.

evidence.quote: uniform Hausdorff measure estimates  $H^{n - 4}(\mathcal{S}(X) \cap B_1) < C(n, \nu)$
Here H^1 denotes the one-dimensional Hausdorff measure.
Except for a set of zero H^1-measure
H^n for the n-dimensional Hausdorff measure on a metric space, respectively.
throughout this paper,  $\mathcal{H}^n$  denotes the  $n$ -dimensional Hausdorff measure

nodes.label: H^n volume notation
nodes.summary: The n-dimensional Hausdorff volume used to measure hypersurface areas and cylindrical cross-sections throughout the width estimates.

nodes.label: Heat equation
nodes.summary: The parabolic PDE given by vanishing heat operator, here written as \partial_t v - \Delta v = 0.

nodes.label: Heat flow on a metric flow
nodes.summary: A family of functions u_t on time-slices of a metric flow such that u_t(x) is obtained by integrating earlier data u_s against the conjugate heat kernel nu_x;s. It represents the forward heat equation in the synthetic setting.

evidence.section: 3.2 (Conjugate) Heat flows on a metric flow

nodes.label: Heat kernel estimate
nodes.summary: Analytic bounds for the heat kernel on manifolds with Ricci curvature control, used as preliminary analytic input in the paper.

nodes.label: Heat kernel estimates and convergence
nodes.summary: Analytic estimates and convergence results for heat kernels on the approximating manifolds and their limits, used in entropy and Hessian estimates.

evidence.quote: 4.5. Heat kernel estimates and heat kernel convergence 437

nodes.label: Heat trace invariants
nodes.summary: Spectral invariants obtained from the heat trace expansion; in this paper they are used to force an isospectral domain to be close to a small-eccentricity ellipse in all C^n norms.

nodes.label: Hecke transform of special type
nodes.summary: A reflexive sheaf transform of an extension along a subsheaf appearing in the HN filtration on the exceptional divisor. Optimal extensions are related by such transforms up to equivalence.

nodes.label: $\mathcal{C}_{\mathbb{H}}^1$-rectifiable set
