nodes.summary: A set E in H^n is C_H^1-rectifiable when, for Koranyi H^{2n+1}-almost every point, its tangent measure is the Koranyi Hausdorff measure restricted to a (2n+1)-dimensional homogeneous subgroup and it has positive lower (2n+1)-density. This is the intrinsic codimension-one rectifiability notion used in the paper.

evidence.quote: a set $E \subseteq \mathbb{H}^n$ is said to be $\mathcal{C}_{\mathbb{H}}^1$-rectifiable if
$\operatorname {T a n} _ {2 n + 1} \left(\mathcal {H} _ {\| \cdot \|} ^ {2 n + 1} \llcorner E, x\right) = \left\{\mathcal {H} _ {\| \cdot \|} ^ {2 n + 1} \llcorner V _ {x} \right\}$

nodes.label: Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: The first Heisenberg group is the ambient sub-Riemannian group, represented here in coordinates R^3 with horizontal directions spanned by X and Y and central vertical coordinate z.

evidence.quote: measures with density in the Heisenberg groups $\mathbb{H}^n$

nodes.label: Half-space in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A Heisenberg half-space is a model monotone set bounded by a plane in exponential coordinates. Extended-monotone sets are shown to be close to such half-spaces.

evidence.quote: Extended-monotone sets are close to half-spaces

nodes.label: Horizontal line in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A horizontal line is a left translate of a one-dimensional horizontal subgroup. Such lines are the line objects in the kinematic formula and in definitions of monotonicity and non-monotonicity.

evidence.quote: 2.5. Measures on lines and the kinematic formula 95
12.4. Characteristic curves are close to lines 186

nodes.label: Heisenberg horizontal vector fields
nodes.summary: The left-invariant horizontal derivatives on the Heisenberg group are X = partial_x + (1/2)y partial_z and Y = partial_y - (1/2)x partial_z. They define the horizontal gradient and horizontal perimeter quantities used throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: $\mathsf {X} f (h) \stackrel {\text {d e f}} {=} \frac {\partial f}{\partial x} (h) + \frac {1}{2} y \frac {\partial f}{\partial z} (h)$

nodes.label: Kinematic formula for Heisenberg lines
nodes.summary: The kinematic formula is an integral-geometric identity that rewrites or estimates quantities in the Heisenberg group by integrating over horizontal lines.

evidence.quote: 2.5. Measures on lines and the kinematic formula 95
9. The kinematic formula and the proof of Proposition 7.4 148

nodes.label: Heisenberg nilmanifold
nodes.summary: A compact quotient of the three-dimensional Heisenberg group by a lattice. It is one of the non-abelian nilmanifold fiber types appearing in the collapse.

nodes.label: Heisenberg tangent measure
nodes.summary: A Heisenberg tangent measure is a weak limit of rescaled blow-ups of a measure around a point, using the Heisenberg metric structure and dilations. The paper uses h-dimensional tangent cones, especially for h = 2n+1.

evidence.quote: $\operatorname {T a n} _ {2 n + 1} \left(\mathcal {H} _ {\| \cdot \|} ^ {2 n + 1} \llcorner E, x\right)$
measures with density have only flat tangents

nodes.label: Heisenberg vertical L1 square-function inequality
aliases: vertical Sobolev inequality in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: The endpoint inequality controls the L_4(dt/t) aggregation of L_1 norms of vertical difference quotients by the L_1 norm of the horizontal derivatives Xf and Yf.

evidence.quote: Every compactly supported smooth function  $f\colon \mathbb{R}^3\to \mathbb{R}$  satisfies

nodes.label: Heisenberg vertical translation
nodes.summary: Vertical translation is motion in the central z-coordinate, h=(x,y,z) mapped to (x,y,z+t). It is the primitive operation behind the vertical difference quotient and vertical perimeter.

nodes.label: Helicoid
nodes.summary: A classical embedded minimal surface in Euclidean three-space appearing as one possible smooth local limit of H-disks in the paper's dichotomy.

evidence.quote: converge smoothly with multiplicity one or two on compact subsets of \mathbb{R}^3 to a helicoid M_{\infty} containing the origin

nodes.label: Hermitian connection
aliases: metric connection on a Hermitian line bundle
nodes.summary: A connection on a Hermitian line bundle compatible with the Hermitian metric. In the paper it is varied together with the section and has curvature F_nabla.

evidence.quote: nabla is a Hermitian connection on L with curvature F_nabla

nodes.label: Hermitian line bundle
aliases: complex line bundle with Hermitian metric
nodes.summary: A complex line bundle L -> M equipped with a Hermitian fiber metric. Its sections provide the Higgs fields in the abelian Higgs model.

evidence.quote: Given a Hermitian line bundle L -> M

nodes.label: Higgs double-well potential
nodes.summary: The nonlinear potential W(u)=1/4(1-|u|^2)^2 on the Hermitian line bundle. Its precise self-dual coefficient is important for the paper's estimates and vortex structure.

nodes.label: Higher derivative estimates for Kähler potentials
nodes.summary: Uniform bounds for derivatives of a Kähler potential beyond order zero. The paper's central estimate says such bounds follow for cscK potentials once the potential is uniformly bounded.

evidence.quote: higher order derivatives can be estimated in terms of a C^0 bound
any higher derivative estimate of φ is also uniformly bounded

nodes.label: Higher-rank CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A CAT(0) space of rank at least 2.

evidence.quote: higher rank, i.e. rank at least 2

nodes.label: Higher rank rigidity for CAT(0) spaces
nodes.summary: The rigidity principle predicting that a higher-rank CAT(0) space with a geometric group action is a Riemannian symmetric space, a Euclidean building, or splits nontrivially as a metric product.

nodes.label: Hodge Laplacian on p-forms
nodes.summary: The Hodge Laplacian acting on differential p-forms, treated here as the c=1 instance of the Lichnerowicz Laplacian.

evidence.quote: The Hodge Laplacian is a Lichnerowicz Laplacian for c=1

nodes.label: Holomorphic function construction on gravitational instantons
nodes.summary: A method for constructing holomorphic functions on gravitational instantons, especially on ALF, ALG, and ALH ends, using weighted analytic estimates. It is central to understanding the ends holomorphically.

evidence.quote: Holomorphic functions on ALF and ALG instantons

nodes.label: Holomorphic quadratic differential
nodes.summary: A holomorphic section of the square of the canonical bundle on a Riemann surface; such differentials define flat metrics with horizontal and vertical foliations.

evidence.quote: moduli space of holomorphic quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Holonomy control of geodesic loops
aliases: control of holonomy of geodesic loops
nodes.summary: Estimates or structural control for holonomy associated with geodesic loops. The paper uses this as a step in extracting the asymptotic fibration.

evidence.quote: Control of holonomy of geodesic loops

nodes.label: Dynamical type of an A'P+-holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A classification of A'P+-form sequences using the growth of alpha(D_k), beta(D_k), alpha(D_k)+beta(D_k), and whether tau_k is identically one. The types govern the local conformal dynamics analyzed in the proof.

nodes.label: Holonomy sequence for conformal transformations
nodes.summary: For a sequence of conformal transformations, a sequence p_k in P recording the fiber-direction divergence after lifting to the Cartan bundle. It is defined by convergence of f_k xhat_k p_k^{-1} in the Cartan bundle.

nodes.label: Homogeneous dimension of the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: With the Koranyi metric, H^n has Hausdorff or homogeneous dimension 2n+2 even though its underlying Euclidean manifold has dimension 2n+1. Thus 1-codimensional objects have dimension 2n+1.

evidence.quote: underlying Euclidean space $\mathbb{R}^{2n + 1}$, it has Hausdorff dimension $2n + 2$

nodes.label: Homogeneous elliptic symmetric eigenvalue operator
nodes.summary: A symmetric degree-one homogeneous function of eigenvalues, elliptic in the sense that each partial derivative with respect to an eigenvalue is positive on the admissible cone.

evidence.quote: $f(\lambda)$  is assumed to be invariant under permutations of the components of  $\lambda$

nodes.label: Homogeneous holomorphic section on an HYM cone
nodes.summary: A holomorphic section s on the cone satisfying nabla_{partial_r}s = beta r^{-1}s. Its homogeneity degree equals the degree function value of suitable limiting sections.

nodes.label: Homogeneous model barrier functions
nodes.summary: Functions on R^4 that are homogeneous of degree 1+mu, vanish on C_11, and have the perturbed leaves as level sets. They serve as model barriers before the final restacking step.

evidence.quote: serve as model super- and sub-solutions

nodes.label: Homogeneous Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A connected Riemannian manifold whose isometry group acts transitively.

evidence.quote: called homogeneous if its isometry group acts transitively

nodes.label: Homogeneous-space Ricci formula using Killing fields
aliases: algebraic Ricci formula for homogeneous spaces
nodes.summary: An algebraic formula computing Ricci curvature on a homogeneous space using Killing fields that need not lie in a reductive complement.

evidence.quote: does not require the Killing fields to be in a reductive complement
evidence.section: New algebraic formulae for the Ricci curvature of a homogeneous space

nodes.label: Homogeneous subgroup of the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A homogeneous subgroup of H^n is a subgroup invariant under the Heisenberg dilations. The (2n+1)-dimensional homogeneous subgroups play the role of codimension-one tangent planes.

nodes.label: Homology class of a curve
aliases: homology cycle represented by a curve
nodes.summary: The first-homology cycle represented by a closed curve on the surface; the paper studies whether primitive curve components give linearly independent such cycles.

nodes.label: Homotopy equivalence
nodes.summary: A map of topological spaces inducing inverse maps up to homotopy. The Generalized Smale Conjecture asks whether a natural inclusion has this property.

nodes.label: Hopf-Lax semigroup
nodes.summary: A Hamilton-Jacobi type semigroup on metric spaces used to evolve potentials. It is part of the temporal theory for intermediate-time Kantorovich potentials.

nodes.label: Horizontal and vertical foliations
nodes.summary: The two directional foliations of a flat or quadratic-differential surface by horizontal and vertical trajectories.

evidence.quote: the same leaf of the horizontal foliation and to the same leaf of the vertical foliation

nodes.label: Horizontal geodesic completeness at infinity
nodes.summary: For the submetry at infinity, delta-near points are almost near, so horizontal geodesics extend to antipodes and extensions are piecewise horizontal.

evidence.quote: geodesic xi eta extends to every antipode

nodes.label: Horizontal geodesic for a submetry
nodes.summary: A geodesic between delta-near points that a submetry maps isometrically to a geodesic in the base; finite unions of such geodesics are piecewise horizontal.

evidence.quote: Such geodesics in X are called horizontal

nodes.label: Horizontal holomorphic twistor map
nodes.summary: A holomorphic map from S^2 to the twistor space whose (1,0)-differential lies in the horizontal distribution of the twistor projection. Such maps project to harmonic maps into S^{2m}.

nodes.label: Horizontal Killing field in a semidirect product
nodes.summary: The case in which the surface-preserving Killing field lies in the horizontal distribution of a metric semidirect product. The excerpt only reveals this as a proof case.

nodes.label: horizontal path
nodes.summary: An absolutely continuous curve whose derivative lies in the distribution Delta almost everywhere, with the prescribed endpoints when used to connect two points.

nodes.label: Horizontal perimeter in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: Horizontal perimeter is perimeter measured only in the horizontal directions of the Heisenberg group; for smooth functions, the relevant density is |Xf|+|Yf|.

nodes.label: Horizontal projection in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: The horizontal projection pi_H maps a point of H^n to its horizontal R^{2n} coordinate. It is used to analyze the projection of supports of measures.

nodes.label: Horizontal and vertical distributions of a submersion
nodes.summary: For a Riemannian submersion, the vertical distribution is tangent to fibers and the horizontal distribution is its orthogonal complement.

evidence.quote: vertical distribution V, a subbundle of TM

nodes.label: Hörmander L2 dbar estimate
nodes.summary: An L2 estimate for solving dbar equations on complete Kähler manifolds with plurisubharmonic weights, used in the paper to decompose Kähler forms and construct holomorphic functions.

evidence.quote: We will use the following version of Hörmander’s estimate
construct holomorphic functions by the Hörmander technique

nodes.label: Horosphere in a Hadamard manifold
nodes.summary: A hypersurface in a simply connected nonpositively curved manifold asymptotic to a point at infinity; in the paper, end cross-sections arise as compact quotients of horospheres.

evidence.quote: a compact quotient manifold of a horosphere

nodes.label: Hsu lift characterization of singular paths
nodes.summary: A singular horizontal path is characterized by a nonzero lift to the annihilator Delta-perp whose derivative lies in the kernel of the restricted canonical symplectic form.

nodes.label: Huber one-quarter upper bound for large-genus closed hyperbolic surfaces
nodes.summary: For any sequence of closed hyperbolic surfaces with genus tending to infinity, the limsup of the first nonzero Laplacian eigenvalue is at most 1/4. This explains why 1/4 is the asymptotically optimal target.

evidence.quote: Huber proved in [Hub74] that in this case

nodes.label: Huisken monotonicity formula
nodes.summary: A weighted monotonicity identity or inequality for mean curvature flow using the backward heat kernel. It gives entropy monotonicity, Gaussian densities, and backward selfsimilarity of tangent flows.

nodes.label: Hermitian-Yang-Mills cone connection
nodes.summary: A direct sum of simple HYM cone connections obtained from polystable reflexive sheaves on CP^{n-1} via the cone construction psi_* pi^*. The limiting connection A_infty is of this type.

evidence.quote: direct sum of simple HYM cones

nodes.label: HYM-constructed torsion-free sheaves on the exceptional divisor
nodes.summary: Torsion-free coherent sheaves on CP^{n-1} associated to the degree-filtered graded modules. They become the graded pieces and HN factors of optimal algebraic tangent cones constructed from the HYM connection.

evidence.quote: define E^mu to be the torsion-free sheaf
evidence.section: 3.2 Construction of torsion-free sheaves on D

nodes.label: Hermitian-Yang-Mills equation for a Chern connection
nodes.summary: The curvature of the Chern connection satisfies i Lambda_omega F_A = c Id for a real Einstein constant c. This is the curvature equation in the admissible HYM definition.

nodes.label: HYM recovery of optimal algebraic tangent cones
nodes.summary: The method by which the admissible HYM connection and its degree filtration produce optimal extensions and recover all optimal algebraic tangent cones up to equivalence and special Hecke transforms.

evidence.quote: recover all the different optimal algebraic tangent cones

nodes.label: Hyperbolic parametrix
nodes.summary: A parametrix adapted to hyperbolic dynamics, used to represent semiclassical propagation in the presence of stable and unstable directions.

nodes.label: Hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: A smooth complete orientable surface equipped with a Riemannian metric of constant curvature -1. The paper uses this as the ambient geometric category for both finite-area non-compact and closed surfaces.

evidence.quote: finite-area hyperbolic surface, that is, a smooth surface with Riemannian metric of constant curvature  $-1$
all hyperbolic surfaces are assumed complete and orientable

nodes.label: Laplacian spectral gap of a hyperbolic surface
aliases: spectral gap between zero and the rest of the spectrum
nodes.summary: The gap between the zero eigenvalue of the Laplacian and the remaining spectrum. For finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surfaces, this gap is bounded above by 1/4.

evidence.quote: the spectral gap between 0 and the rest of the spectrum has size at most  $\frac{1}{4}$

nodes.label: Hyperkähler 4-manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth four-dimensional Riemannian manifold whose holonomy is contained in SU(2). Equivalently in dimension four it admits a compatible hyperkähler triple, and such metrics are Ricci-flat.

evidence.quote: holonomy group is contained in SU(2)

nodes.label: Period map for hyperkähler K3 metrics
nodes.summary: The map from the hyperkähler K3 moduli space to the locally symmetric period domain, sending a metric to the oriented maximal positive subspace of cohomology represented by its self-dual harmonic forms.

nodes.label: Hyperkähler metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric compatible with a triple of complex structures satisfying quaternionic relations, equivalently in dimension four with a hyperkähler triple of closed Kähler forms. Hyperkähler metrics are Ricci-flat Kähler metrics for each complex structure.

nodes.label: Hyperkähler triple
nodes.summary: A triple of symplectic/Kähler forms satisfying the algebraic identities that determine a hyperkähler metric. The paper first constructs an approximate version of such a triple.

evidence.quote: Construction of the approximate hyperkähler triple
Such a triple is called a hyperkähler triple

nodes.label: Mean curvature of a hypersurface
nodes.summary: The scalar mean curvature of a hypersurface in a Riemannian manifold is the trace of its second fundamental form with respect to a chosen unit normal.

evidence.quote: the mean curvature of the hypersurface  $\{u_i = 0\}$  with respect to  $g$

nodes.label: sidedness of a hypersurface
nodes.summary: The distinction between hypersurfaces with a globally defined unit normal and those without one. The proof treats two-sided separating, two-sided nonseparating, and one-sided cuts separately.

nodes.label: I-functional normalization
nodes.summary: The use of the level set of the I-functional, H0 or E^1_0, to fix additive constants of Kähler potentials. This prevents trivial constant-direction geodesic rays and is used in properness and stability definitions.

nodes.label: Ideal boundary with cone topology
nodes.summary: For a CAT(0) space X, the ideal boundary equipped with the cone topology, denoted in the paper by partial_infty X.

nodes.label: Improved neck structure for boundary balls
nodes.summary: A bootstrap refinement for balls close to the half-space model: balls centered at boundary points remain boundary balls at all scales, while sufficiently small balls centered away from the boundary become N-symmetric. It drives boundary topological regularity and boundary measure convergence.

evidence.quote: balls centered at boundary points are still delta'-boundary balls at any scale, while balls centered at interior points become (N,delta')-symmetric
evidence.section: 8.1 Improved neck structure theorem and Boundary volume rigidity

evidence.quote: for any x in ∂X cap B_1(p) and for any 0<r<1 ... d_GH(∂X cap B_r(x),B^{R^{N-1}}_r(0)) <= 2 epsilon r
evidence.section: 8.1 Improved neck structure theorem and Boundary volume rigidity

nodes.label: Incompressible subset of the Tits boundary
nodes.summary: A subset A of the Tits boundary is incompressible if every Stone-Cech boundary operator T^omega maps A isometrically.

evidence.quote: called incompressible, if T^omega maps A isometrically

nodes.label: Index-one CMC sphere
nodes.summary: A CMC sphere whose stability operator has Morse index one. Such spheres are the source objects for the invariant limit surfaces studied later in the paper.

evidence.quote: invariant limit surfaces of index-one spheres

nodes.label: Induced Lorentzian metric on a timelike graph
aliases: trace of the Minkowski metric on Sigma
nodes.summary: The Lorentzian metric induced on the graph hypersurface by the ambient Minkowski metric, with components g_{alpha beta}=m_{alpha beta}+partial_alpha u partial_beta u and inverse g^{alpha beta}. It supplies the geometric coefficients of the equation.

evidence.quote: The main geometric object is the metric $g$ which is the trace of the Minkowski metric in ${\mathfrak{M}}^{n+2}$ on $\Sigma$
evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Induced metric on a Euclidean submanifold
nodes.summary: The Riemannian metric on a submanifold induced by the ambient Euclidean inner product. It is used to define intrinsic differential operators and to compare Hessians in the equality case.

nodes.label: Induced submetry on a round sphere
nodes.summary: Under antipode-extension hypotheses, the submetry at infinity restricts to a submetry on a round sphere, in particular on a top-dimensional round sphere or the Tits boundary of a periodic Morse flat.

evidence.section: 5.3 Induced submetries and the dimension of the base

nodes.label: infinite-length obstruction for monodromic trajectories
nodes.summary: Monodromic transverse-singular trajectories necessarily have infinite length, so they cannot occur as finite-length singular horizontal paths starting from their limit point.

evidence.quote: length of any monodromic trajectory is infinite

nodes.label: Infinite abundance of maximal geodesic submanifolds
aliases: infinitely many maximal totally geodesic submanifolds
nodes.summary: The property that a finite-volume complex hyperbolic manifold contains infinitely many maximal properly immersed totally geodesic submanifolds of real dimension at least two.

evidence.quote: containing infinitely many maximal totally geodesic submanifolds of real dimension at least 2

nodes.label: Infinitesimal Mobius transformation space
nodes.summary: The finite-dimensional kernel $H_{n,0}=H_{n,1,2}\oplus H_{n,2,3}$ of the linearized conformal-isoperimetric quadratic form. It is identified with the Lie algebra of infinitesimal Mobius transformations of the sphere.

evidence.quote: Lie algebra of infinitesimal Mobius transformations

nodes.label: Infinitesimally Hilbertian metric-measure space
nodes.summary: A metric-measure space whose Cheeger energy is a quadratic form, equivalently in this paper's introduction corresponding to H^{1,2} being Hilbert. This is the Riemannian/Hilbertian input in the RCD condition.

evidence.quote: Any metric measure space such that Ch is a quadratic form is said to be infinitesimally Hilbertian.
the Cheeger energy E on M is a quadratic functional on L^2(M,m)
if the associated heat flow is linear

nodes.label: Inner and outer flows of a level set flow
nodes.summary: For a hypersurface separating an interior and exterior region, the outer and inner flows are the spacetime boundaries of the level set flows of those complementary regions. The paper calls these boundaries the outermost flows.

evidence.quote: the outer flow, $\mathcal{M}$ is the space-time boundary of the level set flow $F_{t}(V)$ of the interior $V$ of $M$. Similarly the inner flow $\mathcal{M}^{\prime}$ is the space-time boundary of the level set flow $F_{t}(V^{\prime})$ of the exterior $V^{\prime}$ of $M$.
we will refer to $\mathcal{M}(t),\mathcal{M}^{\prime}(t)$ as outermost flows

nodes.label: Integer rectifiable cycle
nodes.summary: An integer-multiplicity rectifiable current with zero boundary. The curvature-current limit in the paper is such a cycle of dimension n-2.

evidence.quote: converge (weakly), as epsilon -> 0, to an integer rectifiable cycle Gamma

nodes.label: Integral Brakke flow
nodes.summary: A Brakke flow whose time slices are integer two-rectifiable for almost all times, ruling out non-integer multiplicities.

nodes.label: integral varifold
nodes.summary: A rectifiable varifold whose density function takes integer values. Integrality is essential for interpreting min-max limits as integer-multiplicity minimal hypersurfaces.

nodes.label: Integrated local Weyl law
nodes.summary: An asymptotic formula for the integral of the on-diagonal spectral projector kernel over a subset $W$, with leading term proportional to $\operatorname{vol}_g(W)\lambda^n$.

evidence.quote: we obtain improved asymptotics for both pointwise and integrated Weyl Laws

nodes.label: Interior gradient and elliptic compactness estimates
nodes.summary: A priori estimates for bounded solutions of the anisotropic minimal graph equation that bound C^1 and then higher C^m norms on compact subsets. These estimates allow extraction of a smooth entire limit from solutions on expanding balls.

nodes.label: Interior parametrix on the hyperbolic plane
nodes.summary: A parametrix method for the interior part of the spectral problem, based on spectral theory of the hyperbolic plane. The excerpt identifies it as a proof component but omits details.

evidence.quote: 5.1 Background on spectral theory of hyperbolic plane

nodes.label: Intermediate K-semistable cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The possible K-semistable weighted asymptotic cone W appearing between the affine model of X and the metric asymptotic cone C in the two-step degeneration theory.

evidence.quote: there is an algebro-geometric 2-step degeneration from X to C, via a possible intermediate K-semistable cone W

nodes.label: Intermediate-time Kantorovich potential
nodes.summary: A Kantorovich potential associated to an intermediate time along an optimal-transport interpolation. The paper develops first-, second-, and third-order temporal theory for these potentials.

evidence.quote: Temporal theory of intermediate-time Kantorovich potentials

nodes.label: Intersection cohomology with perversity
nodes.summary: Intersection cohomology of a stratified space determined by a perversity, including lower and upper middle perversities. The paper identifies several weighted QFC cohomology groups with such intersection cohomology groups of the stratified compactification.

nodes.label: Gromov-Hausdorff collapse to an interval
nodes.summary: A collapse phenomenon in which a family of higher-dimensional Riemannian manifolds converges in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense to a one-dimensional interval.

evidence.quote: Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces which collapse to an interval

nodes.label: Interval Gromov-Hausdorff collapse of Kähler-Einstein cone metrics
nodes.summary: The collapse statement that the metric spaces (X, omega_beta) converge to an interval in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense in the positive Fano anticanonical case.

nodes.label: Intrinsic ball on a surface
nodes.summary: The open metric ball in a surface with respect to intrinsic distance. The chord arc statement assumes a large intrinsic ball is contained in the interior of the H-disk.

evidence.quote: the open intrinsic ball B_{\Sigma}(\mathbf{0}, CR) of radius CR centered at \mathbf{0}

nodes.label: Intrinsic curvature of a surface
nodes.summary: The Gaussian curvature of the Riemannian metric induced on a spacelike surface. The paper studies nonpositivity and rigidity properties of this curvature for complete maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: any complete maximal surface has non positive induced curvature, while Barbot surfaces are the only flat ones
If the intrinsic curvature of $\Sigma$ is zero at a point

nodes.label: Intrinsic distance on a surface
nodes.summary: The distance between points measured along paths lying in a surface. It is used in the disk diameter bounds and in the H-disk chord arc estimate.

evidence.quote: the intrinsic distance in M_n between any two points in \overline{\Delta} is less than 10

nodes.label: Intrinsic gradient on a submanifold
nodes.summary: The gradient of a function on Sigma with respect to the induced metric. It appears in the Sobolev integrand and in the auxiliary divergence equation.

nodes.label: Intrinsic graph in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: An intrinsic graph is a graph in the Heisenberg group formed relative to complementary horizontal and vertical subgroups rather than by an ordinary Euclidean graphing direction.

evidence.quote: 2.2. Intrinsic graphs and intrinsic Lipschitz graphs 87
Obtaining an embedding from an intrinsic graph

nodes.label: Intrinsic Lipschitz graph in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: An intrinsic Lipschitz graph is an intrinsic graph satisfying the Heisenberg intrinsic cone or Lipschitz condition. These graphs are the restricted surface class on which the paper proves the perimeter inequality.

evidence.quote: Such surfaces can still be very complicated

nodes.label: Intrinsic metric flow
nodes.summary: A metric flow is intrinsic at a time t when its time-slice (X_t,d_t) is a length space; it is intrinsic if this holds for all times and almost always intrinsic if it holds for almost every time. The paper proves this property survives F-limits under its hypotheses.

nodes.label: Invariant CMC surface
nodes.summary: A constant mean curvature surface invariant under a nontrivial one-parameter family of ambient isometries, equivalently under a Killing field in the ambient metric Lie group setting.

nodes.label: Invariant limit surface of index-one spheres
nodes.summary: A limit surface obtained from sequences of index-one CMC spheres and carrying an invariant-surface structure. The excerpt exposes this concept through the title of the main technical section.

evidence.quote: The geometry of invariant limit surfaces of index-one spheres

nodes.label: Irreducible nonarithmetic lattice
nodes.summary: An irreducible lattice in a semisimple Lie group that is not arithmetic. The introduction uses such lattices to reduce nonarithmetic locally symmetric spaces to real or complex hyperbolic rank-one cases.

evidence.quote: Gamma < G is an irreducible nonarithmetic lattice

nodes.label: Irreducible Tits boundary
nodes.summary: A Tits boundary with no nontrivial spherical join decomposition; reducibility corresponds to metric product splitting of the CAT(0) space.

evidence.section: 5.3 Induced submetries and the dimension of the base

nodes.label: Isolated conical singularity
nodes.summary: A cone singularity confined to the vertex, with the cone otherwise smooth. This hypothesis is part of the Hardt-Simon foliation setting.

nodes.label: Isolated conical singularity of a hypersurface
aliases: conical singularity modeled on a cone
isolated singularity modeled on a smooth cone
nodes.summary: A hypersurface singularity at a single point whose blow-up is modeled by a smooth cone, while the hypersurface is smooth away from that point. This is the class of nonsmooth initial hypersurfaces treated by the paper.

evidence.quote: a smooth hypersurface with an isolated conical singularity modeled on a smooth cone $\mathcal{C}$ at $\mathbf{0}$
a smooth compact hypersurface with an isolated singularity ... modeled on any smooth cone $\mathcal{C}$

nodes.label: Isometric embedding into a Riemannian manifold
aliases: Riemannian embedding of a metric space
nodes.summary: A map from a metric space into a Riemannian manifold that preserves pairwise distances. For Euclidean L, the defining equality is d(iota(x),iota(y)) = |x-y|.

evidence.quote: there exists \iota : L \to M such that for all x, y \in L, d(\iota(x), \iota(y)) = |x-y|

nodes.label: Isometric group action
nodes.summary: A group action on a metric space by isometries; geometric actions in the paper are a special class of such actions.

evidence.quote: an isometric action is called geometric

nodes.label: Isometry group of a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: The group of diffeomorphisms preserving a given Riemannian metric. In the paper it is compared with the full diffeomorphism group.

nodes.label: Isoperimetric eigenvalue inequality problem
nodes.summary: The problem of finding exact values of Lambda_k(M) and identifying metrics or degenerating metric sequences that attain the supremum. In this paper it is solved for all k on the real projective plane.

nodes.label: Isoperimetric volume deficit for a sphere map
nodes.summary: In the isometric stability problem, the additional deficit $(1-|V_n(u)|)_+$ measures the failure of the image to have the normalized volume of a round sphere. It supplies the missing global control not captured by local stretch alone.

evidence.quote: represents in this setting the isoperimetric deficit

nodes.label: Isotropic conformal vector field
nodes.summary: A conformal vector field whose values are lightlike on the relevant set, and in the analytic setting can force conformal flatness via Frances's result. The final contradiction produces such a field from many closed isotropic orbits.

nodes.label: Iterated fibration structure
nodes.summary: A compatible collection of fiber bundles on the boundary hypersurfaces of a compact manifold with corners, organized by a partial order and compatibility maps on intersections. It is the boundary data from which manifolds with fibered corners and their associated stratified spaces are built.

nodes.label: Iwasawa decomposition
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a suitable semisimple Lie group into compact, abelian, and nilpotent factors KAN, used in the paper to choose a solvable subgroup AN.

nodes.label: J_chi functional
nodes.summary: For a closed real (1,1)-form chi, J_chi is the functional whose variation involves tr_phi chi minus its average. It is used to build twisted K-energies and the continuity path.

evidence.quote: The Jχ functional for any (1,1)-form χ is defined by
the critical point of the Jχ functional is exactly the solution to the J-equation

nodes.label: J-equation
nodes.summary: For Kähler metrics ω0 and χ, the J-equation asks for a Kähler potential metric ωφ satisfying tr_{ωφ}χ = c, equivalently χ∧ωφ^(n-1)/(n-1)! = c0 ωφ^n/n! in the normalized form.

evidence.quote: the J-equation is defined as tr_{ωφ} χ = c

nodes.label: J-equation as a dHYM limit
nodes.summary: The J-equation arises as a scaling limit of the dHYM phase equation, using the limit k(π/2−arctan(kλ_i))→1/λ_i. This explains the parallel stability results for J-equation and dHYM.

evidence.quote: lim k→∞ Σ_i k(π/2 − arctan(kλi)) = Σ_i 1/λi
the J-equation is exactly the limit of the deformed Hermitian-Yang-Mills equation

nodes.label: J-equation subsolution cone condition
nodes.summary: The positivity condition cω^(n−1) − (n−1)χ∧ω^(n−2) > 0 imposed along the generalized J-equation. It is the elliptic cone condition used by the continuity and regularization arguments.

nodes.label: J-equation topological constant
nodes.summary: The constant c0 is fixed by the cohomological identity equating ∫M χ∧ω0^(n-1)/(n-1)! with c0∫M ω0^n/n!. It is the right-hand side of the normalized J-equation.

evidence.quote: Let  c0 > 0  be the constant such that ∫M χ ∧ ω0^(n-1)/(n-1)! = c0 ∫M ω0^n/n!

nodes.label: Coercivity of the Jχ functional
nodes.summary: The lower bound Jχ(φ) ≥ ε Jω0(φ) − C for positive ε and constant C. It is one of the stability-equivalent formulations of J-equation solvability in the paper.

evidence.quote: The Jχ functional is coercive, in other words ... Jχ(φ) ≥ ε1.1 Jω0(φ) − C1.2

nodes.label: Uniform J-slope intersection inequality
nodes.summary: The explicit uniform subvariety inequality ∫V ((c0−(n−p)ε)ω0^p − pχ∧ω0^(p−1)) ≥ 0 for every p-dimensional subvariety V. It gives the intersection-number form of uniform slope J-stability used in the proof.

evidence.quote: for all p-dimensional subvarieties V with p = 1, 2, ..., n

nodes.label: J-stability numerical invariant
nodes.summary: A numerical invariant attached to a Kähler test configuration and a Kähler class. Uniform J-stability compares J_[χ](X,Ω) to J_[ω0](X,Ω) with a uniform positive constant.

evidence.quote: the numerical invariant J[χ](X, Ω) ... satisfies J[χ](X, Ω) ≥ ε1.1 J[ω0](X, Ω)

nodes.label: Jacobi field on a minimal cone
nodes.summary: A solution of the cone Jacobi equation, representing an infinitesimal minimal deformation of the cone. The paper uses their spectral behavior in the regularity analysis.

nodes.label: Jacobi field on a self-expander
nodes.summary: A function solving the linearized expander equation $L_\Sigma v=0$. The paper uses a positive Jacobi field with linear growth at infinity to build barriers over outermost expanders.

evidence.quote: which yields a positive Jacobi field $L_{\Sigma}v=0$ with $v$ growing linearly at infinity
Linearising the expander equation ... yields solutions to the linearized equation ... We call such a function a Jacobi field.

nodes.label: Homogeneity exponents for cone Jacobi fields
nodes.summary: The radial growth rates for homogeneous Jacobi fields on the cone. The proof selects an index $J$ with $\gamma_J^+=1$ and chooses weights between adjacent exponents.

nodes.label: Jacobi operator and Jacobi field of a harmonic map
nodes.summary: The Jacobi operator is the second-variation operator J_{g,Phi}(V)=Delta_g^Phi V - tr_g R^N(V,dPhi)dPhi on sections of Phi^*TN. A Jacobi field is a field in ker J_{g,Phi}, often arising from an infinitesimal harmonic deformation.

nodes.label: Jacobi operator of a minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: The linearized mean-curvature or second-variation operator on normal variations of a minimal hypersurface. Its kernel controls degeneracy and local foliations near the hypersurface.

nodes.label: Jacobi operator on a minimal cone
nodes.summary: The linearization of the mean curvature operator at the cone. It governs Jacobi fields and the linear problem in the fixed-point argument.

nodes.label: Jacobi spectral projection $\Pi_J$
nodes.summary: A projection onto the finite-dimensional space of boundary modes associated with the first $J$ Jacobi exponents. It fixes the prescribed low-mode boundary data in the linear problem.

nodes.label: Jones β-number
nodes.summary: A Jones β-number measures the normalized deviation of a set inside a ball from the best-fitting line, for example β_{∞,Γ}(B(x,r)) = r^{-1} inf_L sup_{y∈Γ∩B(x,r)} dist(y,L). It is a comparison geometric square-function quantity for rectifiability.

evidence.quote: an analogue of the ε^2-conjecture for the β-numbers introduced by Jones

nodes.label: Jordan curve
nodes.summary: A Jordan curve is a simple closed planar curve. In the paper it is the principal class of boundaries for which Carleson's ε² tangent-point characterization is proved.

evidence.quote: Suppose Γ is a Jordan curve.
Let Ω^+⊂R^2 be either a Jordan domain or a two-sided corkscrew open set

nodes.label: Jordan decomposition of conformal isotropy
nodes.summary: The decomposition of an isotropy one-parameter subgroup in P into semisimple, unipotent, and elliptic components. The paper uses it to split fixed point analysis into hyperbolic and unipotent cases.

evidence.quote: Jordan decomposition of a corresponding isotropy flow
evidence.section: 5.1. Jordan decomposition of isotropy of a conformal flow

nodes.label: Jordan domain
nodes.summary: A Jordan domain is a planar domain whose boundary is a Jordan curve. The paper treats Jordan domains as one of the two admissible classes of Ω^+ for the finite-square-function rectifiability theorem.

evidence.quote: Let Ω^+⊂R^2 be either a Jordan domain or a two-sided corkscrew open set, let Γ=∂Ω^+

nodes.label: K3 surface
nodes.summary: A compact complex surface with trivial canonical bundle and no fundamental group; in this paper it is the compact four-manifold carrying the collapsing Ricci-flat Kähler metrics.

evidence.quote: Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces

nodes.label: K-energy properness with respect to d1
nodes.summary: The condition that K-energy tends to +infinity for I-normalized potentials whose d1 distance from the origin tends to infinity. The paper proves the existence direction for cscK and twisted cscK metrics.

evidence.quote: proper with respect to L1 geodesic distance
evidence.section: 4. K-energy proper implies existence of cscK

nodes.label: K-polystable Fano cone
nodes.summary: A Fano cone satisfying the polystable form of K-stability. The discussion identifies choosing a Calabi-Yau cone with choosing a K-polystable Fano cone.

evidence.quote: Choose a Calabi-Yau cone C. This is equivalent to choosing a K-polystable Fano cone

nodes.label: K-semistable Fano cone
nodes.summary: A Fano cone satisfying K-semistability in the algebro-geometric/Sasaki sense. The intermediate cone in the paper is treated as such a semistable object.

evidence.quote: via a possible intermediate K-semistable cone W
defines a K-semistable (resp. K-polystable) Fano cone

nodes.label: K-stability
nodes.summary: An algebro-geometric stability condition for polarized or Fano varieties, with polystable and uniform variants. It is the stability side of the classical Yau-Tian-Donaldson conjecture and is compared with Ding-stability in the paper.

evidence.quote: uniform Ding-stability was shown to be equivalent to uniform K-stability

nodes.label: k-symmetric cone
nodes.summary: A cone or tangent cone that splits as a product of a k-dimensional Euclidean factor and a metric cone C(Y).

nodes.label: Kähler class
nodes.summary: A de Rham cohomology class represented by Kähler forms on a compact complex manifold. The paper fixes such a class and studies canonical representatives in it.

nodes.label: Kähler cohomology class
nodes.summary: A cohomology class represented by a Kähler form. Uniform J-stability and the dHYM phase conditions are stated in terms of the classes [ω0] and [χ].

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein cone metric along a divisor
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric with cone singularities of angle 2 pi beta along D satisfying a Kähler-Einstein Ricci equation with an additional divisor-current term. The paper studies its degeneration as beta tends to zero.

evidence.quote: Kähler-Einstein metrics \omega_{\beta} with a cone singularity

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric whose Ricci form is proportional to the metric. The twisted current equation in the paper generalizes this metric notion to include a twist current.

evidence.quote: $X$  admits a Kähler-Einstein metric iff  $X$  is K-polystable

nodes.label: Kähler-Einstein metric on a log Fano pair
nodes.summary: A finite energy potential on a log Fano pair solving the singular Monge-Ampere equation whose curvature current is the Kähler-Einstein metric.

evidence.quote: the curvature current ... is a Kähler-Einstein metric

nodes.label: Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric is the background positive (1,1)-form used in the paper. The paper fixes Kähler metrics χ and ω0 on a Kähler manifold and deforms ω0 by Kähler potentials.

evidence.quote: Given Kähler metrics  ω0  and  χ  on  M
Kähler metrics with vanishing Ricci curvature

nodes.label: Kähler metric with cone singularities along a divisor
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric on X \ D that is locally quasi-isometric to the model cone metric in coordinates where the divisor is given by z_1 = 0. Such metrics are incomplete finite-volume metrics and extend as closed positive (1,1)-currents on X.

evidence.quote: is said to have cone singularities along D
locally quasi-isometric to the model cone metric

nodes.label: Kähler potential
nodes.summary: A real-valued function φ used to represent a Kähler metric in a fixed class, commonly written as ωφ. The paper's estimates measure φ and its derivatives.

evidence.quote: C^0 bound for the Kähler potential
Suppose ωφ is a constant scalar curvature Kähler metric

nodes.label: C0 bound for a Kähler potential
nodes.summary: A uniform bound on the Kähler potential φ. In this paper it is the input from which higher derivative estimates for cscK metrics are derived.

evidence.quote: higher order derivatives can be estimated in terms of a C^0 bound for the Kähler potential

nodes.label: Kähler-potential compactness under entropy bounds
nodes.summary: Precompactness of suitably normalized Kähler potentials with bounded scalar curvature and entropy or d1 distance, yielding W^{4,p} and C^{3,alpha} control. This compactness underlies the continuity-path limiting arguments.

nodes.label: Kähler potential deformation
nodes.summary: For a real function $\varphi$ on a Kähler manifold, the deformed form is $\omega_\varphi=\omega_X+i\partial\bar\partial\varphi$. It is the metric quantity whose eigenvalues enter the fully nonlinear equation.

nodes.label: Kähler potential metric
nodes.summary: The deformation of a reference Kähler metric by a real potential φ, written ωφ = ω0 + sqrt(-1)∂∂bar φ > 0. It is the unknown metric in both the J-equation and the dHYM equation in this paper.

nodes.label: Scalar curvature of a Kähler metric
nodes.summary: The scalar curvature R_phi of the Kähler metric omega_phi. Constantness of this quantity defines cscK metrics, and its trace-form variants define twisted cscK equations.

nodes.label: Kähler test configuration
nodes.summary: A degeneration object used to test stability of a Kähler class. The paper uses Kähler test configurations as the domain of the numerical invariant defining uniform J-stability.

evidence.quote: for all Kähler test configurations (X, Ω)

nodes.label: Kantorovich potential
nodes.summary: A dual optimizer in the Kantorovich formulation of optimal transport. The paper studies time-dependent and intermediate-time Kantorovich potentials and their temporal derivatives.

nodes.label: kappa-noncollapsed condition
nodes.summary: A Ricci-flow noncollapsing condition requiring volume lower bounds at scales where curvature is controlled; it rules out collapsed local geometries.

evidence.quote: The noncollapsing property plays a crucial role

nodes.label: Three-dimensional kappa-solution
nodes.summary: An ancient three-dimensional Ricci flow model with completeness, bounded curvature on compact time intervals, nonnegative sectional curvature, positive scalar curvature, and kappa-noncollapsing. Such models describe high-curvature regions in the canonical neighborhood assumption.

nodes.label: Killing field
nodes.summary: A vector field whose flow consists of isometries; Lie algebras of isometric actions are represented by Killing fields in the paper.

nodes.label: klt current or pair
nodes.summary: A Kawamata log terminal singularity condition for the twist current theta or the pair (X,theta). It is necessary for nonnegative lambda solutions and enters the valuation-theoretic log discrepancy.

evidence.quote: only admits a solution when  $\theta$  is  $klt^1$

nodes.label: klt singularities
nodes.summary: A singularity condition for a pair expressed by discrepancies greater than -1 on a log resolution, with effective boundary in the klt case.

nodes.label: Koranyi Hausdorff measure
aliases: Hausdorff measure associated to the Koranyi norm
nodes.summary: Koranyi Hausdorff measure is the Hausdorff measure on H^n built from the metric induced by the Koranyi norm. The paper uses its (2n+1)-dimensional version for codimension-one rectifiability and flat measures.

evidence.quote: $\mathcal{H}_{\| \cdot \|}^k$ is Hausdorff measure associated to the Koranyi norm

nodes.label: Koranyi norm
nodes.summary: The Koranyi norm is the homogeneous norm used to define balls and Hausdorff measure on the Heisenberg group in the paper. Regularity is measured through behavior on Koranyi balls.

evidence.quote: Hausdorff measure associated to the Koranyi norm

nodes.label: Korn-type linear stability on the sphere
nodes.summary: A family of linear stability estimates in which positive combinations of conformal or isometric quadratic forms with the isoperimetric quadratic form control the tangential gradient modulo the relevant infinitesimal rigid directions. The paper frames these as Korn-type inequalities for sphere maps.

nodes.label: Kuznecov sum
nodes.summary: A spectral sum involving Laplace eigenfunctions, often used to study averaged restrictions or periods. The excerpt names Kuznecov sums but does not give their formal definition.

nodes.label: Kuznecov sum improvement under non-looping
nodes.summary: A quantitative improvement for Kuznecov spectral sums derived under non-looping dynamical assumptions.

evidence.quote: quantitative improvements for Kuznecov sums under non-looping type assumptions

nodes.label: L1-L2 optimal-transport interpolation equivalence
aliases: equivalence between L1- and L2-optimal-transport-based interpolation
nodes.summary: A comparison principle identifying the L1- and L2-optimal-transport interpolation structures in the setting needed for globalization. This equivalence is a central proof ingredient.

evidence.quote: we establish an equivalence between  $L^1$ - and  $L^2$ -optimal-transport-based interpolation

nodes.label: L1 optimal-transport interpolation
nodes.summary: An optimal-transport interpolation based on distance cost rather than squared distance cost. In this paper it is tied to transport rays, disintegration, and the CD^1 condition.

nodes.label: $L^2$ conformal rigidity estimate for sphere maps
nodes.summary: A quantitative estimate controlling the $L^2$ distance from a rescaled tangential gradient $\lambda^{-1}\nabla_Tu$ to the tangential gradient of a Mobius transformation. The controlling quantity is the combined conformal-isoperimetric deficit.

evidence.quote: there exist a Mobius transformation $\phi$ and $\lambda>0$

nodes.label: L2 curvature bound under bounded Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: A uniform integral bound on the squared norm of the Riemann curvature tensor on a unit ball of a noncollapsed Riemannian manifold with two-sided Ricci curvature bounds.

evidence.quote: we have the  $L^2$  curvature bound  $\oint_{B_1(p)} |\mathrm{Rm}|^2(x) dx < C(n, \nu)$ , which proves the  $L^2$  conjecture

nodes.label: $L^2$-excess decay near a minimizing quadratic cone
nodes.summary: A scale-improvement estimate for excess relative to a minimizing quadratic cone or Hardt-Simon leaf. This decay is the quantitative route from closeness to graphical regularity.

nodes.label: $L^2$-excess relative to a quadratic cone or leaf
nodes.summary: A scale-dependent $L^2$ measurement of how far the varifold or graphical surface is from the minimizing quadratic cone or associated comparison leaf. The excerpt identifies it as the main quantitative quantity but omits its exact formula.

nodes.label: L2 function space on a hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: The Hilbert space of square-integrable functions on a hyperbolic surface X. The Laplacian in the paper is considered as an operator on this space.

nodes.label: Decay of L2 harmonic forms for exact QFB metrics
aliases: extra decay of QFB harmonic forms
nodes.summary: For suitable exact QFB metrics satisfying fiberwise hypotheses, the QFB metric can be chosen in its quasi-isometry class so that all L2 harmonic forms lie in v^epsilon L2. This decay is what turns weighted cohomology computations into reduced L2 cohomology computations.

nodes.label: $L^2$ isometric rigidity estimate for sphere maps
nodes.summary: A quantitative estimate controlling the normalized $L^2$ distance from $\nabla_Tu$ to $OP_T$ by the isometric stretch deficit plus the isoperimetric volume deficit. In higher dimensions the paper proves the analogue under an a priori homogeneous Sobolev bound.

nodes.label: $L^2$ isometric stretch deficit on the sphere
nodes.summary: The deficit $\|(\sigma_{n-1}-1)_+\|_{L^2}$, or $\|(\sigma_2-1)_+\|_{L^2}$ in dimension three, penalizing only local expansions of a sphere map. It is sharper than the full metric-tensor distance because short maps have zero deficit.

nodes.label: L2 optimal-transport interpolation
nodes.summary: The quadratic-cost optimal-transport interpolation whose curves are Wasserstein geodesics. The paper compares it with L1-based interpolation.

nodes.label: L_{1,q} billiard length set
nodes.summary: The set of lengths of closed billiard trajectories in the Γ(1,q) orbit class.

nodes.label: l-positive curvature operator
nodes.summary: A curvature operator whose lowest l eigenvalues have positive sum.

evidence.quote: the curvature operator of a Riemannian manifold is called l-positive if the sum of its lowest l eigenvalues is positive

nodes.label: Lagrangian phase operator
nodes.summary: The fully nonlinear phase expression Θ(ωφ)=Σ_i arctan(λ_i), where λ_i are eigenvalues of ωφ with respect to χ. The dHYM equation fixes this phase to a constant.

nodes.label: Lagrangian submanifold contribution to the wave trace
nodes.summary: A microlocal component of the wave trace associated with Lagrangian submanifolds corresponding to q-bounce billiard orbits.

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami eigenfunction
nodes.summary: A smooth function u_j satisfying an eigenvalue equation for the Laplace-Beltrami operator, typically L^2-normalized in this paper.

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami eigenfunction basis
nodes.summary: An orthonormal basis of $L^2(M)$ consisting of Laplace-Beltrami eigenfunctions satisfying $-\Delta_g\phi_{\lambda_j}=\lambda_j^2\phi_{\lambda_j}$.

evidence.quote: If $\{\phi_{\lambda_j}\}_{j=1}^{\infty}$ is an orthonormal basis of eigenfunctions, $-\Delta_g \phi_{\lambda_j} = \lambda_j^2 \phi_{\lambda_j}$

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami eigenvalue sequence on a closed manifold
aliases: Laplacian spectrum on a compact Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: The discrete eigenvalue sequence of $-\Delta_g$ on a compact Riemannian manifold, repeated with multiplicity and written as $0=\lambda_0^2<\lambda_1^2\leq\lambda_2^2\leq\cdots$.

evidence.quote: $\{\lambda_j^2\}_{j = 0}^\infty$ be the eigenvalues of $-\Delta_g$, repeated with multiplicity

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami eigenvalue sequence on a closed surface
aliases: Laplacian spectrum on a closed surface
nodes.summary: For a closed Riemannian surface, the Laplace-Beltrami operator has a discrete sequence of finite-multiplicity eigenvalues, written with multiplicity as 0=lambda_0<lambda_1<=lambda_2<=... .

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami operator
aliases: Laplace operator on a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: The second-order differential operator Delta associated with the Riemannian metric, used in both the weighted Laplace and Steklov boundary problems.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

evidence.quote: $\Delta_g$ be the negative definite Laplace-Beltrami operator acting on $L^2(M)$

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami operator on a closed surface
nodes.summary: The metric Laplacian on the closed surface, taken in the paper with the nonnegative sign convention. It appears in the Euler-Lagrange equations for the constrained and subcritical problems.

evidence.quote: our convention for the Laplacian is with the sign that makes it nonnegative

nodes.label: Laplace-density and Schrodinger eigenvalue optimization
nodes.summary: The paper's higher-dimensional eigenvalue optimization quantity V_m(M,g) can be written as a supremum of lambda_m(beta) times the total density, equivalently as a supremum of the integral of potentials V subject to a nonnegativity constraint on the next Schrodinger eigenvalue.

nodes.label: Laplace eigenvalue supremum Lambda_k
nodes.summary: The supremum of the normalized k-th Laplace eigenvalue over all Riemannian metrics on a manifold M.

nodes.label: Laplace isospectral domains
nodes.summary: Two planar domains are Laplace isospectral when they have the same Laplace spectrum for the boundary condition under consideration.

nodes.label: Laplace spectrum of a planar domain
nodes.summary: The multiset of eigenvalues of the Laplacian on a planar domain with a chosen Dirichlet or Neumann boundary condition.

nodes.label: Laplacian bound for the boundary distance
nodes.summary: The upper bound Δ^{ac} d_{∂X} <= -K d_{∂X} on X\∂X for the absolutely continuous part of the measure-valued Laplacian of the distance to the boundary. The paper verifies this in Alexandrov spaces, smooth convex-boundary manifolds, and their noncollapsed limits.

evidence.section: 7.1 Laplacian of the distance from the boundary

evidence.quote: can be verified for Alexandrov spaces with curvature bounded from below, Riemannian manifolds with convex boundary and interior lower Ricci curvature bounds and their noncollapsed pGH limits
evidence.section: 7.1 Laplacian of the distance from the boundary

nodes.label: Laplacian on a hyperbolic surface
aliases: Laplace-Beltrami operator on a hyperbolic surface
nodes.summary: The Laplace-Beltrami operator Delta_X acting on L2 functions on a hyperbolic surface X. Its spectrum and spectral gap are the central analytic invariants in the paper.

evidence.quote: The Laplacian operator  $\Delta_X$  on  $L^2(X)$  has spectrum in  $[0, \infty)$

nodes.label: Laplacian on a metric cone
nodes.summary: The Laplace operator specialized to a metric cone, used for harmonic and eigenvalue analysis on limit cones.

evidence.quote: 4.10. The Laplacian on a metric cone 447

nodes.label: Laplacian spectral projector
nodes.summary: The orthogonal projection in $L^2(M)$ onto the direct sum of Laplacian eigenspaces with frequency at most $\lambda$.

evidence.quote: $\Pi_{\lambda}$ is the orthogonal projection operator onto functions with frequency at most $\lambda$

nodes.label: Large-genus asymptotics of psi-class correlators
aliases: Aggarwal uniform large genus asymptotics of correlators
nodes.summary: A uniform asymptotic formula for psi-class intersection numbers as genus tends to infinity, used as an input for the paper's Masur-Veech volume and random-geometry asymptotics.

evidence.quote: uniform asymptotic formula for intersection numbers of  $\psi$ -classes on  $\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{g,n}$  for large  $g$  proved by A. Aggarwal in 2020
Uniform large genus asymptotics of correlators (after A. Aggarwal)

nodes.label: Lattice point counting problem
nodes.summary: A problem of estimating the number of lattice points lying in a specified region, used here as a counting model linked to disk spectral counting functions.

nodes.label: Left-invariant metric on a Lie group
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric on a Lie group invariant under left translations, identified at the identity with an inner product on the Lie algebra.

evidence.section: The space of left-invariant metrics and the beta-volume

nodes.label: Lelong number
nodes.summary: A numerical invariant measuring the singularity of a plurisubharmonic function or positive current. The paper uses Lelong numbers in its study of psh rays.

nodes.label: Length space
nodes.summary: A metric space in which distances are represented, or approximated arbitrarily well, by lengths of curves. The paper assumes the support of the measure is a length space.

nodes.label: Lens relation
nodes.summary: The boundary-to-boundary geodesic data recording, for geodesics issued from the boundary, the exit point, exit direction, and length of the geodesic.

evidence.quote: measures the point and the direction of exit from  $M$  of geodesics issued from the boundary and the length

nodes.label: Lens rigidity
nodes.summary: The inverse problem or property that the lens relation determines the Riemannian metric up to isometry. It is used when boundary distances are not necessarily realized by unique minimizing geodesics.

evidence.quote: whether we can determine the metric up to isometry from the lens relation
if the boundary distance is not realized by unique minimizing geodesics

nodes.label: Level set flow
nodes.summary: The maximal family of closed sets starting from a closed set and satisfying the avoidance principle. It is one weak formulation used to evolve through singularities.

evidence.section: 2.3 Weak set flows and level set flows

evidence.quote: the level set flow ... of a closed set $X$ is the unique maximal assignment of closed sets $t \mapsto F_t(X)$ with $F_0(X) = X$, such that $F_t(X)$ avoids smooth flows

nodes.label: Leverage pair for a holonomy sequence
nodes.summary: A pair of elements X,Y in the abelian conformal Lie algebra adapted to an ACL holonomy sequence, with X in the stabilizer of the limiting point and Y nonzero there, and X,Y independent at the original point. It provides algebraic constraints on the holonomy sequence.

evidence.section: 6.1. Analysis of holonomy sequences in the absence of a fixed point

nodes.label: Levi decomposition
nodes.summary: The decomposition of a Lie group or Lie algebra into a semisimple Levi factor and solvable radical, used in the general Alekseevskii proof.

nodes.label: Li-Gallot kernel estimate for Lichnerowicz Laplacians
nodes.summary: A Bochner-estimating framework that bounds the dimension of the kernel of a Lichnerowicz Laplacian under Ricci lower, diameter, and curvature-term lower bounds. The paper uses it to convert curvature-term control into Betti-number estimates.

evidence.quote: the work of P. Li and Gallot then implies a bound on the dimension of the kernel of the Lichnerowicz Laplacian
the dimension of the kernel of the associated Lichnerowicz Laplacian ... is bounded

nodes.label: Curvature-term control for Lichnerowicz Laplacians
aliases: control of the Lichnerowicz curvature term
nodes.summary: The paper's algebraic method for bounding the curvature term in Lichnerowicz Laplacians from the action of so(n) on tensors, expressed through the curvature operator and the hat-tensor construction.

evidence.quote: a new method to control the curvature term based on an understanding how elements of so(n) interact with tensors
a general method of controlling the curvature term of the Lichnerowicz Laplacian on tensors
evidence.section: 2. Controlling the curvature term of Lichnerowicz Laplacians

nodes.label: Lichnerowicz Laplacian
nodes.summary: A Laplacian on a tensor subbundle E built from the rough Laplacian and a curvature term. The paper calls a tensor harmonic when it lies in the kernel of this operator.

evidence.quote: For c > 0 the Lichnerowicz Laplacian on E is given by
A tensor T is called harmonic if Δ_L T=0.

nodes.label: Lightlike hyperplane distribution from Weyl curvature
nodes.summary: An analytic H-invariant distribution of lightlike hyperplanes on an open dense set, determined by Weyl curvature and equal to the approximately stable subspace for all relevant ACL holonomy sequences. It is the distribution integrated into the final foliation.

nodes.label: Limit curve of a complete maximal surface
nodes.summary: The boundary curve at infinity of a complete maximal surface in H^{2,n}, lying in the Einstein Universe. The excerpt states that these limit curves are semi-positive loops.

evidence.quote: every complete maximal surface $\Sigma$ has a limit curve $\partial_{\infty}\Sigma$ in $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ which is a semi-positive loop

nodes.label: Limit flow
nodes.summary: A subsequential limit of parabolically rescaled flows where both the base points approach a singular point and the scales shrink to zero. Unlike tangent flows, limit flows need not be selfsimilar.

evidence.quote: a limit flow is any subsequential limit

nodes.label: Limiting curvature cycle
nodes.summary: The weak limit of the normalized curvature-dual currents, an integer rectifiable (n-2)-cycle Poincare dual to c1(L) whose mass measure is bounded by the energy-concentration measure mu.

evidence.quote: an integer rectifiable cycle Gamma which is Poincare dual to c1(L), and whose mass measure |Gamma| satisfies |Gamma| <= mu

nodes.label: Linear heat flow on a metric-measure space
nodes.summary: The property that the heat flow associated with the Cheeger energy is linear. The excerpt states it as an equivalent description of infinitesimal Hilbertianity.

evidence.quote: or, in other words, if the associated heat flow is linear

nodes.label: Linear paradifferential flow for the timelike minimal surface equation
nodes.summary: The paradifferential replacement of the linearized flow, with coefficients paradifferentially quantized, e.g. (partial_alpha T_{g^{alpha beta}} partial_beta - T_{A^gamma} partial_gamma)w=f and conformal variants. It is the core equation for energy and Strichartz estimates.

evidence.quote: A key element in our study of the minimal surface equation is the associated linear paradifferential flow
evidence.section: 3.7 Paralinearization and the linear paradifferential flow
3.7 Paralinearization and the linear paradifferential flow

nodes.label: Linearizable conformal isotropy
nodes.summary: The property that the isotropy subgroup at a point is conjugate inside P into G0, equivalently admits an invariant complement to p in the model Lie algebra. It is required in the ACL setup and fixed-point analysis.

evidence.section: 5.1. Jordan decomposition of isotropy of a conformal flow

nodes.label: Linearized conformal deficit quadratic form
nodes.summary: The quadratic form associated with the nonlinear conformal deficit comparing Dirichlet energy and generalized area. It is one summand of the linearized conformal-isoperimetric form $Q_n$.

nodes.label: Linearized conformal-isoperimetric quadratic form
nodes.summary: The quadratic form obtained by expanding the combined conformal-isoperimetric deficit around the identity sphere map. It combines the Dirichlet part with the signed-volume second variation and has a finite-dimensional kernel.

nodes.label: Linearized expander operator
nodes.summary: The linearization of the self-expander equation at an expander. For a stable outermost expander it is nonnegative and has positive first eigenfunctions on compact pieces.

evidence.quote: the linearized expander operator ... is non-negative $L_{\Sigma}\geq 0$. In particular, there is a positive eigenfunction
where is the corresponding Jacobi operator

nodes.label: Linearized foliation profile equation
nodes.summary: The linearization of the profile ODE at the minimizing leaf. Solving Lf=g with even initial data produces controlled perturbations of the leaf profile.

nodes.label: Linearized isoperimetric deficit quadratic form
nodes.summary: The quadratic form associated with the nonlinear isoperimetric deficit comparing generalized area with signed volume. It includes the second variation of the signed volume term.

nodes.label: Linearized $L^2$ isometric deficit quadratic form
nodes.summary: The quadratic form $\fint |(P_T^t\nabla_Tw)_{sym}|^2$ associated with the full $L^2$ isometric metric deficit. It is paired with the isoperimetric quadratic form in the paper's linear isometric Korn-type estimate.

evidence.quote: quadratic form associated to the full $L^2$-isometric deficit

nodes.label: Linearized Ricci-DeTurck equation
nodes.summary: The linearization of the Ricci-DeTurck perturbation equation for a symmetric two-tensor on a background Ricci flow. Vanishing and decay estimates for this equation on blow-up models underpin the semilocal maximum principle.

evidence.section: 6.5 Ricci-DeTurck flow and harmonic map heat flow

nodes.label: Linearized timelike minimal surface equation
nodes.summary: The equation obtained by linearizing the timelike minimal surface equation around a solution u, written in forms such as (g^{alpha beta} partial_alpha partial_beta - 2 A^gamma partial_gamma)v=0 and in divergence form using the conformal metric hat g.

evidence.quote: the linearized equations take the form $$(g^{\alpha\beta}\partial_{\alpha}\partial_{\beta}-2A^{\gamma}\partial_{\gamma})v=0.$$

nodes.label: Linearly full harmonic map to a sphere
aliases: full harmonic map into a sphere
nodes.summary: A harmonic map to S^n whose image is not contained in any proper equatorial subsphere. Linearly full maps are the setting for the paper's degree, index, and twistor arguments.

nodes.label: Liouville property for half-harmonic 1-forms
nodes.summary: A rigidity or classification property for half-harmonic 1-forms under the paper's analytic hypotheses. The excerpt does not state the precise half-harmonic equation or growth assumptions.

nodes.label: Liouville property for harmonic functions
nodes.summary: A rigidity or classification property for harmonic functions, typically asserting that harmonic functions satisfying specified growth or decay assumptions must be constrained. The paper devotes a section to this input.

nodes.label: Liouville rigidity for sphere self-maps
nodes.summary: Isometric Sobolev self-maps of the sphere are rigid motions, while degree $\pm1$ conformal Sobolev self-maps are Mobius transformations. The paper uses this as the rigid limiting model for stability.

evidence.quote: isometric iff it is a rigid motion

nodes.label: Liouville-type member of the Moser-Trudinger family
nodes.summary: The $p=1$ specialization of the subcritical family, formally giving a Liouville-type exponential equation such as $\Delta_{g_0}u+u=\lambda f e^u$. The paper treats this endpoint separately, with excluded energy values in $4\pi\mathbb N$.

evidence.quote: interpolate between a Liouville-type problem and our critical Moser-Trudinger problem

nodes.label: Lipschitz graph
nodes.summary: A Lipschitz graph is the graph of a Lipschitz function, used as a basic rectifiable model set. The proof estimates the smooth square function on Lipschitz graphs and constructs approximate Lipschitz graphs.

evidence.quote: The smooth square function on Lipschitz graphs

nodes.label: Lipschitz graph varifold
nodes.summary: The multiplicity-one m-varifold induced by the graph of a Lipschitz map u: Omega subset R^m -> R^n, parametrized as Gamma_u={(x,y): y=u(x)}.

evidence.quote: graph Gamma_u of a Lipschitz function

nodes.label: Littlewood-Paley decomposition for spatial frequencies
nodes.summary: The spatial dyadic decomposition by multipliers P_k localized to frequencies |xi| approximately 2^k. The paper deliberately applies it only in space, not time.

evidence.quote: where $P_{k}=P_{k}(D_{x})$ are multipliers with smooth symbols ... localized in the dyadic frequency region

nodes.label: Local Birkhoff rigidity near ellipses
aliases: local version of the Birkhoff conjecture
nodes.summary: A rigidity principle saying that a strictly convex planar domain sufficiently close to an ellipse and rationally integrable must itself be an ellipse.

evidence.quote: local version of the Birkhoff conjecture

nodes.label: Local boundary rigidity
aliases: local boundary rigidity near a strictly convex boundary point
nodes.summary: A local version of boundary rigidity in which boundary distance data known near a strictly convex boundary point determines the metric in a neighborhood of that point, up to diffeomorphism invariance.

evidence.quote: determines  $g$  in a suitable neighborhood of  $p$  in  $M$

nodes.label: Local covering space from short geodesic loops
nodes.summary: A local cover constructed from short geodesic loops in the asymptotic region. It is part of the paper's route from loop geometry to fibration structure.

evidence.quote: Short geodesic loops and the local covering space

nodes.label: Local cscK a priori estimates
nodes.summary: Local versions of the paper's a priori estimates, intended to control cscK potentials on smaller coordinate domains from local hypotheses. The excerpt indicates these estimates may be of independent interest.

evidence.quote: some local versions of these estimates which can be of independent interest

nodes.label: Local Curvature-Dimension condition
nodes.summary: The requirement that the curvature-dimension condition hold locally, for suitable neighborhoods or local restrictions of the metric-measure space. The paper shows this local condition implies the global one in the stated setting.

evidence.quote: verifying the local Curvature-Dimension condition  $\mathbb{CD}_{loc}(K, N)$

nodes.label: Local flatness-to-rectifiability scheme
nodes.summary: This proof scheme converts local flatness information and square-function estimates into rectifiability, using approximate Lipschitz graphs and smallness of bad sets such as low-density and bad-angle regions.

evidence.quote: Part II: From local flatness to rectifiability
Construction of an approximate Lipschitz graph
Small measure of LD and BA

nodes.label: Local flatness via compactness
nodes.summary: Local flatness via compactness is the proof method that obtains flatness from compactness properties and Carleson square-function control. It forms the first part of the paper's proof strategy.

evidence.quote: Part I: Flatness via compactness arguments
General compactness results involving the Carleson square function

nodes.label: Local Gromov-Hausdorff approximation by bi-Lipschitz models
aliases: local approximation by bi-Lipschitz Euclidean images
nodes.summary: A subset satisfies this condition when, at almost every point and at sufficiently small scales, its local metric geometry is close in Gromov-Hausdorff distance to a K-bi-Lipschitz Euclidean metric model. The excerpt states the condition but not the full normalization details.

evidence.quote: at almost every point and each sufficiently small scale,  $E$  is approximated by a bi-Lipschitz image of Euclidean space

nodes.label: Local Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: Convergence of closed sets in Hausdorff distance after restriction to compact subsets. It is used to state the rescaled convergence of a conical level set flow to the cone flow.

evidence.quote: $t^{-1/2} F_t(M)$ converges in the local Hausdorff sense to $F_1(\mathcal{C})$ as $t \searrow 0$
in the sense of local Hausdorff convergence

nodes.label: Local minimizer of the two-phase Bernoulli functional
nodes.summary: A function u:D->R is a local minimizer of J_tp in D if its J_tp energy cannot be lowered by compactly supported domain perturbations: J_tp(u,Omega) <= J_tp(v,Omega) whenever closure(Omega) is contained in D and v equals u outside Omega.

evidence.quote: We say that a function  $u:D\to \mathbb{R}$  is a local minimizer of  $J_{tp}$  in  $D$  if

nodes.label: Local pointed entropy
nodes.summary: A heat-kernel based entropy quantity W_t(x) attached to a point and scale, used to measure or detect conical structure.

evidence.quote: 4.6. The local pointed entropy,  $\mathcal{W}_t(x)$  and its relation to cone structure 438

nodes.label: Local uniform curvature bound on regular parts
aliases: local curvature bounds on regular parts
nodes.summary: Uniform local bounds on the curvature of the smooth Ricci flow spacetime structures carried by regular parts of a sequence of metric flows. These bounds are the hypothesis used to upgrade F-convergence to smooth convergence on a subset of the limit regular part.

nodes.label: Local uniform Legendre-Hadamard condition
nodes.summary: A rank-one second-variation lower bound for F_Psi on bounded gradient sets, used as a local ellipticity input in the excess-decay argument.

nodes.label: Localized avoidance principle for weak set flows
nodes.summary: A local form of the avoidance principle for weak set flows, used to control one-sidedness and separation of weak flows in bounded spacetime regions.

nodes.label: Localized topological monotonicity for mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A localized version of White's topological monotonicity for hypersurfaces moving by mean curvature, used to compare topology in a chosen spatial region across times.

nodes.label: Locally finite energy geodesic ray
aliases: infinite geodesic ray with locally finite energy
nodes.summary: A continuous ray u_t in E^1 with d1(u_t,u_s)=c|t-s| whose every compact time restriction is a finite energy geodesic segment. It is the ray class used in the paper's geodesic stability statements.

evidence.section: 6. Existence of cscK and geodesic stability

nodes.label: Locally finite Yang-Mills energy
nodes.summary: The curvature has finite integral of |F_A|^2 over compact subsets away from the sheaf singular set. It underlies monotonicity, bubbling, and analytic multiplicities.

nodes.label: Locally monotone set in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A locally monotone set has monotone intersections with the relevant horizontal lines in a local region, typically intervals, rays, empty sets, or full line pieces up to negligible error.

evidence.quote: 11.2. Locally monotone sets are bounded by rectifiable ruled surfaces 161

nodes.label: Locally symmetric cusp limit of Kähler-Einstein cone metrics
aliases: conical KE convergence to locally symmetric metric
nodes.summary: The convergence phenomenon in which small-angle conical Kähler-Einstein metrics on a compact pair approach the complete locally symmetric metric on X \ D. This is the negative-curvature limiting picture described in the abstract.

nodes.label: Locally symmetric metric on a divisor complement
nodes.summary: A complete locally symmetric metric carried by the open manifold X \ D. In the negative-curvature case, it is the canonical target for small-angle conical Kähler-Einstein metrics.

evidence.quote: X \setminus D is a locally symmetric space
converges to the locally symmetric metric

nodes.label: Log adjoint line bundle for a cone pair
nodes.summary: The real line bundle K_X + (1-beta)D associated with the pair (X,D) and cone angle parameter beta. Its positivity controls existence of negatively curved conical Kähler-Einstein metrics in the excerpt.

nodes.label: Log beta-volume of left-invariant metrics
nodes.summary: An Aut(n)-invariant smooth potential on the space of left-invariant metrics, defined from the beta-gradient vector field and normalized at a background metric.

evidence.quote: log beta-volume functional on left-invariant metrics

nodes.label: Log discrepancy of a valuation
nodes.summary: The discrepancy invariant A_(X,D)(v) of a valuation relative to a log pair, used with the S-invariant in the valuative criterion.

evidence.quote: A_(X,D)(v) the log discrepancy of v

nodes.label: Log Fano pair
nodes.summary: A normal projective variety X with an effective Q-Weil divisor D such that -(K_X+D) is ample Q-Cartier and the pair has klt singularities.

evidence.quote: L := -(K_X + D) is an ample Q-Cartier divisor

nodes.label: Logarithm map to a space of directions
nodes.summary: For a CAT(0) space and basepoint x, the natural 1-Lipschitz map from the Tits boundary to the space of directions at x.

nodes.label: Logarithmically improved Weyl remainder under near-periodic smallness
nodes.summary: A Weyl remainder improvement in which the on-diagonal integrated spectral projector has error $O(\lambda^{n-1}/\log\lambda)$ under a small-measure assumption for near-periodic geodesics over the integration region.

evidence.quote: Assuming only that the set of near periodic geodesics over $W\subset M$ has small measure

nodes.label: Longest complementary circle arc
nodes.summary: Given a circle centered at x with radius r and a planar open set Ω^+ with exterior Ω^-, I^+(x,r) and I^-(x,r) are the longest open arcs of ∂B(x,r) contained in Ω^+ and Ω^-, allowing the empty arc.

evidence.quote: denote by I^{+}(x,r) and I^{-}(x,r) the longest open arcs of the circumference ∂B(x,r) contained in Ω^{+} and Ω^{-}, respectively (they may be empty)

nodes.label: Lopatinsky-Shapiro local boundary condition
nodes.summary: A local boundary condition satisfying the Lopatinsky-Shapiro ellipticity condition, imposed on harmonic spinors in the approximating smooth domains.

evidence.quote: which satisfies a suitable local boundary condition of Lopatinsky-Shapiro-type

nodes.label: Lorentzian Calderón problem for a potential
aliases: inverse boundary value problem for Lorentzian wave potentials
nodes.summary: The inverse boundary value problem of determining the smooth potential V from the Cauchy data set for the Lorentzian wave equation. It is the central problem addressed in the paper.

nodes.label: Lorentzian curvature upper bound R ≤ K
