nodes.summary: The tensor inequality g(R(X,Y)Y,X) ≤ K(g(X,X)g(Y,Y)-g(X,Y)^2) for all tangent vectors. Unlike a naive sectional-curvature inequality, it gives a nontrivial Lorentzian curvature class used for unique continuation.

nodes.label: Lorentzian distance function in the double-null exterior
nodes.summary: In normal coordinates centered at p, r_p is the positive square root of the signed quadratic form on the spacelike exterior E_p and satisfies <∇r_p,∇r_p>=1. The condition on the exponential map makes it globally well-defined on E_p.

evidence.quote: r_p is a distance function on E_p
evidence.section: 4.2. A distance function in the exterior of the double null cone

nodes.label: Lorentzian Einstein universe
nodes.summary: The projectivized null cone in R^{2,n}, carrying its natural Lorentzian conformal class and identified with the homogeneous model PO(2,n)/P. It is the model space for conformal Lorentzian Cartan geometry.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Lorentzian manifold with timelike boundary
nodes.summary: A smooth Lorentzian manifold whose boundary hypersurface is timelike. This is the ambient geometric setting for the wave equation and boundary measurements in the paper.

nodes.label: Lorentzian metric
nodes.summary: A pseudo-Riemannian metric of signature (1,n-1) or (n-1,1). It supplies the lightlike, timelike, spacelike, orthogonality, and volume notions used throughout the paper.

nodes.label: Lorentzian null form Q0
nodes.summary: The bilinear null form Q0(v,w)=g^{alpha beta} partial_alpha v partial_beta w determined by the induced Lorentzian metric. It records the null structure used in the equation and its linearization.

evidence.quote: The primary null form which plays a role in this article is $Q_{0}$, defined by $$Q_{0}(v,w):=g^{\alpha\beta}\partial_{\alpha}v\partial_{\beta}w$$
evidence.section: 3.5 Null forms and the nonlinear null condition

nodes.label: Cauchy data set for a Lorentzian wave potential
nodes.summary: The set of all boundary traces (u,∂νu)|∂M of smooth solutions to the Lorentzian wave equation with potential V. It is the measured data in the inverse problem.

nodes.label: Lorentzian wave equation with potential
nodes.summary: The linear hyperbolic equation (□+V)u=0 on a Lorentzian manifold. Boundary data and controllability are formulated for this equation.

nodes.label: Lorentzian wave operator
nodes.summary: The canonical second-order hyperbolic operator associated with a Lorentzian metric. It replaces the Laplacian in the Lorentzian version of the Calderón problem.

nodes.label: Lott-Sturm-Villani Curvature-Dimension condition
nodes.summary: A synthetic metric-measure condition expressing Ricci curvature bounded below by K and dimension bounded above by N, formulated through optimal transport and Wasserstein interpolation. The paper proves its local-to-global property under essential non-branching.

evidence.quote: The Lott-Sturm-Villani Curvature-Dimension condition provides a synthetic notion
Let us start by recalling the so-called curvature dimension condition CD(K,N).

nodes.label: Low-bounce validity of Marvizi-Melrose parametrices
aliases: validity for q at least 2 in nearly circular domains
nodes.summary: For nearly circular C^8 domains, the Marvizi-Melrose parametrices remain valid for Γ(1,q) closed billiard trajectories already for q at least 2.

nodes.label: Low-density region
nodes.summary: The low-density region is an exceptional set appearing in the final rectifiability construction. Together with the bad-angle region, it is shown to have small measure so that a large good set remains.

evidence.quote: Small measure of LD and BA

nodes.label: Low-entropy hypersurface
nodes.summary: A closed hypersurface whose entropy is below a specified threshold such as lambda(S^2) or Lambda in (lambda_n, lambda_{n-1}]. This hypothesis excludes many non-round shrinker and cone models.

nodes.label: Low-index sphere harmonic map gradient estimate
nodes.summary: For dimensions 3 through 5 and sufficiently large target sphere dimension, a smooth sphere-valued harmonic map with index at most k+1 has a uniform L^6 bound on du and a W^{1,2} bound on |du|^2.

nodes.label: Low-index sphere harmonic map stabilization
aliases: stabilization in a totally geodesic subsphere
nodes.summary: For a fixed 3-, 4-, or 5-dimensional domain, any harmonic map M to S^k with sufficiently large k and index at most k+1 has image contained in a fixed-dimensional totally geodesic subsphere independent of k.

evidence.section: 3 Improved regularity and stabilization in the sphere-valued case

nodes.label: Low-index Steklov-Laplace supremum equality for surfaces
nodes.summary: For all closed surfaces, the first and second normalized Steklov suprema equal the corresponding normalized Laplace suprema, using the comparison together with known opposite inequalities.

nodes.label: Low-regularity local well-posedness for the timelike minimal surface equation
aliases: Hadamard local well-posedness below Smith-Tataru threshold
nodes.summary: The main well-posedness property proved for Sobolev data with s>s_c+3/8 in two space dimensions and s>s_c+1/4 in higher dimensions, including existence, uniqueness as limits of smooth solutions, persistence of higher regularity, continuous dependence, and weak Lipschitz dependence.

evidence.quote: The Cauchy problem for the minimal surface equation ... is locally well-posed for initial data $u[0]$ in $\mathcal{H}^{s}$

nodes.label: Lower Ahlfors regularity
nodes.summary: A lower measure-density condition at relevant scales, used in this paper as a regularity property of neck regions.

evidence.quote: Proof of Lower Ahlfors Regularity bound

nodes.label: Lower Ricci curvature bound
nodes.summary: A uniform lower bound on the Ricci curvature of the approximating Riemannian manifolds.

nodes.label: Lowest curvature-operator eigenvalue sum condition
nodes.summary: The condition requiring the sum or average of the lowest n-p curvature-operator eigenvalues to be positive or bounded below by κ. This is the paper's main curvature hypothesis for Bochner vanishing and Betti-number estimates.

evidence.quote: a lower bound on the average of the lowest n - p eigenvalues of the curvature operator
If λ_1 + ... + λ_{n-p} > 0, then the Betti numbers b_p(M) and b_{n-p}(M) vanish.

nodes.label: $L^q$ density condition for Monge-Ampère estimates
nodes.summary: The right-hand side density in the complex Monge-Ampère equation is assumed to lie in $L^q$ for $q>1$, the sharp range for Kolodziej-type boundedness estimates.

evidence.quote: right hand side is in  $L^q$ , for  $q > 1$

nodes.label: Lusternick-Schnirelmann min-max argument
nodes.summary: A cohomological min-max argument used to extract consequences from p-sweepouts and compare consecutive widths.

nodes.label: m-rectifiable set
nodes.summary: A subset of R^N covered up to H^m-null set by countably many C^1 m-dimensional submanifolds.

evidence.quote: covered, up to an Hm-negligible set, by countably many C1 m-dimensional submanifolds

nodes.label: Mabuchi K-energy
nodes.summary: A functional on the space of Kähler potentials whose first variation pairs a variation with the scalar curvature defect R_phi - Rbar. Its critical points are cscK metrics, and its extension to E^1 is central to the paper.

nodes.label: manifold with cylindrical ends
nodes.summary: A complete noncompact manifold that, outside a compact set, is isometric to a product cylinder Sigma x [0,infinity) with product metric; the metric may be Lipschitz in this paper.

nodes.label: Manifold with fibered corners
nodes.summary: A compact manifold with corners together with an iterated fibration structure on its boundary hypersurfaces. Collapsing the fibers of the boundary fibrations produces a smoothly stratified space whose strata correspond to the bases of these fibrations.

nodes.label: Martinet surface
nodes.summary: For a rank-two distribution Delta on a 3-manifold, the Martinet surface is the set of points where Delta plus its first bracket distribution fails to span the tangent space. Singular curves for Delta are contained in this surface.

nodes.label: tangency stratification of the Martinet surface
nodes.summary: A regular semianalytic stratification of the Martinet surface into points, one-dimensional tangent strata, one-dimensional transverse strata, and two-dimensional transverse strata relative to Delta.

nodes.label: Marvizi-Melrose microlocal parametrix
nodes.summary: A microlocal parametrix for the wave trace contribution of billiard trajectories in Γ(1,q), expressed through Lagrangian contributions associated with q-bounce orbits.

nodes.label: Mass concentration method for the J-equation
nodes.summary: A proof method that produces a limiting current in a slightly smaller class satisfying the weak J-cone inequality. It is used to pass from approximate solvability to a current-level subsolution.

evidence.quote: 3 Concentration of mass and its application
evidence.section: 3 Concentration of mass and its application

evidence.quote: there exist ... a current ω1.5 ∈ [ω0 − ε1.4χ] such that cω1.5^(n−1) − (n − 1)χ ∧ ω1.5^(n−2) ≥ 0

nodes.label: Mass rectifiability from AC1 and bounded anisotropic first variation
aliases: rectifiability of the mass of varifolds
nodes.summary: The mass-measure rectifiability conclusion for varifolds with anisotropic first variation a Radon measure, characterized by the weaker AC1 condition and supplied by C^1-near-area and ell^p examples.

evidence.quote: AC1 ... equivalent to the rectifiability of the mass
rectifiability of the mass of varifolds

nodes.label: Masur-Veech volume
nodes.summary: The volume of a moduli space or stratum of quadratic differentials with respect to the Masur-Veech measure; the paper uses Vol Q_g as a central input.

evidence.quote: Masur-Veech volume  $\mathrm{Vol}\mathcal{Q}_g$  of the moduli space of holomorphic quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Masur-Veech volume formula for quadratic differentials
nodes.summary: The paper's formula for Vol Q_g, used together with large-genus asymptotic analysis to derive random multicurve and square-tiled-surface asymptotics.

evidence.quote: These results are based on our formula for the Masur-Veech volume  $\mathrm{Vol}\mathcal{Q}_g$

nodes.label: Matching angle hypothesis for polytope faces
nodes.summary: At every boundary point lying on two faces, the inner product of the two outward face normals computed in the metric $g$ equals the Euclidean inner product of the corresponding model normals.

evidence.quote: If  $x$  is point in  $\partial \Omega$  and  $i_1, i_2 \in I$  satisfy  $u_{i_1}(x) = u_{i_2}(x) = 0$ , then  $\langle v_{i_1}, v_{i_2} \rangle = \langle N_{i_1}, N_{i_2} \rangle$  at the point  $x$ .

nodes.label: Maximal connected abelian conformal subgroup
nodes.summary: The connected abelian subgroup H obtained from a maximal abelian subalgebra of conformal vector fields. The proof reduces compactness of the full conformal group to compactness of this H.

evidence.section: 2.1. Gromov's stratification and reduction to a connected, abelian group

nodes.label: Maximal epsilon-separated subset
nodes.summary: A subset S^epsilon of M whose distinct points are at least epsilon apart and which is maximal with this property. It seeds the intrinsic perforation pattern without requiring periodic structure.

nodes.label: Maximal geodesic submanifold
nodes.summary: A geodesic submanifold not contained in any proper geodesic submanifold of strictly larger dimension.

evidence.quote: called maximal if it is not contained in a proper geodesic submanifold of larger dimension

nodes.label: Maximal horizontal cylinder
nodes.summary: A maximal cylinder in a square-tiled surface foliated by closed horizontal leaves; the paper studies the number of such cylinders as a random statistic.

evidence.quote: the number of maximal horizontal cylinders of a random square-tiled surface of genus  $g$

nodes.label: Maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: A spacelike surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space H^{2,n} whose mean curvature vector vanishes everywhere. The paper studies complete and quasiperiodic maximal surfaces and their limit curves in the Einstein Universe.

evidence.quote: A maximal surface in $\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ is a spacelike surface on which the mean curvature vector vanishes everywhere.
We continue our study of complete maximal surfaces in pseudo-hyperbolic spaces of signature $(2,n)$

nodes.label: Maximally symmetric CMC sphere
nodes.summary: A CMC sphere with a center of symmetry p such that every ambient isometry fixing p leaves the sphere invariant.

evidence.quote: S_H is maximally symmetric; that is, there exists a point p in M ... any isometry Phi of M that fixes p also satisfies Phi(S_H) = S_H

nodes.label: maximum principle for minimal hypersurfaces and varifolds
nodes.summary: A comparison principle preventing stationary minimal objects from crossing or touching suitable mean-convex barriers except in forced equality cases. It is used to keep min-max limits out of the boundary and cylindrical region.

nodes.label: Mean convex boundary
nodes.summary: A boundary whose mean curvature with respect to the inward-pointing normal is nonnegative. This condition appears in the definition of Alexandrov embeddedness used in the paper.

evidence.quote: mean convex, i.e., the mean curvature of partial B with respect to its inward pointing normal vector is nonnegative

nodes.label: Mean-convex face
nodes.summary: A boundary face whose mean curvature has the nonnegative sign convention required in the polyhedral comparison theorem.

nodes.label: Mean-convex mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A mean curvature flow whose mean-curvature vector points inward at every regular point of the evolving surface. This inward motion is the local property the paper proves near spherical and cylindrical singularities.

nodes.label: Mean-convex neighborhood property
aliases: mean-convex neighborhood conjecture for neck singularities
nodes.summary: The property that near a spherical or cylindrical multiplicity-one singularity there is a space-time neighborhood in which the flow moves strictly inward, equivalently is mean convex after possibly choosing orientation.

evidence.quote: mean-convex neighborhood conjecture for neck singularities

nodes.label: Mean-convex polytope face
nodes.summary: A boundary face of the polytope is mean-convex with respect to $g$ when the hypersurface defining that face has nonnegative mean curvature along the portion of the face inside the polytope.

evidence.quote: the mean curvature of the hypersurface  $\{u_i = 0\}$  with respect to  $g$  is nonnegative at each point in  $\Omega \cap \{u_i = 0\}$

nodes.label: Mean-convexity
nodes.summary: The property that the mean curvature has a consistent nonnegative sign with respect to the chosen inward normal, so the hypersurface locally moves by mean curvature in a mean-convex direction.

nodes.label: Mean curvature
nodes.summary: The scalar curvature of an immersed surface obtained by averaging its principal curvatures, with sign depending on the chosen normal. Constancy of this quantity is the defining condition for CMC surfaces in the paper.

evidence.quote: it has non-negative constant mean curvature H

nodes.label: Mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A one-parameter family of surfaces moving so that the normal velocity equals the mean-curvature vector. In the paper the surfaces are mostly two-dimensional surfaces in Euclidean three-space.

evidence.quote: moves by mean curvature flow if
Given a smooth hypersurface  $M_0$ , its evolution
A family of smooth hypersurfaces $M(t)$ is a mean curvature flow if $(\frac {\partial}{\partial t} \mathbf {x}) ^ {\perp} = \mathbf {H} _ {M (t)} (\mathbf {x})$.
Mean curvature flow is the gradient flow of area.

nodes.label: Mean curvature operator for graphs over a cone
nodes.summary: The nonlinear mean curvature operator applied to a normal graph over the cone. Its zero set gives minimal graphical perturbations.

evidence.quote: We can write the mean curvature operator as  $\mathcal {M} _ {\mathbf {C}} (u)$

nodes.label: Mean curvature self-expander
nodes.summary: A solution to mean curvature flow that evolves by self-similar expansion. It appears as the positive-time continuation associated to cones and asymptotically conical shrinkers.

evidence.quote: A natural class of solutions to mean curvature flow, starting from an initial (smooth) cone, are self-similarly expanding solutions
We call a self-expander and denote the corresponding immortal solution to mean curvature flow

nodes.label: Mean-curvature vector
nodes.summary: The normal vector field whose direction and magnitude give the mean curvature motion of a surface. It is the velocity vector in the mean-curvature-flow equation used throughout the paper.

evidence.quote: A maximal surface in $\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ is a spacelike surface on which the mean curvature vector vanishes everywhere.

nodes.label: Mean curvature vector of group orbits
aliases: mean curvature vector of the fibers
nodes.summary: For orbit fibers of a Riemannian submersion, the trace of the second fundamental form over a vertical orthonormal frame.

evidence.quote: mean curvature vector of the fibers

nodes.label: Mean curvature vector of a submanifold
nodes.summary: The normal vector field obtained by tracing the second fundamental form with respect to the induced metric. It enters the Sobolev inequality through the term f^2 |H|^2.

evidence.quote: H denotes the mean curvature vector

nodes.label: Mean-normalized displacement space
nodes.summary: The Hilbert space of $W^{1,2}$ vector fields $w$ on $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$ satisfying zero mean and zero averaged normal component $\fint\langle w,x\rangle=0$. It is the normalized space in which the linearized conformal-isoperimetric quadratic form is analyzed.

evidence.section: 4.2 On the coercivity of the quadratic form

nodes.label: Measure Contraction Property
nodes.summary: A synthetic lower Ricci curvature condition controlling the contraction of measure along geodesics toward a point. The paper treats MCP together with CD^1 and uses it in temporal regularity arguments.

nodes.label: Measure density at a point
nodes.summary: The h-density of a measure at a point is the limit of the measure of a radius-r ball centered at that point divided by r^h as r tends to zero. The paper focuses on the h = 2n+1 density in Heisenberg groups.

nodes.label: Measure disintegration
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a measure into conditional measures over the elements of a measurable partition. The paper uses disintegration for optimal-transport partitions.

nodes.label: Measured Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: Gromov-Hausdorff convergence together with convergence of renormalized measures, producing a pointed metric measure limit. This is the refined convergence notion used for collapsed hyperkähler limits.

nodes.label: Measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent space
nodes.summary: A measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent space is obtained as a blow-up limit of a pointed metric measure space, retaining both the metric and the measure. The paper compares its new tangent-measure notion with this concept for doubling measures.

evidence.quote: equivalent to the measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent space when the measure is doubling

nodes.label: Meeks-Yau welding of barriers
nodes.summary: A gluing method used to join local graphical barriers near an expander with far-field barriers, producing global super- and subsolutions for comparison arguments.

evidence.quote: we can weld (in the sense of Meeks–Yau) the graph of $f$ to the graph of a constant function $h$ over $\Sigma$ to obtain a global barrier
we can weld to to obtain a global supersolution to mean curvature flow. Similarly, we can weld to to obtain a global subsolution

nodes.label: Meromorphic continuation of the Ruelle zeta function
nodes.summary: The analytic continuation property that extends the initially convergent Ruelle zeta product to a meromorphic function on the complex plane.

nodes.label: Metric cone
nodes.summary: A metric space of the form C(Y), used as the conical factor in a tangent cone that splits off Euclidean space.

nodes.label: Metric-cone rigidity from constant volume ratio
nodes.summary: The rigidity principle that if the Bishop-Gromov normalized volume ratio is constant, the metric space is a metric cone.

evidence.quote: nu(R) is constant in R, which implies that (W,omega_W) must be a metric cone

nodes.label: Metric distance to a base point
nodes.summary: The distance from a point x to a fixed base point o with respect to the Riemannian metric. The paper denotes this distance by r(x).

evidence.quote: r(x) denotes the metric distance to a base point  $o$

nodes.label: Metric flow
nodes.summary: A synthetic parabolic object consisting of a set with a time function, complete separable metric time-slices, and conjugate heat kernel measures satisfying compatibility, gradient, and reproduction conditions. It is the limiting object for pointed sequences of super Ricci flows.

nodes.label: Metric flow pair
nodes.summary: A metric flow equipped with a conjugate heat flow of full support on almost every time-slice. The conjugate heat flow acts as a rough center or basepoint for noncompact time-slices.

nodes.label: Metric interval
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional compact metric segment serving as the limiting space for the collapsing K3 metrics in the paper.

nodes.label: Metric Lie group
nodes.summary: A Lie group equipped with a left-invariant Riemannian metric. The paper uses CMC surface theory in metric Lie groups as background for the classification proof.

evidence.quote: constant mean curvature surface theory in metric Lie groups

nodes.label: Metric-measure space
nodes.summary: A space given by a metric structure and a reference measure, here denoted (X, d, m). It is the ambient object on which synthetic curvature-dimension conditions are imposed.

evidence.quote: all the metric measure spaces (M_i,d_i,m_i) for i = 1,...,ell
Recall that a metric measure space (M,d,m) is called infinitesimally Hilbertian

nodes.label: Metric product splitting of a CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A decomposition of a CAT(0) space as a nontrivial metric product; for geodesically complete CAT(0) spaces it corresponds to a spherical join decomposition of the Tits boundary.

evidence.quote: induced by a metric product decomposition

nodes.label: Metric rescaling
nodes.summary: The operation of replacing a metric g_j by lambda_j^2 g_j to examine geometry at a different scale. It is used to define bubble limits near curvature concentration.

nodes.label: Metric rescaling at infinity
nodes.summary: The operation of scaling a pointed metric space by factors tending to zero in order to study its large-scale limit at infinity.

evidence.quote: Denote by (X_j,J_j,g_j,Omega_j,p_j) the rescaling of X by a factor lambda^j.

nodes.label: Metric semidirect product
nodes.summary: A semidirect product Lie group equipped with a left-invariant Riemannian metric. It is one of the ambient metric Lie group cases treated in the proof.

nodes.label: Metric space
nodes.summary: A set equipped with a distance function. The paper studies discrete metric spaces, especially Euclidean subsets with their induced Euclidean distances.

evidence.quote: Riemannian embeddings of certain discrete metric spaces are rather rigid

nodes.label: Tangent cone
nodes.summary: A blow-up limit of the metric space at a point. The paper studies which tangent cones occur at points in each singular stratum.

evidence.quote: for  $n - 4$  a.e.  $x \in \mathcal{S}(X)$  that the tangent cone of  $X$  at  $x$  is unique
On a noncollapsed RCD(K,N) metric measure space any tangent cone is a metric cone.

nodes.label: Metric tangent measure
aliases: tangent measure in an arbitrary metric space
nodes.summary: The paper introduces a tangent-measure notion for measures on arbitrary metric spaces. It is designed to agree with Preiss tangent measures in Euclidean ambient spaces and with measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent spaces under a doubling hypothesis.

evidence.quote: a generalisation of Preiss's tangent measures that is suitable for the setting of arbitrary metric spaces
This definition is equivalent to that of Preiss when the ambient space is Euclidean, and equivalent to the measured Gromov-Hausdorff tangent space when the measure is doubling.

nodes.label: Metrically rigid subset of a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A subset X of an n-dimensional complete connected Riemannian manifold M is metrically rigid if every isometric embedding of X into another complete connected n-dimensional Riemannian manifold forces the target to be isometric to M.

evidence.quote: X isometrically embeds in a complete, connected, n-dimensional Riemannian manifold \tilde{M}, necessarily \tilde{M} is isometric to M

nodes.label: Microlocal approach to dynamical zeta functions
nodes.summary: A method that studies dynamical zeta functions through Pollicott-Ruelle resonant states, wavefront conditions, and microlocal analysis on the cotangent bundle.

evidence.quote: microlocal approach to dynamical zeta functions

nodes.label: Microlocal Laplacian observability estimate
nodes.summary: An estimate controlling the global L^2 norm of a function by a microlocal observation Op_h(a)u plus the semiclassical Laplacian residual, assuming a is nonzero on the cosphere bundle.

nodes.label: Min-max construction for the trivial abelian Higgs bundle
nodes.summary: A variational method on the trivial bundle using disk-parametrized families and a width for E_epsilon. Together with Palais-Smale compactness, it produces nontrivial critical pairs with uniform energy control.

evidence.quote: min-max methods may be applied to the functionals E_epsilon to produce nontrivial critical points in the trivial bundle L=M x C
evidence.section: 7.1 Min-max families for the trivial line bundle

nodes.label: Minimal closed symmetric set at infinity
nodes.summary: For a boundary point xi, B_xi is the closure of the minimal visually symmetric subset generated by xi, and is the minimal closed symmetric set containing xi.

evidence.quote: minimal closed symmetric set containing xi

nodes.label: Minimal Einstein submanifold
nodes.summary: A submanifold whose induced metric is Einstein and whose mean curvature vector in the ambient manifold vanishes.

nodes.label: minimal-hypersurface core
nodes.summary: A connected compact manifold with boundary obtained by maximal successive cutting along minimal hypersurfaces. In the contradiction argument its boundary has contracting behavior and interior minimal hypersurfaces satisfy a Frankel-type intersection property.

nodes.label: Minimal hypersurface index form
nodes.summary: The quadratic form Q(f,f)=int_M |grad f|^2 - int_M |A_M|^2 f^2 used to define the Morse index of a two-sided minimal hypersurface in Euclidean space.

nodes.label: Scalar-curvature identity for Euclidean minimal hypersurfaces
nodes.summary: For a minimal hypersurface in Euclidean space, the induced scalar curvature equals minus the squared norm of the second fundamental form. In this paper the sign is central in the monotonicity argument.

nodes.label: Minimal immersion
nodes.summary: An immersion of a manifold as a hypersurface with vanishing mean curvature. The paper studies complete minimal immersions into Euclidean and Riemannian four-manifolds.

nodes.label: Minimal lamination limit of H-disks
nodes.summary: The limiting behavior, after subsequence, of compact embedded H-disks whose boundaries diverge in Euclidean three-space: the sequence has a minimal lamination-type limit, with alternatives including smooth convergence to a helicoid or convergence away from a singular line to a foliation by planes.

evidence.quote: obtain (after passing to a subsequence) minimal lamination limits for any sequence of compact disks M_n embedded in \mathbb{R}^3 with constant mean curvature H_n, when the boundaries of these disks tend to infinity
exactly one of the following two statements hold

nodes.label: Minimal submanifold in Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A Euclidean submanifold whose mean curvature vector vanishes. In this paper, minimality removes the mean-curvature term and yields the sharp isoperimetric inequality from the Sobolev inequality.

nodes.label: Energy minimization on nontrivial line bundles
nodes.summary: For a nontrivial Hermitian line bundle, global minimizers of E_epsilon supply nonzero critical pairs. Competitor constructions using transverse sections give uniform upper bounds, while a lower gap excludes zero energy.

evidence.quote: when the line bundle is nontrivial ... take (u_epsilon,nabla_epsilon) to be a global minimizer for E_epsilon

nodes.label: Minimizing $(p,q)$-singularity
nodes.summary: A singularity of a minimal surface whose local model is an area-minimizing quadratic cone $\mathbf{C}^{p,q}$. It is the class of singularities whose nearby structure the paper analyzes.

evidence.quote: singularities modeled on area-minimizing quadratic cones  $\mathbf{C}^{p,q}$ , i.e., so-called minimizing  $(p,q)$ -singularities

nodes.label: Minimizing quadratic cone
nodes.summary: An area-minimizing cone from the quadratic family $\mathbf{C}^{p,q}$, modeled by a quadratic relation between Euclidean radial variables. These cones are the singular models studied in the paper.

nodes.label: Minkowski area functional for timelike graphs
nodes.summary: The graph area functional measured with the Minkowski metric, L(u)=int sqrt(1-u_t^2+|nabla_x u|^2) dx. Its Euler-Lagrange equation is the timelike minimal surface equation.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Minkowski spacetime
nodes.summary: A flat Lorentzian manifold with signature convention used here as (-1,1,...,1). It is the ambient spacetime in which the timelike hypersurface graph is embedded.

evidence.quote: Let $n\geq 2$, and ${\mathfrak{M}}^{n+2}$ be the $n+2$ dimensional Minkowski space-time.
evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Min-max energy-density maximizer for the first weighted eigenvalue
aliases: V_1 maximizer from min-max harmonic spheres
nodes.summary: In dimensions 3 through 5, the energy densities |du_k|^2 of the stabilized smooth min-max sphere-valued harmonic maps realize V_1(M,g); equality for admissible measures occurs exactly for harmonic-map energy-density measures.

evidence.section: 1.3.2 Existence and regularity of maximizers for V_1
1.3.2 Existence and regularity of maximizers for V_1

nodes.label: Minmax Palais-Smale variational scheme
nodes.summary: A variational construction that uses minmax levels to produce Palais-Smale sequences and hence critical points when sufficient compactness is available. The paper applies it to the subcritical functionals.

evidence.quote: we would like to use a variational method, more precisely a minmax method, to produce a converging Palais-Smale sequence

nodes.label: Mirzakhani frequency of multicurves
nodes.summary: An asymptotic counting frequency for topological types of multicurves in Mirzakhani's framework, used to define or analyze the random multicurve distribution.

nodes.label: Mobius transformation of the sphere
nodes.summary: A conformal sphere self-map represented in the paper as an orthogonal transformation composed with a stereographic dilation map. These transformations form the reference class in the conformal rigidity estimate.

nodes.label: rectifiable mod 2 flat chain
nodes.summary: A mod 2 flat chain with rectifiable boundary used to define relative cycle spaces in the Almgren-Pitts framework.

nodes.label: Mod-Poisson convergence
nodes.summary: A convergence framework for integer-valued random variables based on Poisson-normalized transforms, used here as part of the asymptotic analysis of non-uniform permutation statistics.

nodes.label: Model cone metric along a smooth divisor
nodes.summary: The local product cone metric used to define conical behavior near a smooth divisor, with singular radial weight in the coordinate normal to D and Euclidean directions tangent to D.

evidence.quote: quasi-isometric to the model cone metric

nodes.label: Modified Milnor problem
nodes.summary: The problem asking whether every finitely presented group with vanishing algebraic entropy has at most polynomial growth.

evidence.quote: A finitely presented group with vanishing algebraic entropy has at most polynomial growth.

nodes.label: Moduli space of holomorphic quadratic differentials
nodes.summary: The genus-g moduli space whose points are holomorphic quadratic differentials, denoted in the excerpt by Q_g and equipped with Masur-Veech volume.

evidence.quote: Masur-Veech volume  $\mathrm{Vol}\mathcal{Q}_g$  of the moduli space of holomorphic quadratic differentials

nodes.label: Moment map formula for nilmanifold Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: A real GIT moment-map interpretation of the Ricci curvature of nilpotent Lie groups with left-invariant metrics.

evidence.quote: moment map interpretation of the Ricci curvature
evidence.section: The scalar curvature of the N-orbits

nodes.label: Entropy of the Monge-Ampère density
nodes.summary: The entropy integral of the Monge-Ampere density, represented in the paper by an upper bound for integral e^F F dvol_g when omega_phi^n = e^F omega_0^n. It is a main analytic input for compactness and estimates.

nodes.label: Monge-Ampère $p$-energy
nodes.summary: An energy functional measuring a $p$-moment of the potential against the Monge-Ampère density. In the excerpt it appears as the normalizing quantity in the auxiliary equation and in the Trudinger scale.

nodes.label: monodromic transverse-singular trajectory
nodes.summary: A convergent transverse-singular trajectory whose image meets some transverse section to the characteristic foliation in infinitely many points.

nodes.label: Morrey-type $L^\sigma$ potential bound
nodes.summary: A nonnegative potential $V$ satisfies this condition when its $L^\sigma$ norm over boundary balls has the scale-invariant upper bound used in the Fefferman-Phong trace estimate.

nodes.label: Morse flat
nodes.summary: In this paper, a periodic n-flat is Morse when it does not bound a flat (n+1)-half-space.

evidence.quote: called Morse, if it does not bound

nodes.label: Morse index of the stability operator
nodes.summary: The number of negative directions for the quadratic form associated with the stability operator, counted with multiplicity. The paper's nonexceptional CMC spheres have index one.

evidence.quote: has index 1 and nullity 3 for its stability operator

nodes.label: Euler-Lagrange equation for the constrained Moser-Trudinger functional
nodes.summary: The PDE characterizing positive critical points of $F$ constrained to $\mathcal E_\beta$: $\Delta_{g_0}u+u=2\lambda f u e^{u^2}$ with $u>0$ and positive multiplier $\lambda$.

evidence.quote: $\Delta_ {g _ {0}} u + u = 2 \lambda f u e ^ {u ^ {2}}, \quad u > 0 \text {i n} \Sigma$

nodes.label: Moser-Trudinger Lagrange multiplier
nodes.summary: The positive scalar enforcing the $H^1$-norm constraint in the Euler-Lagrange equation. For the constrained critical Moser-Trudinger functional it satisfies $2\lambda\int_\Sigma u^2 e^{u^2} f\,dv_{g_0}=\beta=\|u\|_{H^1}^2$.

evidence.quote: where  $\lambda > 0$  is given by  $2 \lambda \int_ {\Sigma} u ^ {2} e ^ {u ^ {2}} f d v _ {g _ {0}} = \beta$

nodes.label: Moving plane method
nodes.summary: A reflection and comparison method used to prove symmetry of solutions to geometric or elliptic-parabolic problems.

nodes.label: Moving plane method without assuming smoothness
nodes.summary: A variant of the moving plane method in which smoothness and symmetry are proved simultaneously, allowing application to Brakke flows before smoothness is known.

evidence.quote: moving plane method without assuming smoothness
smoothness and symmetry are established in tandem

nodes.label: Homology independence of multicurve components
nodes.summary: A property of a multicurve saying that its primitive components represent linearly independent classes in surface homology.

evidence.quote: primitive components  $\gamma_1, \ldots, \gamma_k$  ... represent linearly independent homology cycles with asymptotic probability 1

nodes.label: Multiphase principal-eigenvalue shape optimization problem
nodes.summary: A shape optimization problem over pairwise disjoint open subsets Omega_i of a bounded domain D that minimizes the sum of the first Dirichlet eigenvalue lambda_1(Omega_i) plus a positive volume penalty m_i |Omega_i| for each phase.

evidence.quote: regularity of minimizers of the multiphase spectral optimization problem for the principal eigenvalue of the Dirichlet Laplacian
open; \Omega_ {i} \cap \Omega_ {j} = \emptyset f o r i \neq j

nodes.label: Flat disk with integer multiplicity
nodes.summary: A flat hyperplane or disk counted with an integer multiplicity, used as a local tangent or comparison varifold. The paper distinguishes the critical case of multiplicity $Q$ from the lower multiplicity case $<Q$.

evidence.quote: $V$  is close to a flat disk of multiplicity  $Q$
one tangent cone at an interior point  $y$  equal to an (oriented) hyperplane  $P$  of multiplicity  $p / 2$

nodes.label: Multiplicity-one conjecture for embedded two-spheres
aliases: Ilmanen multiplicity one conjecture for two-spheres
nodes.summary: The assertion that for mean curvature flow starting at a closed embedded two-sphere, all tangent flows have multiplicity one. The paper's two-sphere uniqueness application is conditional on this conjecture.

evidence.quote: all tangent flows have multiplicity one

nodes.label: Multiplicity-one tangent flow
nodes.summary: A tangent flow whose varifold or measure multiplicity is one. The mean-convex-neighborhood and embedded-sphere applications assume or prove conclusions from this condition.

nodes.label: Munteanu-Wang Green's-function monotonicity extension
nodes.summary: An extension of Munteanu-Wang's sharp monotonicity formula for Green's-function flux on non-parabolic three-manifolds, modified to include the negative scalar-curvature defect A(s) and weak a priori bounds for F(s).

evidence.section: 4. An extension of Munteanu-Wang's monotonicity formula

nodes.label: Mutual singularity of measures
nodes.summary: A family of measures is mutually singular when its members are supported on pairwise disjoint measurable parts. The erroneous tensorization proof attempted to infer mutual singularity of partial midpoints from mutual singularity of partial marginals.

evidence.quote: the nu_{0,j} for j=1,...,n are mutually singular
does not imply mutual singularity of the partial midpoints

nodes.label: n-flat in a CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A closed convex subset of a CAT(0) space isometric to Euclidean n-space; its Tits boundary is a round (n-1)-sphere.

evidence.quote: closed convex subset isometric to R^n

nodes.label: Perturbation to an N-invariant hyperkähler triple
nodes.summary: A deformation method that averages a nearby definite triple over the nilpotent structure and then solves the hyperkähler equation by an implicit-function argument to obtain a genuinely N-invariant hyperkähler triple with the same collapsed limit.

nodes.label: (N-1)-rectifiable set
nodes.summary: A subset covered up to H^{N-1}-null error by countably many pieces biLipschitz to Borel subsets of R^{N-1}. This is the regularity class proved for the boundary.

evidence.quote: a set E subset X is said to be (N-1)-rectifiable provided ... H^{N-1}(M)=0 and the E_i are biLipschitz to a Borel subset of R^{N-1}
evidence.section: 1.2 An epsilon-regularity theorem for top dimensional singularities

nodes.label: n-torus
nodes.summary: The n-dimensional torus, canonically the product of n circles, appearing as a central example of an aspherical manifold.

nodes.label: n-varifold
nodes.summary: A Radon measure on the Grassmannian bundle of unoriented n-planes over an open set or manifold. Varifolds are the weak objects used as min-max limits.

nodes.label: n-volume functional
nodes.summary: The functional assigning n-dimensional volume to hypersurfaces in an (n+1)-dimensional Riemannian manifold. Minimal hypersurfaces are its critical points.

nodes.label: Nakajima metric on the centered Hilbert scheme
nodes.summary: The natural complete hyperKahler metric on Hilb_0^n(C^2) obtained by hyperKahler quotient construction. The paper uses its QALE/QAC compactification to prove the Vafa-Witten Hodge cohomology prediction.

nodes.label: Natural topology on a metric flow
nodes.summary: A topology on the underlying set of a metric flow generated by parabolic neighborhoods built from its kernel-based geometry. For metric flows associated to smooth super Ricci flows over left-open intervals it agrees with the product topology on M x I.

evidence.section: 3.6 The natural topology on a metric flow

nodes.label: Near-optimal relative spectral gap for random hyperbolic covers
aliases: relative spectral gap for random covers
nodes.summary: For a fixed finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surface X, a uniform random degree n cover has, asymptotically almost surely, no Laplacian eigenvalues below 1/4 - epsilon except old eigenvalues from X, with the same multiplicities. This is the paper's main reusable spectral-gap property.

evidence.quote: has no eigenvalues of the Laplacian in  $[0, \frac{1}{4} - \epsilon)$  other than those of  $X$
and the multiplicities on both sides are the same

nodes.label: Near-periodic geodesic set over a subset
nodes.summary: The set of geodesics based over a subset $W\subset M$ that return close to their initial data at the relevant scales. The excerpt states the condition but omits the precise closeness and time parameters.

evidence.quote: the set of near periodic geodesics over $W\subset M$ has small measure

nodes.label: Nearly circular ellipse
nodes.summary: An ellipse whose eccentricity is below a fixed sufficiently small threshold epsilon_0.

nodes.label: Neck decomposition
nodes.summary: A decomposition of the relevant singular or quantitative-stratum region into neck regions and complementary pieces, used to obtain rectifiability and packing estimates.

evidence.quote: 7.7. Proof of Neck Decomposition of Theorem 7.1

nodes.label: Neck decomposition for noncollapsed RCD spaces
nodes.summary: A quantitative decomposition of a noncollapsed RCD space into neck regions, almost N-symmetric balls, and a residual set of codimension at least two, with summability control over covering radii. It is used to derive rectifiability and measure estimates.

evidence.quote: any noncollapsed RCD(K,N) space can be decomposed into neck-regions, (N,epsilon)-symmetric balls and a set of codimension at least 2

nodes.label: Neck region
nodes.summary: A controlled region around a quantitative stratum where the space is organized by approximate conical or product structure and where harmonic splittings can be analyzed.

evidence.quote: neck region analysis developed by Jiang-Naber-Valtorta
Proving the  $L^2$  -curvature bound on Neck Regions

nodes.label: Neck region for boundary analysis
nodes.summary: A subset of a ball obtained by removing controlled balls around a closed approximate singular set, with every relevant center-scale ball modeled on a half-space and controlled by a δ-splitting map. Neck regions are the main quantitative covering objects for boundary regularity.

evidence.quote: N subset B_2(p) is an (epsilon,delta)-neck region if there exist a closed set C subset B_1(p) and a function r:C->[0,1/8]
for any x in C and r_x <= r <= tau^{-3}, B_r(x) is an epsilon^2-boundary ball
there exists a delta^4-splitting map u:B_{tau^{-4}}(p)->R^{N-1}

nodes.label: Einstein manifold with negative scalar curvature
nodes.summary: An Einstein Riemannian manifold with Einstein constant \lambda<0, hence negative scalar curvature in the paper's normalization.

nodes.label: Negative valuation
nodes.summary: A valuation on the coordinate ring induced by the degree function of a compatible Calabi-Yau metric, taking values in negative real numbers. It is called semistable or polystable when its associated graded ring defines a K-semistable or K-polystable Fano cone.

evidence.quote: one notices that deg is a valuation on R with values in R_{<0}. We call such a valuation a negative valuation.
We say a negative valuation is semistable (resp. polystable) if it defines a filtration whose associated graded ring ... defines a K-semistable (resp. K-polystable) Fano cone

nodes.label: Negatively curved Riemannian 3-manifold
nodes.summary: A three-dimensional Riemannian manifold whose sectional curvatures are negative. In the paper this is the geometric setting where the geodesic flow is Anosov and the Ruelle zeta function is studied.

nodes.label: Net in Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A discrete subset L of R^n such that some delta-neighborhood of L covers all of R^n; equivalently, every point of R^n lies within a fixed finite distance of L. Lattices are examples.

evidence.quote: a discrete set L \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n is a net if there exists \delta > 0 such that d(x, L) < \delta
any n-dimensional lattice in \mathbb{R}^n is a net

nodes.label: Neumann boundary condition
nodes.summary: The boundary condition requiring the exterior normal derivative of the eigenfunction to vanish on the boundary.

nodes.label: Neumann Laplacian counting function
nodes.summary: The function N^N_Ω(λ) counting Neumann eigenvalues μ_n(Ω) not exceeding λ², with multiplicities included.

nodes.label: Neumann Laplacian eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: The boundary value problem -Δu = μu in Ω with ∂_n u=0 on ∂Ω, for domains with sufficiently regular boundary. Its spectrum is discrete, starts at μ_1=0, and has finite multiplicities.

nodes.label: Neutral mode analysis for rotationally symmetric ancient solutions
nodes.summary: A spectral or modal part of the uniqueness argument controlling the neutral coefficient a(tau), whose derivative is decomposed into error integrals I_k(tau) and estimated by regional energy bounds.

nodes.label: Neutral-mode inward quadratic neck expansion
nodes.summary: In the neutral-mode case, the leading quadratic coefficient is negative of order 1/|tau| while mixed neutral coefficients are lower order. This expresses inward bending of the central neck and forces compactness.

nodes.label: Nilmanifold
nodes.summary: A compact quotient of a nilpotent Lie group by a lattice. The paper's regular collapse fibers include torus and Heisenberg nilmanifold examples.

nodes.label: Nilpotent group
nodes.summary: A group whose lower central series terminates in the trivial subgroup after finitely many steps.

nodes.label: Nilpotent Killing N-structure
nodes.summary: The Cheeger-Fukaya-Gromov nilpotent symmetry structure carried by sufficiently collapsed manifolds with bounded curvature. In this paper it organizes the local infranil or nilmanifold fibers of collapsed hyperkähler sequences.

nodes.label: Nilpotent Lie group
nodes.summary: A Lie group whose Lie algebra has a finite descending central series ending in zero. Nilpotent Lie groups underlie nilmanifolds such as the Heisenberg nilmanifold.

nodes.label: Nilpotent structure on a collapsed K3 surface
nodes.summary: The nilmanifold-fibered structure organizing the collapsed K3 geometry over an interval. In this excerpt it is reflected by regular fibers that are tori or Heisenberg nilmanifolds.

evidence.quote: NILPOTENT STRUCTURES AND COLLAPSING RICCI-FLAT METRICS

nodes.label: Integrability of the nilradical-horizontal distribution
nodes.summary: The property that the distribution orthogonal to the nilradical orbits is integrable. It is equivalent here to the nilradical action being polar.

nodes.label: Nilradical of a Lie group
nodes.summary: The maximal connected nilpotent normal Lie subgroup of a Lie group; for non-unimodular groups in the paper it has positive dimension.

evidence.quote: maximal connected, nilpotent, normal Lie subgroup

nodes.label: Nilsoliton geometry of nilradical orbits
nodes.summary: The induced metrics on nilradical orbits are pairwise isometric and locally isometric to a fixed nilsoliton.

evidence.quote: N-orbits are pairwise isometric and locally isometric to nilsolitons
evidence.section: The scalar curvature of the N-orbits

nodes.label: Nilradical polarity and Einstein-leaf rigidity
aliases: nilradical rigidity for noncompact Einstein manifolds with symmetry
nodes.summary: For a negative Einstein manifold with a non-unimodular cocompact principal isometric action, the nilradical action is polar and the manifold is foliated by locally isometric minimal Einstein leaves.

evidence.quote: nilradical N of G acts polarly

nodes.label: Nilsoliton metric
aliases: left-invariant Ricci soliton on a nilpotent group
nodes.summary: A left-invariant Ricci soliton metric on a nilpotent Lie group, equivalently a nilpotent metric whose Ricci endomorphism differs from a scalar multiple of the identity by a derivation.

evidence.quote: left-invariant Ricci soliton on the universal cover of N

nodes.label: no concentration of mass for sweepouts
nodes.summary: A compactness condition for a sweepout requiring the supremal mass in small balls, over all slices and centers, to vanish as the radius tends to zero.

nodes.label: no-corner property for sub-Riemannian minimizers
nodes.summary: A minimizing geodesic that is differentiable from the left and from the right at a point is differentiable at that point, ruling out corner-type singularities.

nodes.label: No mass loss for homogenised eigenfunctions
nodes.summary: The weak limits of harmonic extensions preserve the boundary L^2 orthonormality as weighted L^2(M,beta dmu_g) orthonormality, preventing collapse to lower-index weighted Laplace eigenpairs.

nodes.label: No semistability at infinity
aliases: elimination of the intermediate semistable cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The property that the intermediate K-semistable cone W in the asymptotic two-step degeneration is already isomorphic to the asymptotic Calabi-Yau cone C, so the degeneration can be realized in one step.

evidence.quote: W is isomorphic to C as normal affine algebraic cones.
one can degenerate the variety to C in one step

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean Ding functional
nodes.summary: A non-Archimedean functional defined on test configurations and involving log discrepancies of divisorial valuations with respect to (X,theta). It is the functional used to define twisted Ding-stability in the paper.

evidence.quote: non-Archimedean Ding functional, defined on test configurations and involving the log discrepancy

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean Mabuchi functional
nodes.summary: A non-Archimedean energy assigned to a test configuration by an intersection formula involving the compactified total space and log relative canonical divisor; it is the functional in the G-uniform K-stability inequality.

nodes.label: Non-Archimedean metric
nodes.summary: A metric-like non-Archimedean object associated with algebraic or valuative degenerations of a polarized variety. The paper compares these metrics with geodesic rays.

evidence.quote: the relationship between geodesic rays and non-Archimedean metrics

nodes.label: Non-aspherical target manifold
nodes.summary: A target manifold is non-aspherical in the sense used here when it has a nontrivial higher homotopy group, in particular pi_l(N) nonzero for some l at least 3 in the main construction.

nodes.label: Non-branching geodesic property
nodes.summary: A geodesic non-branching condition saying that geodesics which agree on a nontrivial initial interval do not split into different continuations. It underlies the paper's essentially non-branching hypothesis and transport-ray analysis.

nodes.label: Non-branching metric-measure space
nodes.summary: A structural condition intended to rule out branching of geodesic transport paths. The correction says this hypothesis is insufficient for the cited tensorization arguments and must be replaced by stronger hypotheses.

evidence.quote: the assumption non-branching has to be replaced by the stronger assumption
mutual singularity of the partial marginals (and non-branching property) does not imply mutual singularity of the partial midpoints

nodes.label: Non-concentration of $L^2$-excess
nodes.summary: A quantitative principle preventing the $L^2$-excess from concentrating at the conical singularity. It is one of the proof mechanisms for excess decay.

nodes.label: Non-looping geodesic condition
nodes.summary: A dynamical condition excluding or quantitatively limiting geodesic loops relevant to spectral sums. The excerpt only states that Kuznecov-sum improvements are proved under non-looping type assumptions.

nodes.label: Non-trapped null geodesic
nodes.summary: A null geodesic whose maximal domain of definition is a bounded interval, so both ends leave the manifold after extending slightly beyond the boundary. The paper assumes all null geodesics have this property.

evidence.quote: maximal domain of definition is a bounded interval

nodes.label: Non-uniform random permutation measure
nodes.summary: An explicit non-uniform probability measure on the symmetric group S_{3g-3} whose cycle-count statistics approximate multicurve component counts and square-tiled-surface cylinder counts.

evidence.quote: an explicit non-uniform measure on the symmetric group of  $3g - 3$  elements

nodes.label: Noncollapsed RCD(K,N) space
nodes.summary: An RCD(K,N) metric-measure space whose reference measure is the N-dimensional Hausdorff measure H^N. These are the ambient spaces whose boundary structure and stability are studied in the paper.

evidence.quote: non-collapsed RCD(K,N) spaces, that is, metric-measure spaces (X,d,H^N) with Ricci curvature bounded below.

nodes.label: Noncollapsed Ricci-limit space
aliases: Ricci-limit space with lower Ricci bound
nodes.summary: A pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit of Riemannian manifolds with a uniform lower Ricci curvature bound and a uniform positive lower bound on unit-ball volume.

evidence.quote: Gromov-Hausdorff limit spaces  $(M_i^n, g_i, p_i) \xrightarrow{d_{GH}} (X^n, d, p)$  of Riemannian manifolds satisfying a uniform lower Ricci curvature bound
if  $(M_j^n, d_j, p_j) \longrightarrow (X, d, p)$  is a  $GH$ -limit of noncollapsed manifolds with bounded Ric curvature

nodes.label: Noncollapsing volume condition
nodes.summary: A positive lower bound on the volume of a unit ball in the approximating manifolds.

nodes.label: Laplacian spectrum structure on finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surfaces
aliases: small discrete spectrum and continuous spectrum threshold
nodes.summary: For a finite-area non-compact hyperbolic surface, the Laplacian has finitely many discrete eigenvalues below 1/4 and absolutely continuous spectrum from 1/4 onward. This makes 1/4 the relevant optimal spectral-gap threshold.

evidence.quote: spectrum of  $\Delta_X$  in  $[0, \frac{1}{4})$  consists of finitely many discrete eigenvalues
the spectrum is absolutely continuous in  $[\frac{1}{4}, \infty)$

nodes.label: p-width of a complete noncompact manifold
nodes.summary: For a complete noncompact manifold, the p-width is defined as the limit of p-widths over any compact exhaustion by smooth-boundary submanifolds.

nodes.label: Nonfattening for spherical or cylindrical singularities
aliases: nonfattening conjecture for cylindrical or spherical singularities
nodes.summary: The property that a level set flow does not fatten as long as all its singularities are cylindrical or spherical. The paper obtains it from mean-convex neighborhoods and nondiscrepancy results.

evidence.quote: level set flow does not fatten
Nonfattening of mean curvature flow at singularities of mean convex type

evidence.section: 11 The first non-generic time for flows in R3

nodes.label: Nonfattening of a level set flow
nodes.summary: The property that no positive-time slice of the level set flow develops interior. In this paper nonfattening of the model cone is the hypothesis under which the two outermost evolutions agree for short time.

evidence.quote: We say that the level set flow of is non-fattening if has no interior for each
evidence.section: 2.1. Spacetime and the level set flow

evidence.quote: non-fattening of $\mathcal{C}$ implies short-time non-fattening of $M$

nodes.label: Nonfattening uniqueness for conical level set flows
aliases: short-time uniqueness in the nonfattening cone case
nodes.summary: When the model cone does not fatten, the inner and outer flows of the singular hypersurface agree and are smooth for a short time, giving a unique evolution through the conical singularity.

evidence.quote: modeled on a smooth cone $\mathcal{C}$ which does not fatten. Then, there is $T>0$ such that the outermost flows of $M$ agree (and are smooth) for $t\in(0,T)$. Especially, the evolution of $M$ is unique on this time interval.
If does not fatten under the level set flow, then the inner and outer flows agree

nodes.label: Nonlinear entire anisotropic minimizing graph over R4
aliases: anisotropic Bernstein counterexample in dimension four
nodes.summary: A family of smooth nonlinear functions u:R^4 to R with a uniformly elliptic integrand Phi on R^5 such that graph(u) minimizes A_Phi. The family is parametrized by growth exponents mu in (0,1/2).

evidence.quote: smooth nonlinear function u : R4 -> R
graph of u in R5 minimizes A_Phi

nodes.label: Nonlinear error in the cone mean curvature equation
nodes.summary: The nonlinear remainder after subtracting the Jacobi linearization from the cone graph mean curvature operator. The proof uses scale-invariant structure estimates for this error.

evidence.quote: the non-linear error part  $\mathcal{E}(u)$  satisfies certain, relatively standard scale-invariant structure conditions

nodes.label: Nonlinear null condition for quasilinear waves
nodes.summary: A cancellation condition for derivative-dependent quasilinear wave metrics requiring the cubic contraction of partial_p g with null covectors to vanish on the metric null cone. The paper verifies this property for the timelike minimal surface equation.

evidence.quote: we verify that the minimal surface equation indeed satisfies the nonlinear null condition

nodes.label: Nonnegative definite symmetric two-tensor
nodes.summary: A symmetric bilinear form T satisfying T(v,v) >= 0 for every tangent vector v. The super Ricci flow inequality is interpreted by requiring partial_t g_t + 2 Ric_g_t to have this property.

nodes.label: Nonnegative scalar curvature condition
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric satisfies this condition on a region when its scalar curvature is nonnegative at every point of that region.

evidence.quote: $g$  be a Riemannian metric which is defined on an open set containing  $\Omega$  and has nonnegative scalar curvature at each point in  $\Omega$

nodes.label: Nonnegative scalar curvature metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric whose scalar curvature is everywhere nonnegative.

nodes.label: Nonobtuse side-base angle condition
aliases: side-base angles at most pi over two
nodes.summary: The condition that every side-base dihedral angle of the comparison polyhedron is at most pi/2.

nodes.label: Non-parabolic Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A complete Riemannian manifold is non-parabolic when its Laplacian admits a positive Green's function. Stable minimal hypersurfaces in the paper have this property for n>2.

evidence.section: 2. The Green's function on a stable minimal hypersurface

nodes.label: Nonpositive sectional curvature
nodes.summary: A Riemannian curvature condition requiring every sectional curvature of the manifold to be nonpositive.

nodes.label: Non-separating multicurve
nodes.summary: A multicurve whose removal does not disconnect the underlying surface. The paper separates the non-separating case from separating contributions in its asymptotic analysis.

evidence.quote: Random non-separating multicurves and non-uniform random permutations

nodes.label: Normal coordinates relative to a hypersurface
nodes.summary: A coordinate system adapted to a hypersurface by using normal directions to the hypersurface. The excerpt gives this as an example of the normal gauge.

evidence.quote: normal coordinates relative to a hypersurface

nodes.label: normal extremal
nodes.summary: A trajectory of the sub-Riemannian Hamiltonian vector field whose projection gives a normal minimizing geodesic.

nodes.label: Normal gauge for metric tensors
nodes.summary: A gauge choice for tensor or metric analysis based on normal coordinates relative to a hypersurface. It is used to handle the tensor X-ray transform in the boundary and lens rigidity setting.

evidence.quote: in the normal gauge, such as normal coordinates relative to a hypersurface

nodes.label: Normal-gauge geodesic X-ray transform on 2-tensors
aliases: geodesic X-ray transform in the normal gauge
nodes.summary: The paper's central analytic setup: the geodesic X-ray transform on symmetric 2-tensors analyzed in normal gauge, with weights allowed.

evidence.quote: The key tool is the analysis of the geodesic X-ray transform on 2-tensors
in the normal gauge, such as normal coordinates relative to a hypersurface

nodes.label: Normal graph over a cone or foliation leaf
nodes.summary: A representation of a hypersurface as the graph of a normal function over a cone or over a translated and scaled Hardt-Simon leaf.

nodes.label: Normalization blow-up of sub-level sets
nodes.summary: A rescaling method that normalizes a sequence of sub-level sets of the solution to analyze limiting boundary geometry. The paper emphasizes that C^{1,1} boundaries create non-smooth limiting shapes under this procedure.

evidence.quote: By blowing-up, we mean to normalise a sequence of sub-level sets of the solution.
limit shape is not even $C^1$ smooth after the blowing-up

nodes.label: Normalized Laplace eigenvalue
nodes.summary: The k-th Laplace eigenvalue multiplied by the Riemannian area of the surface, lambda_k(M,g)=lambda_k(M,g) Area_g(M). This normalization is invariant under constant rescaling of the metric on a surface.

nodes.label: Normalized Laplace eigenvalue functional
nodes.summary: The scale-invariant quantity overline{lambda}_k(M,g)=Vol_g(M)^{2/d} lambda_k(M,g,1), used to formulate Laplace isoperimetric eigenvalue suprema.

nodes.label: Normalized Ricci-flow convergence to a space-form metric
nodes.summary: A convergence principle saying that suitable ordinary Ricci flows, after rescaling or normalization, converge smoothly to a constant-curvature metric. The paper invokes positive-curvature convergence for spherical space forms and normalized hyperbolic convergence in the hyperbolic case.

nodes.label: Normalized Steklov eigenvalue functional
nodes.summary: The scale-invariant quantity overline{sigma}_k(Omega,g)=H^{d-1}(partial Omega)^{1/(d-1)} sigma_k(Omega,g). It is the main functional optimized in the paper's Steklov isoperimetric applications.

nodes.label: Null geodesic
nodes.summary: A geodesic whose tangent vector is null with respect to the Lorentzian metric. Null geodesics determine causal propagation, boundary controllability, and Gaussian beams in the paper.

evidence.quote: inextendible null geodesic with initial data
evidence.section: 2.2. Reduction from Cauchy data set to the Dirichlet-to-Neumann map

nodes.label: Null-geodesic simplicity and spacelike exponential condition
nodes.summary: The condition that two points on a null geodesic are causally connected only along that geodesic, and that exp_p maps spacelike vectors diffeomorphically onto E_p. It prevents unwanted causal shortcuts and supplies global normal coordinates in the exterior.

evidence.quote: the only causal path between p and q is along γ

nodes.label: Nullity of the stability operator
nodes.summary: The dimension of the kernel of the stability operator. The paper distinguishes the nullity-one exceptional case from the nullity-three nonexceptional case.

evidence.quote: stable and has nullity 1 for its stability operator

nodes.label: O(4) line bundle over CP1
aliases: total space of the O(4) bundle
nodes.summary: The holomorphic line bundle O(4) over the Riemann sphere CP1, considered through its total space. The paper maps the ALF-D_k twistor space into this total space.

evidence.quote: total space of the  $\mathcal{O}(4)$  bundle over  $\mathbb{CP}^1$

nodes.label: Observability estimate for boundary wave control
nodes.summary: An inequality controlling final Cauchy data of a wave by boundary observations on an earlier boundary portion. In the paper it is obtained from geometric control plus the exterior unique continuation result.

nodes.label: Odd reflection symmetry over C11
nodes.summary: The barriers and bounded-domain solutions are odd under interchange of the two radial variables across C_11, so they vanish on the cone. This symmetry is essential for gluing the two sides into an entire graph.

nodes.label: Off-diagonal spectral projector asymptotics
nodes.summary: Asymptotic estimates for the spectral projector kernel away from the diagonal $x=y$, including logarithmic gains under a two-point geodesic incidence smallness assumption.

evidence.quote: logarithmic gains on asymptotics for the off-diagonal spectral projector $\Pi_{\lambda}(x,y)$

nodes.label: Old Laplacian eigenvalues of a cover
nodes.summary: Eigenvalues of the base surface Laplacian that also occur in every Riemannian cover, with at least the same multiplicity. The main theorem excludes additional small eigenvalues beyond these old eigenvalues.

evidence.quote: any eigenvalue of the Laplacian on  $X$  will be an eigenvalue of any cover of  $X$
these 'old eigenvalues' must always be taken into account

nodes.label: 1-codimensional measure in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A 1-codimensional measure in H^n is a measure whose relevant dimension is one less than the homogeneous dimension Q = 2n+2, namely 2n+1. The paper studies such measures through their (2n+1)-densities and tangent measures.

evidence.quote: study of tangential properties of measures with density in the Heisenberg groups

nodes.label: One-dimensional CD(K,N) density
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional density satisfying the curvature-dimension inequality or its differential characterization on an interval or transport ray. Such densities are the one-dimensional pieces appearing in localization formulations.

nodes.label: One-homogeneous function
nodes.summary: A function on a vector space satisfying degree-one positive homogeneity. In this paper it is a primitive condition imposed on anisotropic integrands.

nodes.label: One-phase Bernoulli free boundary problem
nodes.summary: The one-phase analogue of the Bernoulli free boundary problem controls the regularity of free-boundary points belonging to only one phase. The paper uses known one-phase regularity theory together with its two-phase analysis to describe the full free boundary.

evidence.quote: Combining Theorem 1.1 with the known regularity theory for one-phase problem

nodes.label: One-phase Stefan problem
nodes.summary: A free-boundary phase-transition problem for a nonnegative temperature whose positive phase satisfies the heat equation and whose interface evolves by the Stefan condition.

evidence.quote: the function \theta \geq 0 satisfies \partial_t\theta - \Delta\theta = 0 in the region \{\theta > 0\}

nodes.label: One-sided ancient rescaled mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: An ancient rescaled mean curvature flow that stays on one side of a limiting shrinker and converges to that shrinker as rescaled time tends to negative infinity.

evidence.section: 5 Uniqueness of smooth one-sided ancient rescaled flows

nodes.label: Classification of one-sided ancient flows near shrinkers
nodes.summary: For a smooth compact or asymptotically conical self-shrinker, there is a unique ancient mean curvature flow on one side of the associated shrinking flow, up to parabolic dilation, with controlled entropy and generic singularities.

nodes.label: One-sided curvature estimate for H-disks
nodes.summary: A curvature estimate for H-disks constrained to one side of a comparison surface or region. It is cited as a key ingredient in the proof of the limit lamination and chord arc results.

evidence.quote: a key extrinsic one-sided curvature estimate obtained in [24]

nodes.label: First-mode decay of one-sided ancient rescaled flows
nodes.summary: A nontrivial one-sided ancient graphical rescaled flow has leading asymptotic behavior along the first eigenfunction of the shrinker stability operator, with higher modes decaying faster.

evidence.section: 5 Uniqueness of smooth one-sided ancient rescaled flows

nodes.label: even multiplicity for one-sided min-max components
nodes.summary: When a one-sided minimal hypersurface appears as a component in the confined min-max realization, its integer multiplicity is even.

nodes.label: O'Neill A-tensor
nodes.summary: One of O'Neill's tensors for a Riemannian submersion; it measures the non-integrability of the horizontal distribution.

evidence.quote: A-tensor measures the integrability of H

nodes.label: Optimal algebraic invariant sheaf G_alg
nodes.summary: The reflexive sheaf psi_* pi^*(GrHNS(Ehat))** on C^n associated to any optimal algebraic tangent cone Ehat. It is independent of the optimal extension and carries the canonical HYM cone matched to A_infty.

nodes.label: Optimal algebraic tangent cone
nodes.summary: An algebraic tangent cone whose HN slope spread Phi = mu(E_1) - mu(E_m/E_{m-1}) lies in [0,1). It is the algebraic input from which the paper's canonical invariants are extracted.

nodes.label: Optimal transport
nodes.summary: The theory of transporting one measure to another with minimal cost. The paper compares L1- and L2-cost transport interpolations and uses optimal-transport geometry to formulate CD conditions.

nodes.label: Ordered super-subsolution trapping
aliases: barrier trapping for bounded Dirichlet problems
nodes.summary: The ordered super- and sub-solutions bound solutions of the anisotropic minimal graph equation on balls. The comparison step uses the maximum principle and the ordering across the two sides of C_11.

nodes.label: Orientation-cover descent for partial hyperbolic metrics
aliases: Z_2 descent of canonical partial metric
nodes.summary: For a nonorientable compact hyperbolic 3-manifold, the canonical partial hyperbolic metric is constructed on the orientable double cover and descends through the deck group action. Freeness of the completed action ensures the descended completion is again a compact hyperbolic 3-manifold diffeomorphic to the original.

nodes.label: Outer and inner flows
nodes.summary: Weak evolutions derived from the level set flows of the compact region bounded by an initial embedded surface and of the complementary closed region. Their comparison with the level set flow measures uniqueness through singularities.

nodes.label: Outermost expander of a cone
nodes.summary: The smooth self-expanders asymptotic to the model cone that describe the inner and outer level set flows of the cone. If the cone fattens, the time-one cone flow fills the region between the inner and outer expanders; if it does not, they coincide.

evidence.quote: Let $\Sigma' := \partial F_{1}(W')$ and $\Sigma := \partial F_{1}(W)$ and observe that $\partial F_{t}(W) = \sqrt{t}\Sigma$, $\partial F_{t}(W') = \sqrt{t}\Sigma'$
we will refer to ... $\Sigma$, $\Sigma^{\prime}$ as the outermost expanders
the outermost/innermost flows from a cone ... are modelled on smooth expanders, minizing the expander functional from the outside
evidence.section: 2.6. The level set flow of a cone and the outermost expanders

nodes.label: Outermost self-expander of a cone
nodes.summary: The outer boundary flow of the level set flow of a regular cone, shown in the paper to be a smooth self-similarly expanding mean curvature flow.

nodes.label: Overlap region between tip and cylinder
nodes.summary: The transition region where estimates from the tip and cylindrical analyses are compared and matched in the uniqueness proof.

nodes.label: p-form curvature-term control by curvature-operator eigenvalue sums
nodes.summary: The specialization of the paper's curvature-term control method to p-forms, giving the lower curvature-term bounds needed from sums of the lowest n-p curvature-operator eigenvalues.

evidence.quote: applications of lemma ... to p-forms and algebraic curvature tensors
The required estimates to apply lemma ... are established

nodes.label: p-th Betti number
nodes.summary: The p-th topological Betti number of a closed manifold. On closed Riemannian manifolds it is computed by harmonic p-forms, which is why Hodge-Laplacian estimates give Betti-number bounds.

evidence.quote: vanishing and estimation theorem for the pth-Betti number

nodes.label: p-width
nodes.summary: The min-max value obtained by taking the infimum over p-sweepouts without mass concentration of the maximal mass of a slice.

nodes.label: Palais-Smale condition
nodes.summary: The compactness condition that Palais-Smale sequences admit convergent subsequences. The paper emphasizes that the critical functional $F$ fails this condition, motivating the subcritical approximation.

evidence.quote: the functional  $F$  does not satisfy the Palais-Smale condition

nodes.label: Palais-Smale condition for abelian Higgs energy
nodes.summary: A compactness property for bounded-energy sequences with vanishing first variation, after suitable harmonic gauge transformations. It permits classical min-max theory to produce critical abelian Higgs pairs.

evidence.quote: The functional E_epsilon satisfies the following form of the Palais-Smale condition
evidence.section: 7.1 Min-max families for the trivial line bundle

nodes.label: Palais-Smale sequence
nodes.summary: A variational sequence whose functional values remain controlled while the derivative tends to zero; convergence of such sequences is a standard route from minmax levels to critical points.

evidence.quote: a minmax method, to produce a converging Palais-Smale sequence

nodes.label: Parabolic blow-up limit of a Ricci flow singularity
aliases: blow-up limit at a finite-time singularity
nodes.summary: A limiting ancient Ricci flow obtained by rescaling a solution near a finite-time singularity; Perelman's noncollapsing theorem motivates ancient kappa-solutions through such limits.

evidence.quote: arising as a blow-up limit at a finite-time singularity

nodes.label: Parabolic dimension bound for the Stefan singular set
nodes.summary: The main size estimate that the Stefan singular set has parabolic Hausdorff dimension at most n-1.

nodes.label: Parabolic end
nodes.summary: An end whose associated fundamental group acts by parabolic isometries on the universal cover and fixes a common point in the geometric boundary.

evidence.quote: this group consists of parabolic isometries of the universal cover  $\widetilde{M}$  of  $M$  and fixes a common point

nodes.label: Parabolic Hausdorff dimension
nodes.summary: Hausdorff dimension computed with parabolic space-time scaling, where the time axis has parabolic dimension 2.

evidence.quote: estimate the parabolic Hausdorff dimension of the singular set \Sigma, denoted \dim_{\mathrm{par}}(\Sigma)

nodes.label: Parabolic isometry of a CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: An isometry of a nonpositively curved space acting without an interior fixed point and fixing a point at infinity; the paper uses parabolic isometries fixing a common boundary point.

evidence.quote: parabolic isometries of the universal cover  $\widetilde{M}$

nodes.label: Parabolic neighborhood in a Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A spacetime neighborhood formed by flowing a spatial ball along time-vector trajectories over a forward or backward time interval. It is the local region on which perturbation decay and maximum-principle estimates are applied.

nodes.label: Parabolic obstacle problem for the Stefan problem
nodes.summary: The locally equivalent formulation for u satisfying \partial_t u-\Delta u=-\chi_{\{u>0\}}, u>=0, \partial_t u>=0, and \partial_t u>0 in the positivity set.

evidence.quote: the Stefan problem becomes locally equivalent to the so-called "parabolic obstacle problem"

nodes.label: Parabolic rescaling of mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: The operation of translating a chosen space-time point to the origin and scaling space and time by the parabolic mean-curvature-flow scaling. It is used to form tangent and limit flows.

nodes.label: Parabolic time-projection dimension estimate
nodes.summary: A scaling consequence of parabolic dimension: if a space-time set has parabolic dimension d, then its time projection has Hausdorff dimension at most d/2.

evidence.quote: recalling the definition of parabolic dimension, this implies that \dim_{\mathcal{H}}\big(\pi_t(\Sigma \setminus \Sigma^{\infty})\big) \leq \frac{n - 2}{2}

nodes.label: Paracontrolled distributions by the solution gradient
nodes.summary: A class of distributions z that can be represented as z=T_a partial u + r with controlled paracoefficients and error terms. It captures the limited regularity of the multiplier vector field used in paradifferential energy estimates.

evidence.quote: We say that a function $z$ is paracontrolled by $\partial u$ ... if it admits a representation ... $$z=T_{a}\partial u+r,$$
the vector $X$ to be paracontrolled by $\partial u$

nodes.label: Paradifferential multiplier method for energy estimates
nodes.summary: A paradifferential implementation of the classical wave multiplier method in which a carefully constructed low-regularity timelike multiplier X yields energy identities and flux estimates for the paradifferential flow.

evidence.quote: Both the construction of our energies and the proof of the energy estimates are based on a paradifferential implementation of the multiplier method
one multiplies the equation $\Box_{g}u=f$ by $Xu$ and simply integrates by parts

nodes.label: Parallel p-form
nodes.summary: A p-form with vanishing covariant derivative. In the borderline κ=0 case of the paper's estimate, harmonic p-forms are forced to be parallel.

evidence.quote: In case κ = 0 all harmonic p-forms are parallel.

nodes.label: Flatness criterion from a parallel spinor frame
aliases: orthonormal parallel spinor frame implies flatness
nodes.summary: A full orthonormal frame of parallel spinors forces the Riemann curvature tensor of the metric to vanish, giving the flatness conclusion of the rigidity argument.

evidence.quote: we obtain an  $m$ -tuple of parallel spinors  $s = (s_1, \ldots, s_m)$
$s_1, \ldots, s_m$  are orthonormal at each point in the interior of  $\Omega$ . As a consequence, the Riemann curvature tensor of  $g$  vanishes identically.

nodes.label: Parametric extension of partially defined constant-curvature metrics
nodes.summary: A method for extending finite-dimensional continuous families of partial constant-curvature metrics, already global on a subfamily, to continuous families of globally defined constant-curvature metrics. It uses disk-complement domains and local embedding extension over 3-disks; in the hyperbolic case the model S^3 is replaced by H^3.

nodes.label: Partial optimal transport
nodes.summary: An optimal transport restricted to partial marginals or subplans. The correction identifies the failed step: compositions of such partial optimal transports need not be optimal transports between the composed marginals.
