evidence.quote: the composition of the partial optimal transports is not necessarily an optimal transport of the composed marginals

nodes.label: Partial variable-coefficient normal form transformation
nodes.summary: A normal-form correction applied to the unbalanced part of the paradifferential source term so that the remaining nonlinearity becomes perturbative in the balanced energy estimate.

evidence.quote: the nonlinearity $N$ plays a perturbative role ... only after a well chosen partial, variable coefficient normal form transformation
evidence.section: 3.7 Paralinearization and the linear paradifferential flow

evidence.quote: applying a normal form transformation to the unbalanced part of the source term

nodes.label: Partially defined Riemannian metric
nodes.summary: A pair consisting of an open subset of a manifold and a smooth Riemannian metric on that open subset, equipped with a lower-semicontinuous domain topology. It is the target space for the canonical limiting metric assignment.

nodes.label: PDE comparison method for Monge-Ampère $L^\infty$ estimates
nodes.summary: A PDE method combining comparison with an auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation and a specially chosen test function to obtain sharp $L^\infty$ bounds without using pluripotential theory.

evidence.quote: The PDE approach to  $L^{\infty}$  estimates which we present in this paper combines the methods of Wang, Wang, and Zhou
A key novelty in our paper resides in the choice of auxiliary complex Monge-Ampère equation, as well as of the test function  $\Phi$

nodes.label: PDE construction of codimension-two stationary varifolds
aliases: abelian Higgs PDE proof of stationary varifold existence
nodes.summary: The method of producing nontrivial stationary integral (n-2)-varifolds by first constructing bounded-energy abelian Higgs critical pairs and then passing to their energy-concentration limit. It gives a PDE route to codimension-two stationary integral varifolds on closed Riemannian manifolds.

evidence.quote: As a byproduct, we obtain a PDE proof, in codimension two, of Almgren's existence result

nodes.label: Perelman's compact ancient solution
aliases: Type II ancient solution constructed by Perelman
nodes.summary: The rotationally symmetric noncollapsed compact ancient Ricci flow solution on S^3 that is not a soliton, is Type II backwards in time, and has Bryant soliton or round cylinder backward limits.

evidence.quote: the Type II ancient solution constructed by Perelman

nodes.label: Periodic flat
nodes.summary: A flat whose stabilizer in the full isometry group contains a subgroup acting geometrically on the flat.

evidence.quote: stabilizer in the full isometry group

nodes.label: Set of periodic geodesics
nodes.summary: The collection of geodesic-flow orbits that return exactly to their initial phase point after a positive time.

evidence.quote: when the set of periodic geodesics has measure 0

nodes.label: Periodic invariant limit surface
nodes.summary: An invariant limit surface with a periodicity property. The paper proves existence of such surfaces as part of the proof strategy.

evidence.quote: The existence of periodic invariant limit surfaces

nodes.label: Periodic Morse flat
nodes.summary: A flat that is both periodic and Morse; the main theorem assumes the existence of a periodic Morse n-flat.

nodes.label: Tits boundary of a periodic Morse flat
aliases: boundary sphere of a periodic Morse flat
nodes.summary: The round sphere in the ambient Tits boundary arising as the Tits boundary of a periodic Morse flat; it supplies antipodes and carries the chamber structure used in the proof.

nodes.label: Permutation cycle count
nodes.summary: For a permutation sigma, K_n(sigma) is the number of disjoint cycles in its cycle decomposition.

evidence.quote: Denote by  $\mathrm{K}_n(\sigma)$  the number of disjoint cycles in the cycle decomposition of a permutation  $\sigma$  in the symmetric group  $S_{n}$

nodes.label: Perturbation to genuine hyperkähler metrics
nodes.summary: An analytic correction method that turns approximate hyperkähler data into exact hyperkähler metrics, using elliptic estimates and rigidity inputs to solve the nonlinear problem.

nodes.label: Perturbative foliation blow-up method
nodes.summary: A method that embeds the initial hypersurface in a local foliation, evolves all leaves simultaneously, rescales near a singular point, and extracts a one-sided ancient flow to guide a perturbation of initial data.

nodes.label: Perturbed dHYM equation with volume term
nodes.summary: The auxiliary PDE Im(ωφ+iχ)^n + fχ^n = tan(θ0)Re(ωφ+iχ)^n, used in the continuity proof of the dHYM stability theorem.

nodes.label: Planar billiard map
nodes.summary: The map on boundary phase space that sends a billiard impact to the next reflected impact; complete or rational integrability is formulated for this dynamics.

nodes.label: Planar sector
nodes.summary: A planar sector domain, treated as another class of Euclidean domains for which the paper verifies Pólya's conjecture.

evidence.quote: We also confirm Pólya's conjecture for arbitrary planar sectors

nodes.label: Planar tangent-cone uniqueness for mod-$p$ minimisers
aliases: uniqueness of multiplicity $p/2$ planar tangent cones
nodes.summary: For even $p$, if an area-minimising hypersurface mod $p$ has one tangent cone equal to a hyperplane of multiplicity $p/2$, then that planar tangent cone is unique and the current has generalized multi-valued graphical structure nearby.

evidence.quote: if  $T$  has one tangent cone at an interior point  $y$  equal to an (oriented) hyperplane  $P$  of multiplicity  $p / 2$ , then  $P$  is the unique tangent cone at  $y$
Here we settle this uniqueness question affirmatively
evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Planar vortex energy quantization
nodes.summary: Entire two-dimensional vortex solutions have energy equal to 2pi times an integer degree. The paper uses this quantization in blow-up slices to prove integrality of the limiting varifold.

evidence.quote: the energy of the two-dimensional solution ... is 2pi|p|
evidence.section: 6.2 Integrality of the limit varifold and convergence of level sets

nodes.label: Platonic reflection symmetry group
aliases: tetrahedral, octahedral, or icosahedral full symmetry group
nodes.summary: The full symmetry group of a Platonic solid, generated by reflections across three planes through the origin and admitting a standard wedge fundamental domain in the unit ball.

evidence.quote: generated by reflections across three planes
evidence.section: 7 First Steklov eigenvalue of free boundary minimal surfaces

nodes.label: Platonic-symmetric genus-zero free boundary minimal surface
nodes.summary: An embedded genus-zero free boundary minimal surface in the unit ball invariant under tetrahedral, octahedral, or icosahedral reflection symmetry, with the specified boundary-component counts studied in the paper.

evidence.quote: tetrahedral symmetry ... octahedral symmetry ... icosahedral symmetry

nodes.label: Pluripotential theory
nodes.summary: The analytic framework for plurisubharmonic functions, positive currents, and complex Monge-Ampère operators. The paper's variational proof uses it in place of continuity methods and metric limit theory.

evidence.quote: uses instead pluripotential theory and valuations
pluripotential theory thus provides an interpretation of (TKE) in the singular case

nodes.label: Plurisubharmonic potential on a Kähler manifold
nodes.summary: A function on the Kähler manifold used as an admissible potential, here normalized by a supremum condition. It is the input class for the Trudinger-type energy estimate.

evidence.quote: Let  $\varphi$  be a plurisubharmonic function such that  $\sup \varphi = -1$

nodes.label: Plus-mode fine neck expansion
aliases: fine neck expansion in the plus mode
nodes.summary: In the plus-mode case, after recentering, the graph function over the cylinder has leading term proportional to z e^{tau/2}. This asymptotic drives cap-size control and the noncompact bowl-soliton classification.

nodes.label: Poincare inequality
nodes.summary: An inequality controlling function oscillation by gradient size, used as a standard analytic tool in metric and Riemannian convergence arguments.

nodes.label: Point-picking blow-up argument
nodes.summary: A contradiction method for curvature estimates: choose points where |A| times boundary distance is large, rescale around them, and pass to a limiting complete stable minimal hypersurface.

nodes.label: Point-value recovery by focusing waves and Gaussian beams
aliases: local recovery of V by focused waves
nodes.summary: The recovery mechanism combining point-supported controlled waves with Gaussian beam solutions to show that V_1(p)=V_2(p) at arbitrary points in the recoverable region. It converts equality of boundary maps into pointwise equality of the potential.

evidence.quote: recover point values of V everywhere

nodes.label: Pointed complete Calabi-Yau manifold
nodes.summary: A pointed complex manifold (X,p) with a complete Kähler metric, holomorphic volume form, and Calabi-Yau normalization of the complex Monge-Ampère equation. In this paper it is always considered with Euclidean volume growth and quadratic curvature decay.

evidence.quote: This means that p in X, (g,J,omega) is a complete Kähler metric, Omega is a holomorphic volume form

nodes.label: Pointed Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: Convergence of pointed metric spaces in the Gromov-Hausdorff sense, used here to form the limit space from pointed Riemannian manifolds.

evidence.quote: Gromov-Hausdorff limit spaces  $(M_i^n, g_i, p_i) \xrightarrow{d_{GH}} (X^n, d, p)$
converges in the pointed Gromov-Hausdorff sense to a Calabi-Yau cone

nodes.label: Pointed measured Gromov-Hausdorff convergence
nodes.summary: A convergence notion for pointed metric-measure spaces, realized in the paper through GH, mGH, and pmGH distances. It is the convergence framework for stability and boundary-measure convergence.

evidence.quote: The associated distances will be denoted by d_GH, d_mGH and d_pmGH.

nodes.label: Polar isometric action
nodes.summary: An isometric group action admitting an immersed submanifold that intersects every orbit orthogonally; in the paper this is applied to the nilradical action.

nodes.label: Pollicott-Ruelle coresonant k-forms at zero
nodes.summary: The dual stable-wavefront analogue of Pollicott-Ruelle resonant forms, used in pairings and product identities with resonant forms.

nodes.label: Pollicott-Ruelle resonant k-forms at zero
nodes.summary: Distributional k-forms on the sphere bundle annihilated by contraction with X and invariant under the Lie derivative along X, with wavefront set contained in E_u^*.

evidence.quote: Define the spaces of resonant k-forms

nodes.label: Pólya counting inequalities for Euclidean domains
nodes.summary: The conjectural inequalities comparing Laplacian counting functions with the Weyl leading term: the Dirichlet counting function is bounded above by C_d|Ω|_dλ^d and the Neumann counting function is bounded below by the same term.

evidence.quote: Pólya's conjecture (1954) in spectral geometry states that the eigenvalue counting functions of the Dirichlet and Neumann Laplacian on a bounded Euclidean domain can be estimated from above and below, respectively, by the leading term of Weyl's asymptotics.

nodes.label: Pólya inequalities for the Euclidean disk
nodes.summary: The verified Dirichlet and Neumann Pólya counting inequalities for the planar disk, making the disk a non-tiling planar domain where the conjecture holds.

evidence.quote: we prove Pólya's conjecture for the disk
the first non-tiling planar domain for which the conjecture is verified

nodes.label: Pólya inequalities for planar sectors
nodes.summary: The verified Pólya counting inequalities for arbitrary planar sector domains.

evidence.quote: We also confirm Pólya's conjecture for arbitrary planar sectors

nodes.label: Polyhedral boundary area functional
nodes.summary: A weighted boundary-area expression involving the area of a cutting plane and the side-face areas weighted by cosines of the model side-base angles. The corrected proof shows this functional can be negative under the added cone assumptions.

evidence.quote: Then the infimum \mathcal{T} appeared in (1.4) is negative.

nodes.label: Polyhedral rigidity under dihedral comparison
nodes.summary: The rigidity property that a P-type Riemannian polyhedron with nonnegative scalar curvature, mean-convex faces, and dihedral angles no larger than those of the Euclidean model must be isometric to a flat Euclidean polyhedron, under the corrected assumptions.

evidence.quote: then we have the rigidity statement
if R(g) \geq 0 , each F_{j} is mean convex, and \angle_{ij}(M,g) \leq \angle_{ij}(P,g_{Euclid}) , then (M,g) is isometric to a flat polyhedron in \mathbf{R}^3
Scalar curvature comparison theorems for polytopes were first studied in seminal work of Gromov

nodes.label: Strict dihedral comparison for Riemannian polyhedra
nodes.summary: The comparison principle asserting that, under nonnegative scalar curvature and mean-convex faces, the dihedral angles of a P-type Riemannian polyhedron cannot be everywhere strictly smaller than those of the Euclidean model polyhedron.

evidence.quote: Then the strict comparison statement holds for (M, g). Namely, if R(g) \geq 0 , and each F_j is mean convex, then the dihedral angles of M cannot be everywhere less than those of P.

nodes.label: Polynomial asymptotic convergence to the cone
aliases: polynomial rate to the asymptotic cone
polynomially asymptotic to the Calabi-Yau cone
nodes.summary: A decay property asserting that, after a diffeomorphism from the end of the cone to the end of X, all covariant derivatives of the pulled-back metric, complex structure, and holomorphic volume form converge to their cone counterparts at a polynomial rate.

evidence.quote: There exists a diffeomorphism Psi from the open subset {r > 1} subset C to the complement of a compact set in X
X is polynomially asymptotic to C at infinity

nodes.label: Polynomial-growth group
aliases: group of at most polynomial growth
nodes.summary: A finitely generated group whose word balls grow at most polynomially in the radius.

nodes.label: Ring of polynomial-growth holomorphic functions
nodes.summary: The ring of holomorphic functions on the noncompact Calabi-Yau manifold whose growth at infinity is polynomial. In this setting it is finitely generated and its spectrum gives a normal affine algebraic variety.

evidence.quote: The ring R(X) of holomorphic functions on X with polynomial growth is finitely generated

nodes.label: Polynomial growth implies almost nilpotence
nodes.summary: The group-theoretic principle that a finitely generated group with at most polynomial growth is almost nilpotent.

evidence.quote: a finitely generated group of at most polynomial growth is almost nilpotent

nodes.label: Polynomial growth weight
nodes.summary: A weight with polynomial growth used in weighted analytic estimates. The paper develops elliptic estimates using such weights.

evidence.quote: Elliptic estimates with polynomial growth weights

nodes.label: Polystable reflexive sheaf
nodes.summary: A reflexive sheaf decomposing as a direct sum of slope-stable sheaves of the relevant slope. HYM cone connections are determined by such sheaves on projective space.

nodes.label: Outward unit normals to polytope faces
nodes.summary: For each defining halfspace of a polytope, $N_i$ denotes its outward Euclidean unit normal and $\nu_i$ denotes the outward unit normal to the same face with respect to the Riemannian metric $g$.

evidence.quote: we denote by  $N_i \in S^{n-1}$  the outward-pointing unit normal vector to the halfspace  $\{u_i \leq 0\}$  with respect to the Euclidean metric
we denote by  $\nu_{i}$  the outward-pointing unit normal vector to the halfspace  $\{u_{i} \leq 0\}$  with respect to the metric  $g$

nodes.label: Positive (1,1)-current
nodes.summary: A positive current of bidegree (1,1) on a complex manifold. Twisted Kähler-Einstein currents are positive (1,1)-currents in c1(L).

evidence.quote: A  $θ$ -twisted Kähler-Einstein current is a positive  $(1,1)$ -current

nodes.label: Positive and negative phase sets
nodes.summary: For a real-valued function u, the positive phase is Omega_u^+={u>0} and the negative phase is Omega_u^-={u<0}. They are the positivity sets of u^+=max{u,0} and u^-=max{-u,0}.

evidence.quote: the positivity sets of the functions  $u^{+} \coloneqq \max \{u, 0\}$  and  $u^{-} \coloneqq \max \{-u, 0\}$

nodes.label: Positive continuous Monge-Ampère density
nodes.summary: A right-hand side f for det D^2 u=f that is positive and continuous on the closure of the source domain. This is the density assumption used for the global W^{2,p} estimate.

nodes.label: Positive Green's function on a stable minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A positive harmonic function u on M minus a pole p, with Euclidean Green's-function asymptotics near p, infimum zero, finite energy off compact sets, and compact superlevel sets.

evidence.section: 2. The Green's function on a stable minimal hypersurface

nodes.label: Positive Hölder Monge-Ampère density
nodes.summary: A positive right-hand side f for the Monge-Ampère equation satisfying f in C^α on the closed source domain. This is the density assumption used for global C^{2,α} regularity.

nodes.label: Positive Kähler-Einstein metric
aliases: Kähler-Einstein metric with positive Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric whose Ricci form is a positive scalar multiple of the Kähler form. In the Tian-Yau setup it is assumed on the divisor D.

evidence.quote: assuming that D admits a Kähler-Einstein metric with positive Ricci curvature

nodes.label: Positive loop in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop in the Einstein Universe satisfying the paper's positivity condition, exemplified by the intersection of the Einstein quadric with a subspace of type (2,1). Positive loops are part of the boundary data for maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: the intersection of the quadric with a space of type (2,1) is a positive loop

nodes.label: Positive lower Hausdorff density
nodes.summary: A set has positive lower n-dimensional Hausdorff density at a point if the lower limit of $\mathcal{H}^n(E \cap B(x,r))/r^n$ as $r \downarrow 0$ is positive, up to normalization. This is an added hypothesis in the metric rectifiability criterion.

evidence.quote: under the additional assumption of the positive lower Hausdorff density of  $E$

nodes.label: Positive part of a function
nodes.summary: The pointwise positive part of a real-valued function, $u_+=\max\{u,0\}$, used in the subcritical functional.

nodes.label: Positive scalar curvature metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric whose scalar curvature is positive at every point.

evidence.quote: does not admit a Riemannian metric with positive scalar curvature

nodes.label: Positive second fundamental form
nodes.summary: The condition that the second fundamental form of a boundary or hypersurface is positive in the relevant directions. In the excerpt this is the meaning of strict convexity.

evidence.quote: strict convexity means a positive second fundamental form

nodes.label: Positive smooth weight function
nodes.summary: A function beta in C^infty(M) with beta positive, used as a density in the weighted Laplace eigenvalue problem and to prescribe the limiting boundary measure of perforations.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Positivity in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A causal/order-like positivity structure on the Einstein Universe used to define positive triples, positive quadruples, positive maps, positive loops, and semi-positive loops. The excerpt identifies intersections with subspaces of type (2,1) as basic positive loops.

evidence.quote: This quadric admits a notion of positivity leading to the concept of positive loops and semi-positive loops: for instance, the intersection of the quadric with a space of type (2,1) is a positive loop.

nodes.label: Positivity set
nodes.summary: The subset of space-time where the unknown is strictly positive; in this paper the Stefan temperature and obstacle variable have the same positivity set.

nodes.label: Preiss tangent measure
nodes.summary: A Preiss tangent measure is a nonzero weak limit of suitably rescaled and renormalized measures around a point in Euclidean space. The paper generalizes this notion to arbitrary metric spaces.

evidence.quote: We also introduce a generalisation of Preiss's tangent measures

nodes.label: Prescribed mean curvature functional
nodes.summary: A variational functional whose stationary hypersurfaces satisfy a prescribed mean curvature equation.

nodes.label: Perturbed leaves with prescribed anisotropic mean curvature sign
nodes.summary: Small perturbations sigma plus or minus epsilon f of the foliation profile have the same leading asymptotics as the original leaf but anisotropic mean curvature of opposite prescribed signs. These are the geometric source of the barrier inequalities.

evidence.quote: positive or negative anisotropic mean curvature

nodes.label: Primitive closed geodesic
nodes.summary: A closed orbit of the geodesic flow that is not an iterate of a shorter closed geodesic. The Ruelle zeta product is indexed by these geodesics with length T_gamma.

nodes.label: Primitive component of a multicurve
nodes.summary: The underlying simple closed curve gamma_i in a weighted multicurve before its multiplicity m_i is applied.

evidence.quote: primitive components  $\gamma_1, \ldots, \gamma_k$  of a random multicurve  $m_1 \gamma_1 + \dots + m_k \gamma_k$

nodes.label: Principal Dirichlet Laplacian eigenvalue
aliases: first eigenvalue for the Dirichlet Laplacian
nodes.summary: The first eigenvalue lambda_1(Omega) of the Dirichlet Laplacian on a domain Omega, used as the spectral term in the multiphase shape optimization objective.

evidence.quote: where  $\lambda_{1}(\Omega_{i})$  is the first eigenvalue for the Dirichlet Laplacian in  $\Omega_{i}$

nodes.label: Principal stretches of a sphere map
nodes.summary: The ordered eigenvalues of $\sqrt{\nabla_Tu^t\nabla_Tu}$ for a Sobolev map from the sphere. They measure pointwise tangential stretching and define the isometric stretch deficit.

nodes.label: Probabilistic operator-norm bounds for random covers
nodes.summary: A random-operator estimate used to control norms arising in the random-cover spectral analysis. It is listed as a proof component in the excerpt.

nodes.label: Probability simplex
nodes.summary: The probability simplex Delta^{(q-1)} is the set of vectors v in R^q with nonnegative coordinates and total sum one; its interior encodes strictly positive prescribed cell measures.

evidence.quote: Delta^{(q - 1)} := {v in R^q : v_i >= 0, sum v_i = 1}
prescribed Gaussian measure v in int Delta^{(q-1)}

nodes.label: Product domain in a Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A subset of a Ricci flow spacetime whose points survive through a common time interval and remain in the subset, so it can be identified with a time-slice times an interval. Comparison domains are built from such product domains.

nodes.label: Logarithmic Weyl remainder on product manifolds
nodes.summary: For compact connected product Riemannian manifolds, the eigenvalue counting function has the Weyl leading term with an $O(\lambda^{n-1}/\log\lambda)$ remainder.

evidence.quote: the improved remainder holds on all product manifolds

nodes.label: Product Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A manifold formed as the Cartesian product of Riemannian manifolds and equipped with the direct-sum product metric.

nodes.label: Bubbling maximizing sequence for projective-plane eigenvalues
aliases: round spheres plus projective plane degeneration
nodes.summary: A degenerating sequence of metrics on RP^2 whose limit is k-1 identical round spheres touching a standard projective plane, with projective-plane-to-sphere area ratio 3:2. This sequence saturates the sharp bound for Lambda_k(RP^2).

evidence.quote: union of k - 1 identical round spheres

nodes.label: Projective-plane maximal metric existence reduction
nodes.summary: Under the spectral gap condition Lambda_k(RP^2)>Lambda_{k-1}(RP^2)+8*pi, there is a possibly conical maximizer g on RP^2 and a harmonic map Phi with g=g_Phi and spectral index at most k. This converts the eigenvalue maximization problem into an index-degree problem for harmonic maps.

evidence.quote: exists a metric g on RP2

nodes.label: Projective quadric of a quadratic form
aliases: quadric in projective space associated to q
nodes.summary: The subset of projective space consisting of lines represented by nonzero null vectors of a quadratic form. For the pseudo-hyperbolic model this quadric is the boundary at infinity called the Einstein Universe.

evidence.quote: The pseudo-hyperbolic space naturally embeds in $\mathbf{P}(E)$ and admits a boundary at infinity denoted $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ and called the Einstein Universe which is the quadric in $\mathbf{P}(E)$ associated to $\mathbf{q}$.

nodes.label: Projective unitary group PU(n,1)
nodes.summary: The adjoint projective unitary Lie group associated with a Hermitian form of signature (n,1), appearing as the isometry group of complex hyperbolic n-space.

nodes.label: Propagation of singularities for the wave equation
nodes.summary: The microlocal principle that singularities of wave equation solutions propagate along null bicharacteristics. The paper uses it to show that a distributional solution with vanishing exterior boundary data is smooth in E_p before applying Carleman estimates.

evidence.section: 5.1. Smoothness away from the double null cone

nodes.label: Proper geodesic space
nodes.summary: A geodesic metric space whose closed bounded subsets are compact. The third-order temporal theory for Kantorovich potentials is stated in this generality.

nodes.label: Proper surjective temporal function
nodes.summary: A smooth proper surjective function τ:M→R whose differential is timelike. In the paper it guarantees solvability and a cylinder-type structure over compact time intervals.

nodes.label: Proper visibility CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A complete proper CAT(0) space satisfying the visibility property at infinity. This is the metric-space setting of the paper's central displacement estimate.

nodes.label: Properly discontinuous action
nodes.summary: A group action property used as one half of the definition of a geometric action.

nodes.label: Properly immersed totally geodesic submanifold
nodes.summary: A properly immersed subspace whose geodesics are also geodesics in the ambient manifold. The paper uses this as its standing meaning of geodesic submanifold.

evidence.quote: a geodesic submanifold will always mean a properly immersed totally geodesic subspace

nodes.label: Pseudo-hyperbolic space H^{2,n}
nodes.summary: The projectivized quadric {u in E | q(u) = -1}/±Id for a real vector space E with a quadratic form q of signature (2,n+1). It carries the restricted pseudo-Riemannian metric of signature (2,n) and constant sectional curvature -1.

evidence.quote: Then the pseudo-hyperbolic space of signature $(2,n)$ is $\mathbf {H} ^ {2, n} := \{u \in E \mid \mathbf {q} (u) = - 1 \} / \pm \operatorname {I d}$.
inherits by restriction of the quadratic form $\mathbf{q}$ a pseudo-Riemannian metric of signature $(2,n)$ and constant sectional curvature $-1$

nodes.label: Pseudoquad
nodes.summary: A pseudoquad is a roughly rectangular region on an intrinsic Lipschitz graph, adapted to the graph's characteristic foliation and local orientation. Pseudoquads form the geometric pieces of foliated patchworks and corona decompositions.

evidence.quote: The pieces of this decomposition are roughly rectangular regions that mimic the dimensions and orientation of the features of the surface

nodes.label: psh geodesic ray
aliases: geodesic ray of finite energy potentials
nodes.summary: A geodesic ray in the space of plurisubharmonic Kähler potentials, typically considered in a finite energy class. The paper studies its relationship with non-Archimedean metrics.

evidence.quote: Finite energy potentials and psh geodesics
the relationship between geodesic rays and non-Archimedean metrics

nodes.label: Psi-class intersection number
nodes.summary: An intersection number of cotangent-line psi classes on the Deligne-Mumford compactification of moduli of curves; the paper uses their uniform large-genus asymptotics.

evidence.quote: Uniform large genus asymptotics of correlators

nodes.label: L^p Psi-mean curvature
nodes.summary: A rectifiable varifold has Psi-mean curvature H in L^p on U when its anisotropic first variation is represented by minus integration of H against test fields with respect to the mass measure.

nodes.label: Pulling ultrafilter from a boundary point
nodes.summary: An ultrafilter omega pulls from a boundary point xi if group elements in the ultrafilter follow every subray asymptotic to xi; then xi is the repelling point.

evidence.quote: is said to pull from a point

nodes.label: Punctured copy of a manifold
nodes.summary: A manifold diffeomorphic to a compact manifold with a finite set of points removed. Singular Ricci flow time slices in the paper are described using punctured copies of the initial manifold or S^3.

nodes.label: Pure unrectifiability in a metric space
nodes.summary: A metric space is k-purely unrectifiable when every Lipschitz image of a compact subset of R^k has zero k-dimensional Hausdorff measure. The excerpt uses this to explain why Euclidean rectifiability does not transfer directly to Heisenberg groups.

evidence.quote: it is a $k$-purely unrectifiable metric space
for any compact set $K \subseteq \mathbb{R}^k$ and any Lipschitz function $f: K \to (\mathbb{H}^n, \| \cdot \|)$ we have: $\mathcal {H} _ {\parallel \cdot \parallel} ^ {k} (f (K)) = 0$

nodes.label: Q-Fano variety
nodes.summary: The special case of a log Fano pair with boundary divisor D equal to zero.

evidence.quote: If D = 0, then X is called a Q-Fano variety.

nodes.label: $Q$-valued function
nodes.summary: A function whose value at each base point is an unordered collection of $Q$ values, used to represent multi-sheeted hypersurfaces and branching. The paper uses this language for the local graphical structure near planar tangent cones.

evidence.quote: given by the graph of a  $\frac{p}{2}$ -valued function

evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Local product model for QFB metrics
nodes.summary: Near a boundary hypersurface, a QFB metric is locally quasi-isometric to a restricted product of a cone-type base metric and a lower-depth QFB metric on the fiber. This recursive local model is the input for weighted cohomology calculations.

nodes.label: QFB tangent bundle
nodes.summary: The Lie algebroid whose smooth sections identify with QFB vector fields via an anchor map and which restricts to the ordinary tangent bundle on the interior. QFB metrics are smooth bundle metrics on its dual pulled to the interior.

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered boundary vector field
nodes.summary: A smooth vector field on a manifold with fibered corners that is tangent to every boundary fibration fiber and differentiates the total boundary defining function into v^2 C-infinity. These vector fields form the Lie algebra underlying the QFB geometry.

nodes.label: QFC weighted cohomology-intersection cohomology identification
aliases: weighted QFC cohomology as intersection cohomology
nodes.summary: Under dimension and injectivity hypotheses on the links or fibers, weighted QFC cohomology is naturally isomorphic to intersection cohomology of the stratified space obtained by collapsing the boundary fibrations. In odd-link middle-perversity cases this also computes L2 harmonic forms.

nodes.label: Local model for QFC metrics
nodes.summary: A local QFC metric model on a subset V_i x B_i of a product, with metric quasi-isometric to dv^2/v^2 + v^2 g_Zi + g_Bi. The model is not a warped product globally, which is why the paper develops a half-line-based computation.

nodes.label: Local calculation of weighted QFC cohomology
nodes.summary: A recursive local description of weighted QFC cohomology on neighborhoods of strata: below a weight threshold it is the corresponding cohomology of the fiber, while above it vanishes under an injectivity hypothesis. This is the main local computational mechanism of the paper.

nodes.label: Quadratic-cost optimal transport
nodes.summary: The optimal transport problem with cost proportional to squared Euclidean distance. In this setting optimal maps are represented by gradients of convex potentials satisfying a Monge-Ampère equation with image constraint.

evidence.quote: arises naturally in optimal transportation with the quadratic cost function

nodes.label: Quadratic curvature decay
nodes.summary: A curvature decay condition requiring the Riemann curvature tensor to be bounded by a constant times the inverse square of distance from the basepoint.

evidence.quote: Quadratic curvature decay: there exists a C > 0 such that |Rm_g(q)| <= Cd_g(p,q)^{-2} for all q != p.

nodes.label: Quadratic excess of the graph gradient
nodes.summary: The averaged L^2 oscillation of Du around its mean on a ball, used as the decay quantity in the partial regularity proof.

nodes.label: Quadratic form of signature (p,q)
nodes.summary: A quadratic form on a real vector space whose maximal positive and negative definite subspaces have dimensions p and q respectively. In the paper the relevant signature is (2,n+1).

evidence.quote: a quadratic form $\mathbf{q}$ of signature $(2,n + 1)$ - that is admitting a vector space of dimension 2 on which $\mathbf{q}$ is positive definite, while $\mathbf{q}$ is negative definite on the orthogonal of dimension $n + 1$

nodes.label: Quantitative rectifiability
nodes.summary: Quantitative rectifiability studies rectifiability through uniform or multiscale quantitative estimates such as geometric square functions, β-numbers, and related criteria. The paper positions Carleson's ε² problem within this area.

evidence.quote: geometric square functions stems from the wish to solve different problems ... which depend on (variants of) quantitative rectifiability

nodes.label: Quantitative stratification
nodes.summary: A scale-sensitive refinement of singular stratification that records how close balls are to having specified cone symmetries. It is a main framework for proving rectifiability and measure estimates.

evidence.quote: Our analysis builds on the notion of quantitative stratification introduced by Cheeger-Naber

nodes.label: Quasi-asymptotically conical metric
nodes.summary: A QFB metric for which every maximal boundary hypersurface has identity fibration, so the QFB compactification is a QAC compactification. The paper applies its QFB cohomology machinery to QAC metrics, especially in depth two.

nodes.label: Quasi-asymptotically cylindrical metric
nodes.summary: A QFC metric obtained when the underlying conformally related QFB metric is QAC. It plays the QFC role in the paper's computations for QAC and QALE examples.

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered boundary metric
nodes.summary: A complete Riemannian metric on the interior of a manifold with fibered corners obtained from a smooth bundle metric on the QFB cotangent bundle. It has bounded geometry and infinite volume and is the paper's central class of metrics.

nodes.label: Quasi-fibered cusp metric
nodes.summary: A metric conformal to a QFB metric by the square of the total boundary defining function, g_QFC = v^2 g_QFB. It is the conformal class used to compute weighted L2 cohomology of QFB metrics.

nodes.label: Quasiconvexity of the graph integrand under USAC
nodes.summary: USAC implies a quasiconvex lower bound for F_Psi against compactly supported graph variations, measured relative to the area integrand.

nodes.label: Quasimode
nodes.summary: A function that approximately solves an eigenvalue equation for the Laplacian. The paper uses geodesic-beam decompositions of quasimodes as a technical input.

evidence.quote: decomposing the quasimodes into geodesic beams

nodes.label: Quasiperiodic loop or map in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop or map into the Einstein Universe satisfying the paper's quasiperiodicity condition. It is the boundary-side counterpart of quasiperiodic surfaces and appears together with positivity in the paper's setup.

nodes.label: Quasiperiodic surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: A complete maximal surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space belonging to the paper's quasiperiodic class. The paper characterizes these surfaces by curvature bounds, Gromov hyperbolicity, conformal hyperbolicity, spatial distance, and lamination conditions, and relates them to quasisymmetric boundary curves.

evidence.quote: We study in this paper quasiperiodic maximal surfaces in pseudo-hyperbolic spaces and show that they are characterised by a curvature condition, Gromov hyperbolicity or conformal hyperbolicity.
every quasisymmetric curve in the Einstein Universe bounds a quasiperiodic surface

nodes.label: Quasisymmetric map to the Einstein Universe
aliases: quasisymmetric maps from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe
quasisymmetric curve in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A positive map from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe satisfying a quasisymmetry condition defined via the paper's cross-ratio. Such maps have a uniform Hölder modulus of continuity and provide boundary parametrizations for quasiperiodic maximal surfaces.

evidence.quote: One of the concepts introduced and studied in the current paper is that of quasisymmetric maps from the projective real line to the Einstein Universe.
quasisymmetric maps are defined using a cross-ratio. These maps are positive and enjoy similar properties
every quasisymmetric curve in the Einstein Universe bounds a quasiperiodic surface

nodes.label: Quot scheme for sheaf quotients
nodes.summary: The projective scheme parameterizing quotient sheaves H -> E' -> 0 with fixed Hilbert polynomial. It supplies the algebraic topology in which quotient maps from global sections converge.

nodes.label: Radon measure
nodes.summary: A Radon measure is a locally finite Borel measure on a Hausdorff topological space, used here as the ambient class of measures whose densities and tangent measures are studied.

nodes.label: Random multicurve in large genus
nodes.summary: A closed multicurve sampled from the paper's large-genus probability distribution or frequency model on genus-g surfaces.

evidence.quote: We study the combinatorial geometry of a random closed multicurve on a surface of large genus  $g$

nodes.label: Random operator on the free group for random covers
nodes.summary: An operator model on a free group used in the paper's random-operator part of the proof. The excerpt gives only the section heading, so dependencies are recorded at one layer.

evidence.quote: 6.2 The operator on the free group

nodes.label: Random square-tiled surface in large genus
aliases: random square-tiled surface of genus g
nodes.summary: A square-tiled surface sampled from the paper's large-genus distribution, analyzed through fixed combinatorial types and Masur-Veech volume asymptotics.

evidence.quote: random square-tiled surface of large genus  $g$

nodes.label: Rank at least n for a CAT(0) space
nodes.summary: A CAT(0) space has rank at least n when every geodesic in the space lies in an n-flat.

evidence.quote: every geodesic in X lies in an n-flat

nodes.label: Rank-one semisimple Lie group
nodes.summary: A semisimple Lie group of real rank one. The paper develops superrigidity tools in this setting for target groups not compatible with the source group.

evidence.quote: superrigidity theorems in rank one with target groups

nodes.label: Rank-one superrigidity with noncompatible targets
aliases: superrigidity with target groups not compatible with G
nodes.summary: A method or framework for proving superrigidity theorems in rank-one settings when the target group fails the prior compatibility condition. The excerpt presents this as the new tool needed for SU(n,1).

evidence.quote: new tools for proving superrigidity theorems in rank one with target groups that are not compatible with G

nodes.label: Rational integrability of a planar billiard
nodes.summary: For every integer q at least 3, the billiard has a convex caustic of rotation number 1/q consisting of periodic orbits with q reflections.

nodes.label: RCD(K,N) space
nodes.summary: A metric-measure space satisfying the curvature-dimension condition CD(K,N) and the infinitesimally Hilbertian condition. In smooth settings it corresponds to a dimension bound and a lower Bakry-Ricci bound.

evidence.quote: it is an RCD(K,N) m.m.s. for short if it is a CD(K,N) infinitesimally hilbertian metric measure space.

nodes.label: Real-analytic codimension-one foliation
nodes.summary: A foliation of a real-analytic manifold by analytic hypersurfaces. Producing such a foliation on the simply connected closed analytic setting is a contradiction used in the paper.

evidence.section: 4. Proof given a linear, balanced holonomy sequence

nodes.label: Real GIT stratification of Lie brackets
nodes.summary: A real geometric invariant theory stratification of the GL-action on the vector space of Lie brackets, used to define the beta endomorphism.

evidence.quote: real geometric invariant theory point of view

nodes.label: Real hyperbolic geodesic submanifold
nodes.summary: A properly immersed totally geodesic submanifold of a complex hyperbolic manifold whose induced geometry is real hyperbolic.

evidence.quote: geodesic submanifolds of real dimension k that are real hyperbolic

nodes.label: Real Monge-Ampère equation
nodes.summary: The nonlinear equation for a real-valued function u in which the determinant of its Hessian equals a prescribed density f, written det D^2 u = f. In this paper it is studied for convex solutions on Euclidean domains.

evidence.quote: the Monge-Ampère equation $\det D^2 u = f$

nodes.label: Real projective plane
nodes.summary: The non-orientable closed surface obtained as the quotient of S^2 by the antipodal map. It is the principal surface for the paper's sharp eigenvalue inequality.

nodes.label: Real structure on the O(4) bundle
nodes.summary: The antiholomorphic real structure on the O(4) bundle given in coordinates by (zeta, eta) mapping to (-1/bar zeta, bar eta/bar zeta^4).

evidence.quote: There is a real structure on the  $\mathcal{O}(4)$  bundle

nodes.label: Rectifiable current mod $p$
nodes.summary: A rectifiable current considered modulo the integer $p$, with representative multiplicities taken in the mod-$p$ sense. It is the current-theoretic setting for the paper's applications.

evidence.quote: codimension 1 rectifiable area minimising currents mod  $p$

evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Rectifiable m-varifold
nodes.summary: An m-varifold is a Radon measure on R^N x G(N,m); it is rectifiable when represented by an m-rectifiable support Gamma with positive multiplicity theta and tangent plane T_x Gamma.

evidence.quote: m-dimensional varifold V in RN is a Radon measure

nodes.label: Rectifiable ruled surface in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A rectifiable ruled surface is a countably rectifiable surface swept out by horizontal line segments or characteristic rulings. Locally monotone sets in the paper are bounded by such surfaces.

evidence.quote: Locally monotone sets are bounded by rectifiable ruled surfaces

nodes.label: k-rectifiable set
nodes.summary: A set covered up to Hausdorff k-measure zero by countably many Lipschitz images from Euclidean k-space. This is the measure-theoretic regularity asserted for the strata.

evidence.quote: E⊂R^2 is called rectifiable if there are Lipschitz maps f_i:R→R^2, i∈N, such that H^1(E\∪_{i=1}^∞ f_i(R))=0.
For a metric space  $X$ ,  $E \subset X$  is  $n$ -rectifiable if it can be covered, up to a set of  $\mathcal{H}^n$  measure zero, by countably many Lipschitz images of subsets of  $\mathbb{R}^n$ .

nodes.label: rectifiable varifold
nodes.summary: A varifold determined by an n-dimensional rectifiable set together with a nonnegative density function integrated against n-dimensional Hausdorff measure.

nodes.label: Reduced curvature-dimension condition
nodes.summary: A reduced variant of the curvature-dimension condition for metric-measure spaces. In the correction, tensorization for CD*(K,N) is treated separately and is used as an intermediate step toward tensorization of CD(K,N).

evidence.quote: If all the (M_i,d_i,m_i) are infinitesimally Hilbertian spaces and satisfy CD^*(K,N), then so does their tensor product

nodes.label: Reduced L2 cohomology
nodes.summary: The quotient of closed L2 forms by the L2 closure of exact forms, with a weighted analogue. It is the cohomology naturally compared with L2 harmonic forms and with the images of compactly supported cohomology.

nodes.label: Universal cover of the reduced SU(2)-monopole moduli space
aliases: universal cover of reduced monopole moduli space
nodes.summary: For magnetic charge k, the universal cover of the reduced moduli space obtained from the centered SU(2)-monopole moduli space by quotienting by the circle action. It carries the natural L2 metric appearing in Sen's conjecture.

evidence.section: 6. L2-cohomology of the moduli space of monopoles

nodes.label: Reduced non-Archimedean J-norm
nodes.summary: The infimum of the non-Archimedean J functional over all central-torus twists of a T-equivariant test configuration or graded filtration.

nodes.label: Reeb vector field of a Kähler cone
nodes.summary: For a Kähler cone, the Reeb vector field is J(r partial_r); it is holomorphic and Killing and generates a holomorphic isometric torus action used to define weighted algebraic scaling.

evidence.quote: there is a Reeb vector field xi=J_C(r partial_r), which is holomorphic and Killing

nodes.label: Reflecting hypersphere
nodes.summary: The singular hyperspheres in the boundary sphere of a periodic Morse flat, viewed as mirrors for the induced submetry and horizontal geodesic branching.

evidence.section: 5.3 Induced submetries and the dimension of the base

nodes.label: Reflexive coherent sheaf
nodes.summary: A coherent sheaf equal to its double dual. In this paper it is the underlying sheaf for admissible HYM connections and has singular locus of complex codimension at least three.

nodes.label: Regular cone
nodes.summary: A cone over a smooth submanifold of the unit sphere. It is the asymptotic cone in the definition of asymptotically conical shrinkers and the initial model for expander flows.

nodes.label: Regular end at infinity
nodes.summary: An end of a complete minimal hypersurface that is a graph over a hyperplane with an asymptotic expansion of the form b + a|x|^{2-n} plus lower-order terms.

nodes.label: Regular fibration of a collapsed hyperkähler sequence
nodes.summary: A smooth fiber bundle over compact domains in the regular set of the collapsed limit that is a Gromov-Hausdorff approximation, almost Riemannian submersion, and has infranilmanifold fibers with affine structure group.

nodes.label: Manifold structure off a rectifiable singular set
aliases: bi-Holder manifold structure away from singular set
nodes.summary: The property that after removing a controlled rectifiable closed set, the remaining part of the limit space is epsilon-bi-Holder equivalent to a smooth Riemannian manifold.

evidence.quote: there exists an  $(n-2)$ -rectifiable closed set  $S_\epsilon^{n-2}$  with  $\mathcal{H}^{n-2}(S_\epsilon^{n-2}) < C(n, \mathrm{v}, \epsilon)$ , such that  $X^n \setminus S_\epsilon^{n-2}$  is  $\epsilon$ -bi-Hölder equivalent to a smooth Riemannian manifold

nodes.label: Regular part of a metric flow
nodes.summary: The set R of regular points of a metric flow, open in the natural topology and canonically equipped with a smooth Ricci flow spacetime structure. Its complement X \ R is called the singular part.

evidence.section: 9.2 The regular part of a metric flow

nodes.label: Regular point of a metric flow
nodes.summary: A point of a metric flow that lies in an open natural-topology chart modeled on a smooth Ricci flow, with time-slice local isometries and compatibility of heat and conjugate heat flows with the smooth heat equations. Its local dimension is the dimension of the modeling Ricci flow.

evidence.section: 9.2 The regular part of a metric flow

nodes.label: Regular-representation derivation on tensors
nodes.summary: The infinitesimal so(V)-action on tensor spaces obtained by differentiating the regular O(n)-representation; it gives the derivation LT used to encode curvature interactions with tensors.

evidence.quote: The derivative of the regular representation of O(n) ... induces a derivation on tensors

nodes.label: Regular set of a noncollapsed RCD space
nodes.summary: The set of points whose tangent cone is uniquely Euclidean R^N. The singular set is the complement of this regular set.

evidence.quote: R:={x in X: Tan_x(X,d)={(R^N,d_eucl)}} is the set of regular points.

nodes.label: Regular simplex point configuration
nodes.summary: A configuration of q points in R^n with all pairwise distances equal, possible in the range 2 <= q <= n+1 and serving as the sites for simplicial Voronoi clusters.

evidence.quote: Voronoi cells of q equidistant points
Voronoi cells of q equidistant points in R^n

nodes.label: Regular-singular decomposition of a free boundary
nodes.summary: A free boundary is decomposed into a relatively open regular part, locally a C^{1,eta} graph, and a closed singular part with dimension and rectifiability bounds depending on a critical dimension.

evidence.quote: can be decomposed as a disjoint union of a regular and a (possibly empty) singular part
The regular part  $\operatorname{Reg}(\partial \Omega_u^\pm)$  is a relatively open subset
The singular set  $\mathrm{Sing}(\partial \Omega_u^{\pm})$  is a closed subset

nodes.label: Regular and singular decomposition of a Ricci limit space
nodes.summary: The decomposition of a Ricci limit space into regular points with uniform curvature control in approximating manifolds, smooth manifold points, and the complementary singular set. In the hyperkähler setting regular points are manifold points.

nodes.label: regularity of singular minimizing geodesics in analytic dimension 3
aliases: C^1 regularity of singular minimizing geodesics
nodes.summary: For analytic 3-dimensional rank-two sub-Riemannian structures, singular minimizing geodesics are C^1 and their images are semianalytic, hence finite unions of points and analytic arcs.

nodes.label: Regularization of J-positive currents
nodes.summary: A method that upgrades the weak current satisfying the J-cone inequality to a smooth Kähler form satisfying a strict cone inequality, using induction and local regularization.

evidence.quote: then φ4.29 is smooth on M and ω4.29 ... satisfies cω4.29^(n−1) − (n − 1)χ ∧ ω4.29^(n−2) > 0

nodes.label: Regularized min-max harmonic map construction
nodes.summary: A min-max method using continuous families of W^{1,2} maps parametrized by a ball whose boundary is a homotopically nontrivial sphere map into the target, applied to the regularized energies E_epsilon.

nodes.label: Reifenberg regular set estimate
nodes.summary: A geometric flatness or approximation property controlling how a set is approximated across scales, used with lower Ahlfors regularity for neck regions.

nodes.label: mod 2 relative cycle space
nodes.summary: The quotient space of n-dimensional mod 2 cycles in a compact manifold with boundary, identifying boundary-supported pieces. It is the target of sweepouts and is endowed with flat or mass topology.

nodes.label: Relative eigenvalues of Kähler forms
nodes.summary: The eigenvalues λ_i of the unknown Kähler metric ωφ relative to the fixed Kähler metric χ. They express both the J-equation limit and the dHYM phase equation.

evidence.quote: where λi are the eigenvalues of ωφ with respect to χ

nodes.label: Relative Kähler endomorphism eigenvalue vector
nodes.summary: The endomorphism $h_\varphi$ is defined by comparing $\omega_\varphi$ to the background metric $\omega_X$; $\lambda[h_\varphi]$ denotes its eigenvalue vector.

evidence.quote: let  $h_{\varphi}$  be the corresponding endomorphism relative to the metric  $\omega_{X}$
Let  $\lambda[h_{\varphi}]$  be the vector of eigenvalues of  $h_{\varphi}$

nodes.label: Renormalized limit measure
nodes.summary: The Radon measure obtained as the limit of Riemannian volume normalized by the volume of a unit ball in a Gromov-Hausdorff convergent sequence. It records collapsed volume information on the limit space.

nodes.label: Representation of a complex hyperbolic lattice
nodes.summary: A group homomorphism from a complex hyperbolic lattice into a target algebraic or Lie group. The paper's superrigidity results concern certain such representations.

evidence.quote: certain representations of complex hyperbolic lattices

nodes.label: Rescaled abelian Higgs energy
nodes.summary: The epsilon-dependent functional E_epsilon(u,nabla)=int_M(|nabla u|^2+epsilon^2|F_nabla|^2+epsilon^{-2}W(u)). Its bounded critical points are the main objects whose energy concentrates on codimension-two varifolds.

evidence.section: 2. The Yang-Mills-Higgs equations on U(1) bundles

nodes.label: Rescaled connection sequence under dilation
nodes.summary: Pull back an admissible HYM connection by complex dilations lambda z to study its small-scale limit as lambda tends to zero. Subsequential gauge limits define analytic tangent cone data.

nodes.label: Rescaled flow near a cylindrical blowdown
nodes.summary: The parabolically rescaled flow about a base point, written using logarithmic time so that convergence to the round cylinder can be analyzed as tau tends to negative infinity.

evidence.section: 4.1 Setting up the fine neck analysis

nodes.label: Rescaled mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: The flow obtained by logarithmic time and parabolic spatial rescaling near a shrinking singularity; self-shrinkers are stationary solutions and the equation is x_tau = H + (1/2)x^perp.

nodes.label: Rescaled Tian-Yau limit of Kähler-Einstein cone metrics
nodes.summary: The convergence property asserting that, for a Fano anticanonical pair, a suitable rescaling of the small-angle conical Kähler-Einstein metrics converges to the complete Ricci-flat Tian-Yau metric on X \ D.

evidence.quote: is the complete, Ricci flat Tian-Yau metric

nodes.label: Pushforward identity for products of resonant and coresonant forms
nodes.summary: An identity relating pushforwards of products of resonant and coresonant 2-forms on S\Sigma to harmonic 1-forms on \Sigma.

nodes.label: Restacking level sets into barriers
aliases: composition by concave and convex functions
nodes.summary: A method that composes the homogeneous model functions with suitable one-variable concave or convex functions so they become actual super- and sub-solutions of the anisotropic minimal graph equation without changing their growth rates.

nodes.label: Ricci current
nodes.summary: The Ricci curvature object Ric(omega) associated to a Kähler form or singular positive current. It appears directly in the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation.

nodes.label: Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: The curvature tensor contraction appearing in the Ricci flow equation as the driving term for the metric evolution.

nodes.label: Ricci curvature lower bound
nodes.summary: A lower bound on Ricci curvature, used in the paper's volume comparison step.

nodes.label: Ricci curvature tensor
nodes.summary: The symmetric two-tensor obtained by tracing the Riemann curvature tensor of a Riemannian metric. It is the curvature term in both the Ricci flow equation and the super Ricci flow inequality.

nodes.label: Ricci-DeTurck flow
nodes.summary: A gauge-fixed Ricci flow equation obtained through harmonic map heat flow, yielding a strictly parabolic evolution for metric perturbations. It is the analytic framework used to compare two Ricci flows or Ricci flow spacetimes.

evidence.section: 6.5 Ricci-DeTurck flow and harmonic map heat flow

nodes.label: Ricci-flat Kähler metric
nodes.summary: A Kähler metric whose Ricci curvature vanishes. The paper studies such metrics on K3 surfaces and their collapsing behavior.

evidence.quote: Ricci-flat Kähler metrics on K3 surfaces

nodes.label: Ricci-flat metric
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric with zero Ricci curvature. In the excerpt these are described as metrics governed by the vacuum Einstein equation.

evidence.quote: Kähler metrics with vanishing Ricci curvature

nodes.label: Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A one-parameter family of Riemannian metrics satisfying partial_t g(t) = -2 Ric_g(t).

nodes.label: Ricci flow equation
nodes.summary: The evolution equation for a Riemannian metric g(t), given by time derivative equal to minus twice its Ricci tensor. It is the PDE whose weak continuation through singularities is studied in the paper.

nodes.label: Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A four-dimensional spacetime object encoding a possibly singular three-dimensional Ricci flow by a manifold, time function, time vector field, and spatial metric satisfying the Ricci flow equation on time-slices. Singular points are not included; the object describes the regular part of the flow.

nodes.label: Ricci flow with surgery
nodes.summary: A continuation procedure for three-dimensional Ricci flow in which high-curvature singular regions are cut out and capped, after which ordinary Ricci flow is restarted. The paper contrasts this non-canonical construction with singular Ricci flows and proves convergence as the surgery scale goes to zero.

nodes.label: Ricci limit space
nodes.summary: A metric measure space arising as the measured Gromov-Hausdorff limit of manifolds with a uniform lower Ricci curvature bound. Its regular, smooth, and singular sets are defined relative to the approximating sequence.

nodes.label: Ricci soliton
nodes.summary: A Ricci flow solution evolving only by diffeomorphisms and scaling; the paper contrasts Perelman's compact ancient solution with soliton models.

evidence.quote: is  \kappa -noncollapsed and which is not a soliton

nodes.label: Riemann curvature norm
nodes.summary: The pointwise norm of the Riemann curvature tensor of the metric. It appears in the curvature decay inequality defining the paper's gravitational instantons.

nodes.label: Riemann curvature tensor
nodes.summary: The full curvature tensor of a Riemannian manifold; its pointwise norm $|\mathrm{Rm}|$ is the quantity integrated in the paper's $L^2$ curvature estimate.

nodes.label: Riemannian area measure
nodes.summary: The two-dimensional volume measure associated with a Riemannian metric. It appears in the proof through areas of domains D_m^+ and D_m^- and their convergence to pi.

nodes.label: Riemannian area of a surface
nodes.summary: The total area induced by a Riemannian metric on a surface. It is the scaling factor used to make Laplace eigenvalues area-normalized.

nodes.label: Riemannian collared end
nodes.summary: An end with a collar structure, described here as homeomorphic to a cross-section times the real line and compatible with the Riemannian end geometry.

evidence.quote: each end is parabolic and Riemannian collared

nodes.label: Riemannian distance function
nodes.summary: The metric distance d_g induced by a Riemannian metric g. The boundary distance function is obtained by restricting this distance to boundary pairs.

evidence.quote: distance function  $d_g$  of a Riemannian metric  $g$
where d is the Riemannian distance function in M

nodes.label: Riemannian domain with smooth boundary
aliases: smooth-boundary domain in a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A subdomain Omega of a Riemannian manifold whose boundary is smooth enough to define boundary measure, traces, and the outward unit normal derivative.

evidence.quote: Omega subset M be a domain with smooth boundary
evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Riemannian embedding rigidity of Euclidean planar nets
aliases: rigidity of Riemannian embeddings of planar nets
nodes.summary: If a net in R^2 embeds isometrically into a complete connected Riemannian surface, then the surface is flat and isometric to the Euclidean plane. This is the main rigidity phenomenon of the paper.

evidence.quote: Suppose that there exists a net in \mathbb{R}^2 that embeds isometrically in M. Then the manifold M is flat and it is isometric to the Euclidean plane.

nodes.label: Riemannian fibration
nodes.summary: A fibration compatible with the Riemannian metric, used here as the geometric structure obtained from the analysis of geodesic loops at infinity.

evidence.quote: From geodesic loops to Riemannian fibration

nodes.label: Riemannian geodesic
nodes.summary: A curve determined by the Riemannian metric and used to define minimizing paths, lens data, geodesic flow, and geodesic X-ray transforms.

nodes.label: Riemannian homogeneous three-manifold
nodes.summary: A three-dimensional Riemannian manifold whose isometry group acts transitively. It is the ambient class in which CMC spheres are classified.

evidence.quote: Let M be a Riemannian homogeneous three-manifold

nodes.label: Riemannian isometry
nodes.summary: A diffeomorphism between Riemannian manifolds preserving the metric. Ambient isometries are the equivalences used for CMC spheres in the paper.

evidence.quote: differ by an isometry of M
M is isometric to the Euclidean plane
(M,g) is isometric to a flat polyhedron in \mathbf{R}^3

nodes.label: Riemannian Killing field
nodes.summary: A vector field whose local flow consists of Riemannian isometries. The proof sections classify and constrain Killing fields associated with invariant surfaces.

nodes.label: Riemannian length function
nodes.summary: The curve-length functional induced by a Riemannian metric. In the proof excerpt, lengths of geodesic chord intersections are integrated in the Santaló formula.

evidence.quote: \mathsf {L e n g t h} _ {m} ((x, y) _ {m} \cap D _ {m} ^ {\pm})

nodes.label: Riemannian manifold with boundary
nodes.summary: A smooth manifold M with boundary equipped with a Riemannian metric g. It is the ambient setting for the distance, geodesic, boundary, and lens data in the paper.

evidence.quote: Riemannian metric  $g$  on a manifold with boundary  $M$

nodes.label: Riemannian metric
nodes.summary: A smooth positive-definite inner product on the tangent spaces of a smooth manifold. The paper uses time-dependent Riemannian metrics as the smooth input data for Ricci and super Ricci flows.

evidence.quote: Riemannian metric with positive scalar curvature

nodes.label: Space of Riemannian metrics
nodes.summary: The topological space of smooth Riemannian metrics on a manifold, using the C-infinity or C-infinity-local topology depending on compactness.

nodes.label: Riemannian polyhedron of P-type
nodes.summary: A Riemannian three-dimensional polyhedron modeled on a Euclidean comparison polyhedron P, with corresponding faces and dihedral angles.

evidence.quote: Let P \subset \mathbf{R}^3 be a polyhedron of cone type, (M, g) be of P -type.
Let (M^3,g) be a Riemannian polyhedron of P -type with side faces F_{1},\dots ,F_{k}

nodes.label: Second fundamental form of a hypersurface
nodes.summary: The second fundamental form of a hypersurface measures the normal component of the ambient covariant derivative of tangent vector fields; its vanishing is the total-geodesy condition for a boundary face.

evidence.quote: the second fundamental form of the boundary faces of  $\Omega$  with respect to  $g$  vanishes

nodes.label: Riemannian submersion
nodes.summary: A smooth submersion whose differential restricts to an isometry from the horizontal distribution to the tangent space of the base.

nodes.label: Riemannian symmetric space
nodes.summary: A standard rigidity outcome in higher-rank nonpositive curvature: a Riemannian manifold with geodesic symmetries at every point.

nodes.label: Riemannian universal cover
nodes.summary: The universal covering space of a Riemannian manifold equipped with the lifted Riemannian metric. The paper denotes it by X for the ambient manifold M.

evidence.quote: X denotes the Riemannian universal cover of M
the universal cover of  $M$  is a visibility manifold

nodes.label: Riemannian volume measure
nodes.summary: The measure induced by a Riemannian metric, used to form volumes and integrals in the Weyl law main term.

evidence.quote: $\mathrm{vol}_g(M)$ is the Riemannian volume of $M$, and $\mathrm{dv}_g$ is the volume measure induced by the Riemannian metric

nodes.label: Weighted Ricci tensor formula
nodes.summary: The finite-dimensional weighted Ricci expression Ric_N(xi)=Ric(xi)+Hess V(xi,xi)-1/(N-n)<nabla V,xi>^2. In the excerpt this formula is used componentwise to prove the product lower bound.

nodes.label: Rigid geometric structure
nodes.summary: A geometric structure whose automorphisms are constrained by finite-order jet data. The paper uses that a Lorentzian conformal class is rigid in Gromov's sense.

evidence.section: 2.1. Gromov's stratification and reduction to a connected, abelian group

nodes.label: Rigid motion of the sphere
nodes.summary: A self-map of $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$ of the form $u(x)=Ox$ for an orthogonal matrix $O\in O(n)$. Orientation-preserving rigid motions correspond to $O\in SO(n)$.

nodes.label: Rigorous computer-assisted interval verification
nodes.summary: A rigorous numerical verification method used to handle a remaining short interval of spectral parameter values in the Neumann disk case.

evidence.quote: a rigorous computer-assisted argument needed to cover the short interval of values of the spectral parameter in the case of the Neumann problem in the disk

nodes.label: Rotation number of a billiard orbit
nodes.summary: A number measuring the average winding of a billiard orbit around the table; rational integrability requires caustics of rotation number 1/q.

nodes.label: Rotational symmetry of a Ricci flow solution
nodes.summary: Invariance of the evolving metric under a rotational group action, used to reduce the compact three-dimensional ancient kappa-solution classification to an ODE/PDE profile analysis.

nodes.label: Rotational symmetry via moving planes
nodes.summary: A proof method combining fine asymptotics with a parabolic moving-plane argument to show that the plus-mode ancient low-entropy solution is rotationally symmetric.

nodes.label: Rotationally symmetric compact ancient kappa-solution on S^3
aliases: rotationally symmetric ancient κ-solution on S^3
nodes.summary: A compact ancient kappa-solution on S^3 whose metrics are rotationally symmetric; this is the class treated in the uniqueness part of the paper.

evidence.quote: ancient  \kappa -solutions on  S^3  which are rotationally symmetric

nodes.label: Rough solution as a frequency-truncated smooth limit
nodes.summary: A construction of rough solutions as limits of smooth solutions whose initial data are truncated below dyadic frequencies, with bootstrap bounds on the smooth solutions and their frequency increments.

evidence.quote: we finally obtain our rough solutions $u$ as a limit of smooth solutions $u^{h}$ with initial data frequency localized below frequency $2^{h}$
the bootstrap quantities are uniform energy type bounds for both $u^{h}$ and for their increments

nodes.label: Round cylinder Ricci flow model
nodes.summary: The product round cylinder S^2 x R used as an asymptotic model for rescaled regions of Perelman's ancient solution.

nodes.label: Round hemisphere in the Tits boundary
nodes.summary: An isometrically embedded standard hemisphere in the Tits boundary, used to detect when a round sphere bounds a flat half-space or is singular.

nodes.label: Round shrinking cylinder mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: The selfsimilar mean curvature flow of round cylinders shrinking in the spherical directions. It is the entropy-threshold model and the main cylindrical tangent-flow singularity model.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

evidence.quote: a round cylinder of the form

nodes.label: Round shrinking sphere mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: The selfsimilar mean curvature flow of round spheres shrinking to extinction. It is one of the classified ancient low-entropy models and a spherical tangent-flow singularity model.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Round sphere in the Tits boundary
nodes.summary: An isometrically embedded standard sphere in the Tits boundary, such as the boundary of a flat.

nodes.label: Ruelle zeta function for a geodesic flow
nodes.summary: For a negatively curved Riemannian manifold, the Ruelle zeta function is the product over primitive closed geodesics of factors determined by their lengths, initially convergent for large imaginary part of the spectral parameter.

nodes.label: Order of vanishing of the Ruelle zeta function at zero
nodes.summary: The integer m_R(0) such that lambda^{-m_R(0)} times the Ruelle zeta function is holomorphic and nonzero at lambda = 0.

nodes.label: Embeddedness near a lower-multiplicity flat disk in $S_Q$
nodes.summary: If a varifold in $S_Q$ is close to a flat disk of integer multiplicity less than $Q$, the local structure is smoothly embedded. This is one of the previously known cases reproduced and applied in the paper.

evidence.quote: $V$  is close to a flat disk of integer multiplicity  $< Q$
if  $P$  has multiplicity  $< p / 2$  ...  $T$  is smoothly embedded near  $y$

nodes.label: Structure near a classical cone in $S_Q$
nodes.summary: A local structure result for $S_Q$ varifolds close to a stationary classical cone of density $Q$ whose support consists of three or more half-hyperplanes meeting along an axis. The paper uses this case to recover known mod-$p$ structure results.

evidence.quote: $V$  is close to a stationary cone with vertex density  $Q$  and support the union of 3 or more half-hyperplanes meeting along a common axis
Structure of varifolds in  $\mathcal{S}_Q$  near classical cones

nodes.label: Structure near a multiplicity-$Q$ flat disk in $S_Q$
aliases: structure near density $Q$ planar tangent cones
nodes.summary: The critical planar case of the structure theory: an $S_Q$ varifold close to a flat disk of integer multiplicity $Q$ has controlled branching and generalized $C^{1,\alpha}$ multi-valued graphical structure. This is the main new part of the paper.

evidence.quote: The main new result concerns (i) and gives in particular a description of  $V \in S_{Q}$  near branch points of density  $Q$
$V$  is close to a flat disk of multiplicity  $Q$  (for integer  $Q$ )

nodes.label: saddle singularity of the resolved characteristic foliation
nodes.summary: After resolving the Martinet surface and taking the strict transform of the characteristic foliation, all singularities of the transformed foliation are saddle points.

evidence.quote: all singularities of Lf are saddle points

nodes.label: Santaló formula for Riemannian area
nodes.summary: An integral-geometric formula expressing Riemannian area as an integral of lengths of geodesic intersections against a measure on pairs or geodesics. The proof uses it to convert length integral convergence into area convergence.

evidence.quote: By the Santaló formula, \mathsf {A r e a} _ {m} (D _ {m} ^ {\pm}) = \frac {1}{2 \pi} \int ... \mathsf {L e n g t h} _ {m}

nodes.label: Scalar AC1 condition
nodes.summary: A pointwise scalar upper bound on the quadratic form (B_Psi(T)w,w) by (1+delta)Psi(T)||w||^2 with delta < 1/(m-1).

nodes.label: Scalar atomic condition
nodes.summary: A strict positivity condition pairing B_Psi(T) with the dual B_{Psi*}(S^perp) for distinct m-planes T and S.

evidence.quote: satisfies the scalar atomic condition (SAC) if

nodes.label: Scalar curvature
nodes.summary: The trace of the Ricci curvature; in the paper its maximum R_max(t) measures Type I versus Type II behavior backwards in time.

nodes.label: A priori estimates for scalar-curvature type equations
nodes.summary: Uniform analytic estimates for cscK and generalized scalar-curvature equations, including C^0, metric equivalence, and higher Sobolev control under entropy-type assumptions. They provide the closedness input for the continuity method.

evidence.quote: we derive apriori estimates for constant scalar curvature Kähler metrics
apriori estimates are usually the prelude to the success of any continuity path

nodes.label: Scalar-curvature rigidity for matching-angle convex polytopes
nodes.summary: A matching-angle formulation of polyhedral scalar-curvature rigidity: a compact convex Euclidean polytope carrying a Riemannian metric with nonnegative scalar curvature, mean-convex faces, and face angles matching the Euclidean model must be flat in the interior and have totally geodesic boundary faces.

evidence.quote: Then the Riemann curvature tensor of  $g$  vanishes at each point in  $\Omega$ . Moreover, the second fundamental form of the boundary faces of  $\Omega$  with respect to  $g$  vanishes.

nodes.label: Scalar quasilinear wave equation with derivative-dependent metric
nodes.summary: A second-order wave equation whose Lorentzian principal coefficients depend on u or its first derivatives, written in the paper as g^{alpha beta}(partial u) partial_alpha partial_beta u=N(u,partial u).

evidence.quote: $$g^{\alpha\beta}(\partial u)\partial_{\alpha}\partial_{\beta}u=N(u,\partial u),$$ where ... $g^{\alpha\beta}$ is assumed to be Lorentzian

nodes.label: Schauder estimate for collapsed metrics
nodes.summary: An elliptic Schauder estimate adapted to families of metrics undergoing collapse. The paper lists it as a tool before proving convergence in the positive case.

nodes.label: Schoen-Simon-Yau L^3 second-fundamental-form estimate
aliases: L^3 version of the Schoen-Simon-Yau estimate
nodes.summary: For n<8 stable minimal hypersurfaces, an estimate bounding int_M |A_M|^3 f^3 by a constant times int_M |grad f|^3. The paper uses it with Green's-function cutoffs to force flatness or finite total curvature.

evidence.section: Appendix D. The Schoen-Simon-Yau L3 estimate

nodes.label: Schrodinger controllability from a nonempty open set
nodes.summary: The ability to control the Schrodinger evolution using forcing or observation supported in any chosen nonempty open subset of the Anosov surface.

nodes.label: Schrodinger equation on a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: The dispersive evolution generated by the Laplace-Beltrami operator on a Riemannian manifold.

nodes.label: Schrodinger operator with potential on a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: For a potential V on a closed Riemannian manifold, the operator Delta_g - V has variational eigenvalues nu_m(V). The paper uses the sign of these eigenvalues to formulate an equivalent optimization problem.

nodes.label: Second boundary value problem for the real Monge-Ampère equation
aliases: Monge-Ampère equation with natural boundary condition
optimal transport Monge-Ampère boundary value problem
nodes.summary: The boundary value problem consisting of det D^2 u=f in Ω together with the gradient image condition Du(Ω)=Ω*. The paper studies global regularity for convex solutions when Ω and Ω* are C^{1,1} convex domains.

evidence.quote: we establish the global $C^{2,\alpha}$ and $W^{2,p}$ regularity for the Monge-Ampère equation ... subject to the boundary condition
The boundary value problem (1.1) and (1.2) arises naturally in optimal transportation

nodes.label: Norm of the second fundamental form
aliases: norm of the second fundamental form
nodes.summary: For a surface Sigma in Euclidean three-space, |A_Sigma|(p denotes the norm of the second fundamental form of Sigma at the point p. It is the curvature quantity used to state curvature blow-up and curvature estimates for H-disks.

evidence.quote: |A_{\Sigma}|(p) denotes the norm of the second fundamental form of \Sigma at p

nodes.label: Second fundamental form of a submanifold
nodes.summary: The symmetric bilinear form on tangent vectors whose values lie in the normal space, measuring the extrinsic curvature of a Euclidean submanifold.

nodes.label: Second-order elliptic reduction for cscK
nodes.summary: A method or viewpoint linking the cscK equation to a second-order elliptic equation. It underlies the continuity-path strategy discussed in the paper.

evidence.quote: links the cscK equation to certain second order elliptic equation

nodes.label: Second variation of harmonic map energy
nodes.summary: At a harmonic map, the second variation of Dirichlet energy is a quadratic form on sections of the pullback tangent bundle u^*TN.

evidence.section: 2 Existence and partial regularity of min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Sectional curvature bounds -1 <= K <= 0
nodes.summary: A two-sided sectional-curvature hypothesis requiring all sectional curvatures to lie between -1 and 0.

nodes.label: Self-dual Yang-Mills-Higgs energy
nodes.summary: The energy E(u,nabla)=int_M(|nabla u|^2+|F_nabla|^2+W(u)) for a section and Hermitian connection. It is the unscaled self-dual abelian Higgs energy whose rescalings are studied.

nodes.label: Self-shrinker
nodes.summary: A hypersurface that occurs as a time slice of a self-similarly shrinking mean curvature flow and satisfies H + x^perp/2 = 0.

evidence.quote: in which case  $M_{-1}$  is called a shrinker

nodes.label: Self-similarly shrinking mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A mean curvature flow whose time slices evolve by homotheties, equivalently M_t is a scale multiple of a fixed time slice for negative time.

evidence.quote: solutions to the mean curvature flow that evolve by homotheties

nodes.label: Semi-positive loop in the Einstein Universe
nodes.summary: A loop in the Einstein Universe satisfying a weakened positivity condition. In the paper every complete maximal surface has a semi-positive limit curve, and every semi-positive loop bounds a unique complete maximal surface.

evidence.quote: every complete maximal surface $\Sigma$ has a limit curve $\partial_{\infty}\Sigma$ in $\partial_{\infty}\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ which is a semi-positive loop
every such semi-positive loop is the boundary of a unique complete maximal surface

nodes.label: semianalytic curve
nodes.summary: A compact connected semianalytic subset of a real analytic manifold whose Hausdorff dimension is at most 1.

nodes.label: semianalytic set
nodes.summary: A subset locally expressible as a finite union of sets defined by analytic equalities and strict analytic inequalities; such sets admit locally finite analytic stratifications.

nodes.label: Semiclassical Laplacian quasimode
nodes.summary: A normalized family u_h for which (-h^2 Delta - I)u_h tends to zero at a prescribed semiclassical rate; the paper treats errors o(h/log(1/h)).

nodes.label: Semiclassical measure
nodes.summary: A phase-space weak limit describing the microlocal distribution of a high-energy eigenfunction or quasimode sequence.

nodes.label: Semiclassical quantization on a manifold
nodes.summary: A procedure assigning to a phase-space symbol a semiclassical operator Op_h(a) acting on functions on the manifold.

nodes.label: Semiclassical short-time Strichartz estimates
