nodes.summary: A frequency-localized method for rough-coefficient wave equations in which estimates are proved at dyadic frequency 2^k on time scales T_k=2^{-alpha k} and then summed, producing Strichartz estimates with derivative loss.

evidence.quote: associated to each dyadic frequency scale $2^{k}$, there is a corresponding “ semiclassical ” time scale $T_{k}=2^{-\alpha k}$
Strichartz estimates with loss of derivatives are then obtained by summing up the short time estimates

nodes.label: Standard semiclassical symbol class S^0(T^*M)
nodes.summary: The order-zero symbol class on the cotangent bundle used as inputs to semiclassical pseudodifferential quantization.

nodes.label: Semidirect product construction of Einstein solvmanifolds
nodes.summary: A construction showing that under compatibility hypotheses a semidirect product of the AN factor with a solvable radical carries a left-invariant Einstein metric.

evidence.quote: semi-direct products of Einstein submanifolds are again Einstein

nodes.label: Semilocal decay estimate for Ricci-DeTurck perturbations
nodes.summary: An interior decay estimate showing that the weighted perturbation at a point is smaller than its supremum over a sufficiently large backward parabolic neighborhood. It is proved using blow-up limits and vanishing for the linearized Ricci-DeTurck equation on kappa-solutions.

nodes.label: Semipositive klt current
nodes.summary: A twist current theta that is semipositive and klt. This is the twist hypothesis in the paper's twisted Fano existence and stability theorem.

evidence.quote: $\theta$  a semipositive klt current such that  $c_{1}(X,\theta) = c_{1}(L)$

nodes.label: Semisimple Lie group
nodes.summary: A Lie group whose Lie algebra is semisimple; the paper uses such groups as ambient groups for lattices and target-compatibility questions.

evidence.quote: a connected adjoint semisimple Lie group with no compact factors

nodes.label: Compact moduli space of semistable sheaves
nodes.summary: A compact complex analytic moduli space receiving the weak normalization of the semistable Quot locus, where points determine the same HNS double dual and algebraic blow-up cycle. It is used to compare algebraic and analytic limits.

nodes.label: Semistable torsion-free sheaf
nodes.summary: A torsion-free sheaf whose subsheaves have slope no larger than its slope. Semistable and polystable sheaves enter HNS filtrations and the moduli compactification used in the proof.

nodes.label: Sen Hodge cohomology prediction for monopole moduli spaces
nodes.summary: The prediction that the L2 Hodge cohomology of the universal cover of the reduced SU(2)-monopole moduli space is concentrated in middle degree with a description governed by the natural cyclic action. In the charge-three case, the paper proves the image formula assuming the announced QFB compactification.

nodes.label: Separating multicurve
nodes.summary: A multicurve whose removal disconnects the underlying surface; the paper estimates its contribution separately from the non-separating case.

nodes.label: Shape operator of submersion fibres
nodes.summary: The vertical endomorphism L_X(U)=V nabla_U X describing the second fundamental form of fibers in a horizontal direction X.

evidence.quote: is the shape operator of the fibres

nodes.label: Sharp codimension-two submanifold Sobolev inequality
nodes.summary: The codimension-two specialization of the submanifold Sobolev inequality, with sharp constant n|B^n|^{1/n}. It follows from the general inequality using the unit-ball volume recursion.

nodes.label: Sharp cone-splitting
nodes.summary: A strengthened quantitative cone-splitting principle that promotes independent approximate conical information into sharper Euclidean splitting control.

evidence.quote: including a sharp cone-splitting theorem and a geometric transformation theorem

nodes.label: Sharp energy estimates for Moser-Trudinger critical points
nodes.summary: Analytic estimates used with compactness and quantization to control the energy of Moser-Trudinger type critical points in the limiting argument. The excerpt names the method but does not provide its technical form.

evidence.quote: with compactness, quantization results and with sharp energy estimates

nodes.label: Sharp first Steklov supremum on the sphere
nodes.summary: The first normalized Steklov supremum for genus-zero orientable closed type is sharp on the sphere, with value 8 pi.

nodes.label: Sharp isoperimetric inequality for minimal submanifolds
nodes.summary: For compact minimal submanifolds in Euclidean space of codimension at most two, the boundary measure satisfies |partial Sigma| >= n|B^n|^{1/n}|Sigma|^{(n-1)/n}. Equality is realized only by flat round balls in the stated setting.

nodes.label: Sharp L4 exponent for the Heisenberg vertical inequality
nodes.summary: The exponent 4 in the scale norm of the vertical difference inequality is optimal: no L_q(dt/t) version with q<4 can replace it.

evidence.quote: one cannot replace the  $L_{4}(dt / t)$  norm above by an  $L_{q}(dt / t)$  norm for any  $0 < q < 4$

nodes.label: Sharp $L^\infty$ estimate for complex Monge-Ampère equations
aliases: uniform estimate for complex Monge-Ampère potentials
nodes.summary: A uniform bound for normalized solutions of complex Monge-Ampère equations in the sharp density range $q>1$. The paper's contribution is a PDE proof that also covers degenerating backgrounds.

evidence.quote: sharp  $L^{\infty}$  estimates for the complex Monge-Ampère equation
whether sharp  $L^{\infty}$  estimates can be established by PDE methods and without pluripotential theory

nodes.label: Sharp Poincare inequality on the sphere
nodes.summary: The spectral inequality $\int_{\mathbb{S}^{n-1}}|\nabla_Tu|^2\ge (n-1)\int|u-\fint u|^2$, obtained from the first nonzero spherical harmonic eigenvalue. The paper uses it repeatedly in compactness and linearization arguments.

nodes.label: Sharp projective-plane isoperimetric eigenvalue inequality
nodes.summary: For every k>=1, Lambda_k(RP^2)=4*pi*(2k+1), and for smooth metrics with k>1 the normalized k-th eigenvalue is strictly below this value. The bound is approached by the bubbling maximizing sequence of round spheres plus a standard projective plane.

nodes.label: Sharp Sobolev inequality for Euclidean submanifolds
nodes.summary: A Sobolev inequality for positive smooth functions on compact Euclidean submanifolds, with left side combining the intrinsic gradient, mean curvature vector, and boundary integral, and with a dimension-codimension dependent Euclidean ball constant.

nodes.label: Short geodesic loop
nodes.summary: A geodesic loop of small length used to probe collapsed geometry at infinity. The paper uses such loops to build local covers and eventually an asymptotic fibration.

evidence.quote: Short geodesic loops and the local covering space

nodes.label: Short map on the sphere
nodes.summary: A Lipschitz map $u:\mathbb{S}^{n-1}\to\mathbb{R}^n$ whose tangential metric tensor satisfies $\nabla_Tu^t\nabla_Tu\le I_x$ almost everywhere. The paper notes that the stretch-only isometric deficit vanishes on such maps.

nodes.label: Short-time cone model for conical level set flow
nodes.summary: For hypersurfaces with isolated conical singularities in dimensions $2\leq n\leq 6$, the short-time level set flow is modeled on the cone flow: outermost flows are smooth and rescaled slices converge to the time-one cone flow or to the corresponding outermost expanders.

evidence.quote: the level set flow of a smooth hypersurface $M^n \subset \mathbb{R}^{n + 1}$, $2 \leq n \leq 6$, with an isolated conical singularity is modeled on the level set flow of the cone

there is a $T > 0$ such that the outermost mean curvature flows of $M$ are smooth for $t \in (0, T)$. Moreover, $t^{-1/2} F_t(M)$ converges in the local Hausdorff sense to $F_1(\mathcal{C})$ as $t \searrow 0$.
the outermost expanders approximate the outermost flows

nodes.label: Shrinker mean convexity
nodes.summary: A monotonicity property for a flow relative to parabolic dilations about the shrinker; geometrically, the rescaled flow moves in one direction and supports mean-convex-type regularity arguments.

nodes.label: Stability operator of a self-shrinker
nodes.summary: The linear operator L u = Delta_Sigma u - (1/2)x dot grad_Sigma u + (1/2)u + |A|^2 u, self-adjoint in Gaussian weighted L2, governing the linearized rescaled flow over a shrinker.

evidence.section: 3.1 Spectral theory in Gaussian L2 space

nodes.label: Shrinking round sphere Ricci flow
nodes.summary: The self-similar Ricci flow solution on a round sphere whose radius decreases to extinction; it is one classification alternative for compact ancient kappa-solutions on S^3.

evidence.quote: isometric to a family of shrinking round spheres

nodes.label: Side-base dihedral angle
aliases: angle between a side face and the base face
nodes.summary: For a cone or prism comparison polyhedron, the angle between a side face and the base face, denoted gamma_j in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: \gamma_j be the dihedral angle between F_{j}' and B'
Denote by \gamma_{j} the angle between F_{j}^{\prime} and the base face of P

nodes.label: Sigma-finite metric-measure space
nodes.summary: A metric-measure space whose measure is sigma-finite. The correction notes that a globalization argument originally for normalized metric-measure spaces has been extended to this class.

evidence.quote: the original argument in [4] only applied to normalized mm-spaces but it is extended to sigma-finite mm-spaces in [8]

nodes.label: Signed volume of a sphere map
nodes.summary: For $u:\mathbb{S}^{n-1}\to\mathbb{R}^n$, $V_n(u)$ is the normalized integral of $\langle u,\bigwedge_i\partial_{	au_i}u\rangle$, interpreted through the Hodge dual or determinant. It extends enclosed oriented volume to low-regularity sphere maps.

nodes.label: Signed-volume second variation operator
nodes.summary: The first-order operator $A(w)=(\operatorname{div}_{\mathbb{S}^{n-1}}w)x-\sum_j x_j\nabla_Tw^j$ associated with the second derivative of the signed volume at the identity. It is self-adjoint on the spherical harmonic subspaces used in the proof.

evidence.section: 4.2 On the coercivity of the quadratic form

nodes.label: Simons cone $\mathbf{C}^{3,3}$
nodes.summary: The quadratic cone $\{(x,y)\in \mathbb{R}^8: |x|=|y|\}$. It is the principal example of a minimizing quadratic cone in the excerpt.

nodes.label: Simple Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian manifold with boundary whose boundary is strictly convex and whose any two points are joined by a unique minimizing geodesic. It is a classical geometric restriction in boundary rigidity.

evidence.quote: The most common restriction is the simplicity of  $(M,g)$
the boundary is strictly convex and any two points in  $M$  can be joined by a unique minimizing geodesic

nodes.label: Simple Riemannian manifold with boundary
nodes.summary: A standard class of Riemannian manifolds with boundary used in boundary rigidity, typically requiring convex boundary and well-behaved minimizing geodesics. The excerpt invokes the class but does not define its full hypotheses.

evidence.quote: in a simple Riemannian manifold with boundary

nodes.label: Simple 2-vector representation of G(4,2)
nodes.summary: A representation of unoriented 2-planes in R^4 by nonzero simple 2-vectors modulo sign and scale, using the projection onto span(v1,v2).

evidence.quote: representation of G(4, 2) in terms of the simple 2-vectors
associate the projection on span{v1, v2}

nodes.label: Simplicial Gaussian cluster
nodes.summary: A Gaussian q-cluster obtained as the Voronoi cells of q equidistant points in R^n. These clusters are the proposed and proved Gaussian multi-bubble minimizers for prescribed positive Gaussian cell measures when 2 <= q <= n+1.

evidence.quote: simplicial cluster, obtained from the Voronoi cells of q equidistant points
using simplicial clusters, obtained as the Voronoi cells of q equidistant points in R^n

nodes.label: singular control
aliases: critical control of the endpoint map
nodes.summary: A control for which the differential of the endpoint mapping is not onto, equivalently the endpoint map is not a submersion at that control.

nodes.label: singular endpoint set
aliases: set of singular values from x
nodes.summary: For a starting point x, the set of endpoints reached by singular horizontal paths starting from x.

nodes.label: singular horizontal curve
aliases: image of a singular horizontal path
nodes.summary: The subset of M traced by a singular horizontal path, namely the image gamma([0,1]).

nodes.label: singular horizontal path
nodes.summary: A horizontal path that is realized by a singular control for the endpoint mapping. The property is independent of the chosen spanning vector fields and parametrizing control.

nodes.label: Singular hypersphere in a Morse-flat boundary
nodes.summary: A round hypersphere in the Tits boundary of a periodic Morse flat that bounds an external round hemisphere in the ambient Tits boundary and glues with opposite hemispheres in the Morse-flat boundary to round spheres.

evidence.quote: called singular, if there exists a round hemisphere
evidence.section: 5.1 Tits boundary of a periodic Morse flat

nodes.label: Singular Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A weak three-dimensional Ricci flow through singularities modeled by a complete Ricci flow spacetime satisfying canonical neighborhood assumptions. It replaces finite-scale surgery by singular structure at infinitesimal scale.

evidence.quote: A singular Ricci flow is a Ricci flow spacetime

nodes.label: Space-form component asymptotics in singular Ricci flow
nodes.summary: For spherical space-form or compact hyperbolic initial data, the singular Ricci flow has a unique nontrivial punctured-copy component whose metrics converge, modulo rescaling, to the corresponding constant-curvature metric. This supplies the limiting geometry later pulled back to time zero.

nodes.label: Singular set of a limit space
nodes.summary: The singular part of the Ricci-limit space, filtered by strata according to the symmetries of tangent cones.

evidence.quote: there is a filtration of the singular set,  $S^0 \subset S^1 \cdots S^{n-1} \coloneqq S$
then the singular set  $\mathcal{S}(X)$  is  $n - 4$  rectifiable

nodes.label: Singular special Kähler metric
aliases: singular special Kähler metric on S2
nodes.summary: A smooth special Kähler metric on a punctured Riemann surface with prescribed Type I, Type II, or Type III local models near finitely many points. Such singular metrics are asymptotic to flat special Kähler cone models and have local integral monodromy.

nodes.label: Singular strata of a noncollapsed RCD space
nodes.summary: The nested strata S^k of the singular set are defined by failure of tangent cones to split off R^{k+1}. The top-dimensional stratum S^{N-1} is central to the boundary definition in noncollapsed RCD spaces.

evidence.quote: S^0 subset S^1 subset ... subset S^{N-1}=S:=X\R
S^k:={x in X: no tangent cone at x splits off R^{k+1}}.

nodes.label: Rectifiability of singular strata
nodes.summary: The main structural property that every classical singular stratum S^k is k-rectifiable and has k-symmetric tangent cones at Hausdorff k-a.e. point.

evidence.quote: for  $\mathcal{H}^k$ -a.e.  $x \in S^k$  that every tangent cone  $X_x$  at  $x$  is  $k$ -symmetric

nodes.label: Singular support of the wave trace
nodes.summary: The set of times at which the wave trace is not smooth; for billiard domains, positive singularities can occur only at times in the length spectrum.

nodes.label: Singular-time dimension bound for the Stefan problem in R^3
nodes.summary: In three spatial dimensions, the set of times at which singular Stefan free-boundary points occur has Hausdorff dimension at most 1/2, implying smooth free boundary for almost every time.

evidence.quote: In \mathbb{R}^3, the free boundary is smooth for almost every time t, and the set of singular times \mathcal{S} \subset \mathbb{R} has Hausdorff dimension at most 1/2

nodes.label: Elliptic, parabolic, or hyperbolic Killing field type in SLtilde(2,R)
nodes.summary: A trichotomy for relevant Killing fields in metric Lie groups isomorphic to the universal cover of SL(2,R). The proof treats elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic cases separately.

evidence.quote: K_Sigma is parabolic ... K_Sigma is hyperbolic

nodes.label: Metric Lie group isomorphic to the universal cover of SL(2,R)
aliases: metric Lie group isomorphic to SLtilde(2,R)
nodes.summary: A metric Lie group whose underlying Lie group is isomorphic to the universal cover of SL(2,R). The paper treats this as a separate ambient case.

evidence.quote: Metric Lie groups isomorphic to widetilde{SL}(2,R)

nodes.label: Slope of a torsion-free sheaf
nodes.summary: The normalized degree/rank invariant of a torsion-free sheaf with respect to the standard polarization on CP^{n-1}. It orders Harder-Narasimhan factors and defines optimality.

nodes.label: Small-angle regime for Kähler-Einstein cone metrics
nodes.summary: The asymptotic regime in which the cone angle parameter beta tends to zero for a family of conical Kähler-Einstein metrics. The paper asks whether these metrics converge to canonical metrics, possibly after rescaling.

nodes.label: Smooth Carleson square function
nodes.summary: A smoothed version of the Carleson square function built from a kernel-dependent local quantity a_{Γ,ψ}(x,r) and integrated over scales. The proof estimates this smoothed square function on Lipschitz or approximate Lipschitz graphs to control bad sets.

evidence.quote: The smooth square function on Lipschitz graphs

nodes.label: Smooth Cheeger-Gromov convergence
nodes.summary: A smooth convergence notion for geometric structures using diffeomorphisms from exhausting domains of a limit to the approximating spaces so that pulled-back metrics and related tensors converge smoothly on compact subsets. The paper uses it as the model for smooth convergence of regular parts.

nodes.label: Smooth compact Kähler Monge-Ampère solvability
aliases: Yau solvability for compact Kähler Monge-Ampère equations
nodes.summary: The solvability principle allowing one to solve a smooth normalized complex Monge-Ampère equation on a compact Kähler manifold. The paper invokes it to produce the auxiliary potential $\psi$.

evidence.quote: Let us solve the following auxiliary Monge-Ampère equation with  $\sup \psi = 0$ , which is solvable due to Yau's theorem

nodes.label: Smooth cone
nodes.summary: A cone over a smooth embedded closed link, smooth away from its vertex and invariant under spatial dilation. It is the local model for the isolated singularity and the initial data for the model level set flow.

evidence.quote: Turning to the cone $\mathcal{C}$ ... we note dilation invariance
Consider a smooth, embedded, closed hypersurface. We then call the cone over ... smooth.

nodes.label: Smooth convergence on the regular part
nodes.summary: The upgrade of F-convergence of metric flow pairs to local smooth convergence on an open subset R* of the regular part of the limit, under local uniform curvature bounds on the approximating regular parts. It compares Ricci flow spacetime structures via diffeomorphisms, analogously to smooth Cheeger-Gromov convergence.

nodes.label: Smooth convergence with multiplicity
aliases: smooth convergence with multiplicity one or two
nodes.summary: A mode of convergence of surfaces on compact subsets in which the convergence is smooth and may occur with a specified sheet multiplicity. It is used to describe the helicoid alternative for H-disk sequences.

evidence.quote: converge smoothly with multiplicity one or two on compact subsets of \mathbb{R}^3 to a helicoid

nodes.label: Smooth convex domain exhaustion of a polytope
nodes.summary: A convex polytope is approximated from the inside by an increasing one-parameter family of smooth convex domains whose union is the polytope interior.

evidence.quote: We approximate a given convex polytope  $\Omega$  by a one-parameter family of smooth convex domains  $\Omega_{\lambda}$
The domains  $\Omega_{\lambda}$  form an increasing family of sets, and their union equals the interior of  $\Omega$ .

nodes.label: Smooth diffeomorphism
nodes.summary: A smooth bijection between smooth manifolds with smooth inverse. The paper's n-dimensional result concludes a topological-smooth classification rather than full metric flatness.

nodes.label: Smooth divisor
nodes.summary: A nonsingular codimension-one complex subvariety D in the ambient projective manifold. It is the locus along which the conical metrics are singular.

evidence.quote: D \subset X be a smooth divisor

nodes.label: smooth embedded closed minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A closed smooth embedded codimension-one submanifold that is a critical point of the n-volume functional in an ambient Riemannian manifold.

nodes.label: Smooth-expansion stratification of the Stefan singular set
nodes.summary: A refinement of the singular set into points with smooth local expansions and an exceptional set of parabolic Hausdorff dimension at most n-2.

evidence.quote: The solution admits a C^\infty-expansion at all singular points, up to a set of parabolic Hausdorff dimension at most n - 2

nodes.label: Smooth extension integrand Phi from Psi
nodes.summary: The paper constructs a uniformly elliptic integrand Phi on R^5 by extending the R^4 integrand Psi through a locally uniformly convex auxiliary function and a one-homogeneous formula in the extra variable. This choice fixes the graphical PDE solved later.

evidence.quote: Finally, we define Phi(x, y, z)

nodes.label: Smooth Finslerian space
nodes.summary: A smooth Finsler metric-measure space, forming a principal class of metric-measure spaces that are not infinitesimally Hilbertian. In the correction, smooth Finslerian spaces are the second class for which the tensorization statements remain valid.

evidence.quote: smooth Finslerian spaces (in the sense of [9] and [10])
Finslerian spaces constitute the main class of metric measure spaces that are not infinitesimally Hilbertian

nodes.label: Smooth free boundary outside the Stefan singular set
nodes.summary: The regularity property that the Stefan free boundary is smooth in space and time away from the closed singular set.

evidence.quote: the free boundary ... is C^\infty in space and time, outside some closed set \Sigma

nodes.label: Smooth local arc-deviation quantity
nodes.summary: The quantity a_{Γ,ψ}(x,r) is a smoothed local deviation associated with the boundary Γ, a scale r, and a smoothing kernel ψ. It is the pointwise integrand whose square and scale integral appear in estimates for the smooth Carleson square function.

nodes.label: Smooth n-manifold
nodes.summary: A smooth manifold of dimension n, used as the ambient topological category for Riemannian metrics, connected sums, and torus examples.

evidence.section: 1.2. The Geroch conjecture with arbitrary ends

nodes.label: Smooth planar domain
nodes.summary: A domain in the Euclidean plane whose boundary is smooth; the paper considers bounded smooth planar domains as competitors in the inverse spectral problem.

nodes.label: Smooth positive weight on a closed surface
nodes.summary: A smooth strictly positive function on the closed surface used to weight the exponential integrals in the functionals and Euler-Lagrange equations.

nodes.label: Smooth projective manifold
nodes.summary: A complex manifold that is smooth and projective algebraic. It is the ambient space X for the paper's twisted Kähler-Einstein and stability statements.

nodes.label: Smoothing of currents
nodes.summary: A local smoothing procedure for potentials and currents using convolution kernels; it commutes with derivatives and lets positivity conditions be interpreted for nonsmooth currents.

evidence.quote: For any δ > 0, the smoothing φδ is defined by
evidence.section: 3 Concentration of mass and its application
3 Concentration of mass and its application

nodes.label: Root-space decomposition of so(2,n) for conformal Lorentzian geometry
nodes.summary: The decomposition of the Lie algebra so(2,n) into graded and restricted root spaces relative to a split Cartan subalgebra, with roots alpha and beta. It supplies the weights used to classify holonomy sequences and curvature modules.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Sobolev Cauchy data space for wave evolution
nodes.summary: The product Sobolev space H^s x H^{s-1} used for position and velocity Cauchy data of the wave evolution.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Sobolev sphere map to Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A weakly differentiable map from the unit sphere into Euclidean space, usually measured through its tangential gradient. The paper studies such maps in low regularity classes such as $W^{1,2}$ and $W^{1,n-1}$.

nodes.label: Solenoidal gauge for tensor fields
nodes.summary: A tensor tomography gauge associated with solenoidal tensor representatives. The excerpt mentions it as the earlier framework refined and extended by the normal-gauge analysis.

evidence.quote: refining and extending our earlier results in the solenoidal gauge

nodes.label: Solenoidal spherical harmonic decomposition
nodes.summary: A decomposition of each spherical harmonic subspace into divergence-free harmonic extensions and their orthogonal complements, then into eigenspaces of the signed-volume operator $A$. The eigenspaces have eigenvalues $-k$, $1$, and $k+n-2$.

evidence.section: 4.2 On the coercivity of the quadratic form

nodes.label: Space of directions
nodes.summary: The CAT(1) link at a point of a CAT(kappa) space, equipped with the angle metric and whose points are directions.

evidence.quote: space of directions or link at a point
evidence.section: 2.3 Spaces with an upper curvature bound

nodes.label: Space of Kähler potentials
nodes.summary: The space H of potentials phi for which omega_phi = omega_0 + i partial partialbar phi is positive, giving Kähler metrics cohomologous to omega_0. It is the domain of the K-energy, geodesic distances, and continuity paths.

nodes.label: Spacelike surface in pseudo-hyperbolic space
nodes.summary: A surface immersed in H^{2,n} whose induced metric is Riemannian. Maximal surfaces in the paper are spacelike surfaces with vanishing mean curvature vector.

evidence.quote: A maximal surface in $\mathbf{H}^{2,n}$ is a spacelike surface on which the mean curvature vector vanishes everywhere.

nodes.label: Spacetime convex function
nodes.summary: A smooth function ψ whose Hessian satisfies Hess ψ(X,X) ≥ λ<X,X> at each point for some real λ and all tangent vectors X. The paper uses this weaker alternative to strict pseudoconvexity for unique continuation.

nodes.label: Spatial diameter of a Lorentzian manifold
nodes.summary: The supremum of lengths of inextendible spacelike geodesics in the Lorentzian manifold. It enters the positive-curvature case of the curvature hypothesis.

evidence.quote: supremum of the length of inextendible spacelike geodesics

nodes.label: Special affine metric on a 3-manifold
nodes.summary: A Riemannian metric on a special affine three-manifold of the form V times the flat background metric, where V is a positive harmonic function in special affine coordinates. It is the regular-region structure for three-dimensional collapsed hyperkähler limits.

nodes.label: Special affine structure on a 3-manifold
nodes.summary: An affine structure on a three-manifold whose monodromy lies in the Euclidean translations extended by the sign group Z2. It determines a flat background metric up to scale.

nodes.label: Local integral monodromy for special Kähler surfaces
nodes.summary: The property that each local monodromy matrix of the special Kähler structure around a loop is conjugate to an element of SL(2,Z). It is weaker than global integral monodromy and is forced for two-dimensional collapsed hyperkähler limits in this paper.

nodes.label: Special Kähler manifold
nodes.summary: A Kähler manifold equipped with a torsion-free flat symplectic connection satisfying the special Kähler integrability condition. In the paper, two-dimensional collapsed regular regions inherit this structure.

nodes.label: Special unitary group SU(n,1)
nodes.summary: The special unitary Lie group preserving a Hermitian form of signature (n,1). The paper focuses on rank-one superrigidity tools for G = SU(n,1).

evidence.quote: for G = SU(n,1) superrigidity with compatible targets is not enough

nodes.label: Spectral analysis on the cylinder
nodes.summary: Analysis of perturbations of the cylindrical shrinker through spectral modes of the linearized flow operator around the cylinder.

evidence.quote: promoting the spectral analysis on the cylinder to global geometric properties

nodes.label: Spectral determination of nearly circular ellipses
aliases: spectral uniqueness of nearly circular ellipses
nodes.summary: The property that an ellipse of sufficiently small eccentricity is determined up to isometry among smooth planar domains by its Dirichlet or Neumann Laplace spectrum.

nodes.label: Spectral-energy index comparison for harmonic maps
nodes.summary: A family of inequalities relating the spectral index and energy index of a linearly full harmonic map into a sphere. The paper uses a general lower bound for ind_S in terms of ind_E and stronger bounds for maps from S^2 or RP^2, plus the reverse-type bound ind_E(Phi)>=2(m-1) ind_S(Phi) for harmonic two-spheres in S^{2m}.

nodes.label: Spectral index of a harmonic map
nodes.summary: The number of negative eigenvalues of the conformally covariant Schrodinger operator L_{g,Phi}=Delta_g-|nabla Phi|_g^2 associated to a sphere-valued harmonic map; spectral nullity is dim ker L_{g,Phi}. Equivalently for g_Phi it counts eigenvalues of Delta_{g_Phi} below 2, with nullity the multiplicity at 2.

nodes.label: Spectral index of a sphere-valued harmonic map
nodes.summary: For a harmonic map u to a sphere, the spectral index is the number of negative eigenvalues of the associated Schrodinger operator Delta_g - |du|^2; the spectral nullity is the dimension of its kernel.

nodes.label: Spectral-lattice counting correspondence for the disk
aliases: disk spectral problem to lattice counting link
nodes.summary: A correspondence relating Dirichlet and Neumann eigenvalue counting functions for the disk to lattice counting functions, used to transfer lattice estimates to spectral inequalities.

evidence.quote: we develop the known links between the spectral problems in the disk and certain lattice counting problems

nodes.label: Schwartz kernel of the spectral projector
nodes.summary: The integral kernel of the Laplacian spectral projector, expressed as the sum of products of eigenfunctions with eigenvalue parameter at most $\lambda$.

nodes.label: Sphere bundle of a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: The bundle of unit tangent vectors over a Riemannian manifold. The paper writes this bundle as M = S\Sigma and studies the geodesic flow on it.

nodes.label: Laplace-Beltrami operator on the sphere
nodes.summary: The intrinsic Laplacian on $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$. Its eigenspaces provide the spherical harmonic decomposition used in the linear theory.

nodes.label: Dirichlet energy of a sphere map
nodes.summary: The average $(n-1)$-Dirichlet energy of a sphere map; for $n=3$, $D_2(u)=\frac12\fint_{\mathbb{S}^2}|\nabla_Tu|^2$. It is the conformal-side energy entering Wente's inequality and the conformal-isoperimetric deficit.

nodes.label: Sphere-valued harmonic map
aliases: harmonic map to a round sphere
nodes.summary: A harmonic map whose target is a standard round sphere S^k. In this paper such maps arise from the general min-max construction with target S^k.

nodes.label: Spherical harmonic Fourier decomposition
nodes.summary: The expansion of a sphere map into spherical harmonic modes $u=\sum_{k,j}\alpha_{n,k,j}\psi_{n,k,j}$. Parseval identities for $u$ and $\nabla_Tu$ are used throughout the linear estimates.

nodes.label: Spherical join decomposition
nodes.summary: A join decomposition of CAT(1) spaces; in CAT(0) geometry it models the Tits boundary of a metric product.

nodes.label: Spherical law of cosines
nodes.summary: A trigonometric identity for spherical triangles used to relate face normals, planar triangle angles, and dihedral angles in the tangent cone comparison.

evidence.quote: By the spherical law of cosines, applied to the spherical triangle cut out by E_{\infty} from a small 2-sphere centered at A

nodes.label: Spherical or cylindrical singularity
aliases: round sphere or round cylinder singularity
nodes.summary: A mean-curvature-flow singularity whose tangent flow is a multiplicity-one round shrinking sphere or round shrinking cylinder. These are the singularities around which the paper proves mean-convex neighborhoods and nonfattening.

nodes.label: Spherical orbifold
nodes.summary: An orbifold locally modeled on spherical geometry; in the paper certain submetry bases Delta are shown to be spherical orbifolds.

nodes.label: Spherical space form
nodes.summary: A quotient of the round sphere by a group of orthogonal transformations acting freely; in the excerpt the three-dimensional quotient $S^3/\Gamma_x$ forms the link of the codimension-four tangent cone.

evidence.quote: $S^3 / \Gamma_x$  for some  $\Gamma_x \subseteq O(4)$  which acts freely away from the origin

nodes.label: Three-dimensional spherical space form
nodes.summary: A closed 3-manifold modeled on the round 3-sphere, equivalently carrying a metric of constant sectional curvature +1. The paper treats such forms other than S^3 and RP^3.

evidence.quote: spherical space forms other than S^3 and RP^3

nodes.label: Square-tiled surface
nodes.summary: A flat surface assembled from unit squares, carrying horizontal and vertical directions and conical singularities; in this paper these surfaces are counted in relation to moduli spaces of quadratic differentials.

evidence.quote: random square-tiled surface of large genus  $g$
Square-tiled surfaces, simple closed multicurves and stable graphs

nodes.label: Frequency of square-tiled surfaces of fixed combinatorial type
nodes.summary: A counting frequency for square-tiled surfaces with a fixed combinatorial type, used in the large-genus random square-tiled-surface model.

evidence.quote: Frequencies of square-tilled surfaces of fixed combinatorial type

nodes.label: Squash inequality for coarse blow-ups
nodes.summary: An analytic inequality in the coarse blow-up theory controlling components of the blow-up relevant to branching. The excerpt names it as a proof ingredient but does not state its formula.

nodes.label: Stability of Ricci flow spacetimes
aliases: strong stability of Ricci flow spacetimes
weak stability of Ricci flow spacetimes
nodes.summary: A comparison property asserting that sufficiently close initial time-slices of Ricci flow spacetimes remain geometrically close away from high-curvature regions. The comparison is built on suitable domains and evolves by harmonic map heat flow with controlled Ricci-DeTurck perturbation.

nodes.label: Stability threshold
nodes.summary: A purely algebro-geometric threshold invariant controlling stability in the paper's setting, formulated through valuations and log discrepancies. The paper uses it to describe the greatest twisted Ricci lower bound.

evidence.quote: express the greatest (twisted) Ricci lower bound in terms of a purely algebro-geometric stability threshold
The stability threshold and the greatest Ricci lower bound

nodes.label: Stable CMC surface
nodes.summary: A CMC surface whose stability operator has no negative directions for the relevant second variation. Stability appears in the exceptional product case described in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: S_H is totally geodesic, stable and has nullity 1 for its stability operator

nodes.label: Stable codimension-one stationary integral varifold
nodes.summary: An integral varifold of codimension one that is stationary and satisfies the stability condition on its regular part. This is the basic geometric object in the paper's structure theory.

evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Stable graph combinatorial type
nodes.summary: A graph encoding the combinatorial or topological type of a multicurve or square-tiled surface decomposition; the paper organizes volume contributions by stable graphs.

evidence.quote: Square-tiled surfaces, simple closed multicurves and stable graphs
Volume contribution of stable graphs with 2 vertices

nodes.label: Stable harmonic map
nodes.summary: A harmonic map is stable when its energy Morse index is zero; local stability means the same nonnegativity for variations supported in a neighborhood or domain.

evidence.quote: is said to be stable if
evidence.section: 2 Existence and partial regularity of min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Stable harmonic two-sphere
nodes.summary: A nonconstant stable harmonic map from the 2-sphere to a target manifold; in the paper this is equivalent to a stable branched minimal two-sphere in the target.

nodes.label: stable minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: A minimal hypersurface whose second variation of n-volume is nonnegative for admissible normal variations. The paper cuts along stable minimal hypersurfaces or along hypersurfaces with contracting neighborhoods.

nodes.label: Curvature estimate for stable minimal hypersurfaces in R^4
nodes.summary: A scale-invariant estimate bounding |A_M(p)| times the intrinsic distance from p to the boundary for two-sided stable minimal immersions M^3 -> R^4.

nodes.label: Stable minimal hypersurface flatness in R^4
nodes.summary: The rigidity property that a complete connected two-sided stable minimal immersion M^3 -> R^4 must be a flat affine R^3. This is the paper's central result.

nodes.label: Stable minimal immersion in Euclidean space
nodes.summary: A complete two-sided minimal hypersurface immersion M^n -> R^{n+1} satisfying the stability inequality int_M |A_M|^2 f^2 <= int_M |grad f|^2 for every compactly supported smooth function f.

nodes.label: Stable minimal immersion in a Riemannian manifold
nodes.summary: A two-sided minimal immersion M^3 -> (N^4,g) satisfying the stability inequality with potential |A_M|^2 + Ric_N(nu,nu) for all compactly supported test functions away from the boundary.

nodes.label: Stable mu-bubble
aliases: stable solution to the prescribed mean curvature problem
nodes.summary: A mu-bubble that is stable for the prescribed mean curvature variational problem.

evidence.quote: stable solutions to the prescribed mean curvature problem (called here μ-bubbles)

nodes.label: Stable regular Gaussian cluster
nodes.summary: A Gaussian cluster satisfying the regularity and stability hypotheses used in the paper's variational analysis. The excerpt names this concept but does not provide the full regularity or second-variation stability definition.

evidence.quote: stable regular clusters must have flat interfaces
a certain dichotomy on the structure of stable clusters

nodes.label: Stable self-expander
nodes.summary: A self-expander whose second variation of the expander functional is nonnegative under compact perturbations. Outermost expanders are stable because they minimize the expander functional from one side.

evidence.quote: We call a self-expander stable if the second variation of is non-negative under compact perturbations
the property that minimize from one side implies that both are stable expanders
evidence.section: 2.6. The level set flow of a cone and the outermost expanders

nodes.label: Stable and unstable cotangent annihilator bundles
nodes.summary: The closed conic subsets E_u^* and E_s^* of the cotangent bundle defined as annihilators of the corresponding unstable or stable directions together with the flow direction.

evidence.quote: annihilators of unstable/stable plus flow directions

nodes.label: Stable and unstable foliations of an Anosov flow
nodes.summary: The foliations tangent to the stable and unstable directions of an Anosov flow, encoding asymptotic contraction and expansion.

nodes.label: Stable 0-homogeneous harmonic map
nodes.summary: A stable harmonic map from Euclidean space to the target that is invariant under radial scaling. Nonexistence of such maps in a given dimension improves singular-set dimension bounds.

evidence.section: 2.3 Partial regularity for the min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Standard end classification for fast-decay gravitational instantons
aliases: folklore standard-end conjecture for gravitational instantons
nodes.summary: The classification principle that a connected complete hyperkahler four-manifold with faster than quadratic curvature decay is asymptotic to one of the four standard end families ALE, ALF, ALG, or ALH.

evidence.quote: any gravitational instanton must be asymptotic to one of the standard models of ends
it must be one of the four families: ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH

nodes.label: Standard Gaussian measure on Euclidean space
nodes.summary: The probability measure on R^n with density (2 pi)^(-n/2) exp(-|x|^2/2) with respect to Lebesgue measure, written as e^{-W(x)} dx.

evidence.quote: Let gamma = gamma^n denote the standard Gaussian probability measure on Euclidean space

nodes.label: Standard model end
nodes.summary: A model geometry for the asymptotic end of a gravitational instanton. In this paper the standard model families are ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH, with precise definitions referenced in Section 2.

evidence.quote: asymptotic to one of the standard models of ends
we will give a precise definition of these ends in §2

nodes.label: Standard Weyl remainder bound
nodes.summary: The classical sharp general remainder estimate $E(\lambda)=O(\lambda^{n-1})$ for compact Riemannian manifolds.

evidence.quote: proved $E(\lambda) = O(\lambda^{n-1})$ ; a result that is sharp on the round sphere and is thought of as the standard remainder

nodes.label: Stationary graph inclusion set K_FPsi
nodes.summary: The constraint set of matrices built from X, DF_Psi(X), and X^T DF_Psi(X)-F_Psi(X) id that encodes stationary graph differential inclusions.

evidence.quote: K_FPsi := { A ... X, DF_Psi(X), XT DF_Psi(X) - F_Psi(X) id }

nodes.label: Stationary harmonic map
nodes.summary: A W^{1,2} map is stationary harmonic when it is weakly harmonic and is also critical for the energy under inner variations of the domain, equivalently satisfying the stress-energy stationarity identity.

evidence.quote: known as a stationary harmonic map
evidence.section: 2 Existence and partial regularity of min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Stationary integral varifold
nodes.summary: A rectifiable varifold with integer multiplicity and vanishing first variation. In this paper it is the codimension-two generalized minimal submanifold obtained as the energy concentration limit.

evidence.quote: if a stationary integral  $n$ -varifold  $M$  in the unit ball

nodes.label: stationary varifold
nodes.summary: A varifold with zero first variation for all compactly supported smooth vector fields, with divergence computed using the chosen metric.

nodes.label: Stefan free boundary
nodes.summary: The moving interface bounding the positivity set in the Stefan or equivalent parabolic obstacle formulation.

nodes.label: Stefan free boundary condition
nodes.summary: The interface condition for the Stefan problem, requiring the time derivative of the temperature to equal the squared spatial gradient on the free boundary.

nodes.label: Singular point of the Stefan free boundary
nodes.summary: A free-boundary point for the Stefan obstacle formulation at which the contact set has zero density; these points form the singular set studied in the paper.

evidence.quote: singular points at which the contact set \{u = 0\} has zero density

nodes.label: Stefan singular set
nodes.summary: The space-time set of singular points of the Stefan free boundary. It decomposes into time slices \Sigma_t times their corresponding times.

evidence.quote: let \Sigma \subset \Omega \times (0,T) be the set of singular points

nodes.label: Singular times for the Stefan problem
nodes.summary: The subset of times at which the Stefan singular set is nonempty: times t for which some singular point (x,t) exists.

nodes.label: Steklov eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: On a smooth-boundary domain Omega, find harmonic functions u satisfying partial_nu u = sigma u on the boundary. Its eigenvalues are those of the Dirichlet-to-Neumann operator.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Steklov eigenvalue supremum Sigma_k
nodes.summary: The supremum of the normalized k-th Steklov eigenvalue over smooth domains in M and Riemannian metrics on their closures.

nodes.label: Steklov isoperimetric bound under nonnegative Ricci conformal class
nodes.summary: If a metric is conformal to one with nonnegative Ricci curvature, Steklov eigenvalues of domains satisfy a volume-boundary-k upper bound with exponent 2/d.

evidence.quote: sigma_k(Omega) <= C_d Vol(Omega)^{(d-2)/d} H(partial Omega)^{-1} k^{2/d}
evidence.section: 1.2.3 Transferring bounds for Steklov eigenvalues to bounds for Laplace eigenvalues

nodes.label: Steklov nodal-domain method under reflection symmetry
nodes.summary: A proof method combining reflection invariance of a first Steklov eigenfunction, the two-nodal-domain property, and the fundamental domain geometry to force sigma_1=1.

evidence.section: 7 First Steklov eigenvalue of free boundary minimal surfaces

nodes.label: Steklov-to-weighted-Laplace homogenisation
nodes.summary: For the weighted perforated domains Omega^epsilon, the Steklov eigenvalues converge to the weighted Laplace eigenvalues on M, and harmonic extensions of eigenfunctions converge weakly in H^1 to weighted Laplace eigenfunctions.

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Stereographic dilation sphere map
nodes.summary: The sphere self-map obtained by stereographic projection from $\xi$, dilation by a factor $\lambda$ in the tangent hyperplane, and inverse stereographic projection. It is the normal form used for Mobius transformations in the paper.

nodes.label: Stern rearrangement of the Bochner formula
nodes.summary: A rearranged Bochner formula that relates scalar curvature of a three-manifold to a harmonic function and its level sets, enabling Gauss-Bonnet input in the stability argument.

nodes.label: Stone-Cech dynamics at infinity
nodes.summary: For a geometric group action, ultrafilters in beta Gamma define boundary operators T^omega on the ideal/Tits boundary; these maps are 1-Lipschitz for the Tits metric.

nodes.label: Strichartz estimates for rough Lorentzian wave flows
nodes.summary: Dispersive spacetime estimates for the full, linearized, and paradifferential wave evolutions with rough Lorentzian coefficients. In this paper they control the L^p_t L^infty_x norms needed for the low-regularity theory, with small derivative losses.

evidence.quote: Strichartz estimates play a major role in our analysis. These are applied to several equations, namely the full evolution, the linear paradifferential evolution and finally the linearized equation
the Strichartz estimates ... have derivative losses, namely $1/8$ derivatives ... respectively $1/4$ derivatives

nodes.label: Strictly convex boundary
nodes.summary: A boundary convexity condition defined in the paper as positivity of the second fundamental form. It is an assumption in local boundary rigidity and in the definition of simple manifolds.

evidence.quote: strict convexity means a positive second fundamental form
at which  $\partial M$  is strictly convex

nodes.label: Function with strictly convex level sets
nodes.summary: A function on the manifold whose level hypersurfaces satisfy strict convexity relative to the metric. The existence of such a function is the geometric assumption under which the excerpt says the lens rigidity problem is solved.

evidence.quote: function on  $M$  with suitable convexity properties relative to  $g$
existence of a function with strictly convex level sets

nodes.label: Strictly convex planar billiard table
nodes.summary: A strictly convex planar domain regarded as a billiard table for the billiard map and billiard trajectories.

nodes.label: Strong Sard conjecture in dimension 3
nodes.summary: The assertion that for every starting point x in a 3-dimensional sub-Riemannian setting, the singular endpoint set has Hausdorff dimension at most 1. The paper proves this for analytic rank-two totally nonholonomic distributions.

nodes.label: sub-Riemannian Hamiltonian vector field
nodes.summary: The Hamiltonian vector field on T*M associated with the sub-Riemannian Hamiltonian defined from the metric on Delta and the canonical symplectic structure.

nodes.label: sub-Riemannian minimizing geodesic
nodes.summary: A horizontal path joining two points that minimizes the energy, and hence the length, among all horizontal paths with the same endpoints.

nodes.label: sub-Riemannian structure
nodes.summary: A pair consisting of a totally nonholonomic distribution Delta on a manifold and a smoothly varying inner product g on Delta.

nodes.label: Critical point of the subcritical Moser-Trudinger functional
nodes.summary: A positive critical point of $I_{p,\beta}$, equivalently a positive solution of the subcritical Euler-Lagrange equation. These critical points are produced by minmax methods and then analyzed as $p$ approaches the limiting regimes.

evidence.quote: apply the minmax method to obtain critical points of  $I_{p,\beta}$ , and then prove new compactness and quantization results for such critical points

nodes.label: Euler-Lagrange equation for $I_{p,\beta}$
nodes.summary: The PDE satisfied by positive critical points of the subcritical functional: $\Delta_{g_0}u+u=p\lambda f u^{p-1}e^{u^p}$ with $u>0$.

evidence.quote: its critical points are the solutions of  $\Delta_ {g _ {0}} u + u = p \lambda f u ^ {p - 1} e ^ {u ^ {p}}, \quad u > 0 \text {i n} \Sigma$

nodes.label: Subcritical Moser-Trudinger functional $I_{p,\beta}$
nodes.summary: For $p\in[1,2)$ and $\beta>0$, the functional $I_{p,\beta}$ combines a power of the $H^1$ norm with the logarithm of the weighted integral of $e^{u_+^p}-1$, and is set to $+\infty$ on nonpositive functions. It is introduced as a subcritical family interpolating toward the critical Moser-Trudinger problem.

evidence.quote: $I _ {p, \beta} (u) = \frac {2 - p}{2} \left(\frac {p \| u \| _ {H ^ {1}} ^ {2}}{2 \beta}\right) ^ {\frac {p}{2 - p}} - \ln \int_ {\Sigma} \left(e ^ {u _ {+} ^ {p}} - 1\right) f d v _ {g _ {0}}$

nodes.label: Subcritical-to-critical Moser-Trudinger limiting procedure
nodes.summary: The limiting method in which positive critical points of $I_{p,\beta}$ are taken as $p\uparrow2$ to obtain positive critical points of the constrained critical Moser-Trudinger functional.

evidence.quote: Letting  $p \uparrow 2$  we obtain positive critical points of the Moser-Trudinger functional
introduce a family of subcritical functional  $I_{p,\beta}$ ,  $p \in [1,2)$

nodes.label: Subcritical Trudinger exponential integrability
nodes.summary: For $p\in(1,2)$, Trudinger-type exponential integrability makes $I_{p,\beta}$ finite and $C^1$ on functions with nontrivial positive part.

evidence.quote: By Trudinger's result [52], for  $p \in (1,2)$ ,  $I_{p,\beta}$  is finite and of class  $C^1$

nodes.label: Subdivision algorithm for foliated corona decompositions
nodes.summary: The subdivision algorithm iteratively partitions graph regions into pseudoquad pieces in order to construct a foliated corona decomposition.

evidence.quote: 7. The subdivision algorithm: constructing a foliated corona decomposition 137

nodes.label: Subexponential growth of parabolic end groups
nodes.summary: The property that the fundamental group of an end, acting as a common-boundary-fixed parabolic isometry group, has subexponential growth or vanishing algebraic entropy.

evidence.quote: A key step in the proof ... is to show that this group has subexponential growth

nodes.label: Sub-level set of a convex solution
nodes.summary: A set cut out by an upper bound on the value of a convex solution, used as the geometric object to rescale in blow-up arguments. Such sets encode the local shape of the Monge-Ampère potential.

evidence.quote: a sequence of sub-level sets of the solution

nodes.label: Sublinear orbit displacement for common-boundary-fixed isometry groups
nodes.summary: For a finitely generated isometry group of a complete proper visibility CAT(0) space whose nontrivial elements have the same unique fixed point at infinity, the maximal displacement of elements of word norm at most $k$ is $o(k)$. This is the paper's central technical displacement principle, stated without using the paper-local proposition label as a concept node.

nodes.label: sublinear width bound for compact manifolds
nodes.summary: For compact manifolds, p-widths admit an upper bound of order p^(1/(n+1)), used in the paper as an ingredient in sweepout constructions and contrasted with cylindrical linear growth.

nodes.label: ABP method for submanifold Sobolev inequalities
nodes.summary: An ABP-inspired proof strategy on a Euclidean submanifold: solve an auxiliary divergence equation, combine the intrinsic Hessian with the second fundamental form over normal directions, and compare the resulting Jacobian bounds with Euclidean ball volumes.

nodes.label: Submanifold codimension
nodes.summary: For an n-dimensional submanifold in R^{n+m}, the codimension is m, equivalently the dimension of the normal space. The paper's sharp cases are codimension one and two.

nodes.label: Submanifold divergence
nodes.summary: The divergence operator on vector fields along Sigma, defined using the induced metric and volume form. The proof uses it to specify the auxiliary potential equation.

nodes.label: Submanifold Hessian
nodes.summary: The intrinsic covariant Hessian of a function on Sigma, viewed as a symmetric bilinear form with respect to the induced metric.

nodes.label: Submanifold Laplacian
nodes.summary: The intrinsic Laplacian on Sigma, equivalently the trace of the submanifold Hessian or the divergence of the intrinsic gradient.

nodes.label: Submanifold volume and boundary measure
nodes.summary: The Riemannian volume measure on Sigma and the induced hypersurface measure on its boundary. These measures appear in the Sobolev and isoperimetric inequalities.

nodes.label: Submetry at infinity from minimal symmetric sets
nodes.summary: The decomposition of the Tits boundary into minimal closed symmetric sets B_xi is equidistant and therefore induces a submetry from the Tits boundary to a metric quotient Delta.

evidence.quote: sets B_xi constitute an equidistant decomposition

nodes.label: Submetry base dimension control for rank rigidity
nodes.summary: The proof compares induced submetries on the periodic Morse-flat boundary and top-dimensional round spheres; in the irreducible case the base Delta is an n-dimensional spherical orbifold, forcing either the desired Tits-boundary dimension or reducibility.

evidence.quote: Delta is an n-dimensional spherical orbifold
evidence.section: 5.3 Induced submetries and the dimension of the base

nodes.label: Stratification of a submetry base
nodes.summary: The base of a submetry from a round sphere decomposes into strata according to the largest Euclidean factor split by the tangent space at a point.

nodes.label: Submetry between metric spaces
nodes.summary: A map between metric spaces that sends every closed ball around a point surjectively onto the closed ball of the same radius around its image.

nodes.label: Near points for a submetry
nodes.summary: Two points are delta-near for a submetry delta if their distance equals the distance between their images under delta.

nodes.label: Super Ricci flow
nodes.summary: A smooth family of Riemannian metrics (g_t) on a manifold satisfying partial_t g_t >= -2 Ric_g_t, equivalently partial_t g_t + 2 Ric_g_t is nonnegative definite. It is the smooth class whose pointed compactness is studied by passing to metric flows.

nodes.label: Supercritical dHYM phase range
nodes.summary: The phase interval θhat ∈ (nπ/2−π/4, nπ/2) in which the paper proves the uniform stability criterion for the dHYM equation.

evidence.quote: when the angle is in (nπ/2 − π/4, nπ/2)
Let θhat ∈ (nπ/2 − π/4, nπ/2) be a constant

nodes.label: Superrigidity for complex hyperbolic lattice representations
aliases: superrigidity theorem for complex hyperbolic lattices
nodes.summary: A rigidity property asserting strong constraints on certain representations of complex hyperbolic lattices. The excerpt identifies this as the paper's primary mechanism but does not state the full theorem.

evidence.quote: our primary result is a superrigidity theorem for certain representations of complex hyperbolic lattices

nodes.label: Euler characteristic of a surface
nodes.summary: The topological Euler characteristic chi(X) of a surface X. It indexes the non-compact surface sequence obtained from the random-cover construction.

nodes.label: Genus of a closed surface
nodes.summary: The genus g(X) of a closed surface X. The closed-surface asymptotic question concerns sequences with genus tending to infinity.

evidence.quote: closed hyperbolic surfaces with genera tending to infinity

nodes.label: Surface-preserving Killing field
nodes.summary: A Killing field associated with an invariant surface, whose flow preserves the surface. In the proof it is analyzed according to horizontal, elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic cases.

nodes.label: Steklov-Laplace supremum comparison on surfaces
nodes.summary: For every k and every smooth closed connected surface M, the optimal normalized Steklov value is at least the optimal normalized Laplace value. The proof applies the homogenisation limit with beta equal to 1.

nodes.label: Surgery limit to singular Ricci flow
aliases: limit of Ricci flows with surgery
nodes.summary: The convergence property that Ricci flows with surgery from fixed initial data and surgery scales tending to zero converge to the unique singular Ricci flow spacetime. It is a consequence of the stability theorem and prior existence theory.

evidence.quote: limit of Ricci flows with surgery

nodes.label: sweepout gluing for width upper bounds
nodes.summary: A method for combining p-sweepouts over compact submanifolds with disjoint interiors into a p-sweepout of their union, used to build upper bounds for widths.

nodes.label: Symmetric cone C_kl
nodes.summary: For x in R^{k+1} and y in R^{l+1}, C_kl is the cone in R^{k+l+2} defined by equality of the two Euclidean radii. These cones organize the asymptotics and foliations used in the construction.

nodes.label: Symmetric group
nodes.summary: The group S_n of all permutations of n elements; the paper uses S_{3g-3} for its non-uniform random permutation model.

nodes.label: Symmetric invariant subset from Morse-flat chambers
nodes.summary: In the non-flat case, the incenters of maximal incompressible subsets associated to the chamber structure form a nonempty proper closed Gamma-invariant symmetric subset of the ideal boundary.

evidence.quote: non-empty proper closed symmetric Gamma-invariant subset
evidence.section: 5.2 Periodic Morse flats and symmetric sets

nodes.label: Symmetric subset at infinity
nodes.summary: A subset of the ideal boundary that contains, with each point, all of its antipodes; the visual version uses visual antipodes.

evidence.quote: contains with each point all of its antipodes

nodes.label: Symmetric 2-tensor field
nodes.summary: A rank-two covariant tensor field, such as a metric perturbation, serving as the input to the tensor geodesic X-ray transform considered in the paper.

evidence.quote: geodesic X-ray transform on 2-tensors, corresponding to a metric  $g$

nodes.label: Tangent and normal spaces of a submanifold
nodes.summary: At a point x of a Euclidean submanifold, T_x Sigma is the tangent space and T_x^perp Sigma is the Euclidean orthogonal normal space.

nodes.label: Tangent cone at infinity
nodes.summary: A limiting metric cone obtained by rescaling the geometry at infinity. The paper contrasts it with the full end structure and has a section devoted to classifying it.

evidence.quote: different from the tangent cone at infinity
Classification of tangent cone at infinity

nodes.label: Tangent cone at a polyhedral vertex
aliases: tangent cone of M at the vertex
nodes.summary: The cone obtained by looking infinitesimally at a polyhedral manifold near a vertex; its faces are denoted F_{j,infty} in the correction.

evidence.quote: Consider the tangent cone of M at the vertex p , and the side faces F_{j,\infty}
T_pM can be placed strictly within the tangent cone of P at its vertex

nodes.label: Tangent cone at a Ricci limit point
nodes.summary: A pointed Gromov-Hausdorff limit of rescalings of a limit metric space around a point. Tangent cones encode the first-order singular behavior of the collapsed limit.

nodes.label: Tangent cone of a current or varifold
nodes.summary: A blow-up limit of a current or varifold at a point, used to model infinitesimal structure. The paper studies planar tangent cones and proves uniqueness in the critical mod-$p$ multiplicity case.

evidence.quote: if  $T$  has one tangent cone at an interior point  $y$
whether the tangent cone at  $y$  must be unique
evidence.section: Part 1. Introduction, the main theorem, and notation

nodes.label: Tangent cone of a minimal surface
nodes.summary: A dilation-invariant blow-up limit of a minimal surface at a point, obtained from rescalings around that point.

evidence.quote: then  $\tilde{M}$  is dilation invariant, and any such  $\tilde{M}$  arising in this fashion is called a tangent cone

nodes.label: Tangent flow
nodes.summary: A subsequential Brakke-flow limit obtained by parabolically rescaling a flow about a fixed space-time point at scales tending to zero. Huisken monotonicity implies such tangent flows are backwardly selfsimilar.

evidence.quote: called a tangent flow at X

nodes.label: Tangent flow at infinity
nodes.summary: For an ancient flow, a subsequential limit obtained by parabolically dilating by factors tending to infinity. The paper uses it to analyze coarse asymptotics of ancient low-entropy flows.

evidence.quote: called a tangent flow at infinity

nodes.label: Tangent flow of mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: A subsequential weak limit obtained by parabolically rescaling a mean curvature flow about a spacetime singular point. Such limits are self-similar and, when smooth at time -1, are generated by self-shrinkers.

nodes.label: Tangent point of a planar set
nodes.summary: A point of a planar set or curve is a tangent point when the set has a well-defined tangent line at that point in the measure-theoretic/geometric sense specified for the paper. The excerpt relies on this notion for the characterization of points with finite square function.

evidence.quote: See Section 2.6 below for the precise definition of a tangent.
at H^1-almost every point of G there exists a tangent to Γ

nodes.label: Tangent projection on the sphere
nodes.summary: The tangential derivative of the identity sphere embedding, used as the reference differential for the standard inclusion. Rigidity estimates compare $\nabla_T u$ to $OP_T$ or $\nabla_T\phi$.

nodes.label: Tangent-space characterization of metric rectifiability
nodes.summary: The paper's central criterion characterizes rectifiable subsets of complete metric spaces using local Gromov-Hausdorff approximation by n-dimensional Banach or biLip(K) tangent models, under finite Hausdorff measure and positive lower density hypotheses. The excerpt gives the governing idea but omits some formal quantifiers.

evidence.quote: We characterise rectifiable subsets of a complete metric space  $X$  in terms of local approximation, with respect to the Gromov-Hausdorff distance, by an  $n$ -dimensional Banach space.
if  $E \subset X$  with  $\mathcal{H}^n (E) < \infty$  and has positive lower density almost everywhere

nodes.label: Tangential C^{1,1-epsilon} estimate
nodes.summary: A boundary regularity estimate controlling tangential behavior of the convex potential slightly below C^{1,1}. The excerpt identifies it as a needed ingredient for extending the uniform obliqueness method under weaker boundary assumptions.

nodes.label: Tangential divergence on the sphere
nodes.summary: The divergence operator intrinsic to the unit sphere, applied componentwise to vector fields along the sphere. It appears in the linearized signed-volume operator and in the quadratic forms.

nodes.label: Tangential gradient on the sphere
nodes.summary: The derivative of a sphere map restricted to tangent directions on $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$, represented locally by derivatives against an oriented tangent frame. It is the basic first-order object in all deficits and estimates in the paper.

nodes.label: Compatibility of an algebraic target with a semisimple Lie group
nodes.summary: A compatibility condition, introduced in prior work, between an algebraic group over a local field and a semisimple Lie group. The excerpt names the notion but does not give its formal definition.

evidence.quote: the notion of compatibility of an algebraic group H over a local field k with a semisimple Lie group G

nodes.label: Target without stable harmonic two-spheres
nodes.summary: A target manifold has this property when every stable harmonic map S^2 to the target is constant. This is the key compactness and regularity hypothesis for the paper's min-max construction.

nodes.label: Taub-NUT metric
nodes.summary: A complete four-dimensional hyperkähler Ricci-flat metric with ALF-type asymptotics. The excerpt states that Taub-NUT metrics occur as bubbles in the collapsing K3 families.

nodes.label: Temporal derivatives of Kantorovich potentials
nodes.summary: Time derivatives of intermediate-time Kantorovich potentials along transport interpolations, including the second-order temporal derivative appearing in the density change-of-variables formula.

evidence.quote: depending on a second-order temporal derivative of associated Kantorovich potentials

nodes.label: Tensor product of metric-measure spaces
nodes.summary: The product metric-measure space formed from component metric-measure spaces, written as a tensor product in the excerpt. The paper concerns when curvature-dimension conditions pass from the factors to this product.

evidence.quote: then so does their tensor product (M,d,m)

nodes.label: Test configuration
nodes.summary: An algebraic degeneration of a polarized variety used to test stability. The non-Archimedean Ding functional is defined on such degenerations.

nodes.label: Theta-log discrepancy
aliases: log discrepancy with respect to theta
nodes.summary: The log discrepancy A_theta(v) of a divisorial valuation v measured with respect to the klt pair (X,theta). It is a valuation-theoretic ingredient of the non-Archimedean Ding functional.

evidence.quote: the log discrepancy  $A_{\theta}(v)$  of divisorial valuations  $v$  on  $X$  with respect to the 'klt pair'  $(X, \theta)$

nodes.label: Theta-twisted Kähler-Einstein current
nodes.summary: A positive (1,1)-current omega in c1(L), of finite energy, satisfying Ric(omega)=lambda omega+theta for a closed quasi-positive twist theta. The cohomology relation c1(X,theta)=lambda c1(L) determines lambda.

evidence.quote: A  $θ$ -twisted Kähler-Einstein current is a positive  $(1,1)$ -current  $\omega \in c_1(L)$ , of finite energy

nodes.label: Thick and thin subsets by curvature scale
nodes.summary: A subset is r-thick when its curvature scale is larger than r and r-thin when its curvature scale is smaller than r, with weak variants using non-strict inequalities. This language controls which points belong to comparison domains.

nodes.label: Third-order temporal theory of Kantorovich potentials
nodes.summary: A theory controlling third-order temporal behavior of intermediate-time Kantorovich potentials on proper geodesic spaces. The paper combines this information with the change-of-variables formula.

evidence.quote: a surprising third-order theory for the latter Kantorovich potentials
Combining change-of-variables formula with Kantorovich 3rd order information

nodes.label: Three-sphere
nodes.summary: The compact three-dimensional sphere serving as the underlying manifold for the paper's compact classification problem.

nodes.label: Three-torus
nodes.summary: The compact abelian nilmanifold R^3/Z^3. It is one of the possible regular fibers of the collapse map.

nodes.label: Tian-Yau construction
aliases: Tian-Yau construction of complete Calabi-Yau metrics
nodes.summary: A method for constructing complete Calabi-Yau metrics on complements of anticanonical divisors by building an approximately Ricci-flat Kähler background and solving a noncompact complex Monge-Ampère equation. The paper adapts it to build a Calabi-Yau metric on the intermediate cone.

evidence.quote: the Tian-Yau construction ... produces complete Calabi-Yau metrics out of lower dimensional compact Kähler-Einstein metrics
we will use the unknown Calabi-Yau metric omega on X as a model at infinity to construct a Calabi-Yau metric omega_W on W

nodes.label: Tian-Yau metric
nodes.summary: A complete noncompact Ricci-flat Kähler metric of Tian-Yau type. The excerpt states that such metrics appear as bubble limits and that their asymptotic geometry is analyzed.

evidence.quote: Tian-Yau and Taub-NUT metrics occurring as bubbles

nodes.label: Time projection
nodes.summary: The projection of a space-time set onto the time axis, used to convert information about the singular set into information about singular times.

nodes.label: Time-slice singular set
nodes.summary: For a fixed time t, \Sigma_t is the set of spatial singular points at that time, so that the full singular set is the union of \Sigma_t x {t}.

evidence.quote: let \Sigma_t denote the singular points at time t

nodes.label: Survival under the time vector field
aliases: survival in a Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A point or subset in a time slice survives until another time when the maximal trajectory of the spacetime time vector field is defined up to that time. The paper uses this flow to push limiting metrics back to the initial slice.

nodes.label: Timelike graph constraint
nodes.summary: The pointwise inequality u_t^2 < 1+|nabla_x u|^2 ensuring that the graph hypersurface is timelike and that the induced metric is Lorentzian.

evidence.quote: $$u_{t}^{2}<1+|\nabla_{x}u|^{2},$$ ... expressing the condition that its graph is a time-like surface
evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Timelike hypersurface graph in Minkowski space
nodes.summary: A codimension-one hypersurface represented as the graph of a scalar function u over an (n+1)-dimensional Minkowski spacetime. In this paper it has the form x_{n+1}=u(t,x_1,...,x_n).

evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Timelike minimal surface equation in Minkowski space
aliases: minimal surface equation for timelike surfaces
nodes.summary: The Euler-Lagrange equation for timelike graph area in Minkowski space, equivalently Box_g u=0 for the induced Lorentzian metric. Under the timelike constraint it is a quasilinear wave equation and its left side is the hypersurface mean curvature.

evidence.quote: Interpreting this as a Lagrangian, the minimal surface equation can be thought of as the associated Euler-Lagrange equation
evidence.section: 1.1 The Minimal Surface Equation in Minkowski Space

nodes.label: Tip region of a rotationally symmetric ancient solution
nodes.summary: A high-curvature cap region in the rotationally symmetric compact ancient solution analysis, separated from the cylindrical neck and used in localized energy estimates.

nodes.label: Tip region weights mu plus and mu minus
nodes.summary: Weights associated with the two tip regions in the rotationally symmetric comparison argument; they are used to localize or normalize tip-region estimates.

nodes.label: Tits boundary
nodes.summary: The ideal boundary of a CAT(0) space equipped with the Tits metric, a CAT(1) metric encoding asymptotic directions.

evidence.quote: ideal boundary equipped with the Tits metric

nodes.label: Top-dimensional singular stratum
nodes.summary: The codimension-one part of the singular stratification, S^{N-1} \ S^{N-2}. In this paper it may be nonempty and is the locus whose closure defines the boundary.

evidence.quote: the top dimensional singular stratum S^{N-1}\setminus S^{N-2} is not empty in general

nodes.label: Topological rigidity of Euclidean nets in Riemannian manifolds
nodes.summary: If a net in R^n embeds isometrically into a complete connected n-dimensional Riemannian manifold, then the manifold is diffeomorphic to R^n. This is the paper's all-dimensional rigidity statement.

evidence.quote: Suppose that there exists a net in \mathbb{R}^n that embeds isometrically in M. Then M is diffeomorphic to \mathbb{R}^n.

nodes.label: Toroidal compactification of a ball quotient
nodes.summary: A projective compactification of a ball quotient X^circ by adding a boundary divisor D, with boundary components described in the excerpt as abelian varieties. It supplies the geometric setup for the negative case.

evidence.quote: D := X \setminus X^{\circ} is a disjoint union of abelian varieties

nodes.label: Torus connected-sum manifold
aliases: connect sum of a torus with a manifold
nodes.summary: A manifold obtained as the connected sum of the n-torus with an arbitrary n-manifold.

evidence.quote: the connected sum of a n-torus with an arbitrary manifold

evidence.section: 1.2. The Geroch conjecture with arbitrary ends

nodes.label: Torus connected-sum positive-scalar-curvature rigidity
nodes.summary: For n at most 7, the connected sum of the n-torus with any n-manifold admits no complete positive scalar curvature metric, and any complete nonnegative scalar curvature metric is flat.

evidence.quote: For any n-manifold X, the connect sum T^n # X does not admit
evidence.section: 1.2. The Geroch conjecture with arbitrary ends

evidence.quote: The only complete metric of non-negative scalar curvature on T^n # X is flat
evidence.section: 1.2. The Geroch conjecture with arbitrary ends

nodes.label: Torus positive-scalar-curvature obstruction
nodes.summary: The n-torus admits no Riemannian metric of positive scalar curvature.

evidence.quote: The n-torus does not admit a Riemannian metric of positive scalar curvature

nodes.label: Torus-reduced analytic J functional
nodes.summary: For a K-invariant finite-energy potential, the infimum of the ordinary J functional over pullbacks by elements of the central torus T.

evidence.quote: J_T(phi) := inf_{sigma in T} J_psi(sigma* phi)
evidence.section: 2.3 Analytic criterion for the existence under group actions

nodes.label: Torus twist of a T-invariant valuation
nodes.summary: The action of the cocharacter space N_R on T-invariant valuations, shifting the value on a T-weight vector by the pairing of its weight with xi.

nodes.label: Torus twist of a test configuration
nodes.summary: Given a test configuration generated by a holomorphic vector field eta and a vector xi in N_R, the xi-twist replaces eta by eta+xi; integral xi give ordinary test configurations and real xi give R-test configurations.

evidence.quote: the xi-twist ... is the data (Z,L, eta+xi)

nodes.label: Total boundary defining function
nodes.summary: The product of the chosen boundary defining functions for all boundary hypersurfaces of a manifold with fibered corners. It controls the QFB vector-field condition and the conformal passage from QFB to QFC metrics.

nodes.label: Total Gaussian perimeter of a cluster
nodes.summary: The total Gaussian perimeter of a q-cluster is one half of the sum of the Gaussian-weighted perimeters of all its cells.

evidence.quote: The total Gaussian perimeter of a cluster Omega is defined as

nodes.label: Totally geodesic boundary face
nodes.summary: A boundary face is totally geodesic with respect to the ambient metric when its second fundamental form vanishes.

evidence.quote: the second fundamental form of the boundary faces of  $\Omega$  with respect to  $g$  vanishes

nodes.label: totally nonholonomic distribution
nodes.summary: A smooth or analytic subbundle Delta of TM whose locally generating vector fields satisfy the Hörmander bracket-generating condition, so their Lie algebra spans every tangent space.

nodes.label: Translating bowl soliton mean curvature flow
nodes.summary: The rotationally symmetric translating soliton model for mean curvature flow in R3. It appears as a noncompact ancient low-entropy model and as a possible limit flow near cylindrical singularities.

evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Transnormal submetry
nodes.summary: A submetry from a round sphere is transnormal if every extension of a horizontal geodesic is piecewise horizontal.

nodes.label: Transport ray
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional geodesic component along which L1 optimal transport moves mass. Transport rays support the localization and conditional-measure constructions used for CD^1.

nodes.label: Transposition method for rough wave data
aliases: duality method for rough boundary data
nodes.summary: A functional-analytic method defining solutions with negative Sobolev regularity by duality against smoother adjoint wave solutions. It lets the paper use distributional boundary controls and point-supported target data.

nodes.label: Tripod Gaussian cluster
nodes.summary: The q=3 simplicial Gaussian cluster whose interfaces are three half-hyperplanes meeting along an (n-2)-dimensional plane at 120 degree angles, forming a tripod or Y shape in two-dimensional cross-section.

evidence.quote: the case q = 3 confirms the Gaussian Double-Bubble Conjecture
a tripod-cluster, whose interfaces consist of three half-hyperplanes meeting along an (n - 2)-dimensional plane at 120 degrees angles

nodes.label: Trivial Young measure
nodes.summary: A Young measure concentrated at the weak limit value almost everywhere, equivalent in the paper's setup to convergence in measure and hence strong convergence below the generating exponent.

nodes.label: Trudinger-type energy estimate for Kähler potentials
nodes.summary: An exponential integrability estimate for a normalized plurisubharmonic potential scaled by its Monge-Ampère $p$-energy. The paper obtains this estimate by the same auxiliary Monge-Ampère comparison argument.

evidence.quote: it also gives estimates of Trudinger type

nodes.label: Twisted cscK metric equation
aliases: twisted constant scalar curvature Kähler equation
nodes.summary: A scalar-curvature equation in which R_phi - Rbar is balanced by trace terms from a twisting form, for example R_phi - Rbar = tr_phi beta - beta_bar. It is the Euler-Lagrange equation for twisted K-energy.

nodes.label: Twisted cscK solvability threshold
nodes.summary: The supremum of parameters t0 such that the twisted cscK continuity equation is solvable for all t up to t0. The paper shows this quantity depends only on the cohomology class of the twisting Kähler form.

nodes.label: Twisted Ding-semistability
