nodes.summary: An algebro-geometric semistability condition for (X,L) relative to theta, phrased using the non-Archimedean Ding functional on test configurations. In the paper, existence of a twisted Kähler-Einstein current implies this semistability.

evidence.quote: The notion of Ding-stability used here is phrased in terms of the non-Archimedean Ding functional

nodes.label: Twisted first Chern class
nodes.summary: The cohomology class c1(X,theta)=c1(X)-[theta]. The constant lambda in the twisted Kähler-Einstein equation is fixed by c1(X,theta)=lambda c1(L).

nodes.label: Twisted K-energy
nodes.summary: A modification of the Mabuchi K-energy by adding a J-functional term, such as K_beta = K + J_beta or K_chi,t = tK + (1-t)J_chi. Its critical points solve twisted cscK equations.

nodes.label: Twisting form
nodes.summary: A smooth closed nonnegative (1,1)-form beta, or a fixed Kähler form chi, inserted into the scalar-curvature equation through trace terms. It supplies the twist in twisted K-energy and twisted cscK equations.

nodes.label: Twistor correspondence for harmonic two-spheres
nodes.summary: Horizontal holomorphic maps S^2 -> Z_m project under the twistor projection to harmonic maps S^2 -> S^{2m}, and every harmonic two-sphere in S^{2m} arises in this way. The correspondence turns harmonic-map questions into holomorphic-twistor geometry.

nodes.label: Twistor field along a twistor lift
nodes.summary: A holomorphic section l of the pulled-back holomorphic tangent bundle of Z_m along a twistor lift, equivalently a section of hom_s(L_{<0},L_{>0}), satisfying a vertical derivative condition involving its adjoint and the map partial_{z,-1}. It is the first-order twistor analogue of a Jacobi field.

nodes.label: Twistor lift of a harmonic two-sphere
nodes.summary: For a linearly full harmonic map Phi:S^2 -> S^{2m} with harmonic sequence {L_i}, the map z -> L_{<0}(z)=oplus_{i=-m}^{-1} L_i is the unique horizontal holomorphic map whose twistor projection is +/-Phi.

nodes.label: Twistor map to the O(4) bundle
aliases: map z to the O(4) bundle
nodes.summary: A map from the twistor space of the ALF-D_k instanton to the total space of the O(4) bundle over CP1. The excerpt states that this map commutes with the real structure.

evidence.quote: map from the twistor space of  $M$  to the total space
the map  $z$  commutes with the real structure

nodes.label: Twistor space of an ALF-D_k instanton
nodes.summary: The twistor space associated with an ALF-D_k gravitational instanton. It is used to define a map to the total space of the O(4) bundle over CP1.

evidence.quote: map from the twistor space of  $M$

nodes.label: Twistor space of an even-dimensional sphere
nodes.summary: The twistor space Z_m of S^{2m} is the complex manifold of m-dimensional isotropic subspaces of C^{2m+1}. It comes with a twistor projection to S^{2m} and a vertical-horizontal splitting of its holomorphic tangent bundle.

nodes.label: Twistor space of a hyperkahler manifold
nodes.summary: The complex manifold encoding the sphere of complex structures associated with a hyperkahler manifold. The excerpt refers to maps from the twistor space of M.

evidence.quote: map from the twistor space of  $M$

nodes.label: Two-dimensional Riemannian embedding obstruction for planar-net Euclidean sets
nodes.summary: A nonplanar discrete Euclidean set whose slice in an affine plane is a net cannot be isometrically embedded in any complete two-dimensional Riemannian manifold. The obstruction follows from planar net rigidity.

evidence.quote: Then X does not embed isometrically in any 2-dimensional, complete Riemannian manifold.

nodes.label: Two nodal domains for first Steklov eigenfunctions
nodes.summary: A first nontrivial Steklov eigenfunction has exactly two nodal domains; this is used with reflection invariance to rule out sigma_1 less than 1 for the symmetric surfaces.

evidence.quote: u has exactly two nodal domains
evidence.section: 7 First Steklov eigenvalue of free boundary minimal surfaces

nodes.label: Two-phase Bernoulli free boundary
nodes.summary: For a local minimizer of the two-phase Bernoulli functional, the free boundary is the union of the boundaries of the positive and negative phase sets inside the ambient domain.

evidence.quote: the regularity of the free boundary  $\partial \Omega_u^+ \cup \partial \Omega_u^- \cap D$

nodes.label: Two-phase Bernoulli functional
nodes.summary: The variational energy J_tp(u,D) is the Dirichlet energy of u on D plus weighted Lebesgue measures of the positive and negative phases {u>0} and {u<0}, with fixed positive weights lambda_+ and lambda_-.

evidence.quote: J _ {t p} (u, D) := \int_ {D} | \nabla u | ^ {2} d x + \lambda_ {+} ^ {2} | \Omega_ {u} ^ {+} \cap D | + \lambda_ {-} ^ {2} | \Omega_ {u} ^ {-} \cap D |

nodes.label: Two-phase point set
nodes.summary: The two-phase point set consists of points in the ambient domain lying simultaneously on the boundaries of the positive and negative phases: Gamma_tp = partial Omega_u^+ cap partial Omega_u^- cap D.

nodes.label: Two-point geodesic incidence small-measure condition
aliases: small measure of geodesics passing near two points
nodes.summary: A dynamical smallness condition requiring that the family of geodesics passing near both points $x$ and $y$ has small measure.

evidence.quote: under the assumption that the set of geodesics that pass near both $x$ and $y$ has small measure

nodes.label: Two-rectifiable Radon measure
nodes.summary: A Radon measure in Euclidean three-space that has a two-dimensional tangent plane with positive multiplicity almost everywhere. It is the measure-theoretic replacement for a possibly singular surface.

evidence.quote: two-dimensional tangent plane of positive multiplicity
evidence.section: 1.1 Classification of ancient low entropy flows

nodes.label: Two-sided corkscrew open set
nodes.summary: A two-sided corkscrew open set is a planar open set whose interior and exterior satisfy a scale-invariant corkscrew condition at boundary balls. The paper uses this as a topological/geometric hypothesis under which the ε² square-function theorem still holds without connectedness.

evidence.quote: two-sided corkscrew open sets, which have a scale invariant topological assumption but are not necessarily connected, see Section 2.5 below for the definition
Ω^+ is a 2-sided corkscrew open set

nodes.label: Two-sided hypersurface
nodes.summary: A hypersurface whose normal bundle is orientable, so that a global unit normal can be chosen. This is needed to write the scalar stability inequality with a normal vector nu.

nodes.label: Two-sided Ricci curvature bound
nodes.summary: A uniform absolute bound on Ricci curvature for the approximating manifolds, stronger than a lower Ricci bound.

evidence.quote: under the bounded Ricci and noncollapsing assumptions $|\operatorname{Ric}_{M^n}| \leq n-1$

nodes.label: Type I Ricci flow singularity
nodes.summary: A finite-time Ricci flow singularity with curvature blow-up at the Type I rate; Perelman's compact ancient solution has this behavior forward in time.

evidence.quote: Going forward in time, the solution forms a Type I singularity

nodes.label: Type II backward ancient solution
nodes.summary: An ancient Ricci flow behavior in which (-t)R_max(t) is unbounded as t tends to minus infinity.

nodes.label: Unbounded normalized Steklov eigenvalue with connected boundary
nodes.summary: In every fixed Riemannian manifold of dimension at least three, varying the weight and then connecting boundary components yields domains with connected boundary whose first normalized Steklov eigenvalue tends to infinity.

evidence.section: 1.2.2 Isoperimetric inequalities in dimension at least three

nodes.label: Uniform analytic bounds on perforated domains
nodes.summary: The proof requires epsilon-uniform control of trace operators, harmonic extension operators, and Poincare-Wirtinger inequalities on perforated Voronoi cells despite degenerating boundary geometry.

evidence.quote: trace and extension operators are well behaved

nodes.label: Uniform complex Monge-Ampère solution on an asymptotically conical resolution
nodes.summary: The analytic procedure of solving the noncompact complex Monge-Ampère equation on each crepant resolution with estimates uniform enough to pass to limits.

evidence.quote: we solve the complex Monge-Ampère equation with respect to these background metrics and derive uniform estimates
derive uniform estimates as epsilon tends to 0, which allows us to take a limit
evidence.section: 4. Uniform estimates for the complex Monge-Ampère equation

nodes.label: Uniform density estimate for Monge-Ampère sections
nodes.summary: A quantitative estimate asserting that boundary sections or normalized sub-level sets retain a controlled amount of the relevant domain. The excerpt names it as an estimate needed for relaxing boundary smoothness and for the obliqueness method.

evidence.quote: if one can manage this relaxation for the uniform density estimate

nodes.label: Uniform dHYM stability via subvariety phases
nodes.summary: A uniform stability condition for dHYM: for every p-dimensional subvariety V, θ_V(t) stays in a phase interval bounded below by θhat−(n−p)π/2 plus a uniform positive margin, with θ_M(1)=θhat.

evidence.quote: there exists a constant ε1.1 > 0 and for all p-dimensional subvarieties V ... there exist smooth functions θV(t)

nodes.label: Uniform eigenvalue-lattice counting bound
nodes.summary: A non-asymptotic bound comparing the relevant eigenvalue counting functions with corresponding lattice counting functions uniformly in the spectral parameter.

evidence.quote: the corresponding eigenvalue and lattice counting functions are related not only asymptotically, but in fact satisfy certain uniform bounds

nodes.label: Uniform equivalence for compatible Calabi-Yau metrics
aliases: uniform equivalence conjecture for compatible metrics
nodes.summary: The expected property that all compatible Calabi-Yau metrics inducing a fixed valuation are mutually uniformly equivalent as Riemannian metrics.

evidence.quote: There exists a constant C such that for all g in M_v, we have C^{-1}g_0 <= g <= Cg_0.

nodes.label: Uniform J-stability
nodes.summary: The triple (M,[ω0],[χ]) is uniformly J-stable if there is ε>0 such that every Kähler test configuration satisfies J_[χ](X,Ω) ≥ ε J_[ω0](X,Ω).

evidence.quote: (M, [ω0], [χ]) is uniformly J-stable ... J[χ](X, Ω) ≥ ε1.1 J[ω0](X, Ω)

nodes.label: Uniform measure in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: A uniform measure in H^n is a Radon measure whose measure of metric balls centered on its support is exactly a fixed constant times a power of the radius. The paper applies its flat-tangent analysis to the classification of uniform measures in H^1.

evidence.quote: conclude the classification of uniform measures in $\mathbb{H}^1$
the assumption of local uniformity is far stronger than the mere existence of the density

nodes.label: uniform monotonicity formula for stationary varifolds
nodes.summary: A monotonicity estimate that holds uniformly for stationary varifolds when the ambient metrics are close to Euclidean in C1 norm. It supplies density control in the varying-metric compactness argument.

nodes.label: Uniform obliqueness for the Monge-Ampère second boundary condition
nodes.summary: A boundary estimate giving a positive lower bound for the obliqueness of the gradient-image boundary condition, equivalently controlling the angle between source and target boundary normals under Du. It is a key boundary mechanism in regularity proofs for the second boundary value problem.

evidence.quote: then our method for the uniform obliqueness can be applied

nodes.label: Uniform open-set mass lower bound for Laplace eigenfunctions
nodes.summary: On an Anosov surface, every normalized Laplace-Beltrami eigenfunction has L^2 mass bounded below on any fixed nonempty open subset, uniformly in the eigenvalue.

nodes.label: Uniform random degree n Riemannian cover model
nodes.summary: The probability model assigning the uniform measure to the finite set of degree n Riemannian covering spaces of a fixed base surface. It is the random surface model used in the main result.

evidence.quote: a finite set that we equip with the uniform probability measure
The random model we use is the random covering model

nodes.label: Uniform rectifiability
nodes.summary: Uniform rectifiability is a quantitative strengthening of rectifiability for sets or measures, typically requiring big pieces of Lipschitz images or equivalent multiscale criteria. It appears in the excerpt as background for David-Semmes theory and the BAUP criterion.

evidence.quote: the so-called BAUP criterion for uniform rectifiability found by David and Semmes

nodes.label: Uniform scalar atomic condition
nodes.summary: A uniform quadratic strengthening of SAC: the pairing of B_Psi(T) with B_{Psi*}(S^perp) is bounded below by C||T-S||^2 for all distinct m-planes.

nodes.label: Uniform Schauder estimate for cone metrics
nodes.summary: A uniform elliptic regularity estimate adapted to conical background metrics. It is part of the analytic machinery used for conical metric problems in the paper.

nodes.label: Uniform slope J-stability
nodes.summary: A slope version of uniform J-stability in which the test configurations are degenerations to the normal cone of subvarieties. It is equivalently expressed by a uniform intersection inequality over all subvarieties in this paper.

evidence.quote: for any subvariety V of M, the degeneration to normal cone (X, Ω) ... satisfies J[χ](X, Ω) ≥ ε1.1 J[ω0](X, Ω)

nodes.label: Uniform twisted Ding-stability
aliases: uniform Ding-stability with respect to theta
nodes.summary: A strengthened form of twisted Ding-stability requiring a uniform positive lower bound for the non-Archimedean Ding functional relative to a norm or energy. The paper proves it implies existence of a twisted Kähler-Einstein current, and uniqueness implies it in the stated direction.

evidence.quote: a unique  $\theta$ -twisted Kähler-Einstein current), then  $(X, L)$  is Ding-semistable (resp. uniformly Ding-stable)
if  $(X,L)$  is uniformly Ding-stable with respect to  $\theta$

nodes.label: Uniformly convex domain
nodes.summary: A convex domain satisfying a quantitative strict convexity condition, often expressed by positive curvature of the boundary in smooth settings. Earlier boundary regularity results for this problem assumed uniform convexity, while this paper removes that assumption.

evidence.quote: The uniform convexity of domains is a natural condition for the regularity of solutions
the uniform convexity of domains can be dropped

nodes.label: Uniformly convex sublevel set
nodes.summary: The sublevel body of an integrand is convex with a uniform lower curvature bound. This is the convexity side of ellipticity for the paper's parametric integrands.

nodes.label: Uniformly elliptic integrand
nodes.summary: A one-homogeneous function on Euclidean space that is positive and smooth on the unit sphere and whose unit sublevel set is uniformly convex. Such an integrand defines the anisotropic surface energy used throughout the paper.

nodes.label: Unimodular Lie group
nodes.summary: A connected Lie group whose left Haar measure is right-invariant, equivalently tr(ad X)=0 for every Lie algebra element X.

evidence.quote: left Haar measure is also right-invariant

nodes.label: Unique continuation in the exterior of the double null cone
nodes.summary: If a distributional solution of (□+V)u=0 has both Dirichlet and Neumann traces vanishing on Σ∩E_p, then u vanishes throughout E_p. This is the key analytic result enabling the boundary controllability method without real analyticity.

nodes.label: Unique flat asymptotic cone of a gravitational instanton
nodes.summary: The property that a nonflat gravitational instanton has a unique asymptotic cone and that this cone is flat of dimension one, two, three, or four. This property is the organizing input for the model-end classification.

nodes.label: Unique minimizing geodesic between points
nodes.summary: The property that any two points in the manifold are connected by exactly one geodesic realizing the distance between them. Together with strict boundary convexity it defines simplicity in the excerpt.

evidence.quote: any two points in  $M$  can be joined by a unique minimizing geodesic

nodes.label: Uniqueness of mean curvature flow through embedded two-sphere singularities
nodes.summary: The uniqueness or well-posedness of mean curvature flow of embedded two-spheres through singularities, obtained in the paper assuming the multiplicity-one conjecture.

nodes.label: Uniqueness of singular Ricci flows
aliases: uniqueness of complete Ricci flow spacetimes
nodes.summary: The property that complete Ricci flow spacetimes satisfying the canonical neighborhood assumption are determined up to spacetime isometry by their initial time-slices. This is the paper's uniqueness mechanism for weak Ricci flow through singularities.

evidence.quote: uniquely determined by their initial data
any isometry ... extends to an isometry

nodes.label: Unit-diameter hyperkähler K3 moduli space
aliases: moduli of unit-diameter hyperkähler metrics on K3
nodes.summary: The set of equivalence classes of unit-diameter hyperkähler metrics on the K3 manifold modulo diffeomorphisms, endowed with the Gromov-Hausdorff topology. Its compactification is one of the paper's main applications.

nodes.label: Unit interval metric space
nodes.summary: The one-dimensional compact interval appearing as a possible collapsed Gromov-Hausdorff limit of unit-diameter hyperkähler K3 metrics. In this case the renormalized measure may be nontrivial and encodes topological information of the family.

nodes.label: Unit-regular Brakke flow
nodes.summary: A Brakke flow in which every space-time point of Gaussian density one is regular in a parabolic neighborhood. The condition prevents certain artificial vanishings in weak flow limits.

evidence.quote: Gaussian density one is a regular point

nodes.label: Unit-weight multicurve
nodes.summary: A property of a weighted multicurve in which every multiplicity m_i equals 1.

evidence.quote: all its weights  $m_i$  are equal to 1 with asymptotic probability  $\sqrt{2}/2$

nodes.label: Universal cover
nodes.summary: A simply connected covering space of a connected manifold or topological space.

nodes.label: Universal Teichmüller space
nodes.summary: The Teichmüller space modeled on quasisymmetric homeomorphisms of the circle modulo Möbius transformations. The paper develops an analogue related to quasisymmetric curves in the Einstein Universe.

evidence.quote: a version of the universal Teichmüller space
9. Relationship with the universal Teichmüller space
9.1. An analogue of the universal Teichmüller space

nodes.label: Vafa-Witten Hodge cohomology prediction for Hilbert schemes
nodes.summary: The prediction that the natural inclusion from the image of compactly supported cohomology into the L2 Hodge cohomology of the Nakajima metric on Hilb_0^n(C^2) is surjective. The paper proves the equivalent image formula for all n at least 2.

nodes.label: Valuative criterion for G-uniform stability
nodes.summary: A criterion characterizing G-uniform K- and Ding-stability by vanishing Futaki invariant and a uniform inequality A_(X,D)(v_xi) >= delta_G S_L(v_xi) after twisting each G-invariant divisorial valuation by some xi in N_R.

nodes.label: Vanishing algebraic entropy group
nodes.summary: A finitely generated group whose algebraic entropy is zero, equivalently whose word-ball growth is subexponential.

evidence.quote: this group has subexponential growth. That is, it has vanishing algebraic entropy

nodes.label: Vanishing asymptotic slope
nodes.summary: The property that, at spatial infinity and far back in time, the surfaces open more slowly than any cone of positive angle. It is proved using a blowdown argument and cylindrical-scale control.

evidence.quote: open up slower than any cone

nodes.label: Variance between probability measures
nodes.summary: For probability measures mu1 and mu2 on a metric space, Var(mu1,mu2) is the double integral of squared distance against mu1 x mu2. This quantity controls H-concentration and continuity of metric-flow time-slices.

nodes.label: Variational approach to twisted Kähler-Einstein currents
nodes.summary: A proof strategy based on energy functionals, pluripotential theory, regularization, and valuations rather than continuity methods. It is the main methodological contribution of the paper.

evidence.quote: our variational method easily extends to the setting of twisted Kähler-Einstein currents
variational arguments and regularization techniques from pluripotential theory

nodes.label: Variational construction of abelian Higgs critical pairs
nodes.summary: A construction of nontrivial critical pairs for E_epsilon on arbitrary Hermitian line bundles with energy bounded above and below uniformly as epsilon -> 0. It combines minimization for nontrivial bundles with min-max for the trivial bundle.

evidence.quote: there exist critical couples (u_epsilon,nabla_epsilon) ... 0 < liminf E_epsilon <= limsup E_epsilon < infinity

nodes.label: Variational stability
nodes.summary: The nonnegativity of the second variation of the relevant geometric functional at a stationary solution.

nodes.label: Varifold class $S_Q$
nodes.summary: For $Q\in\{3/2,2,5/2,3,\ldots\}$, $S_Q$ is the class of stable codimension-one stationary integral varifolds admitting no classical singularities of density less than $Q$. The paper proves its main structure theory for this class.

evidence.quote: the class  $S_{Q}$  of stable codimension 1 stationary integral varifolds admitting no classical singularities of density  $< Q$
Varifold class  $\mathcal{S}_Q$  and the main result

nodes.label: varifold convergence
nodes.summary: Weak convergence of varifolds as Radon measures on the Grassmannian bundle. The proof passes from min-max varifolds in smooth approximations to a limiting varifold in the cylindrical-end space.

nodes.label: varifold multiplicity of a minimal hypersurface
nodes.summary: The same underlying minimal hypersurface can define different variational critical objects when counted with different positive integer weights as a varifold.

nodes.label: Varifold rectifiability from AC and bounded anisotropic first variation
aliases: rectifiability of varifolds with locally bounded anisotropic first variation
nodes.summary: The rectifiability conclusion for varifolds whose anisotropic first variation is a Radon measure, available when the integrand satisfies the atomic condition and instantiated by C^2-near-area examples.

evidence.quote: rectifiability of varifolds with locally bounded anisotropic first variation

nodes.label: integrality stability for varying-metric varifold limits
nodes.summary: If integral varifolds stationary for metrics with uniform C1 control converge as varifolds to a stationary limit and the metrics converge on the limit support in C0, then the limit varifold remains integral.

nodes.label: Vector-valued spherical harmonics
nodes.summary: Restrictions to $\mathbb{S}^{n-1}$ of $\mathbb{R}^n$-valued homogeneous harmonic polynomials of degree $k$. They are eigenfunctions of $-\Delta_{\mathbb{S}^{n-1}}$ with eigenvalue $\lambda_{n,k}=k(k+n-2)$.

nodes.label: Veronese harmonic map
nodes.summary: The harmonic map from S^2 to S^{2m} built from an eigenbasis of degree-m spherical harmonics, with degree m(m+1)/2; for even m it descends to a map RP^2 -> S^{2m}. It supplies equality and exceptional cases in the degree-index analysis.

nodes.label: Vertical difference quotient
nodes.summary: For t>0, the vertical difference quotient compares f at h and at the vertical translate of h by t, normalized by sqrt(t).

evidence.quote: $D _ {v} ^ {t} (h) \stackrel {\text {d e f}} {=} \frac {f (x , y , z + t) - f (h)}{\sqrt {t}}$

nodes.label: Vertical perimeter estimate for foliated corona decompositions
aliases: vertical perimeter for graphs with foliated corona decompositions
nodes.summary: For graphs carrying a foliated corona decomposition, vertical perimeter can be estimated through the controlled pseudoquad pieces and their local geometry. This is the bridge from the decomposition to the vertical-versus-horizontal perimeter inequality.

evidence.quote: 6.1. Vertical perimeter for graphs with foliated corona decompositions 132

nodes.label: Vertical perimeter in the Heisenberg group
nodes.summary: Vertical perimeter is a scale-dependent perimeter quantity measuring variation under vertical translations in the central direction. It is compared with horizontal perimeter in the paper's main isoperimetric formulation.

evidence.quote: 2.6. Vertical perimeter and parametric vertical perimeter 96
6. Vertical perimeter and foliated corona decompositions 132

nodes.label: Vertical versus horizontal perimeter inequality
nodes.summary: An isoperimetric-type inequality for surfaces in the Heisenberg group that controls vertical perimeter by horizontal perimeter. The functional inequality for vertical difference quotients is equivalent to this surface inequality, and it suffices to prove it for intrinsic Lipschitz graphs.

evidence.quote: the functional inequality (1.3) is equivalent to a certain isoperimetric-type inequality
it suffices to prove this isoperimetric-type inequality for a more restricted class of surfaces (intrinsic Lipschitz graphs; see §2.2)

nodes.label: Visibility manifold
nodes.summary: A complete simply connected manifold of nonpositive sectional curvature such that any two distinct points on its geometric boundary are joined by a geodesic line.

evidence.quote: if any two distinct points on the geometric boundary can be joined by a geodesic line

nodes.label: Visibility universal-cover condition
aliases: universal cover is a visibility manifold
nodes.summary: The hypothesis that the Riemannian universal cover of the finite-volume manifold is a visibility manifold. This is the extra geometric assumption in the paper's main conjecture and theorem.

evidence.quote: Suppose that the universal cover of  $M$  is a visibility manifold.

nodes.label: Visually antipodal points at infinity
nodes.summary: Two ideal boundary points are visually antipodal when they are the endpoints of a complete geodesic in the CAT(0) space.

nodes.label: Volume comparison growth argument
nodes.summary: A Riemannian comparison method using curvature bounds to convert displacement estimates for group orbits into bounds on group growth.

evidence.quote: Using a standard volume comparison argument

nodes.label: Volume contribution of a stable graph
nodes.summary: The contribution to the relevant Masur-Veech or counting volume coming from surfaces or multicurves with a fixed stable-graph type.

evidence.quote: Volume contribution of stable graphs with a single vertex
Volume contribution of stable graphs with 3 and more vertices

nodes.label: Volume density of a metric cone
nodes.summary: For a Calabi-Yau cone, the volume density is the volume of the unit ball centered at the vertex; it appears as the limiting normalized volume ratio.

evidence.quote: we denote by kappa(C)=Vol(B(O,1)) the volume density of C

nodes.label: Volume-distortion coefficients
nodes.summary: Coefficient functions encoding the model-volume distortion determined by curvature K, dimension N, time, and distance. They enter the density inequalities defining curvature-dimension conditions.

nodes.label: Volume epsilon-regularity for the boundary
nodes.summary: A volume-rigidity principle: if a boundary point has nearly half-Euclidean N-volume, then a smaller ball is GH-close to the half-space model. It bootstraps boundary balls in the improved neck structure argument.

evidence.quote: x in ∂X and H^N(B_1(x)) >= 1/2 omega_N - delta, then d_GH(B_{1/2}(x),B_{1/2}^{R^N_+}(0)) <= epsilon
evidence.section: 8.1 Improved neck structure theorem and Boundary volume rigidity

nodes.label: Volume growth order
nodes.summary: The exponent describing the growth rate of volumes of large metric balls at infinity. The paper uses it to distinguish the ALE, ALF, ALG, and ALH end families.

evidence.quote: According to the volume growth rate, they can be divided into four categories

nodes.label: Volume-penalized shape optimization
nodes.summary: A shape optimization objective includes a term m_i |Omega_i| with positive weight m_i, penalizing the Lebesgue measure of each optimized phase.

nodes.label: volume spectrum
nodes.summary: The sequence of p-widths of a Riemannian manifold. The paper compares its compact sublinear behavior with the cylindrical linear behavior.

nodes.label: Voronoi cell decomposition
nodes.summary: Given finitely many points in Euclidean space, the i-th Voronoi cell is the interior of the set of points whose nearest listed point is x_i.

evidence.quote: Recall that the Voronoi cells of {x_1,...,x_q} subset R^n are defined as
Omega_i = int {x in R^n : min_j |x - x_j| = |x - x_i|}

nodes.label: Vortex equations
nodes.summary: A first-order two-dimensional abelian Higgs system, nabla_partial1 u +/- i nabla_partial2 u = 0 and *F_nabla = +/- 1/2(1-|u|^2), whose finite-energy solutions have quantized energy. Planar blow-ups of the paper's solutions use this structure to prove integrality.

evidence.quote: known as the vortex equations--a two-dimensional counterpart of the instanton equations

nodes.label: W^{1,2} convergence
nodes.summary: Convergence in first-order Sobolev norm or weak Sobolev structure, used for passing analytic estimates to limit spaces.

nodes.label: W^{2,p} estimate for scalar-curvature equations
nodes.summary: An elliptic estimate giving W^{2,p} control of the density potential F, and W^{4,p} control of phi, for solutions of the generalized scalar-curvature system under entropy and background bounds.

nodes.label: Wasserstein geodesic
nodes.summary: A constant-speed geodesic in a Wasserstein space of probability measures, typically induced by L2 optimal transport. The density change-of-variables formula in the paper is formulated along such geodesics.

nodes.label: Wave trace contribution σ-hat_{1,q}
nodes.summary: The contribution to the wave trace coming from closed billiard trajectories in the Γ(1,q) orbit class.

nodes.label: Wave-trace detection of short billiard lengths
aliases: short periodic orbit lengths in wave trace singular support
nodes.summary: For nearly circular domains, lengths of periodic billiard orbits shorter than the perimeter must lie in the singular support of the wave trace.

nodes.label: Wave trace of a planar domain
nodes.summary: The tempered distribution w_Omega(t) = Tr cos(t sqrt(Delta_Omega)), determined by the Laplace spectrum and used to detect billiard length data through its singularities.

nodes.label: Wavefront set
nodes.summary: The microlocal subset of the punctured cotangent bundle recording directions in which a distribution fails to be smooth.

nodes.label: Weak chord arc property for H-disks
nodes.summary: A weaker intrinsic-extrinsic distance comparison for H-disks used as an input to the sharper chord arc result in the paper.

evidence.quote: a weak cord arc result derived in [22]
Chord arc properties for constant mean curvature disks

nodes.label: Weak conical approximation at infinity
nodes.summary: A preliminary diffeomorphic identification of the end of X with the end of its asymptotic cone, giving asymptotic convergence without a quantitative rate and decaying connection difference.

evidence.quote: at this point we know the metric is asymptotic to C at infinity, but without any quantitative rate of decay

nodes.label: Weak flow uniqueness through neck singularities
aliases: uniqueness of mean curvature flow through neck singularities
nodes.summary: The uniqueness property for weak mean curvature flows passing through neck singularities, obtained by combining mean-convex neighborhood information with prior weak-flow results.

evidence.quote: we obtain uniqueness of mean curvature flow through neck singularities

nodes.label: Weak minimizer of K-energy
nodes.summary: An element phi_* of E^1 at which the extended K-energy attains its infimum. The paper's regularity result shows such minimizers are smooth cscK potentials.

nodes.label: Weak set flow
nodes.summary: A closed spacetime set generated by an initial closed set and characterized by avoidance against all classical mean curvature flows with disjoint boundary and initial data.

evidence.section: 2.3 Weak set flows and level set flows

nodes.label: Weakly conformal branched minimal immersion
nodes.summary: A weakly conformal harmonic map from a surface into a sphere, possibly with branch points, viewed as a branched minimal immersion. Harmonic maps from S^2 or RP^2 into spheres have this property in the paper's setting.

nodes.label: Weakly harmonic map
aliases: weak solution of the harmonic map equation
nodes.summary: A Sobolev W^{1,2} map into a target manifold is weakly harmonic when it satisfies the harmonic map Euler-Lagrange equation in the weak sense.

evidence.quote: weakly is said to be weakly harmonic
evidence.section: 2 Existence and partial regularity of min-max harmonic maps

nodes.label: Weakly stable H-surface
aliases: weakly stable constant mean curvature surface
nodes.summary: An oriented H-surface satisfying the weak stability condition for the constant mean curvature variational problem. The excerpt uses compact weakly stable H-surfaces in the proof of a later corollary and invokes curvature estimates for them.

evidence.quote: there exists a compact, oriented, weakly stable H_1-surface M_1 \subset W
Complete minimal surfaces of index one and stable constant mean curvature surfaces

nodes.label: Weighted asymptotic cone at infinity
nodes.summary: The normal affine variety obtained as the limit of weighted algebraic rescalings of X' using the Reeb weights. Equivalently, it is described by weighted leading terms or by the associated graded ring of the metric degree filtration.

evidence.quote: Then the weighted asymptotic cone W=lim Y_j is a normal affine algebraic variety
the leading terms generate the ideal I_W

nodes.label: Weighted $C^{2,\alpha}$ cone norm
nodes.summary: A scale-weighted $C^{2,\alpha}$ norm on cone graphs, defined by taking the supremum of $r^{-\beta}$ times a local $C^{2,\alpha}$ norm over scales $0<r\leq1$.

nodes.label: Weighted elliptic estimate with growth weights
aliases: elliptic estimates with polynomial growth weights
elliptic estimates with exponential growth weights
nodes.summary: Elliptic estimates formulated with polynomial or exponential growth weights. These estimates support the construction of holomorphic functions on asymptotic gravitational instantons.

evidence.quote: Elliptic estimates with polynomial growth weights
Elliptic estimates with exponential growth weights

nodes.label: Weighted elliptic estimates on conical ends
aliases: weighted elliptic theory for conical ends
nodes.summary: Elliptic estimates in weighted function spaces on ends asymptotic to metric cones, used to solve Poisson equations and propagate decay estimates.

evidence.quote: facts on weighted elliptic theory for ends of Riemannian manifolds asymptotic to cones

nodes.label: Product weight on a tensor product of Finsler spaces
nodes.summary: For a tensor product of weighted smooth Finslerian spaces, the product weight is formed as the direct sum of the component weights, V = opulus_i V_i. This construction is used in the proof of tensorization for weighted flag N-Ricci lower bounds.

evidence.quote: with dimension n = sum_i n_i and weight V = \bigoplus_i V_i

nodes.label: Weighted flag N-Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: The finite-dimensional weighted Finsler Ricci curvature Ric_N combining Ricci curvature, the Hessian of a weight, and a gradient-square correction depending on N-n. On smooth Finslerian spaces, lower bounds for this quantity are equivalent to CD*(K,N) and CD(K,N).

evidence.quote: the CD^*(K,N) condition, as well as the CD(K,N) condition, are both equivalent to the weighted flag N-Ricci curvature being bounded from below by K
Ric_N(xi_i) := Ric(xi_i) + Hess V_i(xi,xi) - 1/(N_i-n_i) <nabla V_i,xi_i>^2 >= K |xi_i|^2

nodes.label: Weighted flag Ricci curvature
nodes.summary: A Finslerian weighted Ricci curvature notion whose lower bound by K is equivalent to CD(K,infinity) on smooth Finslerian spaces. The paper uses the tensorization of this lower bound to recover tensorization of CD(K,infinity).

evidence.quote: For smooth Finslerian spaces, the CD(K,infinity) condition is equivalent to the weighted flag Ricci curvature being bounded from below by K
By construction, the weighted flag Ricci curvature bound has the tensorization property

nodes.label: Weighted geodesic-ball perforation
nodes.summary: A family of domains obtained by removing geodesic balls B_{r_{epsilon,p}}(p) centered at the epsilon-net points, with radii chosen so each small boundary area equals beta(p) times its Voronoi cell volume.

nodes.label: Weighted geodesic X-ray transform
nodes.summary: A version of the geodesic X-ray transform in which the integrand is modified by weights. The excerpt states that weights must be allowed in the normal gauge analysis.

evidence.quote: where one also needs to allow weights

nodes.label: Weighted Hilbert space
nodes.summary: A Hilbert space equipped with a growth or decay weight, used as the functional-analytic setting for solving elliptic equations on noncompact gravitational instantons.

nodes.label: Weighted L2 cohomology
nodes.summary: For a complete Riemannian manifold and positive weight, the cohomology of closed weighted L2 forms modulo differentials whose primitives and differentials lie in the required weighted L2 spaces. The unweighted case recovers ordinary L2 cohomology.

nodes.label: Weighted L2 harmonic forms
nodes.summary: Weighted L2 forms annihilated by both the exterior derivative and its weighted formal adjoint. Under closed-range hypotheses they identify with reduced weighted L2 cohomology via Kodaira decomposition.

nodes.label: Weighted Laplace bound from Steklov isoperimetry
aliases: weighted Laplace upper bound via homogenisation
nodes.summary: Passing the Steklov isoperimetric bound through the homogenisation limit gives lambda_k(M,g,beta) times the total beta-mass bounded by C_d Vol_g(M)^{(d-2)/d} k^{2/d}.

evidence.quote: lambda_k(M,g,beta) int_M beta dmu_g <= C_d Vol_g(M)^{(d-2)/d} k^{2/d}
evidence.section: 1.2.3 Transferring bounds for Steklov eigenvalues to bounds for Laplace eigenvalues

nodes.label: Weighted Laplace eigenvalue problem with density
nodes.summary: Given a nonnegative density beta, the weighted Laplace problem solves Delta_g f = lambda beta f, with eigenvalues characterized variationally by Rayleigh quotients using beta in the L^2 denominator.

nodes.label: Weighted Laplace eigenvalue problem
nodes.summary: On a closed Riemannian manifold, find eigenpairs satisfying -Delta phi = lambda beta phi with beta a positive smooth weight. The eigenfunctions are normalized in L^2(M,beta dmu_g).

evidence.section: 1.1 The Laplace and Steklov eigenvalue problems

nodes.label: Weighted lattice point count
nodes.summary: A lattice point count in which lattice points contribute prescribed weights rather than all contributing one. In this paper it is used as an intermediate counting problem on the way to Pólya inequalities.

evidence.quote: From the weighted lattice point count towards Pólya's conjecture

nodes.label: Weighted Moser-Trudinger functional on a closed surface
nodes.summary: The weighted exponential functional $F(u)=\int_\Sigma (e^{u^2}-1) f\,dv_{g_0}$ on $H^1(\Sigma)$. Its constrained critical points are the main limiting objects of the paper.

nodes.label: Weighted Moser-Trudinger inequality on a closed surface
nodes.summary: The finiteness property that the weighted exponential integral is uniformly bounded on the $H^1$-sphere of radius squared $\beta$ exactly for $\beta\le 4\pi$. It supplies the critical threshold for the variational problem.

evidence.quote: $\sup  _ {u \in H ^ {1}, \| u \| _ {H ^ {1}} ^ {2} = \beta} \int_ {\Sigma} e ^ {u ^ {2}} f d v _ {g _ {0}} <   + \infty \quad \Leftrightarrow \quad \beta \leq 4 \pi$

nodes.label: Weighted perturbation quantity Q
nodes.summary: A curvature-scale-weighted norm of the comparison perturbation, roughly exponential in time times rho^E times |h|. It is designed so perturbations at high curvature scales remain small when viewed from lower-curvature regions.

nodes.label: Weighted QFB cohomology
nodes.summary: The weighted cohomology theory attached directly to a QFB metric, with denominators shifted by v^{-1}. Via the conformal relation g_QFC = v^2 g_QFB, it is rewritten as a QFC weighted cohomology group with a degree-dependent weight shift.

nodes.label: Weighted QFB-conormal forms
nodes.summary: Weighted L2 forms whose iterated covariant derivatives along arbitrary QFB vector fields remain in the same weighted L2 class. They allow the weighted QFC cohomology complex to be represented by conormal forms.

nodes.label: Weighted QFC cohomology
nodes.summary: The weighted L2 cohomology of the interior of a manifold with fibered corners equipped with a QFC metric, using multiweights x^a from boundary defining functions. It is the primary computable cohomology theory in the paper.

nodes.label: Weighted Schauder estimate
nodes.summary: An elliptic regularity estimate in weighted Hölder norms, used to control solutions on geometries with decay, ends, or collapsed scales. The excerpt lists it as a dedicated analytic section.

nodes.label: Weighted Steklov eigenvalue problem with boundary density
nodes.summary: On a compact Riemannian manifold with boundary, a nonnegative boundary density rho weights the Steklov eigenvalue problem; the paper denotes the resulting eigenvalues by sigma_m(rho).

nodes.label: Weitzenböck formula for the Dirac operator
nodes.summary: An identity relating the Dirac operator to the spin connection Laplacian and curvature terms; after integration it yields an energy identity containing scalar curvature and boundary contributions.

evidence.quote: We then apply the Weitzenböck formula to  $s^{(l)}$ , and integrate over the domain  $\Omega_{\lambda_l}$ . The resulting integral formula contains a term involving the scalar curvature, as well as a boundary term.

nodes.label: Wente isoperimetric inequality for mappings
aliases: functional isoperimetric inequality for sphere maps
nodes.summary: For $u\in W^{1,n-1}(\mathbb{S}^{n-1};\mathbb{R}^n)$, the quantities satisfy $D_{n-1}(u)^{n/(n-1)}\ge P_{n-1}(u)^{n/(n-1)}\ge |V_n(u)|$. Equality in the first part characterizes generalized conformality, and equality in the second characterizes round-sphere images.

nodes.label: Weyl constant for the Euclidean Laplacian
nodes.summary: The dimensional constant C_d = 1/((4π)^{d/2} Γ(d/2+1)) appearing in the leading term of Weyl's law for Euclidean Laplacian eigenvalue counting functions.

nodes.label: Weyl curvature tensor
nodes.summary: The conformally invariant trace-free curvature component of a conformal Lorentzian structure. The paper analyzes its G0-module structure and uses special Weyl values to define lightlike hyperplane distributions.

evidence.section: 3.1. The Cartan geometry associated to a conformal Lorentzian structure

nodes.label: Weyl law for the Laplace spectrum
nodes.summary: The leading asymptotic for the Laplace eigenvalue counting function: the count grows like the Euclidean unit ball volume times the Riemannian volume times $\lambda^n$, up to a lower-order error.

evidence.quote: is known as the Weyl Law

nodes.label: Weyl law for Laplacian counting functions
nodes.summary: The asymptotic formula N_Ω(λ)=C_d|Ω|_d λ^d+o(λ^d) for either Dirichlet or Neumann Laplacian counting functions as λ tends to infinity.

evidence.quote: the leading term asymptotics of the counting functions is given by Weyl's law

nodes.label: Weyl leading term for Laplacian counting
nodes.summary: The leading asymptotic term C_d |Ω|_d λ^d for Dirichlet or Neumann Laplacian eigenvalue counting functions on a bounded Euclidean domain.

nodes.label: Weyl paraproduct and paradifferential calculus
nodes.summary: The paper's symbolic calculus based on spatial Littlewood-Paley decompositions, Weyl paraproducts T_f g, and paradifferential operators T_a. It is used to formulate the paradifferential flow, commutators, normal forms, and energy functionals.

evidence.quote: For multilinear analysis, we will consistently use paradifferential calculus
we choose to work with the Weyl quantization

nodes.label: Weyl remainder
nodes.summary: The error term left after subtracting the Weyl law leading term from the eigenvalue counting function or a related spectral projector asymptotic.

evidence.quote: We refer to $E(\lambda)$ as a Weyl remainder

nodes.label: Winding number of a closed billiard trajectory
nodes.summary: The integer recording how many times a closed billiard trajectory winds around the billiard table; the paper focuses on winding number one.

nodes.label: Word norm
nodes.summary: The length of a group element with respect to a chosen finite symmetric generating set.

evidence.quote: where  $|\cdot|_w$  is the word norm with respect to  $S$

nodes.label: Yau complex analytic compactification conjecture
nodes.summary: The conjectural principle that every complete Calabi-Yau manifold admits a compactification in the complex analytic sense. The paper proves this in the fast-decay ALG and ALH non-splitting settings.

evidence.quote: Yau conjectured that every complete Calabi-Yau manifold can be compactified

nodes.label: Yau minimal-hypersurface abundance conjecture
nodes.summary: The assertion that a closed three-dimensional manifold contains infinitely many immersed minimal surfaces, generalized here to infinitely many smooth embedded closed minimal hypersurfaces in dimensions 3 through 7.

nodes.label: Yau-Tian-Donaldson conjecture
nodes.summary: The conjectural correspondence relating existence of canonical Kähler metrics to algebro-geometric stability. In the Fano anticanonical setting it predicts Kähler-Einstein metrics exactly under K-polystability.

evidence.quote: relate the existence of a canonical metric in a given Kähler cohomology class to an algebroid-geometric condition of stability

nodes.label: Young measure
nodes.summary: A measurable family of probability measures generated by a weakly convergent sequence, encoding oscillations not seen in the weak limit.

evidence.quote: there exists a measurable map ... nu_x

nodes.label: Zariski tangent cone of a sheaf singular locus
nodes.summary: The C*-invariant reduced subvariety obtained as the tangent cone of Sing(E) at the point. It is excluded from the smooth convergence region and enters the analytic tangent cone singular set.

nodes.label: 0-complete Ricci flow spacetime
nodes.summary: A completeness condition for Ricci flow spacetimes requiring bounded-curvature time-vector or time-slice curves to have limits. It is part of the definition of singular Ricci flow used in the paper.

nodes.label: Zeroth-order coefficient potential
nodes.summary: A smooth scalar coefficient V multiplying u in the wave equation. The paper's inverse problem is to recover this potential from boundary data.

nodes.label: Zeroth-order potential recovery under Lorentzian curvature bounds
aliases: uniqueness for Lorentzian Calderón under curvature bounds
nodes.summary: The uniqueness property that the Cauchy data set determines the smooth potential V on Lorentzian manifolds satisfying the paper's temporal, curvature, causality, nontrapping, and boundary-contact hypotheses. This is the paper's central inverse result, expressed without using a paper-local theorem label.
